title: fs-rand perf lanes: ziggurat strict-gate, SIMD bulk generation, nightly statistical battery
description: SPLIT from frankensim-epic-bedrock-6ys.19 (distributions landed: gamma/beta/Dirichlet/alias/vMF/truncated with deterministic consumption contracts, all cross-ISA bit-deterministic incl sampled values - golden 0x42246e2856de673c). THIS BEAD: (1) ZIGGURAT normal as the PERF path: table generation deterministic (exact-rational layer boundaries or bit-pattern constants - NO libm in table gen), then EITHER prove bitwise cross-ISA equality on both reference machines (trj pipeline) and admit to strict mode, OR gate it out of strict mode explicitly (fast-mode-only) - the bead's own acceptance criterion; Box-Muller stays the strict default regardless. (2) SIMD bulk generation: 4x/8x Philox counter lanes via fs-simd Ops for fill-heavy consumers (LBM init, MC batches); acceptance = BITWISE tier-equivalence vs the scalar stream (the fs-simd twin doctrine). (3) Nightly statistical battery: PractRand-class suite as documented DEV-ONLY oracle (isolated per dependency policy - not in the production graph), inter-stream correlation matrix sweep at scale, CI wiring with failure->issue discipline (coordinate with huq.4 owner; gradcheck + ULP-ledger notes already left there). Key files: crates/fs-rand/src/{lib,dist,philox}.rs, CONTRACT extended-distributions section.

title: fs-fft-perf-multidim: radix-4/8 kernels, SIMD lanes, 2D/3D pencils, roofline gate
description:
SPLIT from frankensim-epic-bedrock-6ys.11 (fs-fft v1), which landed the correctness-first transform core: radix-2 DIF Stockham autosort (OTFFT formulation, butterfly (a+b,(a-b)w), stride doubles as length halves), strict-mode twiddles from fs-math det sin/cos, r2c via half-size packing+untangle, DCT-II/III via FFT folding. All oracle-tested (naive O(n^2) DFT n<=512, Parseval, shift theorem, linearity, round-trip) and cross-ISA bit-deterministic: golden hash 0xbd5568d233f4b4bc identical on M4 Pro (aarch64) and Threadripper (x86-64).

THIS BEAD is the recorded performance + dimensionality scope v1 deliberately deferred (plan section 6.3 full ambition):
1. Radix-4 and radix-8 Stockham kernels (fewer passes over memory; keep the radix-2 stage for odd log2 residues). The naive-DFT oracle + golden-hash tests in crates/fs-fft/src/lib.rs are the acceptance harness: any kernel change must keep the oracle green; a golden-hash bump requires semantic justification recorded here (twiddle-application ORDER changes bits legitimately when radix changes - bump once, document, re-verify cross-ISA on trj).
2. SIMD lanes via fs-simd Ops table (scalar twin stays the reference; fused mul_add discipline preserved so results stay deterministic per the fs-simd contract).
3. c2r inverse for RealFft (v1 ships forward r2c only) + in-place API rework if profitable.
4. 2D/3D transforms as pencil decompositions: cache-blocked transposes (fs-substrate tile geometry), executor tiling via fs-exec two-lane runtime, cancellation polls at pencil boundaries (Decalogue P7).
5. Roofline gate: >=40% of memory-bound peak on both reference machines using fs-substrate STREAM triad numbers as the denominator; results logged as fs-obs events. This is the plan honest-performance bar (Decalogue P6) - measure, do not vibe.
6. Mixed-radix general-n and/or Bluestein for arbitrary sizes: OPTIONAL stretch, only if a consumer bead actually needs non-power-of-two (fs-cheb does not - Chebyshev grids are chosen power-of-two+1 and fold to power-of-two DCTs).

Key files: crates/fs-fft/src/lib.rs (v1 core + tests), crates/fs-fft/CONTRACT.md (no-claim boundaries list exactly what this bead removes), crates/fs-simd (Ops table), crates/fs-exec (two-lane executor), crates/fs-substrate (CACHE_LINE, bandwidth). Cross-ISA verification pipeline: tar minimal workspace to trj:/data/tmp, extract into a fresh mktemp -d dir, private CARGO_HOME/CARGO_TARGET_DIR, cargo test --offline detached with nohup, poll the log. NOTE: recursive-delete commands are blocked by the dcg guard on this machine - always extract into a NEW mktemp dir instead of clearing an old one.

title: fs-la-batched-perf: >=60% peak FLOPs for batched small-dense size classes (capsule microkernels, autotuned interleave, vectorized pivoted LU)
description: SPLIT from 6ys.4 (same discipline as frankensim-xdgf, the GEMM perf lane). The batched small-dense CORRECTNESS layer landed in fs-la::batched with the SoA entry-plane layout (128B-aligned planes via fs_soa::FieldBuf, stride quantum 16 f64), safe auto-vectorized loops shaped lanes-across-elements, and the batch-membership bitwise-invariance contract (matrix m in batch of N == matrix m alone, bitwise, tested; golden 0x0377_a8c9_5992_aee9). THIS bead is the 6ys.4 acceptance criterion deferred at landing: >=60% of peak FLOPs on BOTH ISAs for size classes {4,6,8,12,16,24,32,48}, roofline-table evidence ledgered (fs-roofline conventions). Scope: (a) measure the safe-Rust baseline per size class per ISA first (GFLOP/s tables in test logs) - the autovectorizer may already be respectable for GEMM/Cholesky planes; (b) where short, add arch-specific capsule microkernels (fs-simd registry pattern: <300-line capsule + SAFETY.md + unsafe-capsules.json registration) that keep PER-ELEMENT op order identical to the scalar path (lanes are independent matrices, so bitwise equality with scalar MUST be preserved and asserted - this is stronger than fs-simd's elementwise-only promise and is achievable here BECAUSE there are no cross-lane reductions); (c) autotuned interleave/blocking width per machine via the same autotuner lane xdgf uses (bit-neutral parameter: order within a matrix fixed); (d) lane-vectorized pivoted LU (currently per-matrix scalar; vectorize the update loops, keep per-matrix pivot selection scalar); (e) f32 and f32-store/f64-accumulate batched variants for the LBM moment path. CONSTRAINTS: golden 0x0377_a8c9_5992_aee9 must NOT change (any bump needs semantic justification); batch-membership invariance test must keep passing; no threading (fs-exec drivers own parallelism); Cx chunking stays in drivers. Consumers waiting: fs-feec assembly (tfz.5/.6), fiber-beam sections, IPC contact Hessians, LBM moments.

title: fs-solver saddle-point block preconditioners: Stokes-class Schur approximations over the FEEC mixed spaces
description: SPLIT from tfz.10 (slices 1-3 landed in crates/fs-solver: resumable CG/MINRES/GMRES with deterministic reductions + structured stall diagnoses, matrix-free p-MG over the fs-feec tensor hierarchy with exact-injection transfers and SA-AMG-CG coarse, f32-inner/f64-refine mixed precision). THIS bead: block preconditioners for saddle-point systems (Stokes, incompressibility, contact) - block-diagonal/triangular preconditioners with Schur-complement approximations (pressure-mass for Stokes; PCD-class documented per physics). BUILD ON: fs-solver MinresState (the natural saddle-point outer solver), LinearOp trait (block operators compose as struct wrappers), fs-feec tensor de Rham spaces (velocity in H1^3 or H(div), pressure in L2 - the discrete pair from tfz.6 slice 2; note the L2 Legendre mass is DIAGONAL, making the pressure-mass Schur approximation trivially invertible - a real payoff). FIXTURE: lid-driven-cavity-free Stokes on the unit cube via the tensor spaces (assemble B = div coupling from the Kronecker derivative G; the discrete inf-sup pair Q_r^3/Q_{r-1}^disc is the classic stable choice); MINRES on [[A B^T],[B 0]] with blockdiag(AMG-or-pMG on A, pressure mass on Schur) - iteration counts flat-ish across h (envelope gate). GATES: solutions vs dense LU on fixture scale; divergence-freeness of the velocity solution to solver tol; iteration envelope; resume bitwise (inherited); both-ISA golden. DEPENDS on nothing open (tensor spaces + solvers all landed); the simplicial-vector-families bead (frankensim-dcng) is the UNSTRUCTURED counterpart, independent.

title: fs-feec high-order perf: >=30% peak FLOPs sum-factorized apply at p=4 both ISAs (roofline-gated)
description: SPLIT from tfz.6 (the perf acceptance criterion; correctness landed there with the O(r^4)-per-element sum-factorized tensor apply matching the assembled Kronecker reference to 1e-12). THIS bead: measure and hit >=30% of peak FLOPs at p=4 on M4 Pro AND Threadripper, ledgered per fs-roofline conventions. Scope: (a) baseline measurement of the existing safe-Rust apply (crates/fs-feec/src/highorder/hex.rs apply_stiffness - per-element gather/contract/scatter with dense (r+1)^2 1D matrices); (b) the contractions are small dense GEMMs - route through fs-la batched/GEMM kernels or fs-tilelang lowering where profitable; (c) allocation hoisting (the per-apply scratch vectors); (d) apply-throughput-vs-p sweep table (the bead's perf-lane deliverable); (e) if the target needs intrinsics, the fs-simd capsule lane per xdgf/9ekv precedent. CONSTRAINTS: golden 0xaaf1_076a_196c_6902 must not change (bit-identical restructures only, or semantic-justification bump); release-profile measurements (debug numbers are meaningless for roofline claims); tie into fz2.4 perf-regression CI when it lands.

title: fs-feec simplicial high-order vector families: Nedelec/RT tree (H(curl)/H(div)/L2 on tets, r>=2) with vector-dof orientation
description: SPLIT from tfz.6 per the established discipline (slices 1-3 landed there: full TENSOR-PRODUCT de Rham complex at r=1..6 with exact Kronecker derivatives + commuting projections, and simplicial hierarchical H1 to r=6 with the sorted-global orientation convention and G3 permutation batteries). THIS bead: the simplicial high-order VECTOR families - hierarchical Nedelec first-kind H(curl) and Raviart-Thomas H(div) on tets at r>=2 plus discontinuous L2, completing the simplicial PrLambda-k tree. WHAT EXISTS TO BUILD ON: crates/fs-feec/src/highorder/simplex.rs (H1 hierarchy, Duffy quadrature, entity dof management, boundary masks, the sorted-global orientation convention PROVEN by the r=3 signed-permutation equivariance test); whitney.rs lowest-order edge/face forms (the r=1 members of these families); derham.rs shows the commuting-projection test pattern. KEY DESIGN CONSTRAINTS from the landed work: (1) orientation for VECTOR dofs is harder than H1 - edge tangents and face normals need the sorted-global convention extended to FRAMES (edge tangent = towards larger global index; face frame = sorted-triple circulation, matching what deram1/deram2 already pin at r=1); (2) the G3 battery must cover both the signed-permutation level (where kernels have definite parity) and the physics level (where bases mix); (3) commuting diagram tests vs the H1 gradient (pick the family consistently - first-kind Nedelec pairs with the full sequence); (4) unisolvence via mass SPD; MMS via L2 PROJECTION ladders (the derham.rs precedent - full curl-curl solves need tfz.10) + dd=0 through the discrete operators. Duffy quadrature and Legendre kernels are all in place. ACCEPTANCE: exact-sequence dimension counts at r=1..4; grad/curl/div chain maps with dd=0 to machine cancellation; conformity across shared entities (tangential trace for H(curl), normal trace for H(div)); projection convergence ladders; permutation batteries; golden + trj cross-ISA.

title: ADDENDUM: Epistemic engine for physical claims — governance, phases, risks, identity
description:
## Mission
The plan addendum (`COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md`) is an identity shift, not a feature list. The base plan makes **composition trustworthy** (multi-representation geometry glued by cellular-sheaf cohomology, physics coupled through certified interfaces, error bounds that survive every seam because they compose algebraically). The addendum's thesis: trustworthy composition is the *foundation*, not the product. Everything done *with* a composed object — optimizing it, re-solving variants, budgeting evidence, doubting it, merging concurrent edits, defending it to a regulator — inherits that algebraic structure. Nineteen proposals claim that inheritance.

> **FrankenSim should not be a simulator that emits numbers. It should be an epistemic engine for physical claims — a system that maintains typed, priced, auditable beliefs about designs, where solvers, surrogates, symmetry arguments, and physical experiments are interchangeable evidence sources ranked by cost per unit of certainty, and where agents are the query load. The product is justified belief at minimum cost.**

This epic is the ADDENDUM META/GOVERNANCE tracker: it holds the eight design principles, the phase roadmap (Spine → Flywheel → Leverage → Horizon), the four phase-milestone gates, the risk register R1–R10, and the governance rules that keep nineteen proposals from becoming nineteen parallel obituaries. The nineteen proposals themselves live under the five ADDENDUM LAYER epics (epic-flywheel, epic-epistype, epic-coupling, epic-selfknow, epic-gtm).

## The eight design principles (P1–P8), which every addendum bead must honor
- **P1 — Buy correctness at the level of algebra.** Prefer formulations where the invariant holds identically (δ∘δ=0, discrete de Rham exactness, type-checked couplings) over per-bug patches.
- **P2 — Certificates must compose.** Any accuracy/sensitivity/consistency statement that cannot propagate through the ledger DAG is a side channel, and side channels rot.
- **P3 — Every certificate ships with its cheapest independent falsifier.** Certificates prove what the model claims; falsifiers probe whether the claims connect to reality.
- **P4 — Type the epistemics.** verified/validated/estimated are different kinds of knowledge with different composition rules; the type system must refuse to launder one into another.
- **P5 — Design for the swarm's deficits.** No intuition → certified abstraction ladders + explanation objects. No memory → contracts + tombstones. Miscalibration → falsification budgets. Goodhart tendency → distrust optima by default.
- **P6 — Exploit the check/produce asymmetry.** Verifying a candidate is vastly cheaper than producing one; this asymmetry is the economic engine (speculation, cheap re-verification, third-party audit).
- **P7 — Concentrate research risk.** One research-grade bet at a time, chosen so its hard problem unlocks the most downstream value.
- **P8 — The plan itself must be falsifiable.** Every proposal carries a kill criterion with a measurement and a deadline. Unmeasured survival is not survival.

## The flywheel (why the top four proposals dominate)
Four proposals form a closed economic loop: **certified speculation (9)** makes answers cheap; **incremental recomputation (2)** makes variants cheap; **version control for physics (10)** makes concurrency safe; **compounding swarm memory (E)** makes it all cumulative. Each accelerates the others. On top sits **declarative queries against physics (8)** — the imperative→declarative inversion databases made in the 1970s, available here because there is a closed, priced algebra over the operations. Wrapped around the loop is the **epistemic type system (3, F, 6, D, 12)** that prevents the one fatal failure mode a certificate culture invites: being precisely wrong with a pedigree.

## Scoring, in one line
Nineteen proposals were scored 0–1000 on Useful/Innov/Pract/Accret/Agent, mean-ranked, under declared adversarial self-review. Composite order: 9 › 2 › 10 › 8 › E › 3 › 6 › F › A › C › 1 › B › D › 11 › 13 › 12 › 5 › 7 › 4. The hundreds digit is signal, the tens digit is texture. Two robustness facts matter: (1) 9/2/10 top both the composite and the lens-off (Ex-A) ranking — the headline order survives the confessed agent-lens bias; (2) A/B/C/D's top-tier standing is *conditional on agents being the operator* (Governance Rule 4).

## Members
add-doctrine-governance (P1–P8 + governance rules + scoring discipline), milestone-spine (Phase 0), milestone-flywheel (Phase 1 + Proposal 9's six-month checkpoint), milestone-leverage (Phase 2), milestone-horizon (Phase 3, per-proposal gated), risk-register (R1–R10 early-warning instrumentation + quarterly kill enforcement).

## Success criteria
Every proposal's kill measurement is instrumented before the proposal is funded (P8). Phase gates are defined as measured states, not dates. Quarterly, in writing, each live proposal is checked against its kill criterion; a proposal whose kill measurement was never instrumented counts as killed. WIP outside the current phase (headcount-weighted) is the honest metric of a plan losing discipline (R10) and is watched.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- NOTE: `add-layer:Ln` here is the addendum's THEMATIC layer (economic engine / epistemics / coupling / self-knowledge / GTM), NOT the base L0–L6 architecture stack. Member crates still sit at their true base layer and must respect lower-knows-nothing-of-higher dependency direction.

title: Addendum doctrine & governance: P1–P8 principles, one-research-bet rule, quarterly kill-criteria enforcement, build-composite/sell-Ex-A
description:
## What
The addendum's governance layer as living doctrine, not a preface nobody re-reads. Encodes the eight design principles (P1–P8) and the four governance rules that keep nineteen proposals from becoming nineteen parallel programs (which the risk register calls "an obituary", R10).

## The eight principles (each must be checkable, not aspirational)
- **P1 Buy correctness at the level of algebra** — prefer identical invariants (δδ=0, de Rham exactness, type-checked couplings) over per-bug patches.
- **P2 Certificates must compose** — anything that cannot propagate through the ledger DAG is a side channel; reject side channels in design review.
- **P3 Every certificate ships its cheapest independent falsifier** (Proposal 6 operationalizes this).
- **P4 Type the epistemics** — verified/validated/estimated never launder into each other (Proposal 3 enforces).
- **P5 Design for the swarm's deficits** — abstraction ladders (A) + explanations (B) for no-intuition; contracts + tombstones (E) for no-memory; falsification budgets (6) for miscalibration; distrust optima (D) for Goodhart.
- **P6 Exploit the check/produce asymmetry** — verifying ≪ producing; the economic engine of speculation (9), cheap re-verification (12), third-party audit.
- **P7 Concentrate research risk** — exactly one research-grade bet at a time.
- **P8 The plan itself must be falsifiable** — every proposal has a kill criterion with a measurement and a deadline.

## The four governance rules (Part IV.3)
1. **One research bet at a time.** Phase 1's bet is Proposal 9 (its hard problem — certified a-posteriori bounds — is partially purchasable from the equilibrated-estimator literature and unlocks the widest downstream cone). Proposal 8's general planner and Proposal A's general certification explicitly do NOT open as research programs; they wait for the flywheel's telemetry and the RB beachhead respectively.
2. **Kill criteria enforced quarterly, in writing**, against the measurements named per-proposal. A proposal whose kill measurement was never instrumented counts as killed — unmeasured survival is not survival.
3. **Build in composite order, sell in Ex-A order.** Engineering priority = Mean column; commercial narrative + partnership investment = Ex-A column (12 and 7 lead the sell-side despite mid-table composite ranks).
4. **The circularity discount stands until lifted by evidence.** Proposals A, B, C, D's top-tier standing is conditional on agents being the dominant operator; if the first vertical ships human-driven, they are formally deferred to Phase 3 regardless of their scores.

## Scoring discipline (self-applied, per P8 and §II.4)
The 0–1000 scores are a confident-looking artifact from a confabulation-prone mechanism. Treat the hundreds digit as signal, the tens as texture. Two declared biases: (a) A–F were generated by optimizing the agent lens, so their Agent scores are partially circular — the Ex-A column is the falsifier (strip the lens and A/B/C/D drop to the 725–740 band); (b) the robustness check that passed: 9/2/10 top both composite and lens-off rankings.

## Acceptance criteria
This bead is DONE when: (1) P1–P8 appear as an enforced checklist in the contribution/design-review flow; (2) each of the nineteen proposals has its kill measurement wired to a dashboard BEFORE the proposal is funded; (3) a quarterly written kill-review ritual exists with an owner. This is a standing governance artifact, not a code deliverable.

## Testing
A "governance audit" script (or checklist) that lists every live addendum proposal, its kill metric, whether that metric is instrumented, and its last quarterly review date. Failing to instrument a kill metric flags the proposal as killed-by-default.

## Dependencies
None hard. This bead constrains every other addendum bead.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **FEATURE-FLAG GOVERNANCE (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule):** every [F]/[M] deliverable ships behind a NAMED CARGO FEATURE, disabled by default, with a no-claim boundary in its CONTRACT.md; promotion to the default path requires the proposal's Gauntlet tier + kill-metric green.
- **NOTE ON `add-layer:Ln` LABELS:** the addendum's five architectural layers (L1 Flywheel … L5 GTM) are a THEMATIC grouping and are NOT the base architecture's L0–L6 dependency stack (SUBSTRATE…HELM). Actual crate placement + dependency direction must respect the base architecture (lower knows nothing of higher).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Optimality: the governance-audit emits machine-readable JSON (proposal -> kill-metric -> instrumented bool -> last-review-date) so it can gate CI rather than being a manual checklist.
close_reason: Implemented by extending crates/fs-govern (my crate from xpck.6; added crate:fs-govern label). Two new modules encode the addendum DOCTRINE + PROPOSALS as machine-readable data. doctrine.rs: the eight design principles P1-P8 (principles(), each id/name/statement) and the four governance rules (rules(): one-research-bet, quarterly-kill-enforcement/'unmeasured survival is not survival', build-composite/sell-Ex-A, circularity-discount). proposals.rs: all NINETEEN proposals in composite Mean order, each {id,name,phase,mean,kill_metric,owning_bead,instrumented}; governance_audit() enforces Governance Rule 2 / P8 (every proposal must DECLARE a non-empty kill metric AND an owning bead — a proposal whose kill measurement was never instrumented counts as killed) and counts instrumented (0 baseline); proposals_json() emits the deterministic machine-readable record for CI gating. Kill metrics + owning beads wired for all 19 (9->lmp4.1, 2->lmp4.8, 10->lmp4.12, 8->lmp4.16, E->lmp4.13, 3->qmao.1, 6->qmao.4, F->qmao.3, A->knh1.4, C->knh1.6, 1->bk0o.1, B->knh1.5, D->qmao.5, 11->bk0o.4, 13->knh1.1, 12->qmao.6, 5->knh1.3, 7->jwq8.1, 4->bk0o.7). Tests (tests/governance.rs, 8): P1-P8, 4 rules numbered, all-19-unique-ids, descending-composite-order, per-proposal kill-metric+bead-owner, audit-complete-zero-instrumented, owner mapping, JSON well-formed+deterministic. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-govern 16/16 (8 governance + 8 register), xtask check-all fs-govern-compliant. No-claim: encodes governance DATA (does not measure kill metrics — a dashboard flips instrumented); does not read the beads DB.

title: ADDENDUM MILESTONE — Phase 0 SPINE complete (three-color schema, falsifier gate, Goodhart policy, tombstones, type checker)
description:
## What
ADDENDUM PHASE 0 — SPINE. The exit gate for the schema-and-policy layer that must exist before the flywheel's cheap, aggressive machinery is safe to run. Character: weeks-scale work over EXISTING machinery, Pract ≥ 800 throughout — deliberately chosen so the practical spine ships immediately while exactly one research bet (Proposal 9) is funded separately.

## Contents (the beads that must be green for this gate)
- **Proposal 3 — three-color ledger schema** (color-schema): verified/validated/estimated on every ledger node + type-checked composition rules.
- **Proposal 6 — falsifier registry + budget** (falsifier-pairing): certificate classes cannot register without ≥1 falsifier; consequence×doubt budget allocator live.
- **Proposal D — Goodhart guard policy** (goodhart-guard): optimizer endpoints auto-tagged; escalation ladder defined (pure policy over existing machinery).
- **Proposal E tombstones** (swarm-tombstones): the cheap half of swarm memory — orchestrator-enforced pre-exploration tombstone check.
- **Proposal 13 type checker** (interface-types): coupling-graph static checker over the FEEC periodic-table type lattice.

## Why this is the spine and ships first (Finding 2 + Part IV)
Practicality and innovation anti-correlate across the nineteen; the correct move is not to execute the ranking top-down but to ship the practical spine immediately, fund exactly one research bet, and let the radical interfaces arrive as thin layers over proven machinery. All five spine items are schema/policy over machinery the base plan already built.

## Exit test (from the phase plan table)
- **Laundering tests pass**: adversarial pipelines that attempt to upgrade estimated→verified fail the type check.
- **No-falsifier-no-ship gate live**: a certificate class without a registered falsifier fails Gauntlet review the way untested code fails CI.
- **Tombstone protocol enforced in the orchestrator**: before funding an exploration, the agent must query the tombstone index and either cite a distinguisher or skip.

## Acceptance criteria
All five spine beads closed; the three exit tests above demonstrably pass in CI/Gauntlet; the budget pie (error-by-color) renders on a real report.

## Testing
This milestone is defined as a Gauntlet STATE, not a date (base-plan convention): its pass condition is the conjunction of its member beads' acceptance tests plus the three exit tests.

## Dependencies
Blocks the Phase 1 flywheel milestone. Depends on its five member beads (color-schema, falsifier-pairing, goodhart-guard, swarm-tombstones, interface-types).
close_reason: PHASE 0 SPINE GATE verified and made EXECUTABLE. All five member beads are closed (qmao.1 three-color schema by a fellow agent; qmao.4 falsifier pairing MINE; qmao.5 Goodhart guard; lmp4.13 tombstones MINE; knh1.1 interface types; plus qmao.2's budget-pie in fs-probe), and the milestone's pass condition — a Gauntlet STATE, not a date — now exists as a permanent CI artifact: crates/fs-probe/tests/spine_gate.rs (5 tests, dev-deps only so layer-exempt) running the three exit tests from the phase plan verbatim plus member checks plus the acceptance's final clause. EXIT 1 LAUNDERING: an adversarial pipeline claiming Verified from an Estimated parent is REFUSED by fs-ledger's ColorGraph with the teaching text ('estimates cannot become certificates by assertion'), while the honest derivation at the derived rank passes. EXIT 2 NO-FALSIFIER-NO-SHIP: fs-evidence's ship_gate blocks a certificate class without a registered falsifier BY NAME the way untested code fails CI; paired classes ship. EXIT 3 TOMBSTONE PROTOCOL: fs-ledger's pre_exploration_check blocks a pi-space re-run, refuses a free-text excuse ('trust me'), and funds a 12x-velocity validated distinguisher. MEMBER CHECKS: fs-iface rejects the classic illegal pairing (L2 pressure vs H1 displacement trace as Continuity) and admits legal same-space coupling; fs-opt's GoodhartGuard VETOES a crack optimum (Failed + findings-as-treasure) while remaining HONEST on the genuine optimum (Provisional with 3/4 steps lacking machinery — never Cleared on skipped checks, never honored short of full clearance; the test asserts these exact semantics after the first draft wrongly expected is_honored). BUDGET PIE: fs-probe's BudgetPie renders error-by-color with exact fractions and the operator verdict 'refining the mesh will NOT help' when model-form error dominates. CONJUNCTION EVIDENCE: full member suites re-run green NOW — fs-ledger 60, fs-evidence 23, fs-iface 13, fs-opt 22, fs-probe 16 (incl. the 5 new gate tests), fs-ladder 12 = 146 tests, zero failures. clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, xtask check-all 57 crates 0 violations. The spine is closed: the flywheel's cheap aggressive machinery now runs behind schema (colors), gates (falsifiers, Goodhart), memory (tombstones), and types (couplings).

title: ADDENDUM MILESTONE — Phase 1 FLYWHEEL closes (incl. Proposal 9's six-month checkpoint, the keystone measurement)
description:
## What
ADDENDUM PHASE 1 — FLYWHEEL. The exit gate for the economic engine: incremental recompute (2), version control (10), spectral health (5), and — as the SOLE research bet (P7) — certified speculation (9), starting with equilibrated estimators on the elliptic/FEEC class and warm-start economics from day one. This is the phase where determinism and stable entity IDs are promoted from implementation details to CERTIFIED CONTRACTS with their own Gauntlet tests.

## Contents
- **Proposal 2** (recompute-store/-invalidate/-api): certified-skip layer + slack fields + skip-yield dashboard.
- **Proposal 10** (vcs-commits/-diff/-bisect/-merge): branch/diff/bisect/merge, merge adjudicated by the sheaf.
- **Proposal 5** (spectral-health): λ-gap monitoring feeding merge trust + router conditioning.
- **Proposal 9** (spec-verifier/-proposers/-economics/-dwr/-mining): the research bet. Verifier first (equilibrated flux), then proposer zoo + warm-start economics.

## Exit test
- Skip-yield dashboard live (Proposal 2's health metric: fraction of DAG certifiably skipped on realistic edit traces).
- Proposer accept-rate dashboard live, stratified by physics kernel × regime (Proposal 9 telemetry).
- Merge demo passes swarm concurrency trials (Proposal 10; and Proposal 10's own kill check: <~25% of realistic merges surface harmonic/structural conflicts).
- **Proposal 9's six-month checkpoint** — THE single measurement on which most of the addendum's weight rests: after the first two physics kernels ship with equilibrated estimators, accept rates at customer-realistic tolerances must exceed ~30% AND median warm-start savings must reach ≥1.5×. If not, the speculation economy does not close: keep the estimators (needed for adaptivity regardless) and the warm-start path, retire the proposer zoo and the fleet-learning claim.

## Why the flywheel is the top of the table
The top four proposals form a closed loop and that IS why they are the top four: 9 makes queries cheap → cheap queries keep 2's cache hot → cache + corpus feed 8's planner real cost data → 10 makes the corpus safe to generate concurrently → E makes it cumulative. Each accelerates the others.

## Acceptance criteria
Member beads closed; both dashboards live; merge swarm-trial passing; 9's checkpoint measured and recorded (pass OR the documented fallback executed).

## Dependencies
Depends on milestone-spine (schema/policy must exist first) and its member beads. Blocks milestone-leverage.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

title: ADDENDUM MILESTONE — Phase 2 LEVERAGE (adjoint-vs-DFO + planner-vs-baseline benchmarks, first external audit)
description:
## What
ADDENDUM PHASE 2 — LEVERAGE. The exit gate for the radical interfaces arriving as thin layers over Phase-1 machinery. Character: high-leverage capability where each new proposal is a CONSUMER of infrastructure Phases 0–1 already built (Finding 4: five of the six operator-lens proposals are second-story construction).

## Contents
- **Proposal 1** (adjoint-vjp/-mesh/-certs): end-to-end adjoints — transpose the ledger.
- **Proposal 8** (query-language/-planner/-anytime): the declarative query interface + greedy fidelity-ladder planner.
- **Proposal E contracts** (swarm-contracts): the ambitious half of swarm memory (assume-guarantee envelopes).
- **Proposal F** (objective-epistemics): colors for the goal itself; robust-optimum API.
- **Proposal B v0** (explanation-objects): drag-decomposition flagship as the first explanation object.
- **Proposal 12** (evidence-packages): the first externally-facing stability commitment.

## Exit test
- **Adjoint-vs-derivative-free benchmark**: adjoint-driven optimization beats the best derivative-free baseline (CMA-ES/BO at equal budget) on ≥70% of the wedge vertical's benchmark design tasks (else scope adjoints down to single-representation paths — Proposal 1's kill criterion).
- **Planner-vs-baseline benchmark**: the greedy planner beats a fixed sensible baseline (single mid-rung solve + uniform refinement) by ≥2× cost at equal certified accuracy on the wedge query set — OR the query interface ships anyway on anytime+refusal semantics and planner research freezes (Proposal 8's kill criterion).
- **First evidence package audited externally**: an auditor/certification body engages with the machine-checkable format as at least supplementary evidence.

## Acceptance criteria
Member beads closed; the three benchmarks run and recorded; no optimization in the codebase can run against an un-colored objective (Proposal F's amended optimization contract).

## Dependencies
Depends on milestone-flywheel and its member beads. Blocks milestone-horizon.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- Phase-2-scheduled by DEPENDENCY (beyond IV.2's headline set): Proposal 9 dimensional mining (needs a populated certified corpus), Proposal 3 fidelity-ladder/probes (need the ladder registry), and the Proposal 12 checker + crosswalk land here.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

title: ADDENDUM MILESTONE — Phase 3 HORIZON (per-proposal activation gates: 11, C, A, 4, 13b)
description:
## What
ADDENDUM PHASE 3 — HORIZON. Not a single gate but a holding pen of proposals each individually gated by its OWN activation condition. Nothing here opens as a broad program; each waits for a specific trigger, per Governance Rule 1 (one research bet at a time) and Rule 4 (the circularity discount).

## Contents and their individual activation gates
- **Proposal 11 — reality as a chart** (reality-ingest/-assimilate/-tolerance): activate when metrology partnerships exist AND registration uncertainty on realistic scanned parts is below the geometric deviations being certified (else retain point-sensor assimilation only — R8).
- **Proposal C — value-of-information** (voi-queries): activate once 3's uncertainty inventory + 9/2's cheap sweeps are live; deferred under the circularity discount if the first vertical ships human-driven.
- **Proposal A — abstraction ladder** (abstraction-ladder): activate on the certified reduced-basis beachhead; kill if RB-certified regions cover <20% of wedge query volume.
- **Proposal 4 — spacetime complex** (spacetime-complex): activate ONLY when a paying workload's error budget is demonstrably dominated by splitting error (<20% of budget → leave instrumented-but-uncontrolled).
- **Proposal 13b — symmetry solver** (symmetry-harvest): activate if ≥15% of real workloads present exploitable exact/approximate symmetry (else keep detection only).

## Why gated, not scheduled
Radical systems die of breadth more often than of ambition (R10). The horizon exists so ambitious proposals have a home without diluting the current phase's focus. Each activation condition is a measurement, per P8.

## Acceptance criteria
Each horizon proposal has its activation measurement instrumented; a proposal is only moved out of the horizon when its trigger fires, in writing, at a quarterly review.

## Dependencies
Depends on milestone-leverage. This is the terminal roadmap gate; individual members carry their own cross-layer deps.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- Under Governance Rule 4, Proposals B (explanation-objects) and D (Goodhart guard) ALSO defer to this horizon if the first vertical ships human-driven, in addition to the always-listed 11/C/A/4/13b.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

title: ADDENDUM Risk register R1–R10: early-warning instrumentation + quarterly kill-criteria enforcement
description:
## What
The addendum's risk register (Part V), maintained as a living instrument: ten named risks (R1–R10), each with the mitigation ALREADY embedded in the proposal beads and — the load-bearing part — an EARLY-WARNING METRIC that makes the risk visible before it is fatal. This bead's job is to ensure every early-warning metric is actually instrumented on a dashboard and reviewed quarterly (P8: an uninstrumented kill/warning metric counts as a failure).

## The ten risks
- **R1 Estimator constants** (Proposal 9's hard core): guaranteed a-posteriori bounds beyond the elliptic/FEEC class need genuinely hard reliability constants. Mitigation: constant-free equilibrated-flux estimators for the elliptic class first; DWR accepts carry estimated color; nonlinear/transient candidates are warm-starts only. Early warning: **accept-rate stratified by problem class** — a class stuck at warm-start-only is a class where the analysis has not landed.
- **R2 Determinism at scale**: GPU reductions + dynamic scheduling threaten the bitwise reproducibility that Proposals 2 and 10 stand on. Mitigation: fixed-order reductions + deterministic scheduling as certified contracts with Gauntlet tests; accept the measured perf tax explicitly. Early warning: **reproducibility test failures per release**; tax >~15% on dominant kernels forces a scoped deterministic-mode-for-versioned-work decision.
- **R3 Stable entity identity**: semantic diff + merge (10) require persistent IDs through topology-changing edits — the classic CAD topological-naming problem. Mitigation: FrankenSim owns its kernel; IDs are first-class from day one; edits are ledgered ops that transform IDs explicitly. Early warning: **fraction of diffs falling back to unattributed geometric comparison**.
- **R4 Loose sensitivity bounds**: interval derivatives on nonlinear ops may be so pessimistic that Proposal 2's recompute frontier balloons to the whole DAG. Mitigation: graceful degradation to hash memoization; adjoint-sharpened bounds where 1 is live. Early warning: **the skip-yield metric itself**.
- **R5 Spectral-gap fragility**: the fixable/structural triage (and merge adjudication) degrades in ill-conditioned regions. Mitigation: Proposal 5's monitoring with mandatory low-confidence propagation into 10's merge outputs; the Gauntlet refusal-is-the-pass suite. Early warning: **gap-collapse incidence per assembly class**.
- **R6 Restriction-map quality**: the sheaf faithfully propagates garbage with certificates attached if trace/conversion operators are inaccurate. Mitigation: Proposal 7's conformance suite applied to FIRST-party converters with the same severity as third-party. Early warning: **conformance-tier distribution of converters on the hot path**.
- **R7 Model-form composition**: estimated-color quantities do not compose with the clean algebra of verified bounds. Mitigation: the type system's laundering refusal; discrepancy-probe maps as the empirical substitute; F's weakest-input rule. Early warning: **audit rate of estimated-color claims in decision-critical positions**.
- **R8 Registration well-posedness** (11): scan-to-design alignment error can exceed the deviations being certified. Mitigation: design-for-verification fiducials pushed upstream; point-sensor assimilation as the fallback. Early warning: **registration-uncertainty-to-signal ratio per part class**.
- **R9 Standards-body latency** (12): machine-checkable evidence may sit ahead of what auditors engage with. Mitigation: the crosswalk speaks their vocabulary; the package doubles as internal QA + B2B diligence collateral. Early warning: **auditor engagement rate in the first regulated-vertical cycle**.
- **R10 Breadth death**: nineteen proposals executed as nineteen parallel programs is an obituary. Mitigation: the governance rules (one research bet, quarterly kill enforcement, phase gating). Early warning: **headcount-weighted work-in-progress outside the current phase**.

## Acceptance criteria
Every R1–R10 early-warning metric is a live dashboard signal with a threshold and an owner; a quarterly written risk review exists; each risk links to the proposal bead(s) that own its mitigation.

## Testing
A risk-register audit that fails if any early-warning metric is uninstrumented or unreviewed for >1 quarter.

## Dependencies
Cross-cuts all addendum proposals; no hard blocker. Sibling to add-doctrine (governance).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Optimality: the risk-register audit emits structured output per R1-R10 (metric -> dashboard-present bool -> threshold -> owner -> last-review-date).
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-govern (UTIL layer, no deps; added crate:fs-govern label). The addendum's machine-readable RISK REGISTER (Part V): encodes R1-R10 each with {name, description, mitigation, early_warning metric, action threshold, owning bead, instrumented flag}. register()/risk(id) expose the canonical record; audit()/audit_slice() enforce Governance Rule 2 (every risk must DECLARE a non-empty early-warning metric AND an owner — a risk with no measurement is unmanaged) and count how many metrics are instrumented (0 baseline, honest); to_json() emits a deterministic machine-readable JSON array (JSON-escaped) for dashboards/CI gates. Owners are the real addendum bead ids (R1->lmp4.1 spec-verifier, R2->lmp4.6 recompute-store, R3->lmp4.10 vcs-diff, R4->lmp4.7 invalidation, R5->knh1.3 spectral-health, R6->jwq8.2 plugin, R7->qmao.2 probes, R8->bk0o.4 reality-ingest, R9->qmao.9 crosswalk, R10->xpck.1 doctrine). Tests (tests/register.rs, 8): all-ten-ordered, per-risk field non-emptiness, owners-are-bead-ids, lookup, canonical-audit-complete-zero-instrumented, audit_slice-detects-missing-metric-AND-owner (non-vacuous), JSON well-formed+complete, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-govern 8/8, xtask check-all fs-govern-compliant (only unrelated fs-solver flagged). No-claim: encodes governance DATA; does not itself measure metrics (a dashboard flips instrumented); P1-P8/rules/per-proposal kill criteria are the sibling doctrine bead xpck.1; does not read the beads DB.

title: ADDENDUM CRATE CONTRACTS: CONTRACT.md + no-claim boundaries for every net-new addendum crate (fs-query, speculation, contracts, tombstone, VCS, evidence-package/checker)
description:
## What
ADDENDUM CRATE CONTRACTS — the CONTRACT.md + no-claim-boundary obligation for every NET-NEW crate the addendum introduces. AGENTS.md requires "Each crate should have a `CONTRACT.md` before it becomes a dependency target," and the addendum introduces crates not in the base plan's Appendix-A atlas. This bead is the tracker that ensures none of them becomes a dependency target without its contract.

## Scope — new crates and their load-bearing no-claim boundaries
- **fs-query** (declarative queries frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.15/frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.16/frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.17): determinism class, anytime/refusal semantics, "the planner may be frozen without breaking the language" boundary. (A CONTRACT.md already exists; verify it covers these.)
- **speculation crate** (proposer zoo frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.2 + economics): NO-CLAIM "proposer output is NEVER an answer; correctness never depends on the learned component — a bad proposer can only waste a check."
- **contracts/assume-guarantee crate** (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.14): NO-CLAIM "a composed system claim is never tighter than its weakest member's certificate permits."
- **tombstone/memory crate** (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.13): retrieval-recall + π-space-collision semantics; "a tombstone is advisory, not a hard block."
- **version-control-for-physics** surface (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.9…frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.12, over fs-ledger/fs-geom): merge auto-resolution is licensed ONLY for coboundary components; degraded-gap merges are low-confidence.
- **evidence-package + checker** (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.6/the standalone open-source checker): the public format's stability promise + the checker's "cannot run solvers" boundary.
- Any new crate carrying [F]/[M] work: its CONTRACT.md states the feature flag that keeps it off the default path (see doctrine feature-flag rule).

## Acceptance criteria
Every new addendum crate has a CONTRACT.md with the ten required sections (purpose/layer, public types, invariants, error model, determinism class, cancellation behavior, unsafe boundary, feature flags, conformance tests, no-claim boundaries) BEFORE it is a dependency target; `xtask check-contracts` passes for them.

## Testing
`xtask check-contracts` (the base plan's contract lint) extended to the new crates; the no-claim boundaries above appear verbatim.

## Dependencies
Reuses the base plan's contract-lint infrastructure (foundations: CONTRACT.md lint + shared conformance harness). Cross-cuts all addendum crates. Sibling to the risk register + doctrine.
close_reason: Implemented by extending crates/fs-govern with a crates module (added crate:fs-govern label). The canonical REGISTRY of the seven net-new addendum crates (fs-iface P13, fs-ladder P3, fs-probe P3, fs-spececo P9, fs-verify P9, fs-recompute P2, fs-govern governance), each with {name, purpose, owning_proposal, layer, no_claim boundary}. addendum_crates() exposes the inventory; crate_audit() confirms each declares a purpose + owning proposal + no-claim boundary (AGENTS.md contract discipline made governance-legible); crates_json() emits the deterministic machine-readable record. CONTRACT.md file PRESENCE is enforced by xtask check-contracts (verified: 0 violations across 57 crates — all 7 addendum crates have CONTRACT.md with no-claim boundaries). Tests (tests/crate_registry.rs, 5): 7 unique crates = expected inventory, each declares purpose/owner/layer/no-claim, owner->proposal mapping, audit-complete, JSON well-formed+deterministic. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-govern 21/21 (5 crate_registry + 8 governance + 8 register), xtask check-all 0 violations. fs-govern is now the complete machine-readable governance record: doctrine (P1-P8 + rules) + 19 proposals + R1-R10 risks + the addendum crate inventory. No-claim: tracks contract obligations as data; file presence + required sections are xtask's enforcement (does not read the filesystem itself).

title: EPISTEMIC-ENGINE ACCEPTANCE e2e: declarative query -> colored anytime answer -> evidence package -> third-party re-verification (the whole product for a user)
description:
## What
EPISTEMIC-ENGINE ACCEPTANCE e2e — the TOP-LEVEL runnable proof that the whole addendum works FOR A USER, end to end: a declarative query becomes a colored, priced, auditable answer that a third party can re-verify without trusting the vendor. This is the concrete test of the revised identity — "the product is justified belief at minimum cost." It ties together the four layer e2e suites (flywheel frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.18, epistemic frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.10, coupling frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.8, self-knowledge frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.7).

## Scope — the acceptance script (structured tracing / ledger events, never stdout)
A user (agent or engineer) poses a DECLARATIVE query on the conjugate-heat-transfer wedge: "is max von Mises stress under 180 MPa at 95% confidence, answer for under $50?". The script then asserts, with detailed logging at every hop:
1. The query round-trips through admission (typed QoI, dimensional + chart-compatibility + budget feasibility) and ill-posed variants are refused in milliseconds with ranked-fix teaching errors.
2. The flywheel DISCHARGES it cheaply: speculation proposes + the certified verifier accepts-or-warm-starts; the incremental cache reuses with skip certificates; the greedy planner walks the fidelity ladder. LOG: per-stage accept/skip/rung decisions + costs.
3. The answer returns with ANYTIME semantics: an immediate colored interval that tightens with budget, carrying its color and a "what would tighten this" VoI hint; OR a teaching REFUSAL with the achieved interval + the price of the gap.
4. The result EXPORTS as a signed evidence package; the STANDALONE open-source checker re-verifies it (certificates + composition + signatures + Merkle) with ZERO solver dependency and renders the budget pie.
5. The whole run is DETERMINISTIC (G5 replay reproduces artifact hashes) and survives a CANCELLATION STORM mid-run (G4: zero leaks, consistent ledger).
6. LAUNDERING invariant across the whole path: an estimated-color intermediate never becomes verified anywhere from query to package.

## Acceptance criteria
The end-to-end run produces a colored answer within budget (or a teaching refusal), the answer's color equals its weakest input's color, the evidence package re-verifies solver-free, the run replays deterministically, a mid-run cancellation storm leaves zero leaks, and no color laundering occurs anywhere in the path.

## Testing
The top-level product acceptance harness. Gauntlet: G4 (cancellation storm across the whole path) + G5 (deterministic whole-path replay) + G0/G3 (laundering-across-the-path invariant). Detailed logging: the full query→discharge→answer→package→check trace in one ledgered run. Nightly, both ISAs.

## Dependencies
frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.18 (flywheel closes), frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.10, frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.8, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.7, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.17, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.6, and the standalone checker (qmao.8). This is the final acceptance gate for the epistemic-engine identity; feeds the Horizon milestone.

title: L4 ASCENT: optimization kernel
description:
## Mission
L4 ASCENT: the optimization kernel. Given `minimize J(u(θ),θ) s.t. physics(u,θ)=0, constraints(θ)≤0, θ∈M`, find the best designs — with gradients when they exist, guarantees when they're possible, statistical validity when there's noise, and maximal exploitation of 100+ cores when there's a population.

## Core theses (plan §9, Bets 5/6/7/8)
- Optimization as geometry (Bet 6): CMA-ES/NES implemented as natural-gradient flows on the Gaussian family (information-geometric optimization); shape gradients preconditioned by Sobolev metrics; genuinely Riemannian optimizers on manifold design spaces; elastic shape metrics for geodesics between designs; optimal transport for design diffs/barycenters/DRO.
- Anytime-valid stochastics (Bet 5): every noisy comparison monitored by e-process confidence sequences — valid under optional stopping, composable via e-values, FWER-controlled via e-BH. e-raced generations (Bet 8 [M]) kill dominated candidates mid-solve via asupersync cancellation, reallocating cores within a millisecond, bit-reproducible from seed.
- Certificates everywhere (Bet 7): SOS/Lasserre relaxations produce PROOFS of global optimality/infeasibility; SOS Lyapunov functions certify regions of attraction; persistent homology enforces topology constraints; KKT residuals distinguish "converged" from "stalled".
- Learned accelerators with guarantees (Bet 9): surrogates only ever inside conformal-e-certified validity bands; ML proposes, certified numerics disposes.

## Patch additions folded in
fs-constraint (typed constraint semantics + unsat-core infeasibility diagnosis), fs-archive (MAP-Elites/novelty quality-diversity — design DISCOVERY, not just convergence), fs-voi (value-of-information: spend compute where it changes decisions), fs-gen (proposal-only generative models), conformal-hardening (Mondrian conditional calibration, drift e-tests, false-coverage budgets), fs-fab (manufacturing/code-compliance semantics).

## Members
fs-opt-ir, fs-constraint, gradient-stack, cmaes-igo, bayesopt, fs-eproc-core, e-racing, conformal-hardening, fs-surrogate, fs-sos, topo-simp, topo-levelset, topo-truss, topo-lattice, topo-persistence, multiobjective-robust, fs-archive, fs-voi, fs-gen, fs-fab, ascent-conformance.

## Success Criteria
Optimizers validate against FrankenScipy references; gradient stack passes adjoint-vs-FD gates; e-process validity proven under adversarial stopping; e-racing delivers 2–5× generations/core-hour at identical statistical guarantees.

title: fs-opt problem IR: typed objective/constraint graphs, manifold variables, PDE/stochastic nodes, incremental validation
description:
## What
The optimization problem IR (plan §9.1): optimization problems ARE DATA — typed objective/constraint graphs over manifold-valued variables, storable, diffable, replayable, and constructible incrementally with validation at each step (the agent-ergonomics property).

## Scope
- Typed problem graphs: objectives/constraints as expression nodes over design variables; PDE constraints referencing FLUX studies (physics(u,θ)=0 as a first-class node with adjoint availability metadata); stochastic operators (expectation, CVaR, quantiles) referencing UQ configurations; budgets attached per P4.
- Manifold-valued variables: R^n, spheres, Stiefel, SO(3) (orientations), fixed-volume level-set manifolds — with retraction/transport metadata the gradient stack consumes ("optimize an orientation" never becomes "optimize 9 numbers and renormalize when it explodes").
- Structure representable: multi-objective, chance-constrained, bilevel, multi-fidelity annotations.
- Incremental construction + validation: each added node type-checked (dimensions via Qty, chart compatibility via parameterization metadata, differentiability class propagation — "this objective is non-smooth through that min()" is KNOWN at build time and routed to the right optimizer family).
- Serialization: problems round-trip through the IR/ledger (FrankenScript `ascent.optimize` nodes lower to this); problem hashes for study identity.
- Constraint semantics delegated to fs-constraint (kinds/repair); this bead owns the graph substrate.

## Acceptance criteria
Round-trip stability (build→serialize→parse→identical); validation catches seeded ill-typed problems (dimension mismatch, non-differentiable node fed to L-BFGS) with structured diagnoses naming the offending node; differentiability-class propagation correct on fixture graphs; manifold metadata consumed correctly by a toy Riemannian descent.

## Testing
G0: graph algebra laws (substitution, common-subexpression identity); fuzz: random valid/invalid graph mutations classified correctly. Logs: validation reports, problem hashes, class-propagation traces.

## ADDENDUM AMENDMENT (COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md)
The optimization subsystem's contract is REWRITTEN by two addendum proposals:
- **No optimization may run against an un-colored objective** (Proposal F, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.3): objectives are functionals over `(design, environment random variable)`; loads/hazards/cost models are colored evidence artifacts; the default deliverable is a robust optimum (CVaR/DRO) with hazard sensitivity, and the headline color = the color of the weakest input.
- **"Converged" is redefined as "converged AND guard-cleared"** (Proposal D, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.5): every optimizer endpoint is treated as an adversarial example and its certificate is provisional until escalated falsification (rung k+1 / cross-representation re-solve / δ-perturbation / estimator independence) passes.
Every objective/constraint op also gains a VJP requirement (Proposal 1, frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.1).
close_reason: Shipped fs-opt, the ASCENT problem IR (plan 9.1): optimization problems ARE DATA. Typed expression graphs over manifold variables with INCREMENTAL validation — every constructor checks shapes, fs-qty dimensions (add needs equal dims, mul/dot add exponents, sqrt halves even ones, transcendentals demand dimensionless), ids, ranges, scalar roots — all with teaching errors naming ops and nodes. Hash-consed arena: CSE identity BY CONSTRUCTION. Differentiability CLASS propagates bottom-up so the min() kink is known at build time: route() refuses L-BFGS/Newton NAMING the poisoning node, refuses gradient families on adjoint-less PDE nodes NAMING the study, admits subgradient/gradient-free. First-class PDE residual nodes (FLUX study refs + adjoint metadata) and stochastic operators (expectation/CVaR/quantile over UQ configs, CVaR correctly C0) — represented and validated, execution refused with the executor named. Manifolds (Rn/Sphere/So3-quaternion/Stiefel) carry retractions consumed by a toy Riemannian descent: sphere hits the analytic minimizer staying unit, SO(3) aligns vectors with unit quaternions throughout, Stiefel stays orthonormal to 1e-10 and finds the top invariant subspace. Canonical serialization round-trips BITWISE (floats as bit patterns), FNV-64 body hash = study identity, parser REBUILDS through the validating builder + verifies integrity (tampered text refuses with line numbers). P4 budget stops descent with a receipt; P7 cancellation polls. Evidence: opt-001 600-op fuzz storm matches an independent validity model exactly; opt-002 roundtrip/hash identity/integrity; opt-003 CSE + bitwise substitution law over 200 points; opt-004 routing refusals named + full class trace; opt-005 three-manifold descent; opt-006 budget receipt + executor-naming refusals. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 7 tests green, xtask fs-opt clean (29 crates; only fs-img in-flight contract remains), workspace 105 suites green, ubs clean. COORDINATION: resolved a two-agent collision on this crate (see bead comment) — parallel draft preserved for harvest, one-line cfg park honoring its author's own PARKED header; also pinned fs-la's placeholder golden hash per its set-from-first-run note, verified bit-identical arm64/x86_64. No-claims: FD-toy gradients (exact adjoints = gradient-stack bead, harvest graph.rs reverse-mode), constraint semantics = fs-constraint, FrankenScript lowering = HELM.
