comments.text: COLLISION + RESOLUTION (numerics-spine agent): another agent began implementing fs-opt concurrently with my claim (their lib.rs/Cargo.toml landed over my scaffold; no competing claim comment - likely a race). Their structure (ir.rs/eval.rs/serial.rs w/ ProblemBuilder, Constraint/Sense, EvalBudget, ProblemTag) realizes MORE of the bead scope than my draft, and they are actively editing - CEDING the implementation to them per AGENTS.md (work with the current tree, never revert another agent's work). WHAT I LEFT FOR HARVEST (orphaned in src/, NOT compiled - their lib.rs does not reference them; my tests/ir_battery.rs neutralized to a parked doc so it cannot break their build): (a) manifold.rs - working tangent projection + normalize retraction for Sphere/So3 (draft battery had them keeping the unit invariant to 1e-12 through 300 descent steps); (b) riemann.rs - toy Riemannian descent driver consuming that metadata (sphere linear objective converged to -a analytically); (c) sexpr.rs - a RE-VALIDATING s-expr parser: rebuilds through the typed constructors so a tampered file with a dimension violation is REJECTED at parse time (tested) - recommend keeping this property in serial.rs; (d) graph.rs - hash-consing builder (CSE identity by construction), bottom-up DiffClass propagation with named-node ClassTooRough diagnostics, dimensional checks via fs-qty Dims incl the sqrt-halves-evenly rule, exact reverse-mode gradient on the DAG (FD-verified in the draft battery), and PdeResidual/Expectation structure nodes with typed Unevaluable. NOTE their current Cargo.toml dropped fs-math (their unresolved-import errors when I last compiled) - they will need it (or std) for strict eval kernels; strict-kernel eval matters for the cross-ISA golden-hash discipline (see the eigensolver incident rule in fs-la CONTRACT). To the implementing agent: feel free to delete the orphaned draft files - I cannot (no-deletion rule) but you or the user can; or ping me via bead comment to integrate specific pieces. Un-claiming implementation ownership; bead stays in_progress under your work.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Coordination record (CloudyFinch): two agents built fs-opt near-simultaneously. The numerics-spine agent scaffolded first (expr/graph/manifold/riemann/sexpr + ir_battery) without claiming the bead; I claimed via tracker and my ir/eval/serial slice got wired by the sweeper as lib.rs. The other agent gracefully parked their battery with harvest notes (see tests/ir_battery.rs header). Resolution: their draft modules remain in src/ UNREFERENCED for harvest (reverse-mode gradients in graph.rs and the sexpr re-validating parser are worth lifting into the gradient-stack bead); their battery is mechanically gated behind the parked-ir-battery feature (one cfg line + two doc-indent chars, nothing else touched); CONTRACT.md Feature-flags section documents all of it. Also pinned fs-la's rand_nla GOLDEN_HASH placeholder per its own set-from-first-run comment, verified identical on arm64+x86_64.

title: fs-sos: Lasserre/SOS relaxations, Burer-Monteiro SDP w/ interval-verified dual certificates, SOS Lyapunov ROA [F/M]
description:
## What
`fs-sos`: proof-carrying optimization (plan §9.8 [F/M]) — Lasserre/SOS moment relaxations solved by an in-house first-order SDP solver, returning GLOBAL optimality/infeasibility CERTIFICATES for low-dimensional polynomial subproblems; plus SOS Lyapunov functions certifying regions of attraction.

## Scope
- Moment/SOS hierarchy: polynomial optimization (≤ ~8–10 variables after parameterization — scope discipline per §17 risk) via Lasserre relaxations; monomial bookkeeping, sparsity exploitation (correlative sparsity where structure permits).
- In-house first-order SDP solver: BURER–MONTEIRO low-rank factorization with DUAL CERTIFICATE EXTRACTION; the solver may be approximate but THE CERTIFICATE MUST BE RIGOROUS — extracted duals re-verified in interval arithmetic (fs-ivl; certify-certifiers attacks this).
- THE SHOW-STEALER (§15.1 step 4): SOS LYAPUNOV FUNCTIONS certifying regions of attraction for trim states of aircraft flight dynamics — "stability" becomes a PROVEN BASIN VOLUME, not an eigenvalue vibe; V(x) SOS, −V̇(x) SOS on a domain, sublevel-set volume as the maneuverability-adjacent metric; fallback per risk register: sampled Lyapunov with conformal bounds if SOS scaling disappoints.
- Complementary certificates: interval branch-and-bound for small global problems (fs-ivl-taylor); KKT-residual certificates attached to every returned local optimum stack-wide (the "converged vs stalled" discriminator — implemented here, consumed by gradient-stack).
- Named consumers: linkage synthesis, section-shape polish, safety-margin verification, ROA certification.

## Acceptance criteria
Global optima certified on polynomial batteries with KNOWN solutions (Motzkin-class positivity fixtures, published POP test sets); dual certificates interval-verified (zero false certificates under the adversarial battery); ROA: certified basins for textbook systems (Van der Pol reversed-time, pendulum) match/underapproximate published basins correctly; BM solver: convergence + rank behavior documented across the fixture set.

## Testing
G0: SOS decomposition verification (reconstructed polynomial matches to interval tolerance); hierarchy monotonicity (level k+1 bound ≥ level k); scaling honesty tables (time vs vars/degree — where it breaks, documented). Logs: certificate artifacts (full dual data), verification verdicts, basin volumes.

title: Topology opt SIMP: Helmholtz filter, Heaviside projection, robust erode/dilate, stress p-norm, eigenfrequency — on CutFEM octrees
description:
## What
Density-based topology optimization (plan §9.5 [S]): SIMP with the full modern hygiene stack, running on CutFEM octrees → NO REMESHING PER ITERATION. This powers the P2 marquee demo.

## Scope
- SIMP interpolation with penalization continuation; HELMHOLTZ PDE FILTERING (reusing FLUX's Poisson machinery — one solver, two jobs) for mesh-independent length-scale control; HEAVISIDE PROJECTION with continuation (crisp 0/1 designs without premature lock-in).
- ROBUST ERODE/DILATE formulations: three-field (eroded/nominal/dilated) design giving GUARANTEED minimum length scale on both solid and void (the manufacturable-by-construction trick).
- Constraints/objectives: compliance, volume, EIGENFREQUENCY (via fs-la-eigen with eigenvalue derivatives + clustered-eigenvalue handling); STRESS constraints via adaptive p-norm aggregation (the local-constraint aggregation with adaptive normalization that keeps stress control honest).
- CutFEM-octree execution: density field on the octree, physics via fs-cutfem — the marquee property: topology evolves with zero meshing; DWR adaptivity refines where the OBJECTIVE feels it.
- Optimizer coupling: gradient-stack (adjoint sensitivities through fs-adjoint density chain rules; MMA-class or AL updates documented choice); persistence-based topology constraints via the separate topo-persistence bead.

## Acceptance criteria
Canonical fixtures (MBB beam, cantilever, L-bracket): converged designs match literature-class compliance values at matched volume fractions (envelopes ledgered); robust formulation delivers verified min length scale (measured on results via the medial thickness oracle — geometry layer auditing optimization output); stress-constrained fixtures avoid the singularity trap (documented treatment verified); zero remeshing verified across full runs; adjoint gates green.

## Testing
G0: filter linearity/symmetry, projection monotonicity; sensitivity verification vs FD at multiple continuation stages; determinism across thread counts (a whole topo run replayable). Logs: objective/constraint traces, continuation schedules, thickness audit reports, per-iteration timing (the interactive-cadence claim measured).

title: Topology opt level-set: WENO narrow-band advection, FIM redistancing, topological-derivative hole nucleation
description:
## What
Level-set topology optimization (plan §9.5 [S/F]): shape-gradient velocity advection with TOPOLOGICAL DERIVATIVES for hole nucleation — genuine topology changes with mathematical justification rather than heuristic hole-punching.

## Scope
- Level-set evolution: shape-gradient-derived velocity fields (fs-adjoint Hadamard gradients → Sobolev-smoothed → extended off the interface) advecting the SDF via WENO schemes on octree NARROW BANDS (fs-rep-sdf narrow-band mode — the memory-sane path).
- Redistancing: FAST-ITERATIVE-METHOD (FIM — the parallel-friendly cousin of fast marching, chosen for the many-core machines) with band maintenance; redistancing-frequency policy with drift audits (redistancing perturbs the interface; the audit quantifies it).
- TOPOLOGICAL DERIVATIVES: the asymptotic sensitivity to infinitesimal hole insertion — nucleate holes where the topological derivative says the objective improves (the principled mechanism; SIMP-style density methods change topology implicitly, level sets need this explicit operator).
- Velocity extension: interface-normal extension solvers; CFL management tied to band width; volume/constraint enforcement via augmented Lagrangian or projection (documented).
- CutFEM coupling: the level set IS the geometry — physics evaluated by fs-cutfem directly on the evolving SDF (the vessel flagship's lip channel: "topology of the lip channel may change and that is fine" — §15.3 step 1).

## Acceptance criteria
Fixture convergence: level-set results consistent with SIMP results on shared fixtures (two methods, one truth — cross-validation ledgered); hole nucleation fires correctly on fixtures with known optimal topology changes (short-cantilever classics); interface advection: volume conservation within documented drift budgets; WENO order verified (G1-style on advection fixtures).

## Testing
G0: redistancing preserves zero level set within tolerance (Hausdorff audit); signed-distance property maintenance (|∇φ|−1 statistics); adjoint-vs-FD on level-set velocity sensitivities. Logs: interface evolution snapshots (hashes), nucleation events with topological-derivative values, drift audits.

title: Truss/frame layout: FrankenNetworkx ground structures, PDHG LP/SOCP at 1e6+ members, Michell oracles, code-based sizing
description:
## What
Truss/frame layout optimization (plan §9.5 [S/F]): ground structures → LP/SOCP member-force layout at MILLIONS of candidate members → nonlinear sizing with buckling and constructability. The steel-and-concrete flagship's engine (§15.2).

## Scope
- Ground structures via FrankenNetworkx: k-NN candidate members under fabrication rules (node grids, angle limits, length bounds — fs-fab rule sources); graph generation reproducible + ledgered.
- LAYOUT AS LP/SOCP: member-force (plastic design) formulation — minimize volume s.t. equilibrium + yield; solved by an IN-HOUSE FIRST-ORDER PRIMAL-DUAL SOLVER (PDHG-class) that scales to MILLIONS of candidate members (sparse matvec-dominated = fs-sparse territory; deterministic iterations); warm starts across load cases.
- MICHELL-CONTINUUM LIMITS as analytic sanity oracles: optimal-layout theory's closed-form solutions bound/validate numerical layouts on subproblems (the built-in truth serum; §15.2 names `:oracle (michell :tol 0.08)`).
- Nonlinear sizing pass: cross-section optimization on the surviving topology with EULER + CODE-BASED buckling constraints (AISC-class member checks via fs-constraint Code kind), JOINT-COST regularization (member-count parsimony), CONSTRUCTABILITY rules as graph constraints: member catalogs (discrete sections — snapped via fs-constraint's repair machinery), angle limits, connection families.
- Re-analysis with fs-solid-advanced rods/fiber elements for the nonlinear checks (arclength spot checks for global buckling per §15.2 step 2).

## Acceptance criteria
PDHG: converges to LP oracle solutions (small instances vs exact solver cross-check) and scales to 10⁶⁺ members within memory/time budgets (ledgered); Michell fixtures: numerical layouts within stated tolerance of analytic optima; sizing: code-check compliance verified member-by-member on outputs; catalog snapping preserves feasibility (post-snap re-verification mandatory).

## Testing
G0: equilibrium residuals on layouts; duality-gap tracking (certified near-optimality — the LP's own KKT certificate); determinism. Logs: convergence traces, gap histories, Michell comparisons, constructability audit tables.

title: Lattice/infill: unit-cell homogenization (TPMS/struts), graded macro-opt, de-homogenization w/ validity evidence [F]
description:
## What
Lattice/infill optimization (plan §9.5 [F]): numerical homogenization of unit cells → graded-property macro-optimization → DE-HOMOGENIZATION back to printable/buildable micro-geometry.

## Scope
- Unit-cell homogenization: effective elasticity tensors for TPMS families (gyroid/Schwarz-class via F-rep implicits) and strut families (fs-rep-voxel lattice graphs) — periodic-BC cell problems (fs-solid on fs-cutfem/body-fitted), yielding property manifolds parameterized by cell parameters (relative density, anisotropy knobs); results registered as fs-material homogenized laws WITH validity domains (model cards: separation-of-scales assumptions declared).
- Graded macro-optimization: spatially varying cell parameters as design fields (fs-xform density-field machinery generalized to property fields); compliance/stress objectives through the homogenized laws (gradient-stack + adjoints through material parameters).
- DE-HOMOGENIZATION: projecting graded properties back to conforming micro-geometry — orientation-aligned lattice generation (FrankenNetworkx conforming graphs), TPMS parameter-field realization (F-rep with spatially-varying parameters), transition-region handling; manufacturability audits (fs-fab: min feature size, powder escape for AM).
- Scale-separation honesty: where gradation is too sharp for homogenization validity, Evidence flags it (model-form uncertainty machinery — the validity_domain doing real work).

## Acceptance criteria
Homogenized tensors match published values for standard cells (gyroid stiffness curves vs literature); Hashin–Shtrikman bounds respected (a hard physics audit); graded optimization beats uniform-lattice baselines on fixtures (documented margins); de-homogenized geometry re-analyzed at full resolution agrees with homogenized prediction within declared validity bands (THE closing-the-loop test).

## Testing
G0: tensor symmetries/positive-definiteness of effective properties; periodicity of cell solves; graph-conformity checks on realized lattices. Logs: property manifolds, validity-domain maps, full-vs-homogenized comparison tables.

title: Topology constraints via persistence: Betti targets as diagram penalties (castability/drainability/routing) [F/M]
description:
## What
Topology constraints via persistent homology (plan §9.5/§7.8 [F/M]): Betti-number targets enforced through persistence-diagram penalties — the ONLY principled way to tell an optimizer "no enclosed voids" or "exactly one tunnel" without hand-crafted heuristics.

## Scope
- Persistence-diagram penalties: from validity-certificates' cubical PH on density/level-set fields, construct penalties measuring deviation from target topology (e.g., total persistence of excess H₂ classes = enclosed-void penalty; deficit penalties for required tunnels); differentiable-ish gradients via persistence-cell attribution (the birth/death simplices localize WHERE the field must change — subgradient routing through the filtration).
- Constraint vocabulary: castability (no enclosed voids — mold escape), drainability (monotone channels), cable/duct routing (required tunnels with minimum persistence = minimum feature robustness), connectivity (single component).
- Integration: topo-simp (density fields) and topo-levelset (SDF fields) both consume; fs-constraint hosts the semantics (Hard vs Soft topology constraints); persistence thresholds tied to length scales (a 2-voxel tunnel is noise; the persistence threshold IS the physical feature-size floor).
- [M] discipline: behind `moonshot-topo-persistence`; heuristic fallbacks (connected-component labeling constraints) remain available for comparison; promotion requires beating them on the fixture suite.

## Acceptance criteria
Fixtures with seeded topology violations (enclosed void in a bracket, missing routing tunnel) are driven to target topology by the penalty while preserving objective quality (vs unconstrained runs, documented); attribution gradients point correctly (perturbation tests: moving density where attribution says reduces the penalty); threshold behavior: sub-persistence noise ignored, real features enforced.

## Testing
G0: penalty zero iff diagram matches target (up to threshold); stability under field perturbation (inherited PH stability); integration runs on both SIMP and level-set paths. Logs: diagrams per iteration, attribution maps, penalty traces alongside objectives.

title: Multi-objective/robust: NSGA-II/III, MOEA/D, WFG hypervolume, knee detection, fork-based steering, CVaR-RU, Wasserstein DRO + Sinkhorn OT
description:
## What
Multi-objective and robust formulations (plan §9.9 [S/F]): evolutionary Pareto exploration, hypervolume machinery, interactive steering via world-forking, CVaR tail-risk, and Wasserstein distributionally-robust optimization with the optimal-transport toolkit.

## Scope
- Evolutionary MOO: NSGA-II/III and MOEA/D (population = core count, naturally — sibling-scope waves under e-racing); non-dominated sorting with deterministic tie-breaking; reference-direction management (NSGA-III for many-objective — the ornithoid's 4-objective Pareto atlas).
- Hypervolume: WFG algorithm (exact to moderate dimensions) + Monte Carlo estimation beyond; KNEE-POINT detection; hypervolume-contribution archiving.
- Scalarization sweeps for gradient-based Pareto TRACING (weighted/epsilon-constraint continuation along the front using gradient-stack — fronts with gradient-quality points, not just evolutionary scatter).
- INTERACTIVE STEERING: an agent re-weights objectives mid-campaign and the study FORKS (P9 — ledger world-forking) rather than mutates; steering events are ledgered ops.
- Robustness: CVaR via ROCKAFELLAR–URYASEV reformulation (tail-risk minimization becomes a smooth problem FLUX gradients can serve — the frame's `(cvar frag :beta 0.9 :le 0.02)` and the vessel's viscosity-band robustness both lower to this); chance constraints via fs-constraint + UQ estimates.
- OPTIMAL TRANSPORT toolkit: Sinkhorn-scaled entropic OT (FFT-accelerated where grids allow), Wasserstein distances/barycenters — supplying (a) DRO ambiguity sets over Wasserstein balls [F], (b) shape-difference metrics for design-space DIVERSITY maintenance, and (c) the semantic-diff machinery fs-report uses (`diff(a,b)` transport plans + per-region attribution).

## Acceptance criteria
ZDT/DTLZ/WFG benchmark fronts at published-class hypervolume; WFG hypervolume exact vs brute force (small cases); CVaR-RU: matches analytic CVaR on known distributions and beats mean-optimization on tail-risk fixtures (documented); Sinkhorn: convergence + marginal errors within tolerance, barycenters match closed forms (Gaussians); fork-based steering leaves both branches independently continuable (ledger-verified).

## Testing
G0: dominance-sort correctness (oracle comparison), hypervolume monotonicity; OT: metric properties spot-checked, transport-plan mass conservation exact. Logs: front snapshots + hypervolume traces, steering/fork events, CVaR estimation ledgers, transport-plan summaries.

title: fs-archive: MAP-Elites/CVT QD archives, novelty search, flagship descriptor libraries, diversity-preserving Pareto atlas
description:
## What
`fs-archive` (patch Rev H): quality-diversity and design-memory archives — MAP-Elites, novelty search, and illumination over behavior descriptors. This is how FrankenSim becomes a DESIGN EXPLORER rather than a local optimizer: discovering FAMILIES of weird, useful designs a scalarized optimizer would discard too early.

## Scope
- MAP-Elites / CVT-MAP-Elites: behavior-descriptor space tessellation (fixed grid + centroidal Voronoi for high-dim descriptors), per-cell elite retention, batch emitters (population waves via fs-exec).
- Novelty search + novelty-weighted acquisition; archive-aware restart policies for CMA-ES/BO (restarts seeded from underexplored cells — the exploration/exploitation bridge).
- Descriptor libraries per flagship domain (the patch's lists as first fixtures): aircraft — L/D band, static margin, mass-flow satisfaction, inlet count/topology, wake-coherence metric, wingbeat reduced frequency; frames — material mass, fundamental-mode descriptors, redundancy index, joint complexity, fragility slope, constructability score; vessels — pour coherence, drip probability, lip topology, volume-to-surface ratio, CoM shift across tilt schedule. Descriptors are Qty-typed, evidence-carrying computations (geometry/physics layers supply them).
- Diversity-preserving Pareto atlas: MOO fronts + QD archives unified (per-cell fronts); OT-based diversity metrics (multiobjective-robust's Wasserstein machinery measuring archive spread).
- Ledger-native: archives ARE ledger artifacts (content-addressed elites, replayable lineage per cell — "how did we find this weird wing" is an explain() query); behavior-space active learning (sample cells where surrogate uncertainty is high — fs-voi coupling).

## Acceptance criteria
Standard QD benchmarks (arm-repertoire/rastrigin-class): coverage + QD-score at published-class levels; archive determinism from seeds; descriptor computations verified against their layer-native definitions; end-to-end: a toy vessel study produces a lip-topology-diverse archive demonstrably richer than NSGA-only baseline (coverage metrics ledgered).

## Testing
G0: elite-replacement invariants (monotone per-cell quality), CVT stability; archive serialization round-trips; integration with e-racing (raced evaluations feeding archives keep validity). Logs: coverage/QD-score traces, cell-lineage records, descriptor distributions.

title: fs-voi: value-of-information layer — EVSI/EVC/EVT arbitration, decision-boundary refinement, ranking-change posteriors [M]
description:
## What
`fs-voi` (patch Rev I): value-of-information and ACTIVE VALIDATION — the strategic layer deciding WHAT INFORMATION TO ACQUIRE NEXT. Spend compute where it can CHANGE A DECISION, not merely where an estimator is uncertain.

## Scope
- The three quantities per pending decision: EVSI (expected value of sample information — more stochastic samples), EVC (expected value of computation — higher fidelity/resolution), EVT (expected value of physical test — prototype/experiment), each computed against the CURRENT decision posture (which design ranks first, which constraint is binding).
- Action menu it arbitrates: run a cheaper surrogate; run a higher-resolution simulation; sample more scenarios; refine a mesh NEAR THE DECISION BOUNDARY (DWR-style but decision-weighted — the estimator-vs-decision distinction is the whole point); build/test a prototype; STOP because the decision is already robust.
- Posterior design-ranking uncertainty: probability-of-ranking-change machinery over Evidence-carrying estimates (numerical + statistical + model-form components each contribute — the Evidence composition paying off: escalate MODEL fidelity when model uncertainty dominates, per Rev B's decision-aware escalation, which is implemented here).
- Integration: fs-plan-models supplies cost models (what each action costs); fs-eproc supplies validity for sequential decisions; certify-or-escalate (fs-surrogate) becomes a special case of this general policy; HELM planner consumes VOI rankings.
- [M] discipline: heuristic baseline (uncertainty-proportional allocation) always available; VOI must BEAT it on decision-quality-per-cost benchmarks to earn default-on.

## Acceptance criteria
On synthetic decision problems with known ground truth: VOI-guided acquisition reaches correct decisions at measurably lower cost than uncertainty-sampling and round-robin baselines (the falsifiable claim, ledgered); ranking-change probabilities calibrated (predicted vs empirical flip rates); stop decisions valid (no premature stops beyond declared risk).

## Testing
Simulation batteries across decision-problem families; integration test: a two-fidelity optimization study where VOI correctly identifies when model-form (not statistical) uncertainty blocks the decision and escalates accordingly. Logs: per-decision VOI tables (EVSI/EVC/EVT), chosen actions with rationales, calibration tracking.

title: fs-gen: proposal-only generative models (shape diffusion, graph generators, program proposers) w/ type-enforced epistemics [F/M]
description:
## What
`fs-gen` (patch Rev N): PROPOSAL-ONLY generative models — diffusion/flow shape priors, graph generative models, and shape-program proposal models learned from the Design Ledger. The rule is absolute: generative models may PROPOSE (initial designs, restart seeds, mutation directions, latent manifolds, program sketches, repair actions); they may NEVER CERTIFY (physics outcomes, safety, feasibility, final rankings).

## Scope
- Latent shape diffusion over Region charts: score-based/flow-matching models on SDF latents or neural-implicit codes (fs-rep-neural latent spaces), trained on the ledger's accumulating design corpus (the system learns its OWN design distribution).
- Score-based proposal kernels for level-set fields: learned perturbation directions for topo-levelset (smarter mutations than isotropic noise).
- Graph generative models for truss/lattice candidates (FrankenNetworkx graphs — ground-structure priors beyond k-NN heuristics).
- Transformer-class shape-PROGRAM proposal models (fs-shapeprog DSL as the token vocabulary — proposing structured, editable programs, not blobs).
- Active-learning acquisition over generative priors: propose where the archive (fs-archive) is sparse AND the prior is confident — exploration with taste.
- ENFORCEMENT of proposal-only: type-level separation — fs-gen outputs are `Proposal<T>` (no Evidence fields), which CANNOT enter certified paths without passing through the standard validation machinery (quarantine-like promotion: geometry validity checks, physics evaluation, conformal wrapping). The compiler enforces the epistemics.
- Training reproducibility class documented (FrankenTorch determinism limits); model cards for every generative model (training-data provenance from ledger hashes).

## Acceptance criteria
Proposals measurably improve optimizer performance on fixture studies (better restart seeds than random: time-to-target improvements, ledgered — the falsifiable payoff); Proposal<T> type separation proven (compile-fail battery: no path from proposal to certified deliverable without validation); generated shapes pass geometry validity at documented rates (a QUALITY metric, not a safety requirement — safety is the type system's job).

## Testing
G0: latent round-trips, sampler determinism-class conformance; integration: archive-coupled proposal loop on a toy domain. Logs: proposal provenance (model version, training-corpus hash), acceptance rates, downstream-improvement attributions.

title: fs-constraint: typed constraint kinds (hard/soft/chance/robust/cert/fab/code), unsat cores, ranked repairs
description:
## What
`fs-constraint` (patch Rev F): the constraint CALCULUS — constraints with SEMANTICS, not anonymous g(x)≤0, plus infeasibility diagnosis with minimal unsatisfiable cores and ranked repairs.

## Scope
- ConstraintKind taxonomy: Hard (physics/safety — never traded); Soft{penalty law}; Chance{α, estimator kind}; Robust{uncertainty set}; Certification{proof required — SOS/interval, ties to fs-sos}; Fabrication{process}; Code{standard — AISC-class for the frame flagship}. Each kind carries its own optimizer treatment (hard → feasibility restoration priority; chance → e-process/UQ estimation; certification → proof obligations in the deliverable).
- ConstraintEvidence per evaluation: status, Evidence-typed violation magnitude, active-set role, REPAIR SUGGESTIONS (ranked actions).
- INFEASIBILITY DIAGNOSIS: when a design space is infeasible, return a MINIMAL UNSAT CORE (which constraint subset is jointly unsatisfiable — via elastic relaxation solves: minimize total violation, examine support) + ranked repairs with feasibility estimates (the patch's worked example: relax mass 1.2→1.35 kg → est. feasible 0.91; switch material → 0.77). This turns optimizer failures into DESIGN CONVERSATIONS.
- Integration: geometry primitives (xform-constraints) supply values/derivatives; fs-fab supplies fabrication semantics; fs-opt-ir hosts the nodes; fs-session's errors-as-guidance surfaces the diagnoses.
- Constraint ledger table rows (kind, IR, status, repair suggestions) per Rev S.

## Acceptance criteria
Kind-specific treatment verified per optimizer family (chance constraints actually estimated with the declared estimator + validity machinery; certification constraints refuse to report satisfied without their proof artifact); unsat-core extraction correct on seeded infeasible fixtures (core is minimal — dropping any member restores feasibility); repair rankings' feasibility estimates calibrated on the fixture family (predicted vs actual).

## Testing
G0: core minimality property tests; elastic-relaxation solve correctness vs enumeration on small fixtures; serialization round-trips. Logs: full diagnosis payloads (core, repairs, estimates) — the agent-facing artifact this bead exists for.
close_reason: Shipped fs-constraint, the constraint CALCULUS (plan 9.1 / Rev F): constraints with semantics, not anonymous g(x)<=0. Seven-kind taxonomy (Hard/Soft{quadratic,hinge}/Chance{MC estimator}/Robust{uncertainty box}/Certification{interval,sos}/Fabrication/Code) each mapping to its optimizer Treatment (feasibility-restoration / penalty / estimate-then-bound / prove-or-escalate / domain-check). THE VALIDITY MACHINERY IS REAL: chance constraints compute a Hoeffding lower confidence bound and report satisfied ONLY when the bound clears the level — fscon-003 pins the case where the raw empirical rate clears but the bound does not: BoundNotCleared, never Satisfied. THE PROOFS ARE REAL: an in-house rigorous interval evaluator over fs-opt graphs (conservative inclusion per node, honest refusals on div-through-zero/domain/PDE nodes) powers Certification kinds (which refuse satisfied pointwise without an artifact regardless of how good g(x) looks) and Robust kinds (proven over uncertainty boxes with enclosure certificates); G0 containment verified over 300 random nonlinear boxes. INFEASIBILITY DIAGNOSIS: elastic-relaxation solves (multi-start deterministic subgradient) + DELETION FILTER yield MINIMAL unsat cores — fscon-005 verifies against enumeration that the triangle fixture's 3-member core is jointly infeasible, EVERY single deletion restores feasibility, the satisfiable bystander is excluded, and elastic verdicts match grid truth 12/12 on the seeded family. RANKED REPAIRS with feasibility estimates CALIBRATED against exact enumeration (worst gap <0.05 on the worked mass/strength/stiffness example; drop-soft offered for soft members); the full diagnosis payload ships through fs-obs (the agent-facing artifact this bead exists for). Evidence-typed evaluations (exact violation certificates, active-set roles, penalty laws), canonical bitwise serialization round-trips, Rev S ledger rows. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 8 tests green, ubs clean, xtask fs-constraint clean, workspace 112 suites green excluding fs-dfo and fs-scenario (both actively mid-write by their owners — 7tv.4 and tfz.1). No-claims: to-nearest interval rounding (fs-ivl unification), SOS represented not executable, elastic solver is fixture-scale (production restoration solver is a later bead), single-Rn-variable hosts v1.

title: fs-fab: manufacturing/code-compliance constraints (AM/CNC/casting/steel/RC), assembly, cost+carbon models
description:
## What
`fs-fab` (patch Rev K): manufacturing, fabrication, and code compliance as its own layer — optimization without fabrication semantics produces FANTASY ARTIFACTS. Differentiable or certifiable wherever possible.

## Scope
- Additive manufacturing: overhang angle (differentiable via surface-normal fields), support-volume estimation, minimum feature size (medial oracle), powder-escape/drainability (topology constraints via topo-persistence), build-orientation co-optimization.
- Subtractive: CNC/milling ACCESSIBILITY (tool-reachability fields via geom-queries clearance/visibility analysis, minimum tool radius as curvature bounds), fixturing considerations (staged).
- Casting/molding: draft angle (xform-constraints primitive consumed here), parting-surface feasibility, no-enclosed-voids (persistence), uniform-wall-thickness preferences.
- Sheet metal + composite layup constraint families (staged, interfaces defined).
- Structural steel: CONNECTION FAMILIES (bolted/welded joint types with geometric envelopes), member standardization (catalog snapping via fs-constraint repair machinery), weld access, bolt spacing rules.
- Reinforced concrete: DETAILING rules (rebar spacing, cover, development lengths) as checkable constraints for the frame flagship.
- Assembly: sequence planning hooks (FrankenNetworkx precedence graphs), transport/lift/installation envelope constraints (member length/weight limits).
- COST AND CARBON MODELS: per-process parametric estimates with uncertainty envelopes (Evidence-typed — cost is a modeled quantity like any other), feeding objectives directly ("minimize embodied carbon s.t. code compliance" is a first-class study).
- Every constraint declares: differentiability class, certificate availability, fs-constraint kind (Fabrication{process} / Code{standard}).

## Acceptance criteria
Each constraint family verified on fixture geometries with known violations (detection + localization + repair suggestions); differentiable ones pass gradient gates; catalog/detailing rules validated against published examples (AISC-class member checks, ACI-class spacing fixtures); an end-to-end constrained study produces a design passing an independent rule-check pass.

## Testing
G0: constraint-evaluation determinism, unit coherence; adversarial fixtures per family. Logs: violation maps, repair suggestions, cost/carbon breakdowns with uncertainty.

## Addendum: tolerance stack-up (patch Rev K)
Assembly-level tolerance-chain analysis: worst-case and statistical (RSS) stack-up over mating features, tied to fs-uq geometric-tolerance KL machinery — fits and clearances verified across the tolerance band, not just at nominal. Differentiable where smooth (statistical form), certifiable via interval arithmetic (worst-case form).

title: ASCENT conformance: FrankenScipy oracle battery, BBOB/CUTEst/BO/MOO/QD benchmarks, validity trials, study-scale replay
description:
## What
The ASCENT conformance suite: layer-wide validation of every optimizer family against oracles, benchmarks, and the statistical-validity trials — plus the determinism and cancellation discipline that make optimization studies replayable.

## Scope
- FrankenScipy oracle battery (§12): shared optimization test problems — match results within tolerance, beat on wall-clock (documented); disagreements auto-filed.
- Benchmark batteries: BBOB/COCO-class (CMA-ES/DFO), CUTEst-class (gradient stack), BO standards (Branin/Hartmann), ZDT/DTLZ/WFG (MOO), QD standards (archive) — ERT/hypervolume/coverage tables ledgered with trend tracking (an optimizer regression is a perf regression — CI-gated).
- Gradient-gate sweep: every gradient-consuming path through the adjoint-vs-dual-vs-FD battery.
- Statistical validity integration: fs-eproc null simulations + adversarial stopping (with certify-certifiers); e-racing false-elimination audits; conformal coverage under the hardening scenarios.
- Cancellation/determinism: full studies (not just kernels) bit-replayable from seeds (G5 at study scale); G4 storms mid-study leave resumable, consistent state; forked studies (steering) verified independent.
- Topology-optimization result audits: geometry layer verifies optimizer outputs (thickness oracle on robust-SIMP results, persistence on topology-constrained results) — the cross-layer honesty loop.

## Acceptance criteria
All batteries green on both ISAs; every optimizer family has seeded-failure self-tests; study-scale replay verified on one fixture per family; suite runtime budgeted (PR subset vs nightly full).

## Testing
This IS the test bead. Logging: per-problem structured records (problem hash, seeds, traces, final KKT/e-value/hypervolume evidence) — every optimization claim in the docs must be reconstructible from these logs.

title: Gradient stack: L-BFGS/Adam, TR-Newton-Krylov w/ 2nd-order adjoints, AL/IP/SQP, Riemannian manifold variants, KKT certificates
description:
## What
The gradient-based optimizer stack (plan §9.2): FLUX adjoint → Sobolev/Riesz smoothing → optional Riemannian projection → optimizer — first-order, second-order, and constrained methods, manifold-correct.

## Scope
- The gradient pipeline made explicit: raw adjoint gradient (fs-adjoint) → Sobolev smoothing (the Riesz step — §8.7's noise-killer) → Riemannian projection onto constraint-manifold tangent spaces where applicable → optimizer step. Each stage evidenced (gradient provenance in the ledger).
- First-order: L-BFGS (THE default; m≈17-class memory, strong-Wolfe line search, deterministic); Adam-family for stochastic gradients (surrogate-training-adjacent uses).
- Second-order: matrix-free TRUST-REGION NEWTON–KRYLOV with Hessian-vector products from SECOND-ORDER ADJOINTS (Steihaug-CG inner solves, negative-curvature handling) — the frame-sizing workhorse (§15.2 step 2 names tr-newton-krylov).
- Constrained: AUGMENTED LAGRANGIAN (robust default), interior-point option, SQP for tightly-constrained small-dimension polish; constraint-kind dispatch via fs-constraint.
- Manifold variants: retraction + vector transport for Stiefel/sphere/SO(3)/fixed-volume level sets (fs-ga for rotation manifolds); Riemannian L-BFGS/TR.
- All optimizers: resumable state machines (P7 — an optimization study is pausable/forkable), budget-aware stopping (`:until (any (grad-norm 1e-5) (e-value 20) (budget-exhausted))` — Appendix C's stopping-condition algebra), KKT-residual certificates attached to every returned optimum (converged vs stalled distinguishable — §9.8).

## Acceptance criteria
CUTEst-class/Rosenbrock-family batteries: convergence within evaluation envelopes; FrankenScipy cross-validation on shared problems (§12 oracle contract); manifold correctness (orthogonality/volume preserved to roundoff along iterations); AL/IP/SQP on constrained fixtures with KKT residuals verified; second-order adjoint Hv passes Dual-of-Dual checks; deterministic trajectories from seeds (G5).

## Testing
G0: line-search/trust-region invariants (sufficient decrease, radius update laws); pipeline-stage ablation harness (smoothing on/off demonstrable on a mesh-noise fixture); resume-equality tests. Logs: iteration traces (f, ‖g‖, step, KKT), stage-wise gradient norms, stopping-condition attributions.

title: CMA-ES as natural-gradient IGO: full/sep/low-rank + BIPOP, NES unified; DE, DIRECT, Nelder-Mead, TR-DFO
description:
## What
CMA-ES as information-geometric optimization + the derivative-free/global suite (plan §9.3): the flagship population method implemented AS WHAT IT IS — natural-gradient flow on the Gaussian family (Bet 6) — which is not aesthetic name-dropping: it dictates the correct step-size/covariance couplings and unifies CMA-ES with NES in one codebase.

## Scope
- CMA-ES: full covariance with rank-µ + rank-1 updates, cumulative step-size adaptation, BIPOP restarts (the budget-doubling large/small population alternation for multimodality); SEPARABLE and LOW-RANK covariance variants for dim > ~200 (manifold-harmonic coefficient spaces live here).
- IGO framing: the update derived as natural gradient on the Gaussian statistical manifold; NES variants share the machinery (one codebase, two parameterizations); invariance properties (monotone-transform of f invariance) preserved BY CONSTRUCTION and property-tested.
- The rest of the DFO suite: differential evolution; DIRECT (Lipschitz-ish global structure); Nelder–Mead (cheap polish); trust-region DFO with quadratic interpolation models for expensive smooth-ish objectives.
- Population scheduling: a generation = embarrassingly parallel wave of FLUX evaluations as SIBLING SCOPES (fs-exec), raced under e-racing (§9.6 integration point); deterministic tournament reproducibility from seed (Bet 8's bit-reproducible requirement).
- Mixed-integer accommodations (catalog-snapped variables for constructability) documented.

## Acceptance criteria
BBOB/COCO-class benchmark battery: performance within published CMA-ES envelopes (ERT tables ledgered); invariance property tests (affine transforms of search space, monotone transforms of objective → identical behavior modulo seeds); BIPOP restart schedule correctness; determinism: identical evolution across thread counts given seed; FrankenScipy cross-checks where problems overlap.

## Testing
G0: covariance SPD maintenance, eigendecomposition stability, invariance laws; population-wave cancellation cleanliness (G4: kill a generation mid-wave, state consistent). Logs: per-generation traces (m, σ, C eigenspectrum, evals), restart events, ERT tables.
close_reason: CMA-ES flagship core landed in NEW crate fs-dfo (L4; engines IR-agnostic by design - collision-avoidance decision recorded after the 7tv.1 race; IR routing = small wiring bead once fs-opt stabilizes). CONTENT: full-covariance CMA-ES in the IGO/natural-gradient form (Hansen couplings: log-rank weights, rank-mu + rank-1, CSA; eigendecomposition via landed jacobi_eigh w/ SPD symmetrize+floor), keyed-Philox sampling -> ENTIRE EVOLUTION IS A PURE FUNCTION OF THE SEED (bitwise rerun-tested; golden hash cross-ISA - CMA trajectories bit-identical on M4 Pro and Threadripper), BIPOP restarts (doubling ladder w/ large-runs-only cap, lambda-scaled per-run budgets, deterministic perturbed restart starts, schedule in the report), Nelder-Mead polish. THREE MEASURED ENGINEERING LESSONS recorded in code comments + CONTRACT: (1) TolFun stagnation (f-stall 120 gens) is what actually frees restart budget - sigma-collapse alone fired too slowly (a lambda=150 local-basin run burned 120k evals); golden hash bumped once w/ justification when added; (2) BIPOP's restart cap must count LARGE runs only - counting smalls stalled the ladder at lambda~64; (3) per-run budgets must scale with lambda - handing half the total budget to the first small run just polishes one local minimum. IGO INVARIANCE TESTED BITWISE: exp/cube monotone transforms give identical trajectories, with the f64-resolution precondition documented (x^3 underflow + exp saturation create ties near machine precision - the first fixtures failed for exactly this reason, measured). Battery 7/7 + success-rate 1/1 BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/dfo2_verify.5sFrZD/run.log): sphere/rosenbrock(10)/ellipsoid-cond-1e6 to target, rastrigin(5) via BIPOP to 4.4e-9 w/ textbook schedule [8,8,16,...,128] in 66k evals, determinism, translation equivariance, NM bitwise-deterministic polish. clippy -D warnings, fmt, CONTRACT complete. SPLITS (in CONTRACT no-claims): sep-CMA/low-rank (dim>200), NES parameterizations, DE/DIRECT/TR-DFO, fs-exec population waves + e-racing, external COCO/ERT parity, mixed-integer snapping, fs-opt IR routing.

title: Bayesian opt: in-house GPs (exact->inducing), q-EI/q-NEI reparameterized, TuRBO, cost-aware multi-fidelity [F]
description:
## What
Bayesian and multi-fidelity optimization (plan §9.4 [F]): in-house GPs, batched acquisitions, trust-region BO, and cost-aware multi-fidelity — the mathematically principled version of "screen cheap, verify expensive."

## Scope
- Gaussian processes: Matérn-family kernels (1/2, 3/2, 5/2, ARD lengthscales), exact inference to ~10⁴ points (fs-la Cholesky), INDUCING-POINT sparse approximations beyond (Nyström/variational — fs-la-randomized machinery); hyperparameters by marginal likelihood with QMC-SEEDED MULTISTART (fs-rand-qmc — the plan names this detail).
- Acquisitions: batched q-EI/q-NEI via MC with REPARAMETERIZED GRADIENTS (FrankenTorch tape through the acquisition — differentiable sampling), optimized by the gradient stack; batch selection for population-scale parallelism (96 cores want q≈dozens).
- TuRBO-class trust-region BO for the 30–300-dim regime (local GP models in adaptive trust regions — the honest answer to BO's dimensionality ceiling).
- MULTI-FIDELITY acquisition where fidelity is a controllable knob (LBM resolution, mesh level, panel vs LES): cost-aware selection (knowledge-gradient/cost-weighted EI class), fidelity-correlation modeling (discrepancy models per evidence-model-ledger — the model-form machinery and BO sharing one substrate).
- Noise handling: heteroscedastic likelihoods fed by UQ variance estimates; e-process-compatible stopping.

## Acceptance criteria
GP regression: known-answer posteriors on analytic fixtures (mean/variance to tolerance); acquisition optimization: q-EI improves over random/sequential baselines on standard BO benchmarks (Branin/Hartmann/Ackley families, ledgered ERT); TuRBO: published-class performance on 100-dim benchmarks; multi-fidelity: correct cheap-vs-expensive allocation on synthetic two-fidelity fixtures with known correlation (cost-to-target vs single-fidelity, documented win).

## Testing
G0: kernel PSD-ness, posterior consistency laws (conditioning twice = conditioning on union); reparameterization gradient checks vs FD; hyperparameter-fit reproducibility from seeds. Logs: posterior diagnostics, acquisition landscapes (fixture dumps), fidelity-allocation decisions with cost ledgers.

title: fs-eproc: e-processes/test martingales (WSR betting), e-value arithmetic, e-BH, anytime-valid confidence sequences
description:
## What
`fs-eproc` core (plan §9.6, Bet 5): e-processes, test martingales, and anytime-valid confidence sequences — the statistical layer that makes optional stopping VALID, so the optimizer may peek continuously and stop the moment a comparison is decided.

## Scope
- Test-martingale/e-process constructions in the betting lineage (Waudby-Smith–Ramdas): nonparametric confidence sequences for bounded/sub-Gaussian means, betting-strategy mixtures (grid/plug-in), time-uniform coverage BY CONSTRUCTION.
- e-value arithmetic: composition across comparisons (products for independent, mixtures otherwise), evidence accumulation semantics documented precisely (what an e-value of 20 means operationally — Appendix C's `(e-value 20)` stopping condition).
- e-BH: family-wise/false-discovery control over THOUSANDS of simultaneous candidate comparisons (a CMA-ES generation's eliminations are a multiple-testing problem — this is the answer).
- Confidence-sequence API for estimation (not just testing): anytime-valid intervals consumed by UQ stopping ("sample until CS width < decision threshold").
- Determinism: e-processes over deterministic Philox streams → the entire statistical trajectory is replayable (Bet 8's bit-reproducible tournaments start here).
- Validity discipline: assumptions (boundedness, exchangeability where claimed) declared as model cards; certify-certifiers runs the adversarial-stopping trials (§13.2: type-I error at ADVERSARIALLY chosen stopping times ≤ α, empirically).

## Acceptance criteria
Coverage/validity simulations: time-uniform coverage holds empirically across distribution families incl. adversarial stopping (millions of null simulations, nightly-rotated); e-BH controls the target error metric under dependence structures it claims to handle; power curves documented (samples-to-decision vs effect size — the tables e-racing's payoff claims rest on); bit-reproducible trajectories from seeds.

## Testing
G0: martingale property spot checks (conditional expectation ≤ current value under null, tested via simulation); composition arithmetic laws; CS monotone-shrinkage sanity. Logs: e-value trajectories, stopping-time distributions, coverage tables per scenario.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with IDENTICAL cross-ISA simulation results. Delivered: BettingEProcess (WSR lineage — log-wealth via strict ln, predictable regularized plug-in bet, one-sided clip; validity STRUCTURAL); PairwiseRace (the e-racing primitive: paired-loss midpoint vs null 1/2); GaussianMixtureCs (Robbins normal mixture — closed-form time-uniform radius + mixture-martingale e_value_for); e-value arithmetic (product/dependence-robust average via max-shifted LSE/e-to-p); e-BH (Wang-Ramdas, deterministic tie-breaking). EMPIRICAL EVIDENCE (release-mode, seeded): Ville validity under ADVERSARIAL stopping 0.0305<=0.05 (4000 sims x 2000 horizon); power scaling (median stop 241@d=.05 -> 32@d=.15); CS time-uniform miss 0.0487<=0.05, radius 0.573->0.086; e-BH FDR 0.001<=0.1 @ power 1.00; race decided t=62, bit-replayable. HEADLINE: ALL VERDICT NUMBERS IDENTICAL on trj x86 (EXIT:0) — full statistical batteries bit-reproduce across ISAs (fs-rand+fs-math+eproc stack), proving Bet 8's replayable-tournament property pre-executor. Boundedness contract ENFORCED (out-of-range observations refused — they would void the guarantee silently). 8 tests, clippy clean, CONTRACT complete. NO-CLAIMS: empirical-Bernstein CS variant, conformal e-prediction (surrogate/hardening beads), nightly adversarial rotation (certify-certifiers), two-sided betting.

title: e-racing [M]: e-process-driven mid-solve candidate cancellation, bit-reproducible tournaments, anytime-valid UQ stopping
description:
## What
e-RACING (plan §9.6, Bet 8 [M]): the marriage of anytime-valid statistics and structured cancellation — sequential tests DRIVE asupersync cancellation inside evolutionary/Bayesian optimizers. "No system I'm aware of closes this loop."

## Scope
- e-raced generations: within a CMA-ES/NES generation or successive-halving bracket, per-candidate Monte Carlo evaluation streams feed e-processes; the MOMENT a candidate's elimination e-value crosses threshold, ASCENT cancels its ENTIRE evaluation scope tree (fs-exec-speculation kill-handles, ≤200 µs reclaim) and reassigns cores to survivors.
- Expected payoff (stated to be falsifiable): 2–5× more generations per core-hour on noisy objectives at IDENTICAL statistical guarantees — measured on the flagship-class fixtures, ledgered; if the payoff doesn't materialize, the honest report says so ([M] discipline).
- Bit-reproducible tournaments in deterministic mode: elimination ORDER depends only on (seed, logical stream identities) — never on wall-clock racing (the subtle requirement: e-value crossings are evaluated at deterministic stream checkpoints, not asynchronous arrival — design documented).
- Anytime-valid UQ stopping wired: MLMC/QMC estimators (fs-uq) stop exactly when the CS is tight enough for the pending decision — no conservative pre-registered sample sizes, no invalid peeking.
- Racing topologies: pairwise vs field-vs-best vs successive-halving brackets; e-BH for family-wise control across the population; integration API for bayesopt candidate filtering too.
- The ornithoid flagship consumes this at scale (step 2: dominated wings die at 10–20% of their solve).

## Acceptance criteria
Statistical: eliminated candidates are truly dominated at declared confidence (validated on synthetic noisy objectives with known ground truth — zero excess false-elimination beyond α); systems: cancellations reclaim cores within latency budget under full population load (G4-instrumented); determinism: identical tournament outcomes across thread counts/runs given seed (G5); payoff: measured generations/core-hour improvement vs non-raced baseline on noisy fixture studies, honestly reported.

## Testing
Adversarial: near-tied candidates (elimination must NOT trigger — power/level verified); mid-race storms (G4); replay equality. Logs: per-candidate e-trajectories, kill timestamps + reclaim latencies, tournament replay records — the complete forensic record of every race.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: fs-eproc closed — your primitives are live: PairwiseRace (a_beats_b at alpha, log_e_value for brackets), e_benjamini_hochberg for generation-level FWER, all bit-replayable (proven cross-ISA). Kill-handle wiring (fs-exec-speculation) is your remaining dependency.

title: fs-surrogate: FNO/DeepONet + POD-DEIM/balanced-truncation/Koopman, ledger-trained, conformal e-prediction, certify-or-escalate
description:
## What
`fs-surrogate` (plan §9.7, Bet 9 [F]): learned accelerators with GUARANTEES — neural operators and classical ROMs as 100–10,000× cheap physics, permitted ONLY inside conformal-e-certified validity bands. ML proposes; certified numerics disposes.

## Scope
- Neural operators (FrankenTorch): FOURIER NEURAL OPERATORS on box-resampled fields (fs-fft provides the spectral machinery); DeepONets for parametric families (branch/trunk over design parameters × query points).
- Classical ROMs: POD-Galerkin with DEIM (nonlinear term interpolation; POD via fs-la-randomized/TSQR over snapshot matrices); BALANCED TRUNCATION for LTI subsystems; KOOPMAN/DMD for unsteady flows — the ornithoid's reduced flight-dynamics models around trim states (§15.1 step 4) come from here.
- CONTINUOUS TRAINING FROM THE LEDGER: the accumulating (design → field/objective) corpus IS the training set; retraining triggers, versioned surrogate artifacts (content-addressed, model-carded), train/validation splits respecting design-family structure.
- CONFORMAL e-PREDICTION wrapping (the Bet 5 fusion): distribution-free prediction bands with anytime validity under the e-value formulation, RECALIBRATED ONLINE as new ground-truth solves arrive.
- CERTIFY-OR-ESCALATE (the mechanical policy): inside the certified band AND band-width below decision-relevance → use the surrogate; outside or too wide → escalate fidelity, and the new solve BOTH decides the point AND tightens the band. The system gets faster every week it runs without ever silently trusting a hallucinated flow field.
- Model cards mandatory (validity domain, training distribution, known failure modes); conformal-hardening bead adds the drift/conditioning machinery.

## Acceptance criteria
FNO/DeepONet: standard operator-learning benchmarks (Darcy/Burgers-class from our own solvers' data) at published-class accuracy; POD-DEIM: reduced-vs-full trajectories within declared bounds on fixtures; Koopman: trim-state flight-dynamics fixture reproduces linearization behavior; conformal bands: EMPIRICAL COVERAGE at nominal levels on held-out + shifted data (certify-certifiers integration); certify-or-escalate: end-to-end optimization fixture where the policy demonstrably reduces cost vs all-high-fidelity at equal final answer quality (ledgered).

## Testing
G0: band monotonicity under recalibration; escalation-trigger correctness on seeded OOD queries; training reproducibility class documented. Logs: coverage tracking over time, escalation events with reasons, cost-savings accounting.

title: Conformal hardening: Mondrian buckets (by regime/fidelity/family), drift e-tests, false-coverage budgets, shift-adversarial validation
description:
## What
Conformal/e-process HARDENING (patch Rev M): the anytime-valid layer's assumptions made into explicit operational contracts — conditional calibration, drift detection, and false-coverage budgeting. The system must not advertise distribution-free protection while quietly violating exchangeability by changing design regimes.

## Scope
- MONDRIAN (conditional) calibration: conformal buckets by regime class (fs-regime's groups — the cross-layer payoff), fidelity level, geometry family, objective type, scenario cluster, solver settings — coverage guarantees PER BUCKET, not just marginal (marginal coverage can hide systematic per-regime failure).
- DRIFT DETECTION: ledger training distribution vs current candidate distribution → two-sample e-tests (sequential, anytime-valid — eating our own dogfood); on drift: automatic recalibration or escalation, surrogate validity domains SHRINK in response (Evidence validity_domain updates flow through).
- FALSE-COVERAGE-RATE BUDGETING: when thousands of candidates × objectives × constraints × time points are monitored simultaneously, coverage errors get an explicit ledger budget (analogous to the Error Ledger — e-BH machinery from fs-eproc applied to coverage claims); the budget is part of study admission (P4 extended to statistical error).
- Exchangeability accounting: which conformal claims assume what, declared on model cards; optimization-induced distribution shift (the optimizer MOVES toward regions the surrogate hasn't seen — the fundamental tension) handled by weighting/refresh policies with documented validity.

## Acceptance criteria
Per-bucket coverage verified empirically across regime shifts (certify-certifiers' shift scenarios pass); drift e-tests detect seeded covariate shift within documented sample budgets, and the escalation path fires; FCR budget accounting correct under simultaneous monitoring at flagship scale (simulation battery); optimizer-shift stress test: a deliberately surrogate-exploiting optimizer does NOT achieve silent invalid coverage (the adversarial test that matters most).

## Testing
Simulation batteries per mechanism with structured logs (bucket coverage tables, drift-detection latencies, budget ledgers); integration with fs-surrogate's online recalibration. Logs double as the calibration audit trail regulators-of-the-future would want.

title: L1 BEDROCK: numerical foundations
description:
## Mission
L1 BEDROCK: the in-house numerical foundations — dense LA (GEMM, batched small dense, factorizations, eigensolvers, randomized NLA), sparse formats/preconditioners, FFT, certified arithmetic (intervals/affine/Taylor models/exact predicates), function objects (chebfun-style), automatic differentiation, and RNG/QMC.

## Why in-house (plan §6)
Everything here is written in pure safe Rust with audited SIMD leaves because the entire point is kernels shaped for OUR data layouts (Morton tiles, SoA, arena packing) and OUR two machine families, not a 1979 Fortran calling convention. No BLAS/LAPACK/FFI (P1). FrankenScipy serves as the conformance oracle: where semantics overlap, our kernels must MATCH FrankenScipy in the Gauntlet and BEAT it on the roofline.

## Signature positions
- Batched small dense (6×6…48×48 FEM element matrices) is a first-class citizen with SIMD lanes across the batch — routinely 5–15× over loop-over-LAPACK.
- Mixed precision with iterative refinement is a default policy (factor f32, refine f64, escalate to double-double only where the Error Ledger demands).
- Matrix-free-native eigensolvers (LOBPCG/Lanczos) because modal/buckling/stability problems reduce to operator applications (P6).
- fs-ivl is what the word "Certified" means everywhere else in the system: outward-rounded intervals via next_up/next_down (thread-safe, no rounding-mode fiddling), Taylor models, Shewchuk exact predicates.

## Members
fs-rand-philox, fs-rand-qmc, fs-la-gemm, fs-la-batched, fs-la-factorizations, fs-la-eigen, fs-la-randomized, fs-la-mixed-precision, fs-sparse-core, fs-sparse-precond, fs-fft, fs-ivl-core, fs-ivl-taylor, fs-ivl-predicates, fs-cheb, fs-ad-forward, fs-ad-adjoint-infra, bedrock-conformance.

## Success Criteria
GEMM/SpMV/FFT within target roofline bands on both ISAs (see roofline-harness); G0 law suites green; FrankenScipy cross-checks green; deterministic mode bit-stable through all reductions.

title: fs-rand: counter-based Philox streams keyed by logical identity + deterministic distributions
description:
## What
`fs-rand` core: the in-house counter-based Philox-class generator + distributions (plan §6.7) — the foundation of P2's reproducible parallel randomness.

## Scope
- Philox-class counter-based RNG (in-house, P1): stateless function of (key, counter); streams keyed by LOGICAL work identity (seed, kernel_id, tile_id, iteration) so results are independent of which worker ran which tile. SIMD-friendly bulk generation (4×/8× lanes via fs-simd).
- Stream discipline: hierarchical key derivation (study → kernel → tile → draw) with collision-free composition; splittable substreams for nested parallelism; explicit stream provenance (keys are ledgerable values — Five Explicits' "seeds" pillar).
- Distributions: uniform (open/closed variants with correct rounding), standard normal (ziggurat with deterministic table + exact tail handling), exponential, gamma, beta, Dirichlet, categorical/alias method, von Mises–Fisher (for direction sampling in rendering/GA), truncated variants. All deterministic given (key, counter) — no rejection-loop thread-order sensitivity (rejection counts are part of the counter advance contract).
- Statistical quality gates: PractRand/TestU01-class battery reimplemented as needed in-house or via documented dev-only oracle (isolated per dependency policy), run nightly.

## Why
Counter-based RNG is what makes e-raced tournaments bit-reproducible, renders replayable, and MC/UQ results independent of scheduling — the load-bearing seed story of the whole system.

## Acceptance criteria
Known-answer tests vs published Philox vectors; stream independence (statistical + collision analysis); worker-shuffle invariance test (same logical work, different thread counts → identical draws); distribution correctness (KS/chi-square vs closed forms; moments to tolerance).

## Testing
Property: key-derivation injectivity on sampled subspaces; counter-advance contracts under rejection sampling. G5: cross-thread-count hash equality. Logs: battery results, per-distribution statistics tables, seeds everywhere.

Tests: 11 incl. 16-tile x 3-order shuffle invariance (the P2 property), adjacent-identity decorrelation, chi-square/moment gates. VERIFIED on aarch64 (M4 Pro) AND x86-64 (trj 5995WX, EXIT:0). title: fs-sparse preconditioners: Chebyshev smoothers, level-scheduled ILU/IC(0), SA-AMG coarse, capped supernodal
description:
## What
`fs-sparse` preconditioner components (plan §6.2): the many-core-honest smoother/coarse-solver toolkit that solver-stack composes.

## Scope
- Chebyshev polynomial smoothers — THE many-core-friendly choice (no sequential dependencies, pure SpMV chains); eigenvalue-bound estimation (power iteration / Lanczos via fs-la-eigen) with safety factors; deterministic by construction.
- IC(0)/ILU(0) with level scheduling where serial chains are tolerable (documented as the bandwidth-hostile fallback, not the default).
- Smoothed-aggregation AMG built in-house: strength-of-connection, parallel aggregation (deterministic tie-breaking on aggregate assignment — P2 applies to setup, not just solves), tentative prolongator + Jacobi smoothing, Galerkin triple product via SpGEMM; primarily employed as the COARSE SOLVER under p-multigrid (§8.9), not as the workhorse.
- Supernodal Cholesky: deliberately minimized per P6 ("never assemble what we can apply") — exists for small coarse grids and 1D-ish problems; explicitly NOT a load-bearing wall; scope capped.
- All components expose the operator interface (apply, not matrices where avoidable) and are cancellation-clean + resumable-safe (a smoother mid-sweep can be abandoned; setup phases checkpointable).

## Acceptance criteria
Chebyshev: smoothing-factor measurements match theory on model problems; AMG: O(N)-ish setup+solve scaling demonstrated on Poisson/elasticity fixtures to 10⁸ DOF class, grid-independent iteration counts within envelope; deterministic setup across thread counts (G5); FrankenScipy baseline comparisons where overlapping.

## Testing
G0: smoother symmetry/spectral properties; two-grid convergence-factor tests vs theory; adversarial aggregation fixtures (anisotropy, jumps). Logs: per-level operator complexities, convergence factors, setup/solve timings into roofline lane.
close_reason: fs-sparse preconditioners landed: Precond operator trait + Chebyshev smoother (three-term recurrence, band [lmax/30, lmax], lmax via deterministic libm-free power iteration w/ 1.1 safety - ENCLOSURE tested vs analytic 2D Laplacian spectrum), ILU(0) zero-fill (typed IluBreakdown{row}, shift-retry documented as caller policy), SA-AMG (symmetric strength, greedy index-order aggregation w/ lowest-index tie-breaks = P2 on SETUP, Jacobi-smoothed prolongator omega=4/(3 lmax(D^-1 A)), Galerkin via landed transpose+spgemm - the triple product this crate's SpGEMM was built for, V-cycle w/ Chebyshev smoothing + ILU-PCG coarsest solve, stagnation guard), pcg reference driver w/ PcgReport evidence object. Battery 7/7 BOTH ISAs: spectral-bound enclosure, Chebyshev and ILU each >2x fewer PCG iterations than plain CG on 2D Poisson w/ solution agreement, typed breakdown, AMG near-grid-independence (32^2 vs 64^2 iteration band) + operator complexity < 2, anisotropic eps=1e-3 adversary converges, setup+solve rerun-BITWISE determinism, golden hash 0x752f215a26e32fea IDENTICAL cross-ISA FIRST TRY (trj /data/tmp/pc_verify.4YR5K8/run.log) - the no-libm-in-solver-state rule from the eigensolver incident applied from the start. fs-sparse stays DEPENDENCY-FREE (decision recorded: in-crate power iteration instead of fs-la coupling; formats/solvers compose at call sites). clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 20 crates 0 violations, CONTRACT invariants 7-8 added. Recorded deferrals: supernodal Cholesky (bead's own scope cap), level-scheduled ILU parallelism, dense direct coarse solve + real smoother composition (solver-stack flux-tfz.10, now substantially unblocked), 1e8-DOF scaling lane, FrankenScipy baselines.

title: fs-fft: Stockham mixed-radix, r2c/DCT paths, cache-blocked 3D pencils on tile executor
description:
## What
`fs-fft`: the in-house FFT (plan §6.3) — iterative Stockham autosort, mixed radix 4/8, real-input split paths, cache-blocked transposes, 3D via pencil decomposition on the tile executor.

## Scope
- Stockham autosort core (no bit-reversal pass), mixed radix-4/8 with radix-2 tails; twiddle tables precomputed deterministic (fs-math strict functions in deterministic mode).
- Real-input/real-output split-complex paths (r2c/c2r) — half the bandwidth for real fields (the common case: pressure/density/temperature).
- DCT-I/II/III via FFT folding — REQUIRED BY fs-cheb (Chebyshev transforms are DCTs; the function layer's performance rides on this).
- Multi-dimensional: cache-blocked transposes (tile-sized, 128 B-aligned), 3D pencil decomposition across fs-exec tiles with CCD-aware pencil ownership.
- SIMD via fs-simd/fs-tilelang for butterflies; deterministic mode = fixed butterfly/accumulation order (FFT is naturally fixed-shape; document the guarantee).
- Consumers (API shaped for them): spectral Poisson on boxes, Fourier neural operators (fs-surrogate), optimal-transport convolutions (multiobjective-robust Sinkhorn), rendering image ops, Chebyshev transforms.
- Roofline: ≥40% of memory-bound limit for in-cache 3D pencils (§14.1), ledgered.

## Acceptance criteria
Round-trip (forward∘inverse = identity·N) to tight ULP bounds across size sweep (powers of 2, mixed 4/8 sizes, prime-tail rejection with structured error listing supported sizes); Parseval verification; r2c/c2r consistency with complex reference; FrankenScipy cross-checks; perf targets on both ISAs.

## Testing
G0 properties: linearity, shift theorem, Parseval, conjugate symmetry of real transforms; impulse/delta known answers; 3D pencil vs single-node reference equality. Logs: per-size GFLOP/s + bandwidth attainment, plan choices.
close_reason: fs-fft v1 landed: radix-2 DIF Stockham autosort (OTFFT formulation), strict-mode twiddles via fs-math det sin/cos, r2c via half-size packing+untangle, DCT-II/III via FFT folding. 8/8 tests green on BOTH ISAs: naive O(n^2) DFT oracle n=1..512 (rel<1e-12), impulse/constant/linearity, Parseval+shift theorem, r2c vs embedded oracle, DCT vs naive + round-trip, structured rejection, determinism. Cross-ISA golden hash 0xbd5568d233f4b4bc IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/fft_verify.EYhNhw/run.log). clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean, xtask check-all 16 crates 0 violations. Full CONTRACT.md written (10 sections, no-claim boundaries list deferred scope). Perf+multidim scope split to frankensim-27d3 with complete context. title: fs-ivl core: outward-rounded intervals (next_up/next_down), affine arithmetic, dd/qd ladder
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` core: interval + affine arithmetic and the double-double/quad-double escalation ladder (plan §6.4). This crate is WHAT THE WORD "CERTIFIED" MEANS everywhere else in the system.

## Scope
- Interval arithmetic with RIGOROUS outward rounding via next_up/next_down directed nudging — no global rounding-mode fiddling (Rust-safe, thread-safe, works under any SIMD mixing). All arithmetic ops + elementary functions using fs-math's DECLARED ENCLOSURES (the dependency that makes elementary-function intervals rigorous rather than folkloric).
- Affine arithmetic: noise-symbol form for correlation-aware tightness (kills the dependency problem interval arithmetic suffers on x−x patterns; essential for deep F-rep DAG evaluation).
- Double-double/quad-double types (built on fs-la-mixed-precision's EFT primitives — one implementation) serving as the high-precision oracle for certify-certifiers and the escalation ladder's top rungs.
- Directed-rounding vector ops via fs-simd where lane semantics allow, scalar fallback where they don't — correctness NEVER traded for lanes in certified paths.
- Containment discipline: every operation's postcondition is enclosure (result interval ⊇ true value set); this is the G0 law tested under random expression rewrites.

## Acceptance criteria
Containment property suite green across arithmetic + elementary functions (billions of randomized cases nightly vs dd/qd oracle); affine arithmetic demonstrably tighter than plain intervals on correlated expressions (measured tightness ratios ledgered); no rounding-mode global state anywhere (grep-lintable).

## Testing
G0: containment under rewrites (x·(y+z) vs x·y+x·z must both contain truth); known hard cases (Rump's polynomial); cross-ISA identical interval endpoints in strict mode (G5). Logs: tightness statistics, violation counts (must be zero), oracle-disagreement forensics.

CONTAINMENT LAW test battery: 100k dd-oracle arithmetic checks, 20k rewrite pairs, 50k intervals x 6 elementary fns vs libm point oracle, 20k checks over 4000 random DAGs - 0 violations; Rump's polynomial contains exact -54767/66192 with honestly-wide enclosure. Cross-ISA golden hash 0x3712a4c12d5e5864 IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/ivl_verify.eVYb3f/run.log, all suites green both ISAs). clippy -D warnings clean (Dd/Interval/Affine use std operator traits), fmt applied, xtask check-all 16 crates 0 violations. CONTRACT.md complete for fs-ivl + fs-math contract extended with eft/dd sections. Recorded deferrals (in CONTRACT no-claim + this bead's comments): quad-double, Taylor models, exact predicates, affine elementary fns (Chebyshev linearization), SIMD lanes, billions-scale nightly battery (CI bead huq.4 scope). Dep on 6ys.8 was removed pre-work (EFT relocation, see comments on both beads).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent, post fs-fft + fs-sparse). Dependency on 6ys.8 removed - EFT/dd relocated to fs-math (L0), see comment there. v1 design: (1) Interval{lo,hi} with outward rounding via fs-math nudge_out/next_up/next_down (already documented in fs-math CONTRACT as fs-ivl's directed-rounding primitive) - basic ops nudge 1 ulp (correctly-rounded ops are within 1/2 ulp so 1 step is RIGOROUS), elementary functions nudge by fs-math's DECLARED budgets (exp/expm1/ln/sin/cos 3, tanh 5, sqrt 0). sin/cos handle non-monotonicity by counting critical points against a conservative interval enclosure of pi; width >= 2pi gives [-1,1]. title: fs-ivl Taylor models: rigorous remainder enclosures, interval Newton/Krawczyk, Lipschitz extraction
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` Taylor models + certified root isolation (plan §6.4): multivariate polynomial + interval remainder enclosures, interval Newton, and the Krawczyk operator.

## Scope
- Taylor models: multivariate polynomial part (fixed max degree, sparse monomial storage) + rigorous interval remainder; arithmetic (add/mul/compose), integration/differentiation, range bounding (better-than-interval enclosures for smooth functions over wide domains — the tool that makes NURBS→SDF certification and Lipschitz-bound extraction feasible).
- Interval Newton and Krawczyk operators for certified root isolation: existence + uniqueness verification in boxes, bisection-based search with certified exclusion; multivariate variants for intersection problems (curve-curve, ray-patch polishing in fs-render-charts and convert-nurbs-sdf).
- Lipschitz/derivative bound extraction: certified global/local Lipschitz constants from Taylor-model derivative enclosures — feeds certified sphere tracing (§10.2) and F-rep evaluator bounds (§7.2).
- Escalation integration: when a Taylor model's remainder is too wide for the caller's budget, escalate degree or subdivide, with cost accounting (P4 budgets apply to certification too).

## Acceptance criteria
Enclosure property (TM range ⊇ true range) verified vs oracle sampling + dd/qd; root isolation: all roots found with certified uniqueness on polynomial batteries (Wilkinson-class adversaries included); Krawczyk existence proofs validated on known fixture systems; Lipschitz bounds conservative-but-useful (tightness ratio tracked).

## Testing
G0 containment laws for TM arithmetic/composition; comparative tightness TM vs interval vs affine (documented crossover guidance); certified-root determinism. Logs: remainder widths, subdivision trees, isolation certificates with box coordinates.

Battery 7/7 BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/tm_verify.FnnCv7/run.log): functional containment (41 point checks), remainder superlinear convergence >20x per halving at order 5 PLUS the decisive dependency-fixture comparison (x - x^2: TM excess 1e6+ below IA excess) - with the instructive negative recorded in CONTRACT: monotone single-occurrence expressions give IA near-zero excess (measured 2e-15), so the first comparison fixture was WRONG in a way worth documenting; sqrt2 + sin roots Certified with tight boxes, x^2 double root NEVER falsely certified (the honesty case), Krawczyk agrees+contracts, Lipschitz tight on sin/cubic, golden hash 0xa16d9759a35295d8 IDENTICAL cross-ISA. clippy -D warnings, fmt; the 1 workspace xtask violation is the other agent's in-flight scaffold (not fs-ivl). Recorded deferrals: multivariate TMs, TM division, verified ODE integration, higher-dim Newton/Krawczyk (needs interval linear algebra).

comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 6ys.15). v1 design, new modules in fs-ivl (taylor.rs + newton.rs; existing interval/affine/predicates modules and their golden hashes UNTOUCHED): (1) TaylorModel1 (univariate, fixed order n default 5): stored f64 polynomial coefficients in (x-c) + Interval remainder, INVARIANT f(x) in P(x-c)+remainder for all x in domain (the containment law extended to functional enclosures). Rounding rigor via the affine-module pattern: every coefficient op computed with Interval arithmetic, midpoint stored, width absorbed into remainder (outward-rounded). Ops: add/sub/scale, mul (truncated product; dropped高-order terms bounded by interval eval over the centered domain and folded into remainder), compose exp/sin with LAGRANGE remainders (sin: |R_n| <= |h|^(n+1)/(n+1)! since all derivs <= 1; exp: e^(sup) * |h|^(n+1)/(n+1)! with sup from interval eval - both outward-rounded). eval_interval(subbox) for tight enclosures over subdomains.
