title: fs-ivl exact predicates: Shewchuk adaptive orient2d/3d, incircle/insphere + symbolic perturbation
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` exact geometric predicates (plan §6.4): Shewchuk-style adaptive-precision floating-point expansions providing EXACT orient2d/orient3d/incircle/insphere — the difference between a mesher that works and a mesher that works until Tuesday.

## Scope
- Floating-point expansion arithmetic (grow-expansion, expansion-sum, scale-expansion — exact ops on sums of non-overlapping floats) as safe Rust with the EFT primitives shared from the mixed-precision/ivl family.
- Adaptive-precision predicate ladder: fast filtered path (interval/static filter — computed error bound; if sign is certain, done) escalating through progressively exact stages only when needed; the standard Shewchuk staging, in-house per P1.
- Predicates: orient2d, orient3d, incircle, insphere; plus the derived ones the mesh pipeline needs (segment/triangle orientation tests, symbolic perturbation hooks for degeneracy — SoS-style consistent tie-breaking so Delaunay is deterministic even on cospherical inputs, P2).
- Consumers: fs-mesh-delaunay (BRIO incremental Delaunay), fs-rep-mesh repair (winding numbers use robust orientation), medial axis (filtered Voronoi), self-intersection certificates.

## Acceptance criteria
Exactness verified against arbitrary-precision oracle (dd/qd + exact rational fallback in tests) on adversarial near-degenerate batteries (points on a common sphere perturbed by 1 ulp, collinear grids, Kettner-class breakdown cases that famously crash naive meshes); filter efficiency measured (% resolved at fast stage) — performance without correctness compromise.

## Testing
G0: sign agreement with oracle across generated degeneracy families; symbolic-perturbation consistency (same input ordering → same tie resolution, both ISAs); throughput benchmarks (filter hit rates). Logs: per-stage resolution counts, escalation histograms, worst-case inputs archived as fixtures.
close_reason: Exact geometric predicates landed in fs-ivl (expansion + predicates modules). EXPANSION ARITHMETIC: faithful Shewchuk ports on the shared fs-math EFTs — fast_expansion_sum_zeroelim (branchless magnitude merge), scale_expansion_zeroelim (FMA two_prod, no Dekker splitting), expansion_product (divide-and-conquer distillation), exact diffs, sign-from-largest-component; exactness proven by RESIDUAL LAWS (result minus inputs distills to the empty expansion — no tolerance, no oracle ceiling) plus a catastrophic-cancellation battery. PREDICATES: orient2d (full faithful A/B/C/D adaptive ladder with Shewchuk's proven arrangement-specific error-bound constants, epsilon = 2^-53), orient3d / incircle / insphere (stage-A static filter -> exact difference-expansion determinants; exact by translation invariance), all with *_with_stage telemetry. ACCEPTANCE PROVEN: i128 integer-lattice oracle agreement over thousands of seeded rounds in 2D and 3D with degeneracy hits asserted (points on common circles/spheres, collinear grids -> exact Zero); dyadic-scaled and 1-ulp perturbation batteries with analytic ground truth; Kettner-class half-ulp grid at (0.5,0.5) where the naive float determinant demonstrably misjudges while the exact ladder matches exact-integer reformulation truth and stays antisymmetric; filter efficiency measured and logged (>99% of general-position inputs resolve at stage A — performance without correctness compromise). SoS: orient2d_sos implements the Edelsbrunner-Mucke ladder (derived term-by-term in comments: D; x3-x2; y2-y3; x1-x3; +1) — total, antisymmetric, deterministic, proven on coincident/collinear worst cases; orient3d_sos = documented deterministic projection cascade. Documented no-claims in CONTRACT.md: Shewchuk's standard overflow/underflow domain caveat; incircle/insphere SoS not provided (hooks + orientation SoS only; full 3D E-M ladder deferred until fs-mesh Delaunay needs it); exact tail deliberately unoptimized Vec (cold path). 23 lib + 4 conformance + 2 legacy tests green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; xtask check-all 19 crates 0 violations. Note: dsr not present on this machine — verification via local cargo lanes per above.

title: fs-cheb: chebfun-style function objects, spectral collocation eigenproblems (Orr-Sommerfeld-ready)
description:
## What
`fs-cheb`: compute with FUNCTIONS as values — the chebfun-inspired layer (plan §6.5 [F]): 1D/2D/3D functions as adaptively truncated Chebyshev/Fourier expansions with near-machine-precision automatic degree selection.

## Scope
- 1D core: adaptive sampling → DCT (via fs-fft) → coefficient truncation at machine-precision plateau; algebra (+,×,∘), calculus (differentiate, integrate, cumsum), rootfinding (colleague-matrix eigenvalues via fs-la-eigen), extrema, norms.
- 2D/3D: low-rank representations (Chebyshev–Chebyshev outer products, adaptive cross approximation) for smooth multivariate functions.
- Periodic (Fourier) variant for closed curves/angular profiles.
- Spectral collocation eigenproblem machinery: differentiation matrices with boundary-condition row replacement, generalized eigenproblems — THE ORR–SOMMERFELD PATH: the vessel flagship's laminarity objective (§15.3) solves Orr–Sommerfeld eigenproblems along lip streamlines with this exact machinery; API must make "modal growth rates σ₁…σ₈ along a path" a first-class query.
- Named consumers: airfoil sections and spline profiles, BC specification via fs-scenario ("the inlet velocity profile is THIS FUNCTION, not a lookup table"), 1D stability eigenproblems, quadrature of smooth integrands.
- Qty integration: dimensioned function objects (a profile of velocity vs arc-length carries units end to end).

## Acceptance criteria
Machine-precision recovery of analytic fixtures (exp, Runge, oscillatory) with expected degree growth; rootfinding finds all roots on batteries with certified none-missed cross-checks (fs-ivl Newton where certification requested); Orr–Sommerfeld: reproduce published critical Reynolds number for plane Poiseuille (Re_c ≈ 5772.22, eigenvalue to documented digits) — the acceptance test for the whole spectral stack.

## Testing
G0: algebra/calculus identities (d/dx ∫ = id to precision), truncation-plateau detection robustness (noise floors); FrankenScipy special-function cross-checks. Logs: degrees chosen, plateau diagnostics, eigenvalue convergence tables vs collocation order.

Spectral collocation: Chebyshev-Lobatto diff_matrix (Trefethen construction, negative-sum trick stated BITWISE-testably: diagonal exactly negates the off-diagonal sum in construction order - tested), dirichlet_laplace_eigs demo (shift-inverted iteration with shifts from a symmetric FD surrogate via landed jacobi_eigh - deterministic, non-circular, validates the collocation eigen path to 1e-6 vs analytic (k pi/2)^2). Battery 7/7 BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/cheb_verify.jhoCeY/run.log): machine-precision recovery with expected degree growth (exp<=20 < runge<=300, sin20x in band), calculus identities, plateau robustness vs deterministic jitter, 19+3 root batteries, collocation spectral accuracy, eigen demo, golden hash 0xaee480021eea9097 IDENTICAL cross-ISA. clippy -D warnings, fmt, CONTRACT.md complete (no-claims enumerate deferred scope). title: fs-ad forward mode: SIMD-packed Dual<N>, second-order duals, generic-scalar operator contract
description:
## What
`fs-ad` forward mode: `Dual<const N: usize>` with SIMD-lane packing (plan §6.6 regime 1) — for few-parameter sensitivities and for STRESS-TESTING HAND/GENERATED ADJOINTS (its most load-bearing job: the gradient-verification gate compares adjoints against forward-mode duals).

## Scope
- `Dual<N>`: value + N-wide derivative vector, const-generic so lanes pack into fs-simd registers; full arithmetic + elementary functions (via fs-math with derivative enclosures where certified paths need them); Qty-compatible (dimensioned duals — dJ/dθ carries units of J/θ).
- Second-order forward (Dual-over-Dual or dedicated Taylor-2) for Hessian-vector sanity checks against second-order adjoints (gradient-stack's TR-Newton needs verified Hv).
- Batch seeding utilities: seed matrices for Jacobian columns, sparsity-aware seeding (graph coloring hooks via FrankenNetworkx for compressed Jacobians).
- Integration contracts: every FLUX operator generic over scalar type S: Real so duals flow through primal code UNCHANGED (the design that makes "stress-test the adjoint" a one-liner); fs-opdsl emits dual-compatible code by construction.
- NOT here: reverse mode (FrankenTorch tape bridge + adjoint-by-construction live in fs-ad-adjoint-infra).

## Acceptance criteria
Derivative correctness vs symbolic fixtures + complex-step-quality FD on randomized compositions; genericity proven by running the PV-skeleton solver end-to-end on Dual<4> (compile + correct sensitivities); SIMD packing shows expected throughput scaling (N=4/8 near-linear on both ISAs).

## Testing
G0: chain/product/quotient rule laws under random composition; elementary-function derivative tables; NaN/edge propagation (derivative of |x| at 0 → documented convention). Logs: per-function derivative ULP stats, packing throughput tables.
close_reason: Implemented and verified on both ISAs. Dual<T: Real, const N> generic over its scalar — NESTED duals give exact higher-order derivatives from ONE implementation (second_directional: vT-H-v via Dual<Dual<f64,1>,1>, validated vs analytic f'' of sin(x^2)); Real trait = the generic-scalar contract, with f64's impl routing elementary fns through fs-math STRICT det (genericity preserves cross-ISA determinism BY DESIGN); helpers gradient/jvp/second_directional. KEY INVARIANT PROVEN: primal-bitwise fidelity — dual evaluation is bit-identical to the scalar path on 2000 random 3-deep composites (this test CAUGHT a real bug: mul_add desugaring to mul+add broke fused-primal fidelity — fixed with fused value channel + pairwise-fused derivative; without it every gradient check against fused kernels would drift). Also: 500-point grad-vs-central-FD battery (rel<5e-9), GENERIC Newton cbrt differentiated through convergence (d c^(1/3)/dc to 1e-10 — the differentiate-through-solution genericity demo), packed-vs-single-lane bitwise equivalence, documented kink/singularity conventions (abs'(0)=0 subgradient; sqrt'(0)=+inf honest), primal-only comparison semantics. Verified: 8 tests green aarch64 + trj x86 EXIT:0; clippy clean; CONTRACT.md complete. HONEST SUBSTITUTIONS (recorded in contract + claim comment): PV-skeleton-on-Dual acceptance -> generic-solver demo (vskeleton Real-refactor recorded for opdsl era); Qty<S:Real> generalization recorded follow-up; sparsity seeding + explicit SIMD lanes consumer-driven.

Ops + det-backed exp/ln/sin/cos/tanh/sqrt/powi/abs/mul_add; seeding helpers (gradient/jvp/hessian-vector); FD-vs-dual verification battery on random compositions; genericity demo: generic-over-Real CG/Newton mini-solvers differentiated through convergence. title: fs-ad adjoint infra: IFT differentiate-through-solution, revolve checkpointing w/ ledger spill, FrankenTorch bridge, CI gradient gate
description:
## What
`fs-ad` adjoint infrastructure (plan §6.6 regimes 2–3): the FrankenTorch tape bridge and the ADJOINT-BY-CONSTRUCTION machinery — differentiate through THE SOLUTION via the implicit function theorem, never through solver iterations.

## Scope
- IFT contract: for F(u,θ)=0 solved to tolerance, dJ/dθ = −λᵀ∂F/∂θ where λ solves the TRANSPOSED system ∂F/∂uᵀλ = ∂J/∂uᵀ — using the SAME preconditioner infrastructure as the primal (transpose-apply supplied by operators; solver-stack provides transposed solves). Differentiating through Krylov iterations is both wasteful and WRONG (iteration count isn't differentiable); this contract forbids it structurally.
- Tolerance/error interaction: adjoint accuracy budgeted explicitly (inexact-adjoint error terms feed the Error Ledger — gradient error is part of the evidence, not a surprise).
- Time-dependent adjoints: binomial (revolve-style) checkpointing schedules — optimal recompute-vs-store tradeoffs — INTEGRATED WITH THE LEDGER so checkpoints spill to storage gracefully (long-horizon LES/time-history adjoints don't OOM; they degrade to storage per the governor's policy).
- FrankenTorch tape bridge: reverse-mode for learned components (surrogates, neural charts); custom differentiable ops contributed upstream per §12 contract — "adjoint linear solve" node, "IFT node", "conformal calibration layer" — so solver adjoints compose with NN autograd in one graph.
- Verification harness (THE MERGE GATE, §8.7): every registered adjoint checked against Dual<N> forward mode and complex-step-quality FD on randomized problems in CI; a solver without a passing gradient check CANNOT MERGE.

## Acceptance criteria
IFT gradients match forward-mode to tolerance on PV-skeleton + fixture PDEs; revolve schedules verified optimal (checkpoint-count tables vs theory) and Ledger-spill exercised; tape bridge round-trips a surrogate training step with our custom nodes; gate wired into CI (seeded-broken adjoint demonstrably blocks).

## Testing
G0 adjoint-consistency laws (⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ for every linearized operator); checkpoint-schedule property tests (recomputation counts); end-to-end gradient checks with tolerance model documented. Logs: gradient-check tables (adjoint vs dual vs FD, per-direction), checkpoint spill events, transposed-solve residuals.
close_reason: fs-ad adjoint infra v1 landed (fs-ad gains fs-la dep, L1->L1 legal): (1) IFT module - ift_gradient for F(u,p)=0 with dense column-wise dual Jacobians, adjoint solve via fs_la Lu::solve_transpose, IftReport carrying primal residual (the honesty number: gradient formula exact only at F=0) + adjoint residual, singular dF/du -> typed FactorError. Tested vs full-pipeline re-solve central FD (1e-7) AND vs differentiate-through-converged-Newton (1e-10 agreement - the plan's regime-1-vs-regime-2 cross-check). (2) REVOLVE module - binary-treeverse checkpointed reverse sweeps; RevolveStats instruments both resource bounds (peak snapshots <= ceil(log2 L)+1, fwd re-evals <= L*log2 L, both ASSERTED in tests); HEADLINE RESULT: checkpointed adjoint BITWISE EQUAL to full-storage adjoint on a 100-step nonlinear chain (deterministic recomputation reproduces identical states - checkpointing is a memory policy invisible to the numbers); also verified vs forward duals on both dtheta and dx0 channels; insufficient budget refused with structured panic; degenerate 0/1-step chains covered. (3) GRADCHECK module - the CI gradient-gate primitive (plan 8.7), dual vs central-FD scale-aware, JSON-line report; proven to catch the value()/from_f64 derivative-killing bug class (O(1) error detected on seeded specimen). 18/18 tests green on BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/adj_verify.Wls7vD/run.log), clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 19 crates 0 violations, CONTRACT.md sections in force. FrankenTorch bridge + ledger spill + binomial revolve + matrix-free IFT split to frankensim-o3ui with full design context. NOTE for huq.4 owner (CI): fs_ad::gradcheck is ready to wire into merge gates.

(2) REVOLVE checkpointing: recursive Griewank-Walther driver (binomial split), guarantees: peak snapshots <= budget (asserted via instrumented counter), forward re-evaluations <= theoretical bound (asserted), and - the determinism payoff - checkpointed adjoint gradient BITWISE EQUAL to full-storage adjoint (recomputation reproduces identical states because every step is deterministic; this is a headline test). Also vs forward duals + FD on a 100-step nonlinear recurrence. (3) GRADCHECK: the CI gradient-gate primitive gradcheck(f, x, tol) -> GradCheckReport (dual vs central-FD, scale-aware rel err, pass verdict, JSON line) - huq.4's CI wires this into merge gates. Golden hash over IFT+revolve gradients, trj cross-ISA. SPLIT (recorded): FrankenTorch tape bridge (needs ft-* constellation wiring decision - no consumer yet), ledger SPILL for revolve snapshots (needs fs-ledger API coordination - snapshots stay in-memory v1), CI gate WIRING (huq.4 scope, other agent - gradcheck primitive will be ready for them).

title: Bedrock conformance suite: per-crate contracts, FrankenScipy oracle battery, cross-crate integration, G5 sweep
description:
## What
The BEDROCK conformance + G0 suite: the layer-wide executable contract proving L1's crates against each other, against FrankenScipy, and against the Gauntlet's law framework — with the detailed logging that makes failures self-diagnosing.

## Scope
- Per-crate conformance suites registered in the contract harness (contract-conformance-infra): fs-la (GEMM/batched/factorizations/eigen/randomized/mixed), fs-sparse (formats/SpMV/preconditioners), fs-fft, fs-ivl (core/Taylor/predicates), fs-cheb, fs-ad (forward/adjoint-infra), fs-rand (philox/qmc), fs-math.
- FrankenScipy oracle battery (§12 contract): special functions, reference optimizers' test problems, sparse baselines, quadrature/ODE references — MATCH in Gauntlet, beat on roofline; disagreements auto-filed as bug reports with full reproducers (for whichever side is wrong).
- Cross-crate integration cases: FFT-based DCT feeding fs-cheb; EFT primitives shared by mixed-precision and fs-ivl agree bitwise; Dual<N> flowing through fs-la kernels; interval evaluation of fs-math functions.
- Determinism sweep: the whole layer under G5 (hash equality across thread counts, both ISAs).
- Logging standard enforced: every conformance case emits structured JSON (case id, inputs digest, tolerances, computed-vs-reference, margins, timing) — the layer's failures must be diagnosable FROM LOGS ALONE.

## Acceptance criteria
All suites green on both ISAs in CI; oracle battery green; seeded violations per crate (one each) demonstrably caught with actionable logs; suite runtime budgeted (PR-gate subset vs nightly-full split documented).

## Testing
This IS the testing bead — its own meta-tests verify harness discovery, JSON schema validity of logs, and reproducer replay from logged seeds.

title: fs-rand extensions: gamma/beta/Dirichlet/alias/vMF distributions, ziggurat perf lane, SIMD bulk, nightly battery
description:
## What
fs-rand extended distributions + performance lanes: the remainder of the
fs-rand-philox bead's scope, split so DETERMINISM-contract and downstream
statistical consumers are not serialized behind distributions nobody needs
yet. Same doctrine: strict-mode determinism first, replayable consumption
contracts, budget-gated statistical tests.

## Scope
- Distributions (consumer-driven): gamma (Marsaglia-Tsang w/ deterministic
  rejection contract), beta, Dirichlet, categorical/alias method (Vose,
  deterministic table construction), von Mises-Fisher (rendering/GA
  direction sampling), truncated normal/exponential variants.
- Ziggurat normal as the PERF path (table generation deterministic,
  documented layer boundaries; Box-Muller stays the strict default until
  ziggurat passes the same cross-ISA determinism gates).
- SIMD bulk generation: 4x/8x counter lanes via fs-simd ops for fill-heavy
  consumers (LBM initialization, MC batches); tier-equivalence tests vs the
  scalar stream (bitwise).
- Full statistical battery for nightly CI: PractRand-class or documented
  dev-only oracle (isolated per the dependency policy), plus the
  inter-stream correlation matrix sweep at scale.

## Acceptance criteria
Each distribution: moment/KS gates + deterministic-consumption replay test +
cross-ISA spot verification; ziggurat: bitwise-equal across ISAs or
explicitly gated out of strict mode; SIMD lanes: bitwise-equal to scalar
stream; nightly battery wired into CI with failure -> issue discipline.

## Testing
Same harness patterns as fs-rand core (KAT-style fixtures where published
values exist, seeded moment gates, shuffle-invariance extended to new
consumers). Logs: JSONL verdicts per case.
close_reason: fs-rand extended distributions landed (new dist module; philox/qmc untouched, existing evidence intact): gamma via Marsaglia-Tsang squeeze + Ahrens-Dieter boost for alpha<1 (using wf9.14's strict pow - the dependency chain paying off), beta, Dirichlet, Vose alias tables (DETERMINISTIC index-order construction, P2 on setup, bitwise-tested), von Mises-Fisher on S^2 (Ulrich INVERSION - fixed 2-draw consumption, no rejection; deterministic Rodrigues rotation with explicit antipodal branch), truncated normal (Robert rejection) + truncated exponential (pure inversion, 1 draw). THE DOCTRINE EXTENDED: deterministic consumption contracts on every sampler - rejections advance the index like draws, consumed-count is a pure function of stream content (replay-tested mid-stream); fixed-consumption samplers documented AND tested as such. Battery 6/6 BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/dist_verify.iSn7LZ/run.log): moment gates in CLT bands (4 gamma shapes, beta, Dirichlet), alias chi-square + 1-draw contract, vMF unit-norm + 2-draw + mean-resultant matches analytic coth(k)-1/k at 3 kappas, truncated bounds + analytic hazard-ratio mean via erfc, golden hash 0x42246e2856de673c IDENTICAL cross-ISA (sampled VALUES bit-deterministic, not just bit streams). clippy -D warnings, fmt, CONTRACT extended. Ziggurat strict-gate + SIMD bulk lanes + nightly PractRand-class battery split to frankensim-1za9.

title: fs-rand QMC: Sobol Joe-Kuo + Owen scrambling, rank-1 lattice CBC rules
description:
## What
`fs-rand` QMC: quasi-random sequences (plan §6.7) — Sobol' with Joe–Kuo direction numbers + Owen scrambling, and rank-1 lattice rules via component-by-component construction.

## Scope
- Sobol' sequences: Joe–Kuo direction numbers (up to high dimension, table embedded), Gray-code incremental generation, skip-ahead; OWEN SCRAMBLING (nested uniform scrambling driven by Philox keys — randomized QMC with provable variance benefits and deterministic replay via seed).
- Rank-1 lattice rules: CBC construction for prescribed dimensions/weights, embedded/extensible variants, periodization maps for non-periodic integrands.
- Digital-net utilities: t,m,s-quality diagnostics, projections quality checks (2D projection scatter tests that catch bad direction numbers).
- Consumers documented in API: UQ workloads (§8.8) gain 1–2 orders of magnitude over MC at typical dimensionalities; low-discrepancy points also drive Bayesian-optimization multistart (§9.4) and light-transport sampling (§10.1).

## Acceptance criteria
Known-answer tests vs reference Sobol' implementations on standard test integrands (Genz families) demonstrating expected convergence-rate improvement vs MC (slope check, ledgered); Owen scrambling: unbiasedness property tests + variance-reduction demonstration; lattice CBC reproduces published worst-case errors on fixture cases.

## Testing
Equidistribution property tests (star-discrepancy proxies on projections); scrambling determinism (same seed → same points, both ISAs); dimension-stress tests (d up to several thousand for KL-expansion use). Logs: convergence-slope tables MC vs QMC vs RQMC per integrand.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with identical cross-ISA results. Sobol base-2: embedded Joe-Kuo D6 head (dims 1..=10; table preconditions ASSERTED at load — m_k odd, m_k < 2^k — so corruption fails loudly), Gray-code sequential + RANDOM ACCESS point(n); the table's validity is proven MECHANICALLY by exact (0,m,1)-stratification tests (every dim, m=1..8: exactly one point per dyadic bin) + 2D elementary-interval battery (every a+b=m split, exactly one point per box). TRUE Owen nested-uniform scrambling via the Philox lazy random tree (flip bit per binary-tree node keyed by (seed,dim,level,prefix) — zero storage, seed-replayable, RANDOM-ACCESS): tested net-preserving, bitwise-replayable, seed-sensitive. Rank-1 lattices: CBC construction in the gamma=1 Korobov space (B2 kernel) with korobov_error_sq diagnostic — decay 4.46e-4@n=257 -> 5.26e-5@n=1031 (near n^-2 rate), beats naive vectors; baker periodization. THE PAYOFF GATE: scrambled-Sobol RMSE 3.17e-6 vs plain-MC 1.53e-3 at n=4096/dim=5 on Genz product-peak — ~480x, far past the plan's 1-2-orders claim, and the SAME DIGITS on trj x86 (EXIT:0; QMC stack bit-reproduces cross-ISA). 17 tests, clippy clean. NO-CLAIMS: dims>10 (Joe-Kuo full-table import recorded), Owen perf path (hash-based approximation recorded; correct lazy-tree first), higher-order digital nets.

Deferred: 21201-dim table import, higher-order nets, Owen perf path.

title: fs-la GEMM: BLIS-style autotuned microkernel + packing, f64/f32/mixed, >=75% peak target
description:
## What
`fs-la` GEMM: BLIS-style dense matrix multiply — the compute-bound flagship kernel (plan §6.1, targets §14.1).

## Scope
- Architecture: arch-specific register microkernel (shapes chosen by autotuner, typically 8–14 rows × 2–4 vector columns) wrapped in autotuned KC/MC/NC cache blocking, with packing into fs-alloc arena buffers. Microkernels are fs-simd unsafe capsules with scalar twins.
- Precisions: f64, f32, and f32-storage/f64-accumulate mixed mode (the storage-vs-accuracy compromise used throughout).
- Parallelization: tile-level via fs-exec (loop partitioning respecting CCD L3 islands; packing buffers CCD-local); deterministic mode uses fixed loop orders + deterministic reduction for beta-accumulation edge cases.
- Autotuner integration: microkernel shape + blocking sweep registered as a calibration kernel; TilePlans read tuned params.
- Roofline targets (stated to be FAILABLE, re-measured per machine): ≥75% of measured f64 peak on both ISA families (M-class NEON ~0.5 TF order; TR ~6–7 TF all-core). SME2 upside handled by the separate exploratory bead.
- Honest positioning: generic huge-GEMM is NOT FrankenSim's hot loop — batched small dense is (see fs-la-batched) — but GEMM anchors packing/blocking infrastructure, POD/surrogate work, and credibility (risk §17: "pure-Rust kernels miss BLAS-class performance" → mitigated by microkernel discipline + autotuner + CI).

## Acceptance criteria
Correctness vs naive reference to tight ULP bounds across shape sweep (including degenerate: k=0, m=1, tall-skinny, wide); roofline: ≥75% measured peak at large square sizes on both ISAs, ledgered; deterministic-mode bit-stability across thread counts (G5).

## Testing
G0 property tests (associativity-with-tolerance, transpose identities, submatrix consistency); FrankenScipy cross-check battery; perf regression lane (±tolerance band → CI failure). Logs: shape sweeps with GFLOP/s, attainment %, chosen microkernel/blocking parameters.

title: fs-la batched small dense: SIMD-across-batch 6x6-48x48 kernels (FEM hot loop), >=60% peak
description:
## What
`fs-la` batched small dense: the ACTUAL hot loop of FEM-adjacent work (plan §6.1) — element matrices are 6×6…48×48, and they dominate assembly-adjacent compute.

## Scope
- Layout doctrine: interleave ACROSS the batch (SoA-of-matrices via #[derive(Soa)] conventions) so SIMD lanes run across elements, not within one tiny matrix — the design that yields routinely 5–15× over loop-over-LAPACK patterns.
- Operations: batched GEMM (small fixed shapes, monomorphized per size class), batched Cholesky/LU with pivoting strategies appropriate at small scale, batched triangular solves, batched eigen-3×3/6×6 closed-form/Jacobi paths (stress principal directions, metric tensors), batched determinant/inverse for 2×2/3×3/4×4 (Jacobians).
- Size-class dispatch: const-generic kernels for the common family (4,6,8,12,16,24,32,48) + a general fallback; autotuner picks interleave width per machine.
- Cancellation: batches chunked to tile quanta with Cx poll points (bounded latency even inside assembly storms).
- Consumers: FEEC assembly/apply (fs-feec-highorder sum-factorization tails), fiber-beam section state updates (seismic flagship's hot loop per §15.2), IPC contact Hessians, LBM moment transforms where shaped as small dense.

## Acceptance criteria
≥60% of peak FLOPs on both ISAs for the core size classes (roofline table §14.1), ledgered; exact agreement (ULP-bounded) with scalar per-matrix reference across the full size×precision matrix; deterministic across thread counts.

## Testing
G0 properties per op (LL^T reconstruction, PA=LU residuals, inverse·A=I to tolerance) across random well/ill-conditioned batches; special-case batteries (singular members flagged with structured per-element error, batch continues); perf lane vs targets. Logs: per-size-class GFLOP/s tables, interleave choices, condition-number histograms in failures.
close_reason: fs-la::batched LANDED (p0; unblocks tfz.5/FLUX chain). BatchMat/BatchVec: entry-plane SoA layout - one 128B-aligned plane per matrix entry across the batch (fs_soa::FieldBuf storage from wf9.5, stride quantum 16 f64), SIMD lanes run across ELEMENTS. Ops: batch_gemm (beta=0 overwrite house convention; size classes 4,6,8,12,16,24,32,48 monomorphized + generic fallback); batch_det/batch_inv closed-form k<=4 (Jacobian hot path, Singular flagged on exactly-zero det); batch_cholesky + batch_solve_lower/upper + batch_cholesky_solve (NotSpd{index=step} flagged per member, pivot->1.0 finite continuation, batch CONTINUES); batch_lu partial pivoting per matrix (factor::lu's lowest-row-among-max tie-break) + BatchLu::solve; batch_eigh3_values closed-form trigonometric (strict atan2/sqrt/cos kernels, ascending, trace-identity middle root - fixed a real root-ordering derivation bug pre-test: phi+4pi/3 is the MIDDLE root not the min) + batch_eigh general small-k via per-matrix jacobi_eigh. Error taxonomy reuses factor::FactorError as Vec<(member, err)>. KEY CONTRACT ADDITION: batch-membership bitwise INVARIANCE - matrix m in a batch of N is bitwise-equal to matrix m alone in a batch of 1 (structural: independent per-member arithmetic + fixed-order mul_add; TESTED for cholesky+LU at 3 members). EVIDENCE: 9-case battery green BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/fsbatched.9ivqnk): GEMM vs scalar oracle 6 size classes + beta path; LLt reconstruction+solve k in 4..48; poisoned-member isolation (8/9 healthy members bit-correct next to flagged ones); PA=LU residual <1e-11 + solve; det vs LU-diagonal-product-with-parity cross-check; inv*A=I; eigh3 vs Jacobi as sorted sets + eigenvector residuals + degenerate fixtures; 128B plane alignment; GOLDEN 0x0377_a8c9_5992_aee9 recorded M4 Pro, verified IDENTICAL Threadripper x86_64. Gates: clippy all+pedantic zero warnings, fmt, xtask 0 violations for fs-la (fs-session violation = other agent in-flight), CONTRACT.md updated (status line, batched Public types block, Determinism class golden + membership invariance, Cancellation = chunking in fs-exec drivers per fs-simd discipline, Conformance battery, No-claims). DEFERRED per xdgf precedent: the >=60%-peak acceptance is split to frankensim-9ekv (dep added) - correctness+determinism first, capsule microkernels/autotuned interleave/vectorized-LU/f32 variants there. First real consumer of fs-soa (L1->L0 dep added).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; immediately after landing wf9.5 fs-soa which unblocked this). p0 - gates tfz.5 and the FLUX critical path. DESIGN SKETCH (to refine on implementation): new module family in fs-la: batched/ submodule (fs-la already L1 BEDROCK with gemm/factor/eigen/mixed landed by 6ys.3/.5/.8). LAYOUT DOCTRINE: interleave ACROSS batch using fs-soa conventions - a Batch<const K: usize> owns SoA-interleaved storage where lane l of SIMD vector holds matrix m of the current group; group width W picked per fs_substrate::dispatch_tier (Scalar=1, Neon=2, Avx2=4, Avx512=8) at RUNTIME but with IDENTICAL arithmetic per element (determinism across tiers requires per-element ops be the same fs-math ops - scalar fallback must produce bit-identical results to SIMD lanes; NOTE fs-simd landed kernels already solved this pattern - REUSE its dispatch doctrine; if bitwise-across-tiers is impossible for fused ops, follow fs-simd's established policy - check its CONTRACT first). OPS (priority order per consumers): (1) batched det/inverse 2x2/3x3/4x4 closed-form (Jacobians - hottest, simplest); (2) batched GEMM for size classes 4,6,8,12,16,24,32,48 via const-generic monomorphized kernels + general fallback; (3) batched Cholesky LLT + triangular solves (no pivoting; SPD contract with structured per-element failure flags, batch CONTINUES - singular members flagged not fatal); (4) batched LU w/ partial pivoting appropriate at small scale; (5) batched eigen: closed-form symmetric 2x2/3x3 (stress principal directions - deterministic branch-free forms from fs-math det kernels), Jacobi for 6x6. ACCEPTANCE >=60% peak FLOPs both ISAs for core classes - realistically needs the fs-simd capsule path or careful autovectorization; measure with fs-roofline conventions, ledger the tables; if 60% unreachable in safe scalar-only first cut, LAND CORRECTNESS FIRST and record the perf lane as follow-up split bead (precedent: fs-la GEMM perf split frankensim-xdgf). Cancellation: chunk batches to tile quanta, Cx poll points per fs-exec conventions (check fs-exec SolverState API). title: fs-la factorizations: blocked Cholesky/LU/QR, deterministic TSQR, one-sided Jacobi SVD
description:
## What
`fs-la` factorizations: blocked dense Cholesky/LU/QR, TSQR, and one-sided Jacobi SVD (plan §6.1).

## Scope
- Blocked right-looking Cholesky and partial-pivot LU with arena packing, tile-parallel panel/update via fs-exec; blocked Householder QR with compact-WY representation.
- TSQR (tree QR) for tall-skinny blocks — THE tool for Krylov basis orthogonalization and POD snapshot compression; tree shape FIXED by tile index for determinism (the reduction-tree doctrine applied to orthogonalization).
- One-sided Jacobi SVD for accuracy-critical small/medium problems (superior relative accuracy for small singular values — matters for rank decisions in certificates); blocked driver for medium sizes.
- Condition estimation (1-norm estimators), iterative refinement hooks (consumed by fs-la-mixed-precision), and structured error reporting: singularity/indefiniteness surfaces as typed diagnostics with the offending pivot/block, never a panic.
- Deterministic pivoting: tie-breaking by lowest index under equal magnitude (P2).

## Acceptance criteria
Residual bounds met across random + adversarial (Kahan, Hilbert-class) matrices: ‖A−LLᵀ‖/‖A‖, ‖PA−LU‖, ‖A−QR‖, SVD orthogonality + reconstruction to size-appropriate tolerances; TSQR: Q orthogonality to machine precision independent of tree shape × thread count (bitwise stable in deterministic mode); FrankenScipy cross-checks green.

## Testing
G0 properties (factor-reconstruct round-trips, permutation validity, orthogonality invariants); shape/precision sweep incl. degenerate (0-dim, 1-column); determinism battery. Logs: residual tables, pivot-growth statistics, tree shapes.
close_reason: fs-la factorizations v1 landed: blocked right-looking Cholesky (GEMM trailing updates, typed NotSpd{index}), partial-pivot LU (lowest-index tie-break P2 tested with crafted ties, typed Singular{index}, pivot-growth statistic, solve + solve_transpose), Householder QR (blocked panels, reflector-sequential trailing apply, apply_q/apply_qt/solve_ls hooks), deterministic TSQR (binary tree fixed by block count, sign-canonicalized R, short-tail absorption, agrees with direct QR across 3 tree shapes, Gram identity, bitwise rerun-stable), one-sided cyclic Jacobi SVD (reconstruct 1e-13, U/V orthogonality 1e-13, Hilbert-8 spectral cond in the known 1.5e10 band - the small-singular-value relative accuracy property), Hager 1-norm condition estimator (Hilbert-8 kappa1 in band, true ~3.4e10). Battery 9/9 on BOTH ISAs incl adversarial (Kahan growth ledgered, Hilbert), degenerate (n=0, 1x1, single column), LS-vs-normal-equations cross-check; golden hash 0x181f8f9582d687ed IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/la2_verify.5NHUPt/run.log). clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 18 crates 0 violations. CONTRACT.md extended (factorization invariants 5-6, error model, no-claims). Recorded follow-ups (bead comments + CONTRACT no-claims): fs-exec tile-parallel drivers + arena packing + fused-WY GEMM apply + implicit-Q TSQR (perf lane), FrankenScipy cross-check battery (needs constellation wiring decision), refinement DRIVERS = 6ys.8 scope (hooks landed here; 6ys.8 also gets fs-math eft/dd already relocated).

comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post fs-fft/fs-sparse/fs-ivl/fs-la-GEMM). v1 design (correctness + determinism core in safe Rust; fs-exec tile-parallel panel/update and arena packing join the perf lane pattern): (1) CHOLESKY: blocked right-looking, panel factor unblocked (j-loop, sqrt diag, structured SingularityError with offending index if pivot <= 0 - typed diagnostic NOT panic), trailing update via gemm_f64 (landed, bitwise-contracted, KC part of bit contract). (5) ONE-SIDED JACOBI SVD: cyclic-by-rows sweeps, rotation only when |a_pq| > tol*sqrt(a_pp*a_qq), convergence when max off-diag ratio < n*eps over a full sweep, superior small-singular-value relative accuracy (the certificate rank-decision property); deterministic sweep order. title: fs-la eigensolvers: LOBPCG + Lanczos (selective reorth), matrix-free, resumable, adjoint hooks
description:
## What
`fs-la` eigensolvers: LOBPCG and Lanczos with selective reorthogonalization — chosen precisely because they are MATRIX-FREE-NATIVE (plan §6.1): modal analysis, buckling, and stability eigenproblems all reduce to operator applications (P6).

## Scope
- LOBPCG: blocked, preconditioned (accepts any fs-sparse/solver-stack preconditioner as an operator), with basis-degeneracy detection + recovery (soft-locking, orthogonalization via TSQR), constraint deflation (known nullspaces — rigid-body modes in free-free elasticity), and generalized problems (K,M pencils; M-inner products).
- Lanczos with selective reorthogonalization: for extremal + interior (shift-invert via solver-stack solves) spectra; Ritz convergence monitoring with residual-based stopping tied to caller's budget (P4).
- Both: resumable SolverState (pause/checkpoint/fork per fs-exec-speculation), cancellation-clean, deterministic mode (fixed orthogonalization order), adjoint hooks (eigenvalue derivatives via Ritz vectors — needed for eigenfrequency objectives in topo-simp).
- Consumers wired by design: buckling (fs-solid-stability geometric-stiffness pencils), manifold harmonics (Laplace–Beltrami basis), KL expansions (fs-uq), Orr–Sommerfeld validation cross-checks (fs-cheb).

## Acceptance criteria
Standard batteries (diagonal, Laplacian stencils, generalized pencils with known spectra) converge to requested tolerances; selective reorthogonalization keeps loss-of-orthogonality bounded (monitored, asserted); matrix-free contract honored (operator-apply count is THE cost metric, logged); FrankenScipy cross-check on shared problems.

## Testing
G0: eigenpair residuals ‖Av−λMv‖, orthogonality, monotonic Ritz convergence; degenerate/clustered-spectrum stress tests; resumability equality test (pause/resume = uninterrupted). Logs: convergence histories, reorthogonalization events, apply counts.
close_reason: fs-la eigensolvers landed: jacobi_eigh (dense cyclic Jacobi, the Rayleigh-Ritz kernel, exposed pub), matrix-free Lanczos (full reorth v1 - conservative special case of selective; omega-recurrence recorded refinement) and LOBPCG (X-X_prev conjugate direction, pluggable preconditioner), both RESUMABLE with checkpoint=clone and BITWISE split-run==straight-run equality (tested 20+20 vs 40 and 15+15 vs 30). Every EigenPair carries TRUE recomputed operator residual. Battery 6/6 BOTH ISAs: analytic 1D Laplacian spectrum (both ends, full Krylov - both ends of that spectrum are clustered, documented), analytic 2D 5-point Laplacian via matrix-free stencil closure incl degenerate eigenvalue pair (NO fs-sparse coupling - formats compose at call sites per design), dense-Jacobi agreement on random SPD (Lanczos 1e-9, LOBPCG 1e-6 on a clustered small end - honest tolerance documented), orthogonality/reconstruction, resumability bitwise. HEADLINE INCIDENT (evidence the discipline works): first REAL cross-ISA golden-hash divergence of the project - start vectors used platform libm sin, x86 hashed 0x5519... vs ARM 0x325b...; caught by trj verification, fixed with fs_math::det::sin, hash re-recorded 0x87da0cb32344b097 and now IDENTICAL cross-ISA (trj /data/tmp/eigen2_verify.ItYpTq/run.log). Audit then found latent std-powf in the mixed-battery fixture (matched by luck) - hardened to strict exp/ln, justified bump to 0x8e092d4aff1b5028, verified cross-ISA. CONTRACT now states the rule: NO platform libm in any path feeding solver state. clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 19 crates 0 violations. Recorded follow-ups: selective reorth omega recurrence, LOBPCG deflation/soft-locking + real preconditioners (solver-stack bead), eigenvector adjoints (frankensim-o3ui family), shift-invert Lanczos for interior eigenvalues.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 6ys.17). v1 design, all matrix-free (operator = closure apply: Fn(&[f64], &mut [f64]), so fs-sparse CSR spmv plugs in directly but dense/stencil operators work too): (1) LANCZOS (symmetric): k-step with SELECTIVE reorthogonalization (full reorth against converged Ritz vectors + last two basis vectors when the recurrence orthogonality estimate omega exceeds sqrt(eps) - track omega via the classic recurrence, cheaper than full reorth, principled), tridiagonal Ritz problem solved by bisection+inverse-iteration or... v1: solve the k x k tridiagonal eigenproblem via Jacobi on the dense tridiag (svd_jacobi machinery pattern; k is small). Extremal eigenpairs (smallest/largest via sign flip). Deterministic start vector (fixed seed pattern, normalized), fixed iteration order. (2) LOBPCG blocked (block size b): X orthonormalized via tsqr_r-derived... v1 uses qr from factor.rs on the n x b block; W = precond(residuals) with precond = identity closure default; Rayleigh-Ritz on [X W P] (3b x 3b dense symmetric eigen via cyclic Jacobi - implement jacobi_eigh_dense helper, also EXPOSED pub since fs-la lacks a dense symmetric eigensolver and consumers will want it), deflation of converged columns, P = X - X_prev conjugate direction. (3) RESUMABLE: LanczosState/LobpcgState structs (basis, tridiag coeffs / X P blocks, iteration count) with resume(state) - the state is plain data (Clone), checkpoint = clone; test: run 10 iters, clone state, run 10 more vs 20 straight -> BITWISE equal results. (4) Adjoint hooks: expose converged (eigenvalue, eigenvector, residual_norm) triples; eigen-derivative dlambda/dp = v^T (dA/dp) v is CALLER-side via fs-ad duals on the operator - document the pattern in module docs; full eigenvector adjoints (Nelson/adjoint-mode) recorded to frankensim-o3ui-style follow-up. Tests: 1D Laplacian tridiag [-1,2,-1] analytic eigenvalues 2-2cos(k pi/(n+1)) (both ends of spectrum, tolerance vs analytic), 2D Laplacian via fs-sparse CSR spmv closure (5-point, analytic sums), agreement LOBPCG vs Lanczos vs dense Jacobi reference on random SPD (B B^T + n I), Ritz-vector orthogonality to 1e-12, residual norms ||A v - lambda v|| <= tol certified in returned triples, resumability bitwise, deterministic golden hash + trj cross-ISA. NOTE: fs-la has NO dep on fs-sparse and should not gain one (would invert nothing but adds coupling) - the CSR test lives in an integration test using a hand-rolled 5-point closure instead, keeping fs-la dep-free of fs-sparse; fs-sparse users compose at call sites.

title: fs-la randomized NLA: rangefinder/RSVD/Nystrom, sketch-precondition LS, Hutchinson/Hutch++ [F]
description:
## What
`fs-la` randomized NLA (plan §6.1 [F]): randomized range finders, randomized SVD/Nyström, sketch-and-precondition least squares, and Hutchinson/Hutch++ stochastic trace estimation.

## Scope
- Randomized range finder with adaptive rank determination (posterior error estimates, block power iterations for spectral-decay-poor cases); randomized SVD on top; Nyström for PSD kernels (GP approximations in bayesopt).
- Sketch-and-precondition least squares: sketch (subsampled randomized trig transform via fs-fft or sparse sign sketches), QR the sketch, precondition LSQR-style iterations — the modern fast path for tall dense LS.
- Hutchinson + Hutch++ trace estimators: log-det and tr(f(A)) machinery; the named consumer is SENSITIVITY AGGREGATION IN TOPOLOGY OPTIMIZATION and log-det terms in GP likelihoods. Variance-reduced (Hutch++) split with deterministic Philox probe streams — stochastic estimates are still replayable (P2) and e-process-monitorable (stopping when confidence sequence is tight enough, wired later via fs-eproc).
- All randomness through fs-rand keyed streams; all results carry probe counts + variance estimates as evidence metadata (Evidence<T> integration point).

## Acceptance criteria
Rank-r approximation errors within theoretical bounds (oversampling p → expected ‖A−QQᵀA‖ bounds) on synthetic spectra (fast/slow decay, gaps); Hutch++ variance beats Hutchinson at matched probe counts (demonstrated, ledgered); sketch-LS matches direct QR solutions to tolerance with the expected speed crossover documented.

## Testing
G0 property: containment of error estimates (estimated ≥ actual with declared probability, empirically validated); determinism across thread counts; FrankenScipy/dense-SVD cross-checks. Logs: spectra, probe counts, error-vs-bound tables.
close_reason: Randomized NLA landed (fs-la/src/rand_nla.rs; fs-la gains fs-rand dep): range_finder (Gaussian test matrices from KEYED Philox streams w/ stable kernel-id registry, block power iterations w/ re-orthonormalization for slow decay, posterior error estimator w/ declared-band scaling), rsvd (Q^T A -> landed svd_jacobi on B^T -> U=QZ), nystrom_psd (core eigh pseudo-inverse sqrt via landed jacobi_eigh, null-direction drop), sketch_ls (sparse-sign sketch 8 nnz/col from keyed stream, QR-of-sketch preconditioner, preconditioned normal-equation CG), hutchinson + hutch_pp (Rademacher probes, rangefinder deflation, TraceReport evidence). THE DESIGN CENTER HELD: stochastic estimates are pure functions of (seed, kernel, tile) - the golden hash covers RSVD singular values + trace estimates and matched cross-ISA (0x3e928bac8cf9fd48, trj /data/tmp/rn_verify.cShr8n/run.log). Battery 6/6 BOTH ISAs: rank-r residuals within budgeted tails on fast-decay/slow-decay/spectral-gap fixtures + estimate coverage (the G0 containment property for error estimates), RSVD leading sigmas 1e-6, Nystrom rank-8 PSD 1e-6, sketch-LS == direct QR to 1e-8 in <=60 CG iters, Hutch++ MSE < 0.5x Hutchinson at 24 probes x 20 trials (MEASURED, ledgered in test output), keyed determinism bitwise. clippy -D warnings, fmt, CONTRACT sections + no-claims added. Recorded follow-ups: SRTT sketches via fs-fft, LSQR proper, adaptive-rank streaming variant, e-process stopping (fs-eproc wiring), GP/bayesopt consumers.

title: fs-la mixed precision: f32-factor/f64-refine policy engine, EFT/double-double ladder
description:
## What
`fs-la` mixed precision with iterative refinement AS A DEFAULT POLICY (plan §6.1): factor/precondition in f32, refine to f64, escalate to double-double only where the Error Ledger demands it.

## Scope
- Policy engine: given a target accuracy (from the caller's budget, P4) and a condition estimate, choose the precision ladder (f32 factor + f64 refine | f64 direct | f64 factor + dd refine); record the decision + achieved residual as evidence.
- Iterative refinement drivers for LU/Cholesky/QR-based solves with convergence monitoring (residual reduction per step; stall detection → escalate or report structured failure with diagnosis: "condition ~1e12 exceeds f32-factor regime").
- Error-free transformations (TwoSum/TwoProd/Dekker splitting) as the in-house building blocks; double-double arithmetic type (add/mul/div/sqrt with documented relative-error bounds) — shared with fs-ivl's high-precision oracle needs (single implementation, two consumers).
- Integration: solver-stack inherits the policy for Krylov inner/outer precision splits; fs-la-batched gets f32-storage/f64-accumulate variants; Error Ledger (fs-plan-models) receives per-solve precision provenance.

## Why
The bandwidth-starved reference machine (TR: ~3.4 GB/s/core) makes f32 storage a near-2× bandwidth lever; refinement buys back f64 accuracy for well-enough-conditioned problems — but ONLY as a policy with evidence, never a silent downgrade.

## Acceptance criteria
Refinement recovers f64-accurate solutions (componentwise residual targets) on condition-controlled batteries up to the documented threshold; escalation triggers exactly where theory predicts (threshold sweep test); dd arithmetic passes known-answer + property tests (error bounds).

## Testing
G0: EFT exactness properties (TwoSum invariants are EXACT — bitwise testable); refinement convergence-rate checks; policy-decision determinism. Logs: per-solve ladders chosen, residual trajectories, escalation events with reasons.

title: fs-sparse: CSR/BSR/SELL-C-sigma, deterministic tiled assembly, SpMV/SpMM >=85% STREAM
description:
## What
`fs-sparse` core: sparse formats and SpMV/SpMM tuned for BOTH ISAs (plan §6.2).

## Scope
- Formats: CSR (generality), BSR (block CSR matched to FEM block structure — vector-valued unknowns make dense micro-blocks, and BSR turns them into batched-small-dense-adjacent work), and SELL-C-σ (sliced ELL with row sorting) for SIMD-regular SpMV on both NEON and AVX-512.
- Assembly path: COO → parallel sort → dedupe/accumulate → target format, ALL TILED with deterministic accumulation order (assembly is a reduction; P2 applies — same mesh, same matrix, bitwise, regardless of thread count).
- SpMV/SpMM: format-specialized kernels (fs-tilelang where the shape fits), CCD-sharded row ranges with first-touch, software prefetch with autotuned distance; SpMM (sparse × dense block) for block-Krylov/POD workloads.
- Roofline honesty: SpMV is bandwidth-bound; target ≥85% of measured STREAM on both machines (per-CCD placement on TR is what makes this achievable — §14.2); attainment ledgered.
- Pattern utilities: transpose, symmetrization, Gustavson-style SpGEMM (needed by AMG's Galerkin triple product later), graph views for FrankenNetworkx interop (partitioning experiments).

## Acceptance criteria
Format round-trips lossless; SpMV exact vs dense reference on random + FEM-patterned fixtures; ≥85% STREAM attainment at large sizes, both ISAs, ledgered; deterministic assembly verified across thread counts (G5).

## Testing
G0 properties: (A^T)^T=A, SpMV linearity, format-conversion equivalence; adversarial patterns (empty rows, dense rows, single-column); perf lane vs targets. Logs: per-format bandwidth attainment, format-choice recommendations per pattern class.
close_reason: fs-sparse v1 correctness core landed: canonical CSR + BSR + SELL-C-sigma, deterministic COO assembly (stable (row,col,insertion_seq) canonicalization, stream-order-invariance tested bitwise), SpMV/SpMM, transpose/symmetrize/Gustavson SpGEMM. Headline: CSR==BSR==SELL SpMV BITWISE (designed via ascending-column mul_add accumulation; BSR fill-zero inertness proven in module docs; SELL stores true row lengths so pads are never read). 19 lib tests + conformance battery green on BOTH ISAs; cross-ISA golden hash 0xbcf552b6c5bfaed6 IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/sparse_verify.1raqiO/run.log). clippy -D warnings clean, fmt applied, xtask check-all 16 crates 0 violations. Complete CONTRACT.md (6 numbered invariants, no-claim boundaries). The >=85% STREAM roofline acceptance criterion + parallel tiled assembly + SIMD kernels split to frankensim-wsbf (dependent on fs-tilelang wf9.11 and autotuner wf9.12, which it needs; full context recorded there).

comments.author: jemanuel

(2) Deterministic assembly: COO -> stable sort by (row,col,insertion_seq) -> accumulate duplicates in insertion order = canonical order independent of input stream shuffling (G5 test: random shuffles of the triplet stream produce bitwise-identical matrices). (3) Kernels: SpMV all formats, SpMM (bitwise == per-column SpMV, tested), transpose via counting sort ((A^T)^T == A bitwise), symmetrize (result transpose-invariant bitwise), Gustavson SpGEMM with dense SPA accumulator vs dense-multiply oracle. (4) Adversarial fixtures: empty rows, dense row, single column, 5-point Laplacian FEM pattern (+2-dof BSR variant). SPLIT (recorded, consistent with fs-fft precedent): >=85% STREAM attainment + per-CCD sharding + prefetch autotune + fs-tilelang SIMD kernels + FrankenNetworkx graph interop -> follow-up bead, since the roofline work is gated on fs-tilelang (wf9.11) and autotuner (wf9.12) which are open in the substrate lane.

title: ADDENDUM L3 DIFFERENTIATION & REALITY: end-to-end adjoints, reality-as-a-chart, spacetime complex
description:
## Mission
ADDENDUM LAYER 3 — DIFFERENTIATION AND COUPLING TO REALITY. Transpose the ledger to get sensitivities, admit the physical world as one more representation, and extend the complex into time. Where Layer 1 makes the system fast and Layer 2 makes it honest, this layer makes it a *design engine* (gradients through every seam), a *metrology partner* (as-built vs as-designed as a sheaf δ), and — eventually — a *coupled-transient* engine (splitting error made visible in spacetime).

## The three proposals
- **Proposal 1 — End-to-end adjoints: transpose the sheaf** (Mean 770, Useful 950, Agent 950). The ledger composes error forward; the same graph, transposed, composes sensitivity backward — ∂(lift)/∂(spline control point) computed *through* the SDF conversion, *through* the mesh, *through* the solve. Adjoints exist per-solver today but die at every seam exactly the way error bounds do; a ledger-shaped system gets them almost by transposition because restriction maps are linear operators whose adjoints are free. A swarm with gradients is an optimizer; a swarm without them is a random-search farm. Unlocks F, 11, B, and 9's best proposer.
- **Proposal 11 — Reality is just another chart** (Mean 760, Accret 850). A CT scan of the manufactured part is one more representation with restriction maps, so as-built-vs-as-designed is a δ between two sections computed by the watertightness machinery; strain-gauge and wind-tunnel data assimilate through the adjoints. Commercial kicker: **adjoint-driven tolerance allocation** — spend tight manufacturing tolerances only where ∂performance/∂geometry is large, provably loosen everywhere else, deliver the savings as a certified GD&T statement a CFO understands.
- **Proposal 4 — Extend the complex into time** (Mean 590, ranks last). Coupled multiphysics glued in *spacetime*: time slabs as cells of the complex, so operator-splitting/coupling error lands in the ledger — measurable, localized, adaptively controllable. Ranks last not because wrong but because it is a refinement that pays only after strongly-coupled transient workloads are proven demand. Explicitly does NOT claim coupling *stability* (added-mass FSI instabilities are per-coupling analysis problems the sheaf does not solve).

## Cross-layer dependencies
1 needs base solver infra + 6 (FD falsifiers) + 2 (checkpoint storage) + Rep Router (differentiability routing). 11 needs 1 (hard) + 3 (colors) + 9 (robustness sampling) + metrology partnerships. 4 needs 3 (budget attribution).

## Members
adjoint-vjp (1), adjoint-mesh-mitigations (1), adjoint-certificates (1), reality-ingest (11), reality-assimilate (11), reality-tolerance (11), spacetime-complex (4).

## Success criteria
Adjoint-driven optimization beats the best derivative-free baseline (CMA-ES/BO at equal budget) on ≥70% of the wedge vertical's benchmark design tasks; as-built diffs colored *validated* and anchored to metrology calibration certs; tolerance-allocation report ships every loosened tolerance with the certified sensitivity that justifies it.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- NOTE: `add-layer:Ln` here is the addendum's THEMATIC layer (economic engine / epistemics / coupling / self-knowledge / GTM), NOT the base L0–L6 architecture stack. Member crates still sit at their true base layer and must respect lower-knows-nothing-of-higher dependency direction.

title: Proposal 1 — End-to-end adjoints: TRANSPOSE THE LEDGER (per-op VJP registration, transposed BDDC/Krylov solves, revolve checkpoints in the CAS)
description:
## What
Proposal 1's CORE — END-TO-END ADJOINTS by transposing the ledger DAG. The ledger composes error FORWARD; the same graph, TRANSPOSED, composes sensitivity BACKWARD: ∂(lift)/∂(spline control point) computed THROUGH the SDF conversion, THROUGH the mesh, THROUGH the solve. This turns FrankenSim from a simulator into a DESIGN ENGINE. A swarm with gradients is an optimizer; a swarm without them is a random-search farm (Useful 950, Agent 950).

## Why a ledger gets this almost for free
Adjoint methods exist per-solver today but DIE AT EVERY SEAM exactly the way error bounds do — which is why industrial design optimization still runs on finite differences and surrogate hacks. A fragmented toolchain cannot chain adjoints; a ledger-shaped system gets them almost by TRANSPOSITION, since restriction/conversion maps are LINEAR operators whose adjoints are free.

## Scope (differentiate the DAG, not the codes)
- Every ledger op REGISTERS a vector-Jacobian product (VJP). This is the amendment: every op spec gains a VJP requirement OR an explicit non-differentiable declaration with color consequences.
- Restriction/trace/conversion maps: transposes of already-linear operators (nearly free — reuse fs-geom Convert receipts + fs-xform Jacobian actions).
- Solver ops: the DISCRETE ADJOINT of the linearized system, reusing the existing Krylov + preconditioning infrastructure with TRANSPOSED operators — NEVER differentiate through Krylov iterations (BDDC preconditioners transpose naturally). Extends the base plan's fs-adjoint (IFT differentiate-through-solution).
- Time-dependent ops: revolve-style optimal checkpointing, with checkpoints stored in the CONTENT-ADDRESSED store so adjoint sweeps and the incremental cache (Proposal 2) SHARE storage discipline.

## Acceptance criteria
Every registered op has a VJP (or a declared non-differentiable status with color consequences); a seam-crossing gradient (spline CP → lift, through SDF+mesh+solve) matches an independent finite-difference check within tolerance; solver adjoints reuse transposed Krylov/BDDC and never differentiate through iterations; revolve checkpoints live in the CAS and reproduce bit-equal gradients with/without spill.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 1)
If, on the wedge vertical, adjoint-driven optimization does not beat the best derivative-free baseline (CMA-ES or BO at equal budget) on ≥70% of the benchmark design tasks, the seam-crossing-gradient premise is failing in practice — scope adjoints DOWN to the single-representation paths where they demonstrably pay and stop paying the VJP tax on exotic ops.

## Testing
G0: ⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ transpose-consistency suite (reuse fs-adjoint's); G3 adjoint-vs-FD along random directions across seams; end-to-end: a seam-crossing shape-gradient descent reduces the objective monotonically. Logs: VJP registry coverage, per-op transpose checks, seam-crossing gradient-check tables.

## Dependencies
Base plan fs-adjoint + solver infra (Krylov/BDDC) + Rep Router (differentiable routing — see adjoint-mesh) + fs-geom Convert + fs-xform Jacobians; Proposal 6 (FD falsifiers — see adjoint-certs); Proposal 2 (checkpoint storage). Unlocks Proposal F (hazard sensitivities), Proposal 11 (assimilation + tolerance allocation), Proposal B (attribution), Proposal 9's best proposer. AMENDS every op spec (VJP requirement) + the Rep Router fitness (differentiability term).

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Boundary vs base fs-adjoint (flux-tfz.24):** the base crate owns per-solver/per-op discrete adjoints, revolve, and Hadamard forms; Proposal 1 owns ONLY the ledger-DAG TRANSPOSITION that chains those per-op VJPs across representation seams (Convert/trace/restriction transposes). Revolve checkpoint storage is SHARED with Proposal 2's content-addressed store, not re-implemented.
- **Determinism (G5):** transposed reductions reuse the deterministic fixed-shape reduction trees (adjoint sweeps re-run the reduction structure in reverse); bit-equal gradients with/without spill.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Missing-VJP fail-loud case: an op with no registered VJP and no explicit non-differentiable declaration that lands inside a differentiation path raises a STRUCTURED error and BLOCKS the gradient — never a silent-zero/partial gradient (a silently-zero seam gradient is a Goodhart trap). FD-falsifier tolerance is conditioning-aware so an ill-conditioned seam where adjoint and FD legitimately diverge does not fire a false falsifier hit.

title: Proposal 1 — End-to-end adjoints: NON-DIFFERENTIABLE MESHING MITIGATIONS (differentiability-as-routing / Hadamard shape forms / estimated-color+discontinuity flag)
description:
## What
Proposal 1's MESHING PROBLEM, handled HONESTLY. Remeshing is genuinely NON-DIFFERENTIABLE across topology events. Rather than pretend otherwise, apply three mitigations in order of preference — and turn the base plan's multi-representation architecture from a complication into the ASSET that provides differentiable paths where mesh-locked pipelines have none.

## Scope — three mitigations, in order
1. **Make differentiability a ROUTING REQUIREMENT**: the Rep Router PREFERS SDF/spline paths (naturally smooth) when a query requests gradients. This is the key reframing: multi-representation becomes the thing that PROVIDES differentiable paths. This is the amendment adding a DIFFERENTIABILITY TERM to the Rep Router's fitness function.
2. **Shape-derivative (Hadamard boundary-form) formulations** that avoid mesh sensitivities entirely where applicable (reuse fs-adjoint's Hadamard boundary shape gradients on FEEC traces).
3. **Where a remesh event is UNAVOIDABLE inside the differentiation path**: emit the gradient with **estimated color** (Proposal 3) + a declared DISCONTINUITY FLAG, rather than pretending the gradient is exact across a topology change.

## Acceptance criteria
A gradient query routes to an SDF/spline path when one exists (differentiability-aware routing); Hadamard boundary forms compute shape gradients without mesh sensitivities on the applicable fixtures; an unavoidable remesh inside the diff path yields an estimated-color gradient with a discontinuity flag (never a silently-wrong verified gradient).

## Testing
Property: gradient-requesting queries never silently traverse a non-differentiable remesh without downgrading color + flagging; Hadamard-vs-perturbation-resolve agreement on shape fixtures (sphere volume/compliance classics); router prefers smooth paths under a gradient request. Falsifier (Proposal 6): FD spot-check across a smooth path agrees; across a flagged remesh, the discontinuity is real. Logs: chosen paths, color/flag on each gradient, Hadamard-vs-FD tables.

## Dependencies
adjoint-vjp (the VJP framework); base plan Rep Router (AMENDED: differentiability term in fitness) + fs-adjoint (Hadamard shape gradients); Proposal 3 (estimated color + discontinuity flag). Sibling to adjoint-certs.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: a gradient query with NO differentiable path available returns estimated + discontinuity-flag (never a faked verified gradient); router tie-break on equal-fitness paths is deterministic.

title: Proposal 1 — End-to-end adjoints: GRADIENT CERTIFICATES + FD falsifier spot checks (colors on gradients, no-merge-without-a-passing-check)
description:
## What
Proposal 1's GRADIENT CERTIFICATES — gradients are CLAIMS like any other and get COLORS like any other. A gradient without a certificate is folklore; this bead makes every gradient carry evidence.

## Scope
- An INTERVAL BOUND on the adjoint consistency residual (fs-ivl) — a verified-color numerical certificate on the gradient where the path is differentiable.
- MANDATORY finite-difference SPOT CHECKS in random directions via the falsifier registry (Proposal 6) — the independent cross-check that a transpose bug or a sign error would trip.
- Color assignment: a gradient through a fully-differentiable, interval-bounded path is verified; a gradient through a flagged remesh (adjoint-mesh) or a surrogate proposer is estimated; a gradient anchored to assimilated experimental data (Proposal 11) is validated.

## Why this is non-optional
The base plan already mandates a CI GRADIENT GATE (fs-adjoint: "a solver without a passing gradient check cannot merge"). This bead extends that discipline to the SEAM-CROSSING, ledger-transposed gradients of Proposal 1: the same no-merge-without-a-passing-check rule, now applied across representation boundaries, with colors attached.

## Acceptance criteria
Every emitted gradient carries a color + (where verified) an interval bound on the adjoint consistency residual; FD spot checks in random directions run automatically via the falsifier registry and agree within the documented per-conditioning tolerance; a seeded transpose/sign bug is CAUGHT by the FD falsifier and blocks merge.

## Testing
G0: adjoint consistency residual interval soundness; G3: adjoint-vs-FD along random directions (the falsifier). CI gate: a failing gradient check cannot merge (reuse the base plan's ci-gauntlet gradient gate). Property: color correctness on differentiable vs flagged-remesh vs surrogate gradients. Logs: per-gradient color, residual bounds, FD-agreement tables.

## Dependencies
adjoint-vjp + adjoint-mesh; fs-ivl (interval bounds); Proposal 6 (FD falsifier registry — HARD); Proposal 3 (colors); base plan fs-adjoint CI gradient gate. Feeds Proposal D (estimator-independence / perturbation checks reuse these).

title: Proposal 11 — Reality as a chart: INGESTION (scan/point-cloud charts + restriction maps + ledgered registration + design-time fiducials)
description:
## What
Proposal 11's INGESTION — REALITY IS JUST ANOTHER CHART. A CT scan of the manufactured part is one more representation with its own restriction maps, so as-built-vs-as-designed becomes a δ between two sections computed by the WATERTIGHTNESS machinery. This closes the loop through the physical world: the "validated" color stops being a static stamp and becomes a living, regime-tagged belief state.

## Scope — ingestion
- Scan data (CT voxel grids, laser point clouds) admitted as a REPRESENTATION TYPE with restriction maps to interface trace spaces (extends fs-rep-voxel + fs-geom's chart zoo; the representation registry is explicitly OPENED to measured-data chart types — the amendment).
- REGISTRATION (alignment of scan to design) is itself an OPTIMIZATION WITH ERROR, so it is LEDGERED — made well-posed by physical fiducials/datums specified AT DESIGN TIME. This pushes a DESIGN-FOR-VERIFICATION requirement UPSTREAM into the geometry layer (the amendment: fs-geom gains fiducial/datum primitives).

## Scope — as-built diff
- The δ between as-designed and as-scanned sections under a MEASUREMENT-NOISE-AWARE tolerance, colored **validated** and anchored to the metrology INSTRUMENT'S CALIBRATION CERTIFICATE. Reuses the sheaf δ / watertightness machinery unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
Scan data ingests as a chart with restriction maps; registration is a ledgered optimization with its error carried forward; fiducials/datums make registration well-posed on fixtures; the as-built diff is a validated-color δ anchored to the metrology calibration cert.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 11 — R8)
If REGISTRATION UNCERTAINTY on realistic scanned parts EXCEEDS the geometric deviations being certified (signal below noise floor), the as-built loop is premature for that part class — RETAIN assimilation of point-sensor data (strain gauges, thermocouples, which have no registration problem) and DEFER full-field scan integration. Early warning: registration-uncertainty-to-signal ratio per part class.

## Testing
Property: registration error is ledgered + propagated (never dropped); fiducial-based registration is well-posed on fixtures with known ground-truth pose; as-built δ colored validated + anchored. Falsifier: a known-good part scans to a within-tolerance δ; a known-defective part's δ localizes the defect. Logs: registration optimizations + errors, fiducial usage, as-built δ fields, calibration anchors.

## Dependencies
base plan fs-rep-voxel + fs-geom (AMENDED: measured-data chart types + fiducial/datum primitives) + the sheaf watertightness machinery; Proposal 3 (validated color + regime tags); metrology partnerships (the practical gate — Pract 500). Foundation for reality-assimilate + reality-tolerance. Owns risk R8.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: a scan with no fiducials (under-determined registration) is flagged ill-posed and falls to the point-sensor path rather than an over-confident delta.

title: Proposal 11 — Reality as a chart: ASSIMILATION (weak-constraint 4D-Var via adjoints; as-built diff colored validated; registration-free point sensors as fallback)
description:
## What
Proposal 11's ASSIMILATION — strain-gauge and wind-tunnel data update the model through the ADJOINTS. Sensor data updates the MODEL-FORM POSTERIOR that Proposal 3 tracks, per regime, so validation becomes a living belief state rather than a one-time stamp.

## Scope
- WEAK-CONSTRAINT 4D-Var using Proposal 1's adjoints, with OBSERVATION OPERATORS expressed as RESTRICTION MAPS (the same restriction-map algebra as the sheaf — sensor readout is a trace of the field onto the sensor's support).
- Point-sensor assimilation (strain gauges, thermocouples) is the REGISTRATION-FREE path and the Proposal-11 fallback (R8): it has no scan-alignment problem, so it works even where full-field scan integration is premature.
- Assimilation updates the per-regime model-form posterior (Proposal 3's validated/estimated maps); the update itself is ledgered + colored (validated where anchored to calibrated instruments).

## Acceptance criteria
4D-Var assimilation with restriction-map observation operators reduces the model-data misfit on a fixture with synthetic "measurements"; point-sensor assimilation works with no registration step; the assimilated update modifies the per-regime posterior (Proposal 3) and is colored validated + anchored; the adjoint used is the certified one (adjoint-certs).

## Testing
Property: assimilation is consistent (assimilating the model's own output is a near-no-op); observation operators satisfy the restriction-map (functoriality) laws; posterior update is regime-local. Falsifier (Proposal 6): held-out sensor points predicted by the assimilated model within stated bands. Logs: misfit reduction, observation-operator traces, posterior updates, held-out predictions.

## Dependencies
reality-ingest (measured-data charts) OR direct point-sensor input (registration-free); Proposal 1 (adjoints — HARD); Proposal 3 (model-form posterior + colors). Sibling to reality-tolerance.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

title: Proposal 11 — Reality as a chart: ADJOINT-DRIVEN TOLERANCE ALLOCATION (certified GD&T report — the CFO deliverable)
description:
## What
Proposal 11's COMMERCIAL KICKER — ADJOINT-DRIVEN TOLERANCE ALLOCATION. Spend tight manufacturing tolerances ONLY where ∂performance/∂geometry is large; provably LOOSEN everywhere else; deliver the savings as a CERTIFIED statement. GD&T (geometric dimensioning and tolerancing) today is assigned by convention and fear; this replaces fear with a certified sensitivity. A value proposition a CFO understands without a physics lecture.

## Scope
- Minimize manufacturing cost subject to P(performance ∈ spec) ≥ target, driven by ∂QoI/∂geometry SENSITIVITY FIELDS (Proposal 1) mapped through a FIRST-ORDER VARIANCE PROPAGATION.
- ROBUSTNESS-CHECK with Proposal 9's cheap re-solves at SAMPLED TOLERANCE-BAND EXTREMES (first-order variance propagation is only a linearization; the extremes check catches where it lies).
- Deliverable: a GD&T SUGGESTION REPORT where EVERY loosened tolerance carries the CERTIFIED SENSITIVITY that justifies it.

## Acceptance criteria
On a fixture with a known sensitivity structure, the allocator tightens tolerances where ∂QoI/∂geometry is large and loosens them where it is small, subject to the P(in-spec) constraint; the robustness check at band extremes confirms the linearized allocation (or flags where it fails); the GD&T report attaches a certified sensitivity to each loosened tolerance.

## Testing
Property: allocated tolerances are monotone in inverse-sensitivity (loosen where insensitive); the P(in-spec) constraint holds at the sampled extremes; report completeness (no loosened tolerance without a justifying sensitivity). Falsifier: Monte-Carlo over the allocated tolerance box confirms P(in-spec) ≥ target. Logs: sensitivity fields, allocated tolerances, extreme-sample checks, GD&T report.

## Dependencies
Proposal 1 (∂QoI/∂geometry sensitivity fields — HARD); Proposal 9 (cheap robustness re-solves); reality-ingest/assimilate (the as-built loop context). The metrology-facing deliverable of Proposal 11.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

title: Proposal 4 — Extend the complex into time: time slabs as cells, splitting-error in the ledger, adaptive coupling controller [gated on splitting-error demand]
description:
## What
Proposal 4 — EXTEND THE COMPLEX INTO TIME. Coupled multiphysics is glued in SPACETIME: operator splitting hands data across TEMPORAL interfaces exactly as patches hand traces across SPATIAL ones, so making time slabs CELLS OF THE COMPLEX puts splitting/coupling error in the ledger — measurable, localized to specific coupling steps, and adaptively controllable. Ranks last (Mean 590) not because wrong but because it is a REFINEMENT that pays only after strongly-coupled transient workloads are proven demand (sequencing, not merit). Phase 3 horizon, activation-gated.

## Honest scope (what this does NOT claim)
Today, splitting error is INVISIBLE in every production multiphysics code — absorbed by convention and safety factor. Visibility-plus-control is a real step. But this proposal does NOT claim coupling STABILITY: added-mass-type instabilities in fluid-structure interaction and stiff time-scale pathologies are analysis problems about the coupled PDEs, solved per-coupling, the hard way. The sheaf guarantees consistent data TRANSFER, not convergent ITERATION.

## Scope
- Time slabs as CELLS of the complex; the coupling residual on a temporal interface measured as a DEFECT against the monolithic residual over the slab.
- An ADAPTIVE CONTROLLER that subcycles or tightens coupling iterations where the TEMPORAL COCYCLE is large.
- Ledger entries PER COUPLING STEP so the budget pie (Proposal 3) can show "your error is in the FSI handoff at t ∈ [2.1, 2.3]s".
- The parallel-in-time extension (coarse temporal propagators as coarse spaces — the same H⁰ pattern as BDDC) is NOTED and EXPLICITLY DEFERRED.
- Couples to the base plan's fs-time (variational/symplectic integrators, IMEX) + fs-couple (port-Hamiltonian Dirac interconnection).

## Acceptance criteria
Splitting error appears as a per-coupling-step ledger entry and localizes to the correct temporal interval on a fixture; the adaptive controller reduces the temporal cocycle where it subcycles; the budget pie attributes error to the coupling handoff.

## Kill / activation criterion (Proposal 4 — gate ACTIVATION on demand)
Build ONLY when a paying workload's error budget is demonstrably DOMINATED by splitting error. If, after instrumenting the first coupled-transient customer problems, splitting error is <20% of budget, leave it INSTRUMENTED-BUT-UNCONTROLLED (measure it, don't build the controller).

## Testing
Property: the temporal cocycle → 0 as coupling iterations tighten (consistency); budget-pie temporal attribution correct on a seeded splitting-error-dominated fixture. G3: results invariant to slab re-partition within tolerance. Logs: per-step coupling residuals, controller decisions, temporal budget-pie.

## Dependencies
Proposal 3 (budget attribution — HARD); base plan fs-time + fs-couple + the sheaf machinery (temporal interfaces reuse the spatial restriction-map algebra). Phase 3 horizon; instrument first, control only on demand.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- No-false-stability case (honoring the no-stability-claim scope): on an added-mass-unstable FSI fixture the system surfaces non-convergence honestly and emits NO passing coupling certificate — consistent data transfer is never mistaken for convergent iteration. Boundary: a monolithic/single-physics problem yields a temporal cocycle of EXACTLY zero; the controller at maximum subcycling reports the residual honestly rather than looping.

title: Differentiation & reality e2e SUITE: seam-crossing adjoint descent + as-built diff + tolerance allocation, with gradient-check tables
description:
## What
DIFFERENTIATION & REALITY e2e SUITE — a runnable end-to-end battery (with detailed structured logging + gradient-check tables) exercising Layer 3 as a whole: end-to-end adjoints, reality-as-a-chart, and (when activated) the spacetime complex.

## Scope — the e2e script (structured tracing / ledger events, never stdout)
1. **Seam-crossing gradient descent** (frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.1/frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.2/frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.3): compute ∂QoI/∂(spline control point) THROUGH SDF→mesh→solve; assert adjoint-vs-FD agreement in random directions (conditioning-aware tolerance) and monotone objective descent on a fixture (sphere volume / compliance classic). Exercise differentiability-aware routing: a gradient query routes to a smooth SDF/spline path; a forced remesh emits estimated-color + discontinuity flag; a missing-VJP op inside the path raises a STRUCTURED error and blocks the gradient (never silent-zero). LOG: per-op VJP coverage, adjoint-vs-FD residual tables, chosen path, per-gradient color/flag.
2. **As-built loop** (frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.4/frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.5): ingest a scanned fixture (registration ledgered with its error); compute the as-built δ colored validated + anchored to a calibration cert; localize a seeded defect; run point-sensor 4D-Var assimilation (registration-free) and assert misfit reduction. LOG: registration optimizations + errors, as-built δ fields, misfit-reduction trajectory.
3. **Tolerance allocation** (frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.6): produce a GD&T report on a known-sensitivity fixture; Monte-Carlo over the allocated tolerance box confirms P(performance ∈ spec) ≥ target. LOG: sensitivity fields, allocated tolerances (monotone in inverse-sensitivity), extreme-sample checks.
