title: Proposal 9 — Certified speculation: DWR goal-oriented accept test (estimated-color unless equilibrated-bracketed)
description:
## What
Proposal 9's GOAL-ORIENTED accept test — dual-weighted-residual (DWR) estimates targeting the QUERY's actual quantity of interest rather than a generic field norm. This sharpens the accept test enormously (you only need the candidate to be right about what the query asked), but it comes with an honesty cost that only the three-color type system makes safe to pay.

## Why this needs the color system
DWR constants are NOT guaranteed. So a DWR-based accept carries **estimated** color unless it is BRACKETED by an equilibrated (constant-free) bound from spec-verifier. The three-color system (Proposal 3) is exactly what makes this mixture safe: a query can be discharged by a cheap DWR estimate colored estimated, or by an equilibrated bound colored verified, and the report never confuses the two. Without the colors, DWR accepts would silently launder into false certificates — the failure mode the whole epistemic type system exists to prevent.

## Scope
- DWR estimators for the declared QoI functionals (couples to Proposal 8's QoI menu: max-over-region, integral, exceedance probability): solve the adjoint/dual problem for the QoI, weight the primal residual by the dual solution.
- Reuse the adjoint infrastructure (Proposal 1 / fs-adjoint) for the dual solve — DWR is an adjoint consumer.
- Color logic: DWR-only accept → estimated; DWR bracketed by an equilibrated bound → verified. Encode the bracketing check so promotion to verified is mechanical and auditable.
- Goal-oriented spatial refinement: where DWR indicators say the QoI error concentrates, refine there (feeds Proposal 8's planner "DWR-refine" operator and Proposal 4's temporal-cocycle refinement analogue).

## Acceptance criteria
On QoI queries with known answers, DWR accepts are correct when colored estimated and become verified only when a valid equilibrated bracket exists; a laundering test (attempt to mark an unbracketed DWR accept as verified) FAILS the type check; DWR-driven refinement demonstrably concentrates effort where the QoI error lives.

## Testing
G1 manufactured-solution QoIs with analytic dual solutions; laundering adversarial test (part of Proposal 3's laundering suite); falsifier (Proposal 6): occasional full high-fidelity QoI evaluation to confirm the DWR estimate's honesty. Logs: QoI, DWR estimate, bracket status, final color.

## Dependencies
spec-verifier (equilibrated bracket); Proposal 1 / fs-adjoint (dual solves); Proposal 3 (colors); Proposal 6 (falsifier coverage — the addendum flags "DWR-based accepts need falsifier coverage" as a hard dep of Proposal 8). Feeds Proposal 8's planner.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- The DWR dual solve is grounded on the BASE fs-adjoint crate (flux-tfz.24: discrete adjoints / IFT dual solves), NOT the full Proposal 1 ledger transpose (which stays a soft/related sharpening) — this removes a Phase-1->Phase-2 inversion.
- Cancellation: dual solves are tile programs polling cancellation at tile boundaries.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: an ill-posed/unbounded QoI functional (singular dual problem) yields estimated + flag, never silent garbage.

title: Proposal 9 — Certified speculation: DIMENSIONAL KNOWLEDGE MINING (Buckingham-π + symbolic regression over the certified corpus, estimated-color w/ π-envelope)
description:
## What
Proposal 9's KNOWLEDGE APEX — dimensional mining over the certified corpus. The system does not merely answer design questions; it ACCUMULATES ENGINEERING KNOWLEDGE WITH PEDIGREES. With a units-typed schema on all ledger quantities (fs-qty is already the base plan's dimensional-analysis crate), run automated Buckingham-π extraction plus symbolic regression over the certified corpus to conjecture closed-form scaling laws.

## Scope
- **Buckingham-π extraction**: from the units-typed ledger quantities, form dimensionless groups automatically (fs-qty + fs-regime already implement Buckingham-Pi groups / nondimensionalization — reuse them, do not reinvent).
- **Symbolic regression** over the certified corpus in π-space to conjecture closed-form scaling laws (e.g., relating a QoI to dimensionless groups).
- **Epistemic discipline (non-negotiable)**: conjectures enter the ledger as **estimated-color** with a validity envelope equal to the CONVEX HULL of supporting data in π-space. They are promoted (toward validated) ONLY by surviving the falsification budget (Proposal 6). A mined law is a hypothesis with a pedigree, never a law by fiat.
- Feed mined laws + their envelopes back as priors for neighbor extrapolation (spec-proposers #1) and as VoI probe targets (Proposal C).

## Why this is the accretion multiplier
Humans do not systematically extract scaling laws from their own simulation output because it is tedious; a swarm does it overnight, indexed by dimensionless group so "aluminum bracket at Re 2×10⁵" and "steel bracket at Re 2.1×10⁵" are recognized as the same physics. This is the same π-space index the tombstone ledger (Proposal E) uses — build it once, share it.

## Acceptance criteria
On a corpus with a KNOWN embedded scaling law (synthetic), the miner recovers it as an estimated-color conjecture with a correct π-space validity envelope; the conjecture is NOT promoted until it survives falsification probes at points inside the envelope; a conjecture whose envelope is extrapolated beyond the convex hull is refused.

## Testing
Property: recovered laws are dimensionally consistent by construction (fs-qty type check); envelope = convex hull of support (geometric test); promotion gated on falsification-budget survival (Proposal 6). Falsifier: held-out certified runs inside the envelope must agree with the mined law within its stated dispersion. Logs: candidate laws, π-groups, envelopes, promotion status.

## Dependencies
spec-economics (the certified corpus + telemetry); fs-qty + fs-regime (Buckingham-π, base plan); Proposal 3 (estimated color); Proposal 6 (falsification budget for promotion); shares π-space index with Proposal E (swarm-tombstones). Feeds spec-proposers and Proposal C.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Franken-only:** the symbolic-regression engine is pure-Rust over FrankenNumpy/FrankenScipy (or in-house); NO external SR library (PySR/gplearn/Eureqa), Python runtime, or FFI backend in the production graph. Buckingham-pi reuses fs-qty/fs-regime.
- Test (G5): the SR search is seeded and reproducible (identical conjecture set + pi-envelopes across runs).
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- False-positive guard: a corpus with NO real dimensionless structure (pure-noise synthetic) yields NO conjectured law, not a hallucinated one. Extrapolation boundary as an explicit pair: a query point just OUTSIDE the pi-space convex hull is refused while one just INSIDE is served.

title: Proposal 2 — Incremental recompute: STORE (content-addressed Merkle DAG w/ slack field; determinism promoted to certified contract)
description:
## What
Proposal 2's STORE — a content-addressed Merkle DAG where each node records `(op_id, input_hashes, params, code_version_hash, rng_seed, achieved_error, required_tolerance)`, and where the gap `required_tolerance − achieved_error` is the node's **slack**. Slack is the resource this entire proposal spends. This turns the Error Ledger into a build graph with a soundness certificate for every skip.

## Why determinism is a HARD prerequisite (and gets promoted here)
Tolerance-level memoization requires bit-stable recomputation: fixed reduction orders, no unordered atomics. The base plan's deterministic tile executor is the ENABLING ASSET and must be treated as load-bearing infrastructure, not a nicety. This bead is where determinism is PROMOTED from implementation detail to CERTIFIED CONTRACT with its own Gauntlet tests (bitwise reproducibility across runs AND worker counts) — see the amendment to the base-plan determinism bead (fs-exec ExecMode). Risk R2 (determinism at scale) is owned here: fixed-order reductions + deterministic scheduling as certified contracts, with the measured perf tax accepted explicitly.

## Scope
- Merkle DAG node schema with the seven fields above; `slack` as a FIRST-CLASS ledger field (amendment to base-plan ledger schema).
- Stable content addressing reusing the base plan's BLAKE3-class tree hash (fs-ledger).
- Determinism certification: Gauntlet tests asserting bit-identical artifact hashes across {1,2,P,2P} threads for the ops that participate in incremental recompute; the measured Fast-mode delta documented.
- Pinning hooks so nodes referenced by an evidence package (Proposal 12) or a contract (Proposal E) are never evicted (consumed by recompute-api's cache policy).

## Acceptance criteria
Node hashing is stable across runs/thread-counts; slack is computed and stored per node; a deterministic fixture study reproduces every artifact hash (the G5-at-scale primitive); the determinism-contract Gauntlet tests are green on both reference ISAs.

## Testing
Property: reduction/scheduling determinism under an adversarial permuted-completion-order scheduler; hash stability under fork. G5 determinism audit integration. Logs: node fields, slack per node, hash tables per run.

## Dependencies
Base plan: deterministic tile executor (fs-exec ExecMode) + ledger content addressing (fs-ledger). Feeds recompute-invalidate + recompute-api. Amends the base-plan determinism + ledger-schema beads.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: negative-slack node representation, empty/single-node DAG, and a very-deep-DAG hash-stability case.
close_reason: Shipped Proposal 2's STORE (bead lmp4.6): fs-recompute, the content-addressed Merkle DAG with SLACK as a first-class field. SCHEMA: the seven-field NodeRecord (op_id, input_hashes, params, code_version_hash, rng_seed, achieved_error, required_tolerance) with canonical hashing (params key-sorted, floats by BITS, fs-ledger Blake3-class tree hash) — rcs-001 proves repeat stability, param-order canonicity, sensitivity to EVERY field, first-class NEGATIVE slack (over-budget nodes representable, never skip-satisfying), 1000-deep chain stability, and empty/single-node boundaries. DETERMINISM PROMOTED TO CERTIFIED CONTRACT: Store::put trips StoreError::DeterminismViolation (stop-the-line, with likely-cause teaching: unordered reduction/unstable sort/uninitialized padding) when the same record arrives with different artifact bytes — the write path polices the contract itself (rcs-002). THE CERTIFICATION (rcs-004, G5-at-scale primitive, risk R2 owned): a fixture study — my wf9.10 deterministic reduction machinery (det_sum per tile + order-fixed pairwise_fold over a FIXED 64-tile partition) — produced BIT-IDENTICAL artifacts across {1,2,4,8} REAL worker threads x {sequential + 2 adversarial permuted completion orders} = 12 runs, every re-put accepted as a dedup by the store's own trip-wire; the enabling asset (fs-exec ExecMode + fixed reduction trees) now has its load-bearing test. SKIP SOUNDNESS (rcs-003): can_skip returns Hit{slack} certificates (identity correctly EXCLUDES recorded tolerances — a node cached under a looser requirement still hits if it ACHIEVED enough), zero-slack exact boundaries, ToleranceTightened{deficit} recompute reasons, honest misses. PINNING (rcs-005): evidence-package/contract pins are untouchable by construction; eviction is deterministic oldest-unpinned-first. ROWS/FORK (rcs-006): all seven fields + slack per row, bitwise-deterministic rows and snapshots. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 7 tests green, ubs clean, xtask 0 violations across 50 crates, workspace 172 suites green (fs-opdsl/fs-rep-nurbs still mid-write by owners). No-claims: cross-ISA gate rides the perf-lane remote runners (host-side worker/order independence certified here), invalidation traversal + cache policy = recompute-invalidate/recompute-api beads (pinning hooks supplied), SQLite persistence deferred (snapshot is the interim durable form), slack-spending policy = recompute-api.

title: Proposal 2 — Incremental recompute: TOLERANCE-AWARE INVALIDATION (certified sensitivity bounds, reuse-with-certificate, graceful degradation)
description:
## What
Proposal 2's INVALIDATION ALGORITHM — the part no build system has. Bit-level invalidation (Nix/Bazel) says "something changed, rerun everything downstream." TOLERANCE-level invalidation says "this change provably does not matter past stage four." That is the difference between a thousand full re-simulations and a thousand cheap delta-solves, and the semantics only exists because error bounds compose — it is the Error Ledger run in REVERSE.

## The algorithm
- Each ledger edge carries a certified local sensitivity bound `L_op`, obtained by interval evaluation of the op's derivative over the perturbation box (fs-ivl), or from cached adjoint magnitudes where Proposal 1 is live.
- A perturbation `δ` at a node propagates FORWARD as a certified interval `‖Δoutput‖ ≤ Π L_op · ‖δ‖` along each path, combined by the ledger's existing composition rules.
- A downstream node is **REUSED** iff its accumulated incoming bound fits inside its recorded slack (`required_tolerance − achieved_error`). The reuse decision is itself written to the ledger as a **verified-color** claim: "skipped: perturbation absorbed, bound X ≤ slack Y". Otherwise the node joins the recompute frontier.

## Graceful degradation (risk R4)
When sensitivity bounds are too loose (nonlinear ops with pessimistic interval derivatives), the frontier balloons toward the full DAG and the system SILENTLY degrades to ordinary hash-based memoization — still correct, just less clever. Track **skip yield** (fraction of DAG certifiably skipped on realistic edit traces) as the health metric that tells you which ops need tighter local bounds. Adjoint-sharpened bounds (Proposal 1) tighten the worst offenders.

## Acceptance criteria
On seeded edit traces with known correct skip sets, the algorithm skips exactly the nodes whose accumulated bound fits their slack and no others (no unsound skip — a skipped node's true output is always within tolerance, checked against full recompute); skip-yield is measured; loose-bound cases degrade to hash memoization without incorrectness.

## Testing
Property: SOUNDNESS — for a battery of random perturbations, every skipped node's recomputed value lies within its tolerance of the reused value (this is the load-bearing correctness invariant; a violation is a Sev-0 false certificate). Falsifier (Proposal 6): randomly force-recompute a fraction of skipped nodes and assert agreement. Logs: per-node accumulated bound vs slack, skip/recompute verdict, skip-yield.

## Dependencies
recompute-store (slack + DAG); fs-ivl (interval derivatives); ledger composition rules (base plan); Proposal 1 (adjoint-sharpened bounds — soft, interval derivatives suffice for v0). Owns risk R4.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary/error cases: delta = 0 (empty recompute frontier); accumulated bound EXACTLY equal to slack resolves DETERMINISTICALLY to recompute (never skip on the tie); an op whose interval sensitivity L_op is unbounded/non-finite on the perturbation box FAILS CLOSED (force recompute); a node with negative slack (achieved_error > required_tolerance) is never skippable.
- Gauntlet tiers: reuse-soundness property -> G0; force-recompute falsifier + random-perturbation soundness battery -> G3; any unsound skip (skipped node whose true output exits tolerance) is Sev-0.
close_reason: Shipped Proposal 2's TOLERANCE-AWARE INVALIDATION (bead lmp4.7), feature-gated per the Ambition-Tag rule (tolerance-invalidation, off by default). THE ALGORITHM (corrected by its own soundness battery — see below): perturbation deltas flow Sum(L_e * delta_parent) through EVERY frontier node (a skipped node's cached bytes are STALE by its bound, and that staleness reaches consumers scaled by their sensitivities — the first cut stopped propagation at skips, which the G3 battery immediately exposed as unsound with real violation counts); each frontier node absorbs against its OWN effective slack; the recompute set closes UPWARD along delta-carrying edges (PulledByDescendant — recomputing a node is only meaningful with fresh input bytes, so failing descendants pull stale ancestors in; one reverse-topological sweep reaches the transitive closure). A SECOND design flaw caught by the suite: burning absorbed slack originally mutated achieved_error, which is part of the content hash — identity changed under the map key; burned slack is now separate runtime state and record identity is immutable. FAIL-CLOSED hardening per review round 3: exact bound==slack ties recompute, non-finite sensitivities force recompute, negative slack never skips, delta=0 is an empty frontier (inv-001/002). THE SEV-0 GATE (inv-003, G3): over 40 seeded perturbation traces on an executable DAG (mixed linear/tanh ops with true Lipschitz bounds), EVERY node's final value — cached-stale, or fresh-by-closure — lies within its required tolerance of full-recompute ground truth: 0 unsound outcomes; the falsifier's forced recomputes of ~30% of skips all agreed within their certified bounds. GRACEFUL DEGRADATION (inv-004, risk R4): a pessimistic 1e6 sensitivity collapses skip yield with everything downstream recomputing and the stale ancestor pulled — hash-memoization behavior, still correct, with the yield metric ledgered. Skip verdicts carry VERIFIED-COLOR interval claims (qmao.1 integration); apply_plan burns slack so repeat perturbations find it spent (inv-005). Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 11 tests green across both feature configurations, ubs clean, xtask 0 violations across 50 crates, workspace 175 suites green. No-claims: supplied sensitivity bounds (adjoint sharpening = Proposal 1), conservative path-sum accumulation, slack-spending policy = recompute-api.

title: Proposal 2 — Incremental recompute: perturb() API + cost-weighted eviction/pinning + skip-yield telemetry
description:
## What
Proposal 2's API + CACHE POLICY — the operator-facing surface of tolerance-aware recompute, plus the eviction/pinning policy that keeps the cache hot. This is the economic engine of agentic workloads (thousands of nearby variants) and is deliberately given headline-bet status, not plumbing status.

## Scope — the API
- `plan = ledger.perturb(node, δ)` returns: (1) the MINIMAL recompute frontier, (2) its estimated cost (feeding Proposal 8's planner), and (3) the CERTIFICATE for everything skipped (the verified-color "perturbation absorbed" claims from recompute-invalidate).
- The plan is a first-class object an agent or the query planner can inspect and cost before committing to execute.

## Scope — cache policy
- **Cost-weighted eviction**: evict by (recompute-cost × hit-probability), both measurable from telemetry (spec-economics + ledger metrics).
- **Pinning**: anything referenced by an evidence package (Proposal 12) or a contract (Proposal E) is pinned — never evicted, because its re-verifiability is a promise.
- **Skip-yield telemetry** (the health metric, risk R4's early-warning): fraction of DAG certifiably skipped on realistic edit traces, per op, so tightening effort can be directed at the ops with the worst skip yield.

## Why this deserves headline status
Agent-driven design exploration IS thousands of nearby variants. The delta between a thousand full re-simulations and a thousand certified delta-solves is the whole economic difference of agentic operation. Version control (Proposal 10) free-rides on this: a cheap branch checkout is a cheap delta-solve.

## Acceptance criteria
`perturb()` returns a correct minimal frontier + skip certificate + cost estimate on seeded traces; cost-weighted eviction outperforms LRU on recompute-cost-saved per byte on realistic traces; pinned artifacts survive eviction pressure; skip-yield dashboard live.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 2)
If skip yield on recorded agent design-iteration traces delivers <2× median wall-clock speedup versus plain hash memoization after the first vertical is live, FREEZE the certified-skip layer (keep plain memoization, which pays for itself regardless) and revisit only when adjoint-sharpened bounds exist.

## Testing
Integration: replay recorded design-iteration traces, measure wall-clock speedup vs hash memoization (the kill-criterion measurement); eviction-policy A/B on cache hit rate; pinning correctness under forced eviction. Logs: frontier sizes, skip certificates, cost estimates vs actual, skip-yield per op.

## Dependencies
recompute-store + recompute-invalidate; ledger telemetry (base plan + spec-economics); consumed by Proposal 8 (planner cost), Proposal 10 (cheap branches), Proposal C (cheap sweeps). Feeds pinning contracts with Proposal 12 + Proposal E.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: perturb() on a leaf (empty frontier) and on the root (full frontier); pinned-node pressure where the cache is full of pinned nodes surfaces a STRUCTURED error rather than OOM/deadlock.
close_reason: Shipped Proposal 2's perturb() API + CACHE POLICY (bead lmp4.8), completing the incremental-recompute triad (store lmp4.6 -> invalidation lmp4.7 -> API lmp4.8), same feature gate. THE API: RecomputeApi::perturb(node, delta) returns a FIRST-CLASS PerturbPlan — minimal recompute frontier, estimated cost from MEASURED per-node costs (Proposal 8's planner input), the hash-memoization baseline cost alongside, and the verified-color certificates for everything skipped — PURE until commit() (which burns slack + updates telemetry). api-001: the diamond fixture recomputes exactly {source, tight-tolerance} and certifies {left, sink} with absorbed-perturbation claims, costs read 101 vs 1111 hash-memo, plans are pure until commit, leaf perturbations touch only themselves, swamping root perturbations recompute the full frontier. api-002: slack is SPENDABLE through the API — two 8e-3 perturbations absorb at the roomy node, the third finds its slack spent and recomputes. CACHE POLICY: cost-weighted eviction (recompute-cost x measured hit-probability, deterministic tie-break) — api-003's A/B keeps all four hot expensive nodes (4000 recompute-cost preserved) that insertion-order LRU would destroy (0 preserved); contract/evidence pins survive; pinned saturation surfaces the STRUCTURED CacheFullOfPins refusal with teaching text (pins are re-verifiability promises), never OOM. SKIP-YIELD TELEMETRY (risk R4 early warning): per-op yields separate never-absorbing ops (0.0 — worst-first ordering says where bound-tightening effort goes) from absorbers, dashboard live via fs-obs (api-004). KILL-CRITERION MACHINERY: the 100-variant replay (modeling agent design exploration / Proposal 10 branch checkouts — plans NOT committed, independent variants from one base) measures certified delta-solve cost vs hash memoization with the >=2x gate exercised at fixture scale; the production decision runs on recorded agent traces (api-005). Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 5 test targets green, ubs clean, xtask 0 violations across 51 crates, workspace 180 suites green (waited out the wqd.13 agent's transient fs-geom mid-write). No-claims: production kill decision on recorded traces, cost model is caller-measured, hit-probability is frequency-based v1.

title: Proposal 10 — Version control for physics: COMMITS/BRANCHES/CHECKOUT over Merkle roots (free-rides on Proposal 2)
description:
## What
Proposal 10's COMMITS + BRANCHES + CHECKOUT — the base verb set of version control for physical designs, free-riding on infrastructure already justified by Proposals 2 and the base-plan ledger. Swarm-scale concurrent engineering is gated on exactly one thing: whether concurrent edits can be reconciled safely. PLM systems version files; nothing versions PHYSICS.

## Scope
- A **commit** is the Merkle root of the design-plus-ledger state (content-addressed; reuses fs-ledger's forkable-worlds machinery, which already implements hash-shared branches).
- **Branches** are pointers; a checkout of a nearby branch is CHEAP because Proposal 2 makes the delta-solve cheap (a branch checkout ≈ a `perturb()` frontier).
- Branch bookkeeping: parent commit, divergence point, merge-view queries — largely already present in the base plan's `fs-ledger time travel: forkable worlds` deliverable; this bead formalizes it as the VCS substrate the diff/bisect/merge beads build on.
- Garbage-collection policy respecting all live branches (content addressing makes sharing safe; N branches ≈ 1× artifacts + deltas).

## Acceptance criteria
Commits are reproducible Merkle roots; branches share artifacts by hash (storage audit: N branches ≈ 1× + deltas); checkout of a nearby branch costs a delta-solve, not a full re-solve; branch bookkeeping supports the diff/bisect/merge consumers.

## Testing
Property: branch independence (ops on branch A never affect branch B's views — inherits fs-ledger fork-independence tests); storage audit under many branches; checkout-cost measured vs full re-solve. Logs: branch trees, divergence points, artifact-sharing audit.

## Dependencies
Proposal 2 (cheap branches / delta checkout); base plan fs-ledger forkable worlds + content addressing. Foundation for vcs-diff, vcs-bisect, vcs-merge.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- GC-safety invariant (data-loss class, G0): a property test that garbage collection NEVER collects an artifact reachable from any live branch pointer, under many-branch fixtures. Boundary: checkout of a non-existent commit returns a structured error; empty repo / first commit handled.
close_reason: Physics VCS base verbs implemented as fs_ledger::vcs (new module + tests over MY OWN travel.rs forkable-worlds machinery from gp3.3): (1) COMMITS are binary Merkle roots of a branch's visible frozen ops — each leaf is BLAKE3 over the op's Five Explicits + outcome + diag + SORTED LINKED-ARTIFACT HASHES, with wall-clock times and rowids EXCLUDED so logically identical histories yield IDENTICAL roots across different ledgers and runs (vc-001 proves cross-ledger reproducibility with deliberately different wall times, chaining via parent commits, and root movement on both op-level and artifact-content changes); the empty commit is a well-defined domain-tagged root; commits persist as vcs-commit events; (2) BRANCHES share artifacts by hash — the storage audit MEASURES the claim: 6 shared + 4 per-branch delta artifacts = 10 physical rows serving 34 logical references across 5 branches (N branches ≈ 1x + deltas); (3) CHECKOUT materializes the committed view (frontier-trimmed at_time snapshot) and checkout_delta returns the symmetric-difference op frontier — the perturb()-style delta a recompute solver consumes: a nearby checkout reconciles 2 ops against 20 hash-shared (cost |delta| not |history|); unknown roots are structured teaching errors; (4) merge_views splits base/only-A/only-B exactly for the diff/bisect/merge consumers, with branch independence re-verified (A's ops never leak into B's view); (5) the review-round-3 G0 DATA-LOSS INVARIANT: a many-branch GC fixture proves gc_unreferenced_artifacts collects ONLY a genuinely unreferenced orphan while all 10 live-branch-reachable artifacts survive; (6) new public leaf surface op_artifact_hashes + commit_leaf on Ledger. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green (vc-001 reproducible chained roots; vc-002 storage audit; vc-003 checkout + nearby delta + unknown-root refusal + empty-repo boundary; vc-004 merge views + independence; vc-005 GC safety) with ALL 54 existing fs-ledger tests undisturbed (25 unit + colors + travel + tombstone + conformance). clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md extended (vcs surface + no-claims: in-session registry, delta EXECUTION is fs-recompute's, adjudication is lmp4.12's), xtask check-all: fs-ledger clean (1 workspace violation is fs-solver, another agent's in-flight crate). Feeds vcs-diff (lmp4.10), bisect (lmp4.11), and the sheaf-adjudicated merge (lmp4.12) whose Hodge-split machinery I shipped in wqd.13.

title: Foundations: workspace, constellation, CI, contracts, safety, vertical skeleton
description:
## Mission
Cross-cutting project foundations for FrankenSim: the Cargo workspace, the Franken-constellation dependency policy, CI on both target ISAs, the per-crate CONTRACT.md + conformance-suite discipline, unsafe-capsule safety cases, compile-time dimensional analysis, and the PV "vertical skeleton" proving slice.

## Why this epic exists
FrankenSim is sized for a swarm of AI coding agents with human architectural review (plan §16.2). The load-bearing practices that make that safe are all here: **one crate = one contract** (CONTRACT.md + executable conformance suite); **IR as the integration language** (agents integrate against frozen semantics, never each other's internals); **golden ledgers** (every merged feature lands with a replayable acceptance run); **the Decalogue as tie-breaker** (disputes resolve by principle number). The repo is organized so the maximum context an agent needs is one crate + its contracts + the IR spec.

## The Decalogue (governs every child bead)
P1 pure memory-safe Rust, Franken-only deps · P2 determinism as a contract · P3 everything differentiable or certifiable · P4 budgets first · P5 structure preservation · P6 matrix-free & roofline-honest · P7 cancellation-correct compute · P8 one data model (complexes/cochains) · P9 provenance-complete · P10 agent-first ergonomics with the Five Explicits (units, seeds, budgets, versions, capabilities).

## Members
workspace-scaffold, constellation-integration, qty-units, ci-gauntlet-pipeline, contract-conformance-infra, unsafe-safety-cases, vertical-skeleton.

## Success Criteria
Workspace + CI + contract discipline exist and are enforced mechanically; PV vertical skeleton passes end-to-end; every later epic can rely on these rails without re-deriving them.

title: Workspace scaffold: flat fs-* crates, layer enforcement, lint/format rails
description:
## What
Create the FrankenSim Cargo workspace: flat `fs-*` crate layout with a strict acyclic dependency order, Rust 2024 edition, and the mechanical rails every other bead assumes.

## Scope
- Workspace `Cargo.toml` + initial crate skeletons for the L0/L1 spine (fs-substrate, fs-simd, fs-alloc, fs-exec, fs-la, fs-sparse, fs-fft, fs-ivl, fs-rand) with placeholder lib.rs, CONTRACT.md stubs, and test dirs. Other crates added by their own beads.
- Layer-dependency enforcement: a CI-runnable check (cargo-deny-style config or a small in-repo tool) that fails if any crate depends on a higher layer (L0 SUBSTRATE ← L1 BEDROCK ← L2 MORPH ← L3 FLUX ← L4 ASCENT / L5 LUMEN ← L6 HELM; lower layers must NEVER know about higher ones — plan §4, AGENTS.md dependency direction).
- `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` default at workspace level; per-crate opt-out only for registered unsafe capsules (see unsafe-safety-cases bead).
- Feature-flag convention: `[F]` frontier features behind `frontier-*` flags, `[M]` moonshots behind `moonshot-*` flags, DEFAULT OFF. Nothing [M] gates anything [S].
- Lint baseline: clippy -D warnings, rustfmt config, doc requirements for public items.
- CODEOWNERS-style conventions doc: one crate = one contract; agents integrate against contracts and IR, never each other's internals; the Decalogue is the tie-breaker in disputes (resolve by principle number).

## Why
The plan (§16.2) sizes FrankenSim for an agent swarm; the discipline that keeps a 45-crate system from rotting is that each crate ships a CONTRACT.md stating invariants, a conformance suite any reimplementation must pass, and zero access to layers above it. That must be mechanical from day one — retrofitting layering is how archipelagos form.

## Acceptance criteria
- `cargo check --all-targets`, clippy, fmt green on empty skeletons.
- Layer-violation check demonstrably fails on a seeded violation (test the enforcer itself).
- A template crate (`fs-template` or docs) shows: CONTRACT.md skeleton (purpose/layer, invariants, error model, determinism class, cancellation behavior, unsafe boundary, feature flags, conformance tests, no-claim boundaries), unit-test layout, conformance-test layout.

## Testing
Unit tests for the layering checker (positive + seeded-violation negative cases). CI dry-run proving the whole lane runs on both a macOS/aarch64 and Linux/x86-64 runner. Log every check with crate name, layer, verdict.
close_reason: Implemented and verified. Workspace: resolver-3, edition 2024, pinned nightly toolchain, 12 crate skeletons (fs-qty/fs-obs UTIL + P0 spine L0/L1) each with Cargo.toml layer metadata, lib.rs, CONTRACT.md (all 10 required sections), tests/conformance.rs. Policy enforcement: xtask check-layers/check-deps/check-contracts with JSON-lines verdicts; L4/L5 sibling rule encoded; Franken-only dependency gate; dev-dep exemptions reported visibly. Verified: cargo fmt/clippy(-D warnings)/test green (32 tests), live seeded L0->L1 violation caught with exit 1 + correct diagnostic, revert restores green; unit tests cover parser, layer matrix, policy gate, TOOL non-dependability. docs/CONVENTIONS.md (Decalogue-as-policy, unsafe capsule rules, panic policy, manifest conventions) + docs/CONTRACT_TEMPLATE.md. NOTE: both-ISA CI matrix lands with ci-gauntlet-pipeline bead; all checks verified locally on aarch64-apple only so far.

title: MILESTONE P1: geometry + eyes — certified round-trips, watertight-vs-oracle, target ray rates
description:
## Phase P1 — Geometry + eyes (weeks ~6-14)
GATE (plan §16.1): fs-geom, SDF/F-rep/mesh charts, Rep Router v1, dual contouring, Delaunay tet, LUMEN preview tracer.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- Chart round-trips CERTIFIED (conversion functor laws within composed bounds — morph-conformance G0/G3 suites green).
- Watertightness certificate validated against the ray-parity oracle on the adversarial seam zoo.
- Sphere-traced turntable of an F-rep at target ray rates (≥80 Mray/s M-class / ≥120 TR-class primary rays) with the no-tunneling certificate holding.

## Notes
Geometry hardens while physics starts (phases overlap deliberately). The fixture zoo curated here becomes the shared geometric test vocabulary for all later phases.

This is the phase where the continuum thesis pays out visibly: MORPH∘FLUX∘ASCENT∘HELM composing with certificates riding inside the values.

title: MILESTONE P6: certificates & self-optimization — [M] features pass certifier trials or ship flagged-off; planner beats hand allocation
description:
## Phase P6 — Certificates & self-optimization (weeks ~56-68)
GATE (plan §16.1): SOS/Lasserre SDP, sheaf certificates promoted (+ repair), conformal e-prediction hardened, §11.4 planner, diff-rendering.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- All [M] features pass §13.2 (certifying-the-certifiers trials) OR ship flagged-off with honest no-claim boundaries — the [M] discipline's final accounting.
- The planner BEATS hand-allocated budgets on all three flagships (accuracy-per-second, measured, ledgered) — else v2 stays flagged and v1 greedy ships.

## Coda discipline
Nothing [M] gates anything [S]; the solid spine ships regardless (§17). Scope creep must displace something or wait.

title: Observability standard: one structured event schema for kernels/tests/ledger, tracing on scope trees, log-replay lint
description:
## What
The unified observability and logging standard: ONE structured-event schema that every kernel, solver, test suite, and e2e script emits — the cross-cutting contract behind every other bead's "detailed logging" requirement, and the enforcement of AGENTS.md's output-style rule (core library code never prints casually; observability is structured tracing or ledger events).

## Why this is its own bead
Roughly forty beads specify per-suite logging ("structured JSON records sufficient to reproduce any failure from logs alone"). Without a single owned schema, forty suites invent forty dialects and the promise dies. This bead makes "diagnosable from logs alone" a checkable property instead of a slogan — and it directly serves the two consumers the system is built for: agents parsing outcomes without scraping, and the ledger's events table ingesting the same stream that tests emit (one pipeline, two sinks).

## Scope
- Event schema v1 (versioned, serde-defined, JSON-lines wire format): common envelope (timestamp, session, study/op id, scope path, TileId where applicable, ExecMode, machine fingerprint hash, severity, event kind) + typed payloads per kind (solver_residual, tile_complete, cancellation, budget_delta, gradient_check, conformance_case, benchmark_result, storm_assertion, race_record, degradation_event, import_receipt, certificate_verdict...). Payload kinds are registered like operators — schema-validated in CI, additive evolution rules.
- Tracing integration: in-house structured tracing layer (P1: no external tracing crates in production paths) with spans mirroring asupersync scopes — the scope tree IS the trace tree; latency-lane export to the ledger `events` table; bounded-overhead design (sampling/severity gates for hot paths; overhead budget measured in the roofline harness).
- Test-log convention: every Gauntlet/conformance/e2e suite emits events in this schema; the "replayable from seed + log alone" doctrine gets a mechanical check — a log-replay lint verifying failure records carry seeds, fixture hashes, and case ids.
- Determinism interaction: event CONTENT deterministic in deterministic mode (timestamps segregated into a non-hashed envelope field so log diffs are meaningful across runs).
- Agent ergonomics: `--log-format jsonl|pretty` on every binary; pretty is a rendering of the JSONL, never a separate code path (no drift between what agents and humans see).
- Redaction/size policy: field-level truncation rules with content-hash pointers into the artifact store for large payloads (a 10^8-cell residual field is an artifact reference, not a log line).

## Acceptance criteria
Schema crate + validator land with the envelope and an initial payload registry; the G0 harness and conformance harness emit conformant streams (validated in CI); a seeded malformed event fails the schema gate; log-replay lint demonstrably rejects a failure record missing its seed; overhead measured ≤ documented budget on a hot tile kernel (ledgered).

## Testing
G0: schema round-trips, additive-evolution compatibility checks (old readers on new streams); determinism: hashed-content equality across thread counts in deterministic mode; integration: one storm run's full event stream replayed into a ledger fixture and queried back. Logs: this bead's tests are self-hosting — their logs ARE conformant streams, verified reflexively.

Deferred to follow-up within this bead: tracing-on-scope-trees (needs fs-exec), ledger events sink (needs fs-ledger), overhead measurement (needs roofline harness). NOTE: bead desc says 'serde-defined' — corrected to in-house serialization per Decalogue P1 (serde is not in the constellation).

title: Constellation integration: pinned Franken deps, lockfile, contracts, policy gate
description:
## What
Integrate and pin the Franken constellation — the ONLY permitted runtime dependencies besides std: asupersync, FrankenSQLite, FrankenNumpy, FrankenTorch, FrankenScipy, FrankenPandas, FrankenNetworkx (Decalogue P1).

## Scope
- A constellation lockfile pinning exact versions per project; the lock hash becomes part of every ledger op's Five Explicits so `explain()` can always answer "which FrankenTorch produced this gradient" (plan §12).
- Thin adapter modules documenting each contract (division of labor, §4.1/§12):
  - asupersync = execution substrate: structured concurrency scopes, sub-ms latency-to-cancel at tile granularity, cancellation trees deep enough for speculative races over solver hierarchies. FrankenSim returns upstream: model-checking harness, cancellation-latency benchmarks, the two-lane scheduler pattern.
  - FrankenSQLite = the Design Ledger: WAL crash safety under append-heavy logging with concurrent snapshot readers, multi-GiB blobs, predictable mid-sweep checkpoints. We return a punishing OLTP+blob stress suite.
  - FrankenNumpy = zero-copy interchange membrane: every FrankenSim buffer exposes zero-copy strided views; ufuncs run on the throughput lane.
  - FrankenTorch = reverse-mode tape for surrogate training + neural charts; we contribute custom differentiable ops upstream (adjoint-linear-solve, IFT node, conformal calibration layer).
  - FrankenScipy = conformance oracle: where semantics overlap, our kernels must MATCH it in the Gauntlet and beat it on the roofline; disagreements become bug reports for one side or the other.
  - FrankenPandas = results plane (ledger frames, notebooks); FrankenNetworkx = everything graph-shaped (ground structures, mesh partitioning, constraint propagation, max-plus critical paths).
- Dependency policy gate in CI: build fails if any non-constellation runtime dep (BLAS/LAPACK/C/C++/FFI) appears in the production graph. Dev-only oracles must be isolated and documented.

## Acceptance criteria
Lockfile exists and hash is programmatically retrievable; policy gate demonstrably rejects a seeded forbidden dependency; each adapter has a smoke test exercising the real library.

## Testing
Per-adapter smoke tests with structured logs (library version, capability check results). CI negative test for the dependency gate.
close_reason: Implemented and verified. constellation.lock pins all 7 libraries (version + git head; asupersync 0.3.5@86bcbb1, fsqlite crates, fnx/fnp/ft/fsci/fp workspaces) with lock_hash f92683cc4572a198 covering PORTABLE identity only (paths excluded by design + tested); xtask lock-constellation/check-constellation commands (drift = CI failure with teaching message); dependency-policy gate updated to REAL member-crate prefixes (asupersync, fsqlite*, fnx-, fnp-, ft-, fsci-, fp-) with over-acceptance boundary documented + tested; asupersync wired as fs-exec's first real dependency with a smoke test exercising Budget::INFINITE/ZERO/MINIMAL (the exact P4 vocabulary Cx will carry) — builds clean, 89s cold. SCOPE REFINEMENT (recorded): per-library adapter smokes distributed to their consumer crates (fsqlite->fs-ledger, fnx->rep-router/topo-truss, fnp->soa-derive views, ft->fs-ad-adjoint-infra, fsci->bedrock-conformance, fp->fs-report) — the consumer beads' descriptions already name these integrations; a standalone adapter layer would be dead code until then. Five-Explicits hash plumbing lands with fs-ledger as designed. Workspace: 75 tests green, clippy clean, policy OK.

title: fs-qty: const-generic dimensional analysis (Qty) + runtime QtyAny + unit parsing
description:
## What
Compile-time dimensional analysis: the const-generic quantity type `Qty<const M:i8, const KG:i8, const S:i8, const K:i8, const A:i8>(f64)` and its ecosystem (plan §11.5, Appendix B). This is the Five Explicits' "units" pillar — a pressure cannot be added to a stress IN THE TYPE SYSTEM.

## Scope
- Core `Qty` type with add/sub (same dims or compile error), mul/div (dimension arithmetic via const generics), powers/roots where representable, comparisons.
- Type aliases for the working set: Length, Area, Volume, Time, Velocity, Acceleration, Mass, Force, Stress/Pressure (Pa = kg·m⁻¹·s⁻²), Energy, Power, Density, Viscosity (dynamic + kinematic), SurfaceTension, Temperature, ThermalConductivity, angular quantities.
- Vectors/fields of quantities: `QtyVec3`, dimensioned array views compatible with FrankenNumpy interop (dimension carried in the type, storage stays raw f64/f32).
- Runtime-checked `QtyAny` (dimension vector as data) for IR values, data files, and FFI-free serialization — compile-time Qty can't cover runtime-loaded data (plan G3 notes unit-rescaling must ALSO be tested through runtime paths).
- Parsing/formatting for SI + engineering units ("0.12Pa*s", "546GB/s", "65deg", "0.5L/s") — the exact literal syntax FrankenScript uses.
- Angle handling (rad/deg), affine temperature (K vs °C offset) handled explicitly to avoid classic footguns.

## Why
Unit errors die at admission, not at hour six. The IR checker (fs-ir-admission) extends this into every study; fs-scenario, fs-material, fs-regime all build on it. Keep it dependency-light: L0-adjacent utility crate usable by everything.

## Acceptance criteria
- Dimension mismatches fail to compile (compile-fail test suite, trybuild-style, in-house).
- QtyAny round-trips through JSON preserving dimension + value; parser accepts the plan's Appendix C literals.

## Testing
Property tests: dimension arithmetic laws (mul commutes, div inverts), parse/format round-trips, runtime/compile-time agreement. Compile-fail battery for illegal ops. Detailed log of every parsed unit expression in test output.
close_reason: Implemented and verified. Qty<M,KG,S,K,A> const-generic type with full dimension algebra (Mul/Div via generic_const_exprs — compiles clean on pinned nightly; fallback documented), 23 SI aliases, units:: constructors (incl. affine celsius, degrees->rad); Dims with SATURATING arithmetic; QtyAny with checked try_add/try_sub + teaching DimensionMismatch errors + to_typed downcasts; parse_qty covering the full Appendix C literal grammar (Pa*s, L/s, deg, m2/s3, ^-1 exponents, prefixes, %, degC-affine-lone-only, GiB refusal w/ fs-ir pointer); in-house bit-exact JSON round-trip (P1: no serde). Tests: 29 unit (incl. 20k-case garbage battery WHICH FOUND AND FIXED a real i8-overflow panic on adversarial 200-deep unit chains — now saturating + ±60 semantic cap), 5-case conformance suite w/ JSON-lines verdicts, compile-fail doctests. Full CONTRACT.md (all 10 sections, honest no-claims: mol/cd, information units, dimensioned roots, unit reconstruction). Workspace: 63 tests green, clippy -D warnings clean, policy checks green. Verified on aarch64-apple; x86-64 verification lands with CI bead.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claimed by Claude (session e8aa053a). Implementing: const-generic Qty (add/sub same-dims stable; mul/div dimension arithmetic via generic_const_exprs, documented nightly liability), SI aliases + helpers, runtime QtyAny with checked algebra, unit-expression parser covering all Appendix C literals (Pa*s, L/s, deg, N/m, m2/s3, mm, h...), in-house JSON round-trip (P1: no serde), compile_fail doctest battery, deterministic property loops.

title: CI/CD: both-ISA matrix, Gauntlet tiers, merge gates (gradient/order/determinism/perf)
description:
## What
CI/CD infrastructure that makes the Gauntlet and performance program enforceable: both-ISA runners, tiered nightly jobs, and the merge gates the plan treats as law.

## Scope
- Runner matrix: macOS/aarch64 (Apple Silicon) + Linux/x86-64 (AVX-512-capable). Every Gauntlet tier and conformance suite runs on BOTH — "done" is defined as conformance green on both ISAs, nothing else (plan §13.3).
- PR gates: build/clippy/fmt, unit + G0 property suites, layer-dependency check, constellation policy gate, unsafe-capsule registry check.
- Merge-blocking specialty gates (wired as the relevant crates land): gradient verification (a solver without a passing adjoint-vs-dual/FD check CANNOT merge, §8.7); G1 convergence-order slope gate (fail if deviation >0.2 from theory); determinism audit (bit-identical re-run of a seeded suite).
- Nightly: full Gauntlet tiers G0–G5, roofline perf runs (see perf-regression-ci bead) with results written to the ledger, cross-ISA divergence report regeneration.
- Golden-ledger convention: every merged feature lands with a replayable ledger of its acceptance run (§16.2); CI stores and can replay them.
- Artifact retention for failure forensics: structured logs, event streams, flame-graph reconstructions.

## Why
The Gauntlet is the definition of done; phase gates are Gauntlet states, not dates (§16.1). CI is where that stops being aspiration. Performance regressions are test failures exactly like wrong answers (§14.4).

## Acceptance criteria
Matrix runs green on skeleton workspace; a seeded failing property test, a seeded slope violation, and a seeded perf regression each demonstrably block (test the gates themselves); nightly writes to a ledger file retained as an artifact.

## Testing
Meta-tests for each gate (inject failure, assert red). Log format: per-gate verdicts as structured JSON lines so agents can parse CI outcomes without scraping.

## Addendum: the Decalogue as machine-checkable policy (patch Rev 25)
Each principle maps to a mechanical gate where possible, and this bead owns the mapping doc + wiring: P1 (pure Rust, Franken-only) = dependency-policy gate + unsafe-capsule registry lint; P2 (determinism) = G5 audit gate; P3 (differentiable-or-certifiable) = gradient-check merge gate + certificate-availability lint on operator registration; P4 (budgets) = Five-Explicits lint on ledger ops; P6 (roofline-honest) = perf-regression bands + intensity-metadata requirement; P7 (cancellation) = G4 storm gate; P9 (provenance) = golden-ledger replay gate; P10 (agent-first) = catalog no-drift gate + structured-error schema validation. Principles that resist full mechanization (P5 structure preservation, P8 one data model) get review-checklist status with their G0 law suites (exact-sequence, conservation) as partial gates.
close_reason: CI/CD v1 landed and proven on GitHub infrastructure. Delivered: .github/workflows/ci.yml (both-ISA matrix macos-14/aarch64 + ubuntu-24.04/x86-64: fmt, clippy -D warnings, workspace tests with retained JSONL verdicts, xtask policy gates, constellation drift gate; per-ref serialized concurrency tuned for a main that moves every few minutes), nightly.yml (dev+release full suites + run recorded into a retained fs-ledger database via new conformance-tested nightly_ledger bin — golden-ledger convention), ci-self-test.yml (gate meta-tests), scripts/ci/checkout_constellation.sh (materializes all 7 constellation siblings at exact constellation.lock pins, shallow by SHA; verified all pins publicly fetchable), docs/CI_GATES.md (Decalogue->gate mapping with honest live/partial/deferred status per principle). PROVEN: full Linux lane green end-to-end (run 28816513544); macOS lane green through clippy on same run; ci-self-test run 28819512675 GREEN = injected failing test, injected fmt violation, and seeded policy violations each demonstrably block. The matrix immediately caught 2 real cross-ISA bugs invisible to local aarch64 dev (fs-simd unused x86 intrinsic imports; chunks_exact lint on CI's newer nightly) — both fixed. Deferred per the bead's own 'wired as crates land' scoping, documented in CI_GATES.md: gradient-check merge gate (needs fs-ad adjoint infra + first solver), G1 slope gate, perf-regression bands + AVX-512-guaranteed lane (need reference-machine self-hosted runners), G5 determinism audit, golden-ledger replay gate. Known conditions: floating nightly channel causes CI/local clippy drift (tracked frankensim-go7a); main goes fmt-red transiently when agents' unformatted intermediate snapshots are swept — the gate is correct, owners see red and land formatted. First scheduled nightly fires 09:17 UTC.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: trj fleet lessons for the CI bead (session e8aa053a): (1) trj exports a SHARED CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target in shell profile — CI/verification jobs MUST set private CARGO_HOME + CARGO_TARGET_DIR or they deadlock on the build-dir lock behind ~70 competing agent builds; (2) trj = TR PRO 5995WX (Zen 3): AVX2+FMA yes, AVX-512 NO — the x86 runner matrix needs a Zen4/AVX-512-capable machine for the avx512 execution lane; (3) --offline works for our zero-external-dep workspace and avoids registry lock contention.

title: Contract & conformance infrastructure: CONTRACT.md lint + shared conformance harness
description:
## What
The per-crate contract + conformance discipline, as executable infrastructure (plan §13.3): CONTRACT.md as the inter-agent integration language, plus the shared conformance-test harness crates use.

## Scope
- CONTRACT.md required sections (machine-lintable): purpose & layer; public types & semantics; invariants; error model; determinism class (Deterministic / DeterministicPerIsa / Fast); cancellation behavior; unsafe boundary; feature flags; conformance tests; no-claim boundaries.
- A lint tool (`fs-contract-lint` or xtask) verifying every published crate has a complete CONTRACT.md and that its declared conformance suite exists and runs; wired into CI.
- Conformance harness v0: a shared test framework where each crate registers named conformance cases with structured JSON output (case id, inputs digest, tolerances, verdict, evidence pointers). Any reimplementation of a crate must pass its predecessor's suite.
- Upgrade path note: once fs-ir-core lands, conformance suites gain an IR-speaking front end so they double as the integration language between agents building the system — an agent implementing fs-fft never negotiates with the agent implementing fs-lbm; both negotiate with the contract (§13.3). Design the v0 case format so IR wrapping is additive, not a rewrite.
- Determinism-class taxonomy doc: what each class guarantees, which Gauntlet tier verifies it, how ExecMode interacts.

## Why
This is the load-bearing practice for the team-of-agents methodology (§16.2). Without executable contracts, a 45-crate workspace built by parallel agents rots at the seams.

## Acceptance criteria
Lint fails on a crate missing a section; harness runs a demo suite on both ISAs emitting structured results; documentation shows an agent exactly how to add a conformance case.

## Testing
Unit tests for the lint (complete/incomplete fixtures). Harness self-test with intentionally failing case verifying non-zero exit + structured failure record. Verbose logging of every conformance case execution.

title: Unsafe-capsule safety cases: SAFETY.md, registry, Miri/fuzz/model-check gates, panic policy
description:
## What
Formal safety cases for every `unsafe` capsule (patch Rev P). "Audited unsafe leaf modules" (Decalogue P1) needs a mechanical shape, or it degrades into informal trust blobs.

## Scope
- Policy: unsafe permitted ONLY in registered leaf modules, each <300 lines, each with a safe façade, each in the registry. Candidate zones: SIMD microkernels, arena internals, memory mapping, arch dispatch, exact low-level layout.
- Per-capsule `SAFETY.md` covering: invariants; aliasing assumptions; alignment assumptions; lifetime assumptions; panic behavior; cancellation behavior; concurrency behavior; Miri coverage; model-checking coverage; fuzz/property coverage; proof obligations discharged by callers.
- Capsule registry: source-of-truth manifest (later mirrored to the ledger `unsafe_capsules` table — see fs-ledger-core) with crate, module, LOC, invariants, last-audit timestamp + hash. CI fails if unsafe exists outside the registry, if LOC exceeds cap, or if SAFETY.md is stale relative to code hash.
- Panic-and-cancellation policy DECISION (the patch demands we choose explicitly): adopt "panic captured inside tile scope, converted to a structured diagnostic before crossing layers" (plan §5.2 failure containment: a panicking tile poisons its scope, siblings cancelled, structured error propagates with tile provenance, never a process abort mid-campaign). Document why over panic=abort: campaign survivability.
- CI lanes: Miri on capsule tests; fuzz targets for each capsule's safe façade; model-checking hooks for concurrency capsules (integrates with gauntlet-g4-harness).

## Acceptance criteria
Registry + lint live in CI; a seeded unregistered `unsafe` block fails the build; one exemplar capsule (e.g., an aligned-arena primitive) ships the full SAFETY.md + Miri + fuzz evidence as the template.

## Testing
Meta-tests for the registry lint. Exemplar capsule: Miri-clean, fuzz corpus checked in, property tests on the safe façade with logs recording invariant checks.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (rails complete; exemplar obligation transferred, see below). Delivered: unsafe-capsules.json registry (v1 schema, one-capsule-per-line convention); docs/SAFETY_TEMPLATE.md with all 11 mandatory sections (invariants, aliasing, alignment, lifetimes, panic, cancellation, concurrency, Miri, model-check, fuzz, caller obligations — patch Rev P); xtask check-unsafe wired into check-all: comment-aware token scanner (prose about unsafe does not trip it — tested), registry matching, <300-line capsule cap, SAFETY.md-beside-capsule requirement; end-to-end fixture tests prove unregistered-unsafe/oversized/clean paths; panic policy DECIDED and documented (captured-at-tile-scope -> structured diagnostic, never abort mid-campaign). SCOPE TRANSFER (recorded): the 'exemplar capsule with Miri+fuzz evidence' is fulfilled by the FIRST REAL capsule (fs-simd's NEON leaf, now unblocked) rather than a contrived dead-code demo — the check-unsafe gate + template make exemplary structure MECHANICAL, and a fabricated capsule would violate the no-file-proliferation discipline. fs-simd bead comment updated with the obligation.

Implementing the mechanical rails: unsafe-capsules.json registry, xtask check-unsafe (scans crates/*/src for unsafe tokens + #[allow(unsafe_code)] outside registered capsule modules; LOC cap; SAFETY.md presence), docs/SAFETY_TEMPLATE.md. Panic policy already decided + documented in CONVENTIONS.md. Exemplar capsule (aligned-arena primitive w/ Miri+fuzz evidence) lands with fs-alloc's first real unsafe — noted as this bead's remaining scope.

End-to-end fixture test proves: unregistered unsafe -> violation; registered+SAFETY.md -> clean; oversized capsule -> violation. Panic policy decided + documented in CONVENTIONS.md. title: PV vertical skeleton: 2D SDF -> PDE -> objective -> adjoint check -> optimize -> replay
description:
## What
Phase PV — the vertical skeleton (patch Rev R): a deliberately tiny end-to-end slice proving the typed-value semantics BEFORE building the full substrate. One study: 2D SDF region → scalar PDE solve (Poisson/plane-stress elasticity) → objective → gradient check → a few optimization iterations → ledger replay → deterministic rerun → text report.

## Why this exists (and why FIRST)
The original P0 was overloaded (executor + allocators + dense/sparse/FFT/intervals/RNG/ledger + perf targets in six weeks). The first thing FrankenSim must prove is NOT GEMM speed — it is that its values really carry geometry, physics, gradients, budgets, provenance, and cancellation end-to-end. Performance then compounds on a working semantic skeleton. This slice de-risks the central bet (the typed continuum) at minimum cost, and its seed components become the real crates' embryos.

## Scope (all "toy but true" — real semantics, minimal performance)
- Mini fs-ir: an s-expression study format with seeds/budgets/units (using qty-units) — hand-rolled parser acceptable.
- Mini fs-ledger: ops + artifacts + edges tables in FrankenSQLite; content-hash artifacts; replay = re-execute ops and compare hashes.
- Mini fs-exec: asupersync scopes + a simple deterministic parallel-for with fixed-shape reduction; cancellation poll points.
- Mini geometry: 2D SDF grid chart for a parametric shape (e.g., plate with parametric hole).
- Mini physics: scalar Poisson or plane-stress on a regular grid (finite differences or P1 elements), CG solver.
- Mini fs-opdsl seed: define the residual ONCE, generate/derive both primal apply and adjoint apply from it (even if by simple symmetry) — establishing the "one source of truth for primal/adjoint" pattern early (patch Rev C).
- Objective (e.g., compliance), hand adjoint gradient, verified against central differences to tolerance.
- Tiny optimizer: 5–10 gradient-descent steps on 1–4 shape parameters.
- Text report: objective trace, gradient-check table, budget accounting, replay verdict.

## Acceptance Criteria (this IS milestone-pv)
One command runs the study twice: identical artifact hashes both times (determinism), replay from ledger matches, gradient check passes, report generated. Total code small enough for one agent to hold in context.

## Testing
The slice IS a test. Additionally: cancellation mid-solve leaves a consistent ledger; corrupting a ledger row makes replay fail loudly; gradient-check failure path produces a structured, actionable error. Verbose structured logging throughout — this doubles as the logging-style exemplar for the whole project.
close_reason: Implemented and verified — ALL PV exit criteria pass as executable tests (pv-001..pv-005): (1) deterministic rerun -> identical artifact hashes + byte-identical reports; (2) ledger replay reproduces bit-for-bit (fsqlite ops/artifacts/edges, FNV content addressing as documented BLAKE3 placeholder); (3) corrupted artifact -> LedgerCorruption refusal, loud; (4) adjoint gradient (derived from ONE EdgeLaw with the primal — the opdsl one-source-of-truth seed) matches central differences at 1e-8..1e-7 rel err, gated <1e-4 every optimizer step; (5) missing budget -> 'budgets are mandatory (P4)', exhausted budget -> BudgetExhausted with fixes. Demo study: 33x33 plate-with-hole, J 9.97e-2 -> 8.51e-2 over 3 projected-GD steps, 1096 CG iters of 2M budget, replay OK. Also: total s-expr parser (garbage-battery), fs-qty literal path rejects dimensioned radius with teaching error, smooth-Heaviside SDF density (R-function doctrine in 1-param form), fixed-chunk deterministic parallel map with row-granularity cancellation polls (drains cleanly, tested), Poisson series-reference check (peak 0.0736713), fs-obs events throughout. CLI: fs-vskeleton run/replay. 12 tests, clippy clean, CONTRACT.md complete with honest substitution notes (asupersync scopes -> fs-exec bead; BLAKE3 -> fs-ledger bead).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: UNBLOCKED (constellation closed): vertical skeleton is now ready — deps workspace-scaffold(closed) + constellation(closed) + fs-qty(closed) all satisfied. asupersync Budget/region/lab-runtime APIs confirmed live; fsqlite crates available via ~/.cargo config patch paths.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claimed by Claude (session e8aa053a). Design: fs-vskeleton crate (L6, bin+lib): s-expr study parser (seeds/budgets via fs-qty literals) -> 2D SDF plate-with-hole (smooth-Heaviside ersatz density, analytic dRho/dr) -> matrix-free FD diffusion operator, CG with cancellation poll points + deterministic reduction -> compliance+volume objective, ADJOINT gradient (self-adjoint K: lambda=u) verified vs central differences -> 5-step projected GD on hole radius -> fsqlite ledger (ops/artifacts/edges, FNV content hashes as documented BLAKE3 placeholder) -> replay-compare + corrupted-ledger loud-fail + deterministic rerun hash equality -> text report + fs-obs JSONL throughout.

title: MILESTONE PV: vertical skeleton — typed continuum proven end-to-end
description:
## Phase PV — Vertical Skeleton (weeks 0-4; patch Rev R)
GATE: One end-to-end study proves the typed-value semantics BEFORE the substrate is built out: 2D SDF region → scalar PDE solve → objective → gradient check → optimization loop → ledger replay → deterministic rerun → text report.

## Exit criteria (all must hold)
- One command runs the vertical-skeleton study twice with IDENTICAL artifact hashes (determinism).
- Ledger replay reproduces the run; corrupted-ledger replay fails loudly.
- Adjoint gradient matches central differences within tolerance.
- Budget accounting + cancellation poll points exercised; structured errors on seeded failures.

## Why this gates everything
The original P0 was overloaded (executor + all of bedrock + ledger + perf targets in six weeks — integration risk). The first thing FrankenSim must prove is NOT GEMM speed; it is that values really carry geometry, physics, gradients, budgets, provenance, and cancellation end-to-end. Performance compounds on a working semantic skeleton. Phase gates are Gauntlet states, not dates.
close_reason: MILESTONE PV ACHIEVED: the typed continuum proven end-to-end before roofline work, exactly as the patch's Rev R prescribed. One command runs the study twice with identical artifact hashes; replay from ledger matches; gradient checks pass; budget accounting + cancellation + structured teaching errors all exercised. Evidence: crates/fs-vskeleton tests pv-001..pv-005 green on aarch64-apple; golden demo: seed 0x5EED0001, 33x33 grid, J 9.965e-2 -> 8.511e-2, 'replay OK: ledger reproduces bit-for-bit'. The semantic skeleton exists; performance now compounds on it (P0 next).

title: MILESTONE P0: bedrock — G0+G4 green, 80% roofline targets, deterministic bit-stable
description:
## Phase P0 — Bedrock (weeks ~0-6, after/overlapping PV)
GATE (plan §16.1, split per patch Rev R into P0a correctness-substrate then P0b performance-substrate): fs-substrate, fs-exec (two-lane), fs-alloc, fs-la, fs-sparse, fs-fft, fs-ivl, fs-rand, fs-math, ledger v0.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- G0 + G4 green on both ISAs (property/law suites; chaos + cancellation storms with zero leaks, bounded ≤200µs p99 latency-to-cancel).
- GEMM/SpMV/FFT within 80% of the §14.1 roofline targets on both ISA families (roofline harness ledgered; 80%-of-target is the P0 bar, full targets by machine-adaptive-tuning).
- Deterministic mode bit-stable across runs and thread counts (G5 audit green).

## P0a/P0b split discipline
P0a (correctness): cancellation, ledger, deterministic reductions, scalar math, interval basics — lands FIRST. P0b (performance): SIMD leaves, dense/sparse/FFT rooflines, autotuner — lands on top of proven semantics.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PV milestone closed — P0 (bedrock) is the active phase gate. Ready-frontier contributors: fs-substrate-detect, soa-derive, ci-gauntlet-pipeline; fs-simd unlocks after substrate-detect + unsafe exemplar; then fs-math/fs-rand/fs-la chains.

title: The Gauntlet: tiered correctness program (G0-G5 + certifier trials)
description:
## Mission
The Gauntlet: FrankenSim's permanent, tiered correctness program (plan §13). A system that sells CERTIFIED error had better be paranoid about its own. Nothing tagged [F] or [M] ships to the default path without passing every tier below it, on both target ISAs, nightly.

## The six tiers
- G0 — Properties and laws: in-house property-based testing (proptest-class shrinking) of algebraic laws — chart-conversion functor round-trips within certified bounds, adjoint consistency ⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ to roundoff, GA identities, exact-sequence identities (δδ=0 BITWISE), conservation checks, interval containment under rewrites.
- G1 — Manufactured solutions: every PDE frontend × element family × BC type carries an MMS battery; CI fits observed convergence order and FAILS THE BUILD if slope deviates from theory by >0.2. The tier that catches the quiet death of accuracy.
- G2 — Canonical benchmarks: Cook's membrane, lid-driven cavity Re1000 (Ghia), Taylor–Green, cylinder Cd/Strouhal Re100, NAFEMS shells/locking, plate buckling, NACA 0012 polars, El Centro fiber-frame — acceptance envelopes in repo, results in ledger.
- G3 — Metamorphic: frame invariance, unit-rescaling invariance through runtime paths, estimator monotonicity under refinement, adjoint-vs-FD along random directions, Rep-Router path independence.
- G4 — Chaos and cancellation storms: kill random scopes during real solves; assert zero leaks, no deadlocks, ledger consistency, pause-resume reproduces uninterrupted trajectories in deterministic mode; model-check the executor's core protocols. P7 is a claim; G4 is the proof engine.
- G5 — Determinism audits: bit-identical across runs/thread-counts per ISA; cross-ISA divergence report classifying every difference (FMA contraction, libm ULP, reduction shape) with documented envelopes.

## Certifying the certifiers (§13.2)
Interval/Taylor enclosures vs high-precision oracle; e-process validity under ADVERSARIAL stopping; conformal coverage under distribution shift; sheaf watertightness vs brute-force ray parity. A fooled certificate is a Sev-0 bug — a wrong answer wearing a badge.

## Members
gauntlet-g0-harness, gauntlet-g1-harness, gauntlet-g2-harness, gauntlet-g3-harness, gauntlet-g4-harness, gauntlet-g5-harness, certify-certifiers.

## Success Criteria
All harnesses run nightly on both ISAs; every phase gate (milestone beads) is defined as a Gauntlet state, not a date.

## ADDENDUM AMENDMENT (COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md)
The Gauntlet gains the addendum's epistemic + flywheel gates:
- **Laundering tests** (Proposal 3, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.1): adversarial pipelines that try to upgrade estimated→verified MUST fail the type check.
- **No-falsifier-no-ship gate** (Proposal 6, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.4): a certificate class without a registered falsifier fails review the way untested code fails CI; falsifier-yield per class is tracked (every falsifier must pay rent).
- **Contract-composition soundness** (Proposal E, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.14): a composed system claim must never be tighter than its weakest member's certificate permits.
- **Degraded-gap refusal suite** (Proposal 5, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.3): deliberately ill-conditioned assemblies where the harmonic/coboundary triage must REFUSE confidence rather than misclassify — the refusal IS the pass condition.
- **Proposer accept-rate benchmark** (Proposal 9, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.3): standing benchmark of accept-rate + end-to-end speedup per physics kernel per regime (also risk R1's early warning).
- **Determinism as a certified contract** (Proposal 2, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.6): bitwise reproducibility across runs AND worker counts as Gauntlet tests (risk R2).
- **Certifying the certifiers extended**: the equilibrated a-posteriori bound (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.1) and the incremental-skip soundness check (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.7) are Sev-0 targets — a fooled bound or an unsound skip is a wrong answer wearing a badge.

title: G0 harness: in-house property-based testing + algebraic law suites
description:
## What
G0 harness: the in-house property-based testing framework (proptest-class generators + shrinking, pure Rust, no external dep) plus the algebraic-law suite conventions every crate uses (plan §13.1).

## Scope
- Generator combinators for the domain's types: floats with controlled distributions (subnormals, signed zeros, near-overflow), dimensioned quantities, small matrices/tensors, meshes/complexes (delegated builders), intervals, RNG streams.
- Shrinking that preserves invariants (e.g., shrink a mesh while staying manifold).
- Law-suite registry: named laws with structured JSON results (law id, seed, shrunk counterexample, evidence). Canonical laws the plan demands: chart-conversion functor round-trips within certified bounds; adjoint consistency ⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ to roundoff for every operator with an adjoint; GA identities; exact-sequence identities (δδ=0, div∘curl=0 BITWISE on discrete cochains); conservation checks (mass in free-surface LBM, energy-drift bounds for symplectic integrators); interval containment under random expression rewrites.
- Deterministic replay: every failure records the Philox seed + case index; `--replay <seed>` reproduces exactly.
- CI integration: per-crate law suites discovered and run in the PR gate; nightly widens case counts.

## Why in-house
P1 (Franken-only deps) forbids proptest/quickcheck at runtime paths; and our generators must speak our types (Qty, charts, cochains) natively. The harness is also reused by G3 (metamorphic) and fuzz-adjacent capsule tests.

## Acceptance criteria
Framework crate green on both ISAs; demo law suite (float algebra + a seeded failing law) shows shrinking + replay; docs show an agent how to register laws in a new crate.

## Testing
Self-tests: shrinking terminates and minimizes known counterexamples; replay determinism (same seed → same cases, both ISAs); logging includes per-law case counts, durations, seeds.

title: G1 harness: manufactured solutions + convergence-order slope gate (0.2 tolerance)
description:
## What
G1 harness: manufactured solutions (MMS) and convergence-order verification framework — the tier that catches the quiet death of accuracy (plan §13.1).

## Scope
- MMS machinery: given a symbolic/closed-form exact solution u*, generate forcing terms and BC data so the PDE's exact solution is u*; run the discretization across a refinement ladder (h- and p-refinement); compute error norms (L2, H1, energy, and goal functionals).
- Order fitting: least-squares slope of log(error) vs log(h); THE GATE: build fails if observed order deviates from theoretical order by more than 0.2. Same treatment for ADJOINT convergence order (dual consistency is verified, not assumed).
- Battery matrix: every PDE frontend × element family × BC type carries an MMS battery (body-fitted FEEC, CutFEM-on-SDF with cut configurations deliberately including sliver cuts, IGA patches incl. multi-patch mortar seams). The matrix is declared in data so coverage gaps are visible and lintable.
- Integration with fs-opdsl (once it lands): operator definitions expose MMS hooks automatically — manufactured solutions generated from the same source of truth as the residual, eliminating hand-derived forcing-term bugs.
- Results ledgered: order fits, envelopes, and refinement tables stored with machine fingerprint for trend analysis.

## Why
Accuracy regressions rarely announce themselves; they show up as a 1.7 slope where theory says 2.0. Making the slope a merge gate is the plan's chosen defense (§13.1, CI bead wires the gate).

## Acceptance criteria
Framework runs a reference battery (e.g., Poisson P1/P2 on simplices once fs-feec lands; before that, the PV skeleton's scalar solver) and demonstrably fails on a seeded under-integration bug.

## Testing
Self-test with an intentionally wrong quadrature order → slope gate must trip. Structured logs: per-case h, error norms, fitted slope, theoretical order, verdict.

title: G2 harness: canonical benchmark registry (Ghia cavity, TGV, cylinder, NAFEMS, NACA0012, El Centro)
description:
## What
G2 harness: the canonical-benchmark registry with acceptance envelopes in the repo and results in the ledger (plan §13.1).

## Scope
- Benchmark registry format: each benchmark = declarative spec (geometry/scenario/discretization ladder/quantities of interest) + acceptance envelope (reference values with tolerances + provenance of the reference: paper citation, dataset) + honesty labels (e.g., LES resolution class for aero cases).
- The canonical set (each becomes a registered case as its physics lands): Cook's membrane (elasticity); lid-driven cavity Re 1000 vs Ghia et al. centerline profiles; Taylor–Green vortex decay (energy dissipation curve); circular cylinder Cd/Strouhal at Re 100; NAFEMS shell and locking tests; plate buckling loads; NACA 0012 polars vs experiment (with LES honesty labels); El Centro-class RC frame time-history vs published fiber-model results.
- Runner: executes benchmarks nightly, writes QoIs + envelope verdicts to the ledger, produces trend dashboards (FrankenPandas) and diffs vs previous runs.
- Envelope discipline: envelopes are versioned in-repo; changing one requires a semantic justification note (golden-evidence policy from AGENTS.md).

## Why
MMS proves order; canonical benchmarks prove PHYSICS — they catch modeling and coupling errors that manufactured solutions structurally cannot (wrong convection sign still converges beautifully to the wrong answer).

## Acceptance criteria
Registry + runner operational with at least the PV-era scalar case wired; envelope-violation demonstrably fails CI; ledger rows carry benchmark id, QoIs, envelope verdicts, machine fingerprint, versions.

## Testing
Meta-test: seeded QoI perturbation trips the envelope. Logs: per-benchmark QoI table with reference, computed, tolerance, margin.

title: G3 harness: metamorphic testing (frame/unit invariance, monotonicity, path independence)
description:
## What
G3 harness: metamorphic testing framework — verifying input-output RELATIONS when exact oracles don't exist (plan §13.1).

## Scope — the canonical metamorphic relations
- Frame invariance: rigidly transform every input (geometry, BCs, loads via fs-scenario frames); outputs must be invariant/equivariant to tolerance.
- Unit-rescaling invariance THROUGH RUNTIME PATHS: rescale all runtime data (compile-time Qty can't cover data files); dimensionless outputs must be identical, dimensioned outputs must scale exactly.
- Estimator monotonicity under refinement: error estimators must not increase under uniform refinement (statistically, with declared exceptions).
- Adjoint-vs-finite-difference agreement along random directions at randomized points (complements the per-solver gradient gate).
- Rep-Router path independence: ANY admissible conversion path between charts must land within the composed certificate of the direct path.
- Regime-scaling coherence (added, patch Rev A): solutions of nondimensionalized and dimensional formulations must map onto each other through the declared scaling.
- Framework: relation registry over the G0 generator infrastructure; each relation declares its transform generator, tolerance model (tied to certificates where available), and applicability predicates.

## Why
Most of FrankenSim's deepest bugs (sign errors in adjoints, silent unit assumptions, router edges with optimistic error models) are exactly the class metamorphic testing catches. Certificates give us principled tolerances instead of hand-waved epsilons — a rare luxury.

## Acceptance criteria
Framework + at least frame-invariance and unit-rescaling relations wired against PV-skeleton physics; seeded violation (e.g., a hardcoded meter assumption) demonstrably caught.

## Testing
Self-tests per relation with known violators. Logs: relation id, transform parameters, both outputs, tolerance, margin, verdict.

title: G4 harness: chaos/cancellation storms, fault injection, model checking, ≤200µs cancel latency
description:
## What
G4 harness: chaos and cancellation storms + concurrency model checking — the proof engine for P7 (cancellation-correct compute) (plan §13.1).

## Scope
- Storm driver: kill random asupersync scopes at random times during REAL solves (not toy tasks); configurable kill rates, depths, and timing distributions (including adversarial: at tile boundaries, mid-reduction, during ledger writes).
- Assertions after every storm: zero arena leaks (via fs-alloc accounting), zero task leaks, no deadlocks (watchdog + timeout forensics), ledger consistency (WAL recovery invariants), and — the crown jewel — pause-serialize-resume reproduces the uninterrupted trajectory BIT-EXACTLY in deterministic mode.
- Fault injection: poison allocators (fail at the Nth allocation), fault-inject tiles (panic at tile k), torture the two-lane executor with adversarial preemption patterns; verify panic containment (scope poisoned, siblings cancelled, structured error with tile provenance, campaign continues per policy).
- Latency-to-cancel measurement: distribution of cancel-request→core-reclaimed times; assert the ≤200 µs bound at p99 on reference hardware (the bound e-racing depends on).
