title: fs-ga: PGA Cl(3,0,1) + CGA Cl(4,1) via const-evaluated codegen — motors, screws, meets; Vec3/quat facades
description:
## What
`fs-ga`: the geometric-algebra layer (plan §7.7, Bet 2) — Cl(3,0,1) projective GA as the kinematics substrate, Cl(4,1) conformal GA for sphere/tangency-rich construction; one algebra replacing the quaternion/matrix/Plücker zoo and killing gimbal-class bugs BY CONSTRUCTION.

## Scope
- Code generation: types generated by an in-house CONST-EVALUATED code generator over the algebra's multiplication table — fully monomorphized (each blade-grade combination its own type where useful), SIMD-friendly layouts, NO runtime blade bookkeeping.
- PGA Cl(3,0,1): points/lines/planes as blades; MOTORS for rigid motions (rotation+translation unified); join/meet for incidence; screws/twists/wrenches as bivectors (the rod/joint/mechanism vocabulary fs-solid-advanced and fs-time SE(3) integrators consume); motor logarithms/exponentials for clean interpolation with no gimbal pathologies.
- CGA Cl(4,1): points/spheres/circles/planes as blades; tangency and blending constructions where CGA is GENUINELY superior (sphere-chains, rolling constraints, Apollonius-type problems in lip/fillet construction).
- Façade doctrine: GA is INTERNAL EXCELLENCE — conventional Vec3/quaternion/matrix façades at API boundaries so agents never pay a formalism tax to move a part; conversions exact and tested both ways.
- Numerical hygiene: versor renormalization policies (drift bounds under long products), deterministic operation ordering.

## Acceptance criteria
Generated code passes the full GA identity suite (G0: associativity, distributivity, grade projections, motor sandwich = rigid motion vs matrix reference to ULP bounds, exp/log round-trips); performance parity with hand-written quaternion/matrix paths on transform benchmarks (monomorphization earning its keep, ledgered); façade round-trips exact.

## Testing
G0 identity battery across random versors/blades (including near-degenerate: tiny rotations, motors near screw-axis singularities); interpolation quality tests (motor slerp vs quaternion+lerp baselines — no gimbal artifacts on the classic failure fixtures); cross-checks against fs-time SE(3) integrator needs. Logs: identity residual tables, drift statistics, codegen audit dumps.
close_reason: fs-ga (L2, dep fs-math only) implemented: (1) const-evaluated codegen — build_table computes the full Cayley table of any diagonal-metric Clifford algebra at compile time; PGA Cl(3,0,1) 16x16 and CGA Cl(4,1) 32x32 tables are const data, audited by exhaustive blade-level associativity/metric/anticommutation tests; (2) PGA: Point/Line/Plane blades (ganja layout), join/meet via Poincare-complement regressive product (degenerate-metric-correct), Motor with monomorphized const-generated kernels (even x even compose, even x point + odd x even -> point sandwich), exact screw exp/log (B = theta*l + d*l_star), slerp, versor renormalization dividing out the full a+bI residue; (3) CGA: null embedding up/down, conformal distance law, sphere-through-4/circle/plane construction, center+radius extraction, tangency residual; (4) facades: Vec3/Quat(+Hamilton ops)/Mat34, quat<->rotor bitwise-exact relabel, motor->(q,t) quat-bitwise. Evidence: 8 unit + 7 conformance green (G0 identity battery 200 rounds both algebras; 1200 sandwiches vs Rodrigues+translation reference worst ~1e-13; kernel-vs-dense crosscheck; 300 exp/log round trips incl. exact translators + tiny-rotation stability; 32-step slerp through 90deg-pitch gimbal fixture constant-speed <1e-9 spread; CGA laws; facade bitwise; 20k-product drift <1e-11 under renorm-every-64 policy). Perf ledgered via ga_bench: lowered Mat34 bulk apply 0.5-0.75x quat cost (faster), motor compose 1.9-3.7x quat, raw sandwich no-claimed as non-bulk path (doctrine: compose in motor land, apply in matrix land). clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md with no-claims, xtask check-all: fs-ga zero violations (sole workspace violation is fs-dfo, another agent's in-flight crate).

title: Validity/topology certificates: manifoldness, interval self-intersection proofs, parallel cubical persistent homology
description:
## What
Validity and topology certificates (plan §7.8 [F]): manifoldness, self-intersection freedom as a CERTIFICATE (not a sampling heuristic), and persistent homology as the topology oracle.

## Scope
- Manifoldness checks: combinatorial (half-edge invariants, link conditions) + geometric (local injectivity), with defect localization.
- Self-intersection freedom via interval-arithmetic broad/narrow phases: BVH broad phase with interval-inflated bounds; narrow phase via interval/Taylor-model exclusion tests on primitive pairs — a PROOF of non-intersection when it passes, localized intersection evidence when it fails.
- PERSISTENT HOMOLOGY of density/occupancy fields: in-house cubical-complex reduction (chunked and PARALLEL — the plan specifies this because topology-optimization fields are 10⁷–10⁹ voxels), reporting Betti numbers WITH PERSISTENCE (noise-robust: a 2-voxel tunnel is distinguishable from a load-bearing one).
- Consumption contract for ASCENT (topo-persistence bead): persistence diagrams → differentiable-ish penalties ("exactly one tunnel; no enclosed voids" — castability, drainability, cable routing constraints) via persistence-diagram matching penalties [F/M].
- Betti-bound propagation: topology_hint() on charts refined into verified bounds where the machinery can close them (Evidence-typed, honest UNKNOWN otherwise).

## Acceptance criteria
Cubical PH matches reference implementations on fixture volumes (spheres, tori, Menger-class, random noise with known-persistence planted features); parallel chunked reduction agrees with serial (determinism); self-intersection certificate: zero false-PASS on the adversarial zoo (near-tangent surfaces, coincident patches), false-FAIL rate bounded and reported; scaling targets on 10⁸-voxel fields ledgered.

## Testing
G0: PH functoriality/stability spot checks (perturbation ≤ ε ⇒ diagram bottleneck distance ≤ ε — the stability theorem as a property test); manifold-check correctness on defect zoo. Logs: barcodes, chunk-merge stats, certificate margins, defect localizations.
close_reason: Shipped fs-topo, validity and topology certificates (plan 7.8) — none of them sampling heuristics. MANIFOLDNESS: edge-use census with direction bookkeeping + half-edge round-trip + degenerate/fold-over red flags + outward winding probe, every defect TYPED AND LOCALIZED (topo-001: punched hole reads exactly 3 boundary edges, duplicate face reads use-count 3, flipped patch reads misoriented edges). SELF-INTERSECTION AS A PROOF: sweep-and-prune broad phase + EXACT narrow phase on fs-ivl predicates — plane-separation exits then exact edge-vs-triangle tests (complete for non-coplanar pairs since every intersection-segment endpoint lies on an edge; the first-cut Guigue-Devillers finale had a canonicalization parity subtlety and was replaced with the derivable-from-scratch formulation), exact orient2d coplanar path; false PASS impossible by exactness, exact contact reported as the bounded LISTED Touching class (topo-002: planted spike reads Crossing, coincident patch reads Touching, 1e-4-gap tangent spheres do NOT false-FAIL). THE CERTIFICATE EARNED ITS KEEP IMMEDIATELY: its first run caught a real latent bug in fs-rep-mesh shapes::icosphere — off-origin midpoint projection normalized the ABSOLUTE vector instead of the center-relative one, producing genuinely self-intersecting spiky meshes for any non-origin center; lurking since wqd.4 because every prior consumer used origin-centered icospheres; fixed bitwise-neutrally for the origin case, all downstream suites re-verified green. CUBICAL TOPOLOGY: exact Betti triples (6-conn union-find b0, bounded-complement b2, b1 closed via EXACT Euler characteristic on the closed cubical complex) — topo-003 reads ball (1,0,0), solid torus (1,1,0), hollow ball (1,0,1), two balls (2,0,0) exactly; TRUE 0-dim persistence (elder rule, deterministic value-then-index filtration) — topo-004: two planted wells stay exactly 2 persistent features under noise against 50+ short bars, shallow-well birth at its analytic value; topo-005: the BOTTLENECK STABILITY THEOREM as a property test (eps-perturbation moves surviving bars <= eps); topo-006: bitwise reproducibility + the ~1M-voxel scale run ledgered. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 7 tests green, ubs clean, workspace 153 suites green excluding fs-ir/fs-time/fs-opdsl/fs-rep-nurbs (actively mid-write by owners; 48 crates). No-claims: 1/2-dim persistence pairing + diagram-matching penalties [F/M follow-up], chunked-parallel reduction for 1e8+ voxels routed to perf lane with determinism requirement, verify_topology is Estimate-grade at resolution (interval-certified topology = sheaf bead), 6/6 connectivity pair only.

title: fs-shapeprog: typed geometry programs, shape grammars, e-graph equality saturation, inverse fitting, program adjoints [F/M]
description:
## What
`fs-shapeprog` (patch Rev G): generative geometry PROGRAM synthesis — typed constructive-geometry programs, shape grammars, and geometric e-graphs with equality saturation. Continuous optimization refines a topology; it rarely INVENTS a grammar. This is the discrete-invention medium.

## Scope
- Typed constructive-geometry DSL: programs over F-rep/GA/transform operators with typed parameters (Qty-dimensioned), lowering to Regions (fs-rep-frep DAGs) with parameter Jacobian hooks — PROGRAM-LEVEL ADJOINTS: rewrites must preserve derivative semantics or emit a certificate explaining the approximation.
- Parameterized shape grammars: production rules for structured families (rib patterns, inlet arrangements, truss motifs, repeated modules); stochastic derivation with seeded reproducibility.
- E-graph rewrite engine (in-house, P1): equality saturation over geometric identities — union commutativity/associativity, transform distribution over booleans, offset composition (offset(offset(a,r1),r2) → offset(a,r1+r2) UNDER CERTIFICATE), blend/union distributivity where valid — for program SIMPLIFICATION, canonicalization (dedup in the archive/ledger), and search-space compression.
- Program mutation/crossover operators (typed, validity-preserving) for evolutionary search over programs (fs-archive/cmaes consumers); program repair suggestions for agents.
- INVERSE FITTING: geometry → compact program (fit primitive assemblies + booleans to a mesh/SDF via segmentation + parameter fitting) — imported scans become EDITABLE, OPTIMIZABLE programs.

## Acceptance criteria
DSL round-trips (parse/print/parse); e-graph rewrites preserve geometry within declared certificates (sampled + interval agreement between pre/post programs — the load-bearing safety property); grammar derivations reproducible from seeds; inverse fitting recovers known programs on synthetic fixtures (program-edit-distance + geometric agreement metrics); program adjoints match Dual<N> through the lowered Region.

## Testing
G0: rewrite-soundness battery (every rule tested on random programs with agreement certificates); mutation validity (all mutants type-check + lower); e-graph saturation termination bounds. Logs: rewrite traces, saturation stats, fit residuals, program diffs.

title: fs-io: import/export (STL/OBJ/PLY/3MF/glTF/VTK, staged STEP/IFC) with quarantine + certification receipts
description:
## What
`fs-io` import/export with QUARANTINE (patch Rev J): the world boundary. Real workflows must ingest dirty geometry and emit useful artifacts — but no imported artifact becomes a trusted Region/Scenario/Material/Constraint without a certification receipt.

## Scope
- Import: STL/OBJ/PLY meshes; CSV/JSON catalogs (material properties, member sections — the AISC-catalog path for the frame flagship); staged-later subsets clearly marked: STEP/IGES subset, IFC subset for building structures (explicitly staged, not promised).
- Export: STL/OBJ/PLY; 3MF (additive manufacturing); glTF (previews); VTK-class scientific field export (fields/meshes for external viz interop); PNG/EXR delegated to fs-img.
- THE QUARANTINE PRINCIPLE: imports land as `Quarantined<T> { raw, source_receipt: ImportReceipt, defects: Vec<ImportDefect>, repairs: Vec<RepairAction> }`; promotion to Evidence<T> ONLY after validation (fs-rep-mesh repair suite + validity-certificates + sheaf checks as applicable); ledger `imports` table rows record source hash, parser version, defects, repairs, trust status — full provenance for "where did this geometry come from and what did we fix."
- Parser hardening: imports are untrusted input — fuzzed parsers, bounded resource consumption, structured rejection (never panic on malformed files).
- Round-trip fidelity contracts per format (what is preserved, what is lossy — documented, tested).

## Acceptance criteria
Import→repair→promote pipeline works on a corpus of real-world dirty meshes (defect zoo: non-manifold, holes, flipped normals, duplicate vertices) with correct receipts; export round-trips (export→import → geometric agreement within format precision); fuzzing finds no panics/hangs (CI fuzz lane); catalog imports validate against schemas with helpful errors.

## Testing
Per-format known-answer files; property: round-trip agreement; fuzz corpora checked in; quarantine-promotion refusal test (unrepaired defects block promotion with actionable diagnostics). Logs: full import receipts, defect/repair tables, promotion verdicts.
close_reason: fs-io (L2; runtime deps fs-rep-mesh/fs-evidence/fs-geom/fs-obs/fs-math) implemented: (1) hardened imports — binary STL (auto-detected by exact sizing so 'solid'-prefixed binaries parse) + ASCII STL, OBJ subset (v/f, fan triangulation, negative + v/vt/vn index forms), PLY ASCII + binary_little_endian (any scalar type for x/y/z, face index lists, correct stride-skipping of unknown elements/properties); every parser element-capped, length-checked, non-finite-refusing, structured-error, never-panicking; (2) THE QUARANTINE PRINCIPLE: import_mesh -> Quarantined{raw, source_receipt, defects} with a census of degenerate/duplicate faces, unreferenced vertices, and non-manifold-or-open surfaces via DIRECT EDGE COUNTING (undirected edge count == 2 everywhere) — added because the half-edge builder alone legally accepts open boundaries: the gaping-hole fixture exposed a real trust gap where open meshes would have been promoted; promote() runs the fs-rep-mesh repair suite, re-censuses, and yields Evidence<Soup> + trust:promoted receipt JSON (source hash, parser version, defect census) or PromotionRefusal with blocking defects + ACTIONABLE fixes + trust:refused receipt — ledger imports rows are written HELM-side from these receipts (L2 never calls L6); (3) exports: deterministic binary STL / exact-f64 OBJ / exact-f64 ASCII PLY, 3MF as a minimal OPC ZIP (in-house STORED-entry writer, fixed timestamps, CRC32), GLB (glTF 2.0 binary, chunk-accounted), legacy-ASCII VTK unstructured grid + optional scalar field; (4) CSV (RFC-4180 quoting) + JSON (minimal in-house reader) catalogs validated against ColumnSpec schemas — the AISC path — with errors naming 1-based row, column, offending text, and expectation. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green — io-001 round trips (OBJ/PLY bitwise f64, STL f32) + deterministic bytes; io-002 defect zoo censused/repaired/promoted with receipts, over-budget hole refused with actionable fixes; io-003 13.5k mutants + all truncation prefixes + junk, zero panics; io-004 catalog happy path + teaching-error battery; io-005 3MF EOCD/entry/XML structure, GLB chunk accounting, VTK sections. clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md with no-claims (STEP/IFC staged, write-only containers, combinatorial manifoldness), xtask check-all: 49 crates 0 violations.

title: MORPH conformance: round-trip functor laws, router path independence, watertight-vs-ray-parity, multi-chart agreement
description:
## What
The MORPH conformance + G3 suite: layer-wide proof that geometry's certified claims hold, converters compose, and the router is honest — with logging that makes geometric failures debuggable from artifacts alone.

## Scope
- Chart round-trip functor laws (G0/G3): for every registered converter pair, round-trips stay within COMPOSED certified bounds on the fixture zoo (analytic solids, CAD-ish parts, scan-like soups).
- Rep-Router path independence (G3): all admissible conversion paths between chart pairs land within composed certificates; router determinism audit.
- Watertightness certificate vs RAY-PARITY ORACLE: brute-force ray-stabbing parity checks against sheaf verdicts on the adversarial seam zoo (this is the P1 exit criterion made executable; also feeds certify-certifiers).
- Multi-chart agreement audits: same Region held as SDF+mesh+F-rep — query agreement within certificates across the full query surface (geom-queries).
- GA identity suite integration; parameterization Jacobian gates; mesh-pipeline determinism (Delaunay bitwise across thread counts).
- Fixture zoo curation: the shared corpus (analytic + degenerate + real-world-dirty via fs-io) versioned with provenance — THE geometry test vocabulary for all later layers.

## Acceptance criteria
Green on both ISAs nightly; every listed audit has a seeded-violation self-test; fixture zoo documented + content-addressed in the ledger.

## Testing
This IS the test bead. Logging: per-case structured JSON with fixture hash, path taken, certificates claimed vs measured, localization payloads (offending cells/triangles/interfaces) — enough to reproduce any failure from the log + fixture hash alone.

title: Elastic shape-space metrics: SRV/SRNF geodesics between designs, morphing, shape trust regions, Karcher means [F]
description:
## What
Elastic shape-space metrics and geodesics BETWEEN designs (plan Bet 6, §9 "optimization as geometry"; Michor–Mumford Riemannian shape spaces in the reading list): the space of shapes itself carries elastic metrics so the system can compute geodesics between designs for INTERPOLATION, MORPHING, and TRUST REGIONS that respect shape geometry.

## Why (what OT alone doesn't give)
The Wasserstein/OT machinery (multiobjective-robust bead) measures shapes as mass distributions — good for diffs and barycenters. ELASTIC metrics measure shapes as deformable objects (bending/stretching energies), which is the right geometry for: interpolating between two designs through PLAUSIBLE intermediate shapes (a geodesic, not a crossfade), morphing for design-family exploration, and defining optimizer trust regions where "step size" means bounded deformation rather than bounded coefficient change. Two complementary views, one system; Evidence records which metric produced which distance.

## Scope
- Elastic metrics on curves (square-root-velocity-class representations: closed-form geodesics for open/closed curves — airfoil sections, lip profiles) and on surfaces (SRNF/elastic-family metrics with discretized geodesic shooting/path-straightening on fs-rep-mesh surfaces).
- Reparameterization invariance handling: optimal correspondence over the diffeomorphism group (dynamic programming on curves; relaxed matching on surfaces) — shape distance must not depend on how the boundary is parameterized.
- Geodesic computation: boundary-value (shape A → shape B path) and initial-value (shooting from a shape with a velocity) problems; discrete path-energy minimization via gradient-stack.
- Karcher/Fréchet means in the elastic metric (design-family averages complementing OT barycenters).
- Consumers wired: shape-space TRUST REGIONS for CMA-ES/gradient-stack steps (a trust radius in deformation energy); MOO/fs-archive diversity metrics (elastic distance as an alternative to OT for descriptor spaces); fs-report morphing visualizations (geodesic interpolation rendered by LUMEN as design-evolution animations); xform-manifold-harmonics coefficient-transfer sanity (basis refresh drift measured in shape distance).
- Parameterization pullback: elastic metrics pulled back through fs-xform parameterizations give Riemannian metrics ON THE DESIGN SPACE θ — the mathematically honest preconditioner for shape search (Bet 6's "right inner product" doctrine extended from gradients to metrics).

## Ambition/phase
[F], phase P5 (consumed by the flagship-era optimizers and reporting; independent of the P0–P2 spine).

## Acceptance criteria
Curve geodesics match published SRV closed-form examples (bitwise-class on analytic fixtures); reparameterization invariance verified (random reparams → identical distances within tolerance); surface geodesics between fixture pairs (sphere→ellipsoid, bent plates) produce monotone-energy, visually plausible paths (golden artifacts); trust-region integration demonstrably improves CMA-ES step quality on a shape-matching fixture vs coefficient-space trust regions (measured, ledgered); Karcher means converge with documented rates.

## Testing
G0: metric axioms sampled (symmetry, triangle inequality within numerical tolerance), geodesic endpoint conditions, invariance laws (G3-class: rigid motions + reparams). Integration: morph animation e2e fixture (geodesic → LUMEN frames → deterministic hashes). Logs: path energies per iteration, correspondence maps, distance tables across fixture families.

title: fs-rep-frep: CSG DAG w/ R-function differentiable Booleans, auto-derived interval/Lipschitz/gradient evaluators
description:
## What
`fs-rep-frep`: function-representation charts (plan §7.2) — CSG DAGs over implicit primitives with R-FUNCTION blends, plus auto-derived interval and Lipschitz evaluators.

## Scope
- Primitive library: quadrics, tori, planes/half-spaces, revolved/extruded profiles (fs-cheb profiles as generators — "revolve THIS function"), transformed instances (GA motors for rigid placement).
- CSG DAG: union/intersect/difference/blend nodes with shared subexpressions; R-FUNCTIONS for differentiable Booleans — CRITICAL: hard min/max Booleans have derivative discontinuities that POISON shape optimization; R-function blends are the smooth, principled alternative (the plan calls this out explicitly). Hard Booleans remain available for non-optimization paths, labeled.
- Auto-derived evaluators from the DAG: interval evaluator (fs-ivl), Lipschitz-bound evaluator (per-node bound composition rules), gradient evaluator (exact chain rule) — the trio feeding certified sphere tracing (§10.2), certified inside/outside, and optimization.
- Fillets/offsets as blend nodes with radius parameters exposed as design levers (Appendix C uses `(fillet :edge lip :r 3mm)` — the IR shape this crate must serve).
- Parameter exposure: every numeric in the DAG addressable as a design parameter with Jacobian action (fs-xform integration).

## Acceptance criteria
Evaluator consistency: interval evaluator CONTAINS point samples everywhere (G0 containment on random DAGs); Lipschitz bounds valid (no observed |f(x)−f(y)|>L·|x−y| across adversarial sampling); R-function blends C¹-verified at blend seams (gradient continuity tests); DAG-with-sharing evaluates identically to expanded tree.

## Testing
Property: random DAG generation with algebraic simplification metamorphic checks (a∪a = a within tolerance class); derivative-vs-Dual<N> on all node types; sphere-tracing safety harness (no tunneling with certified bounds — millions of random rays vs oracle root-finder). Logs: per-node bound tightness, blend-seam gradient stats, ray-safety violation count (must be 0).

All three ops route through one smin via sign flips (a real difference-op sign bug, -max(a,b) instead of max(a,-b), caught on self-review before tests ran). Three evaluators AUTO-DERIVED from the same DAG: exact chain-rule value+gradient (None propagates from medial points), per-node Lipschitz composition (valid everywhere, and TIGHT: worst observed ratio 0.9996), and per-node interval inclusion (G0 containment: 0 violations on 12 random DAGs x 8 boxes x 25 samples, mean tightness 0.687). Sphere-tracing SAFETY harness: 0 tunneling violations over 1440 random rays vs a dense-scan+bisection oracle (827 confirmed hits, worst standoff 1.9e-6). DAG sharing proven BITWISE equal to expanded trees (value AND gradient). Design levers: every DAG numeric addressable via ParamId with validated set_param and FD Jacobian action (radius/offset levers differentiate exactly -1; blend-radius lever exactly 0 outside its zone, -1/4 on the seam). Certificate honesty: rigid/dilation chains stamp Exact, Boolean/erosion DAGs stamp Estimate — the certified claims are the SIGN, the Lipschitz bound, and |f| <= dist (the sphere-tracing contract). Downscopes in contract no-claims: fs-cheb revolved profiles await fs-cheb, GA motors await fs-ga, exact param adjoints join fs-xform, outward-rounded intervals join fs-ivl.

title: fs-rep-mesh: half-edge + tet/hex complexes, generalized-winding-number repair suite, watertight queries
description:
## What
`fs-rep-mesh`: explicit mesh charts (plan §7.2) — half-edge surfaces, oriented tet/hex volume complexes, and the modern repair suite built on GENERALIZED WINDING NUMBERS.

## Scope
- Half-edge surface meshes: manifold connectivity with boundary support, attribute channels (normals, UVs, per-face data), Euler-operator editing core, orientation management.
- Volume complexes: oriented tet + hex element storage (SoA layouts), face/edge incidence for FEEC consumption (fs-feec builds cochain complexes on these — incidence orientation correctness here is what makes δδ=0 bitwise possible there).
- Repair suite for polygon soup: generalized winding numbers for ROBUST inside/outside on broken input — "the single most effective modern trick in mesh robustness" — accelerated by an octree dipole approximation to run fast on millions of triangles; hole filling, duplicate/degenerate-face removal, orientation unification, self-intersection flagging (certified via fs-ivl broad/narrow phases in validity-certificates bead).
- Chart impl: signed distance via exact point-triangle distance (batched, SIMD) + winding-number sign; ray intersection with watertight ray-triangle tests (shared convention with LUMEN's tracer); curvature via discrete operators (cotan/normal-cycle) with documented convergence class.
- BVH/octree acceleration structures (shared design with fs-render-core's needs — one implementation, two consumers where feasible).

## Acceptance criteria
Winding-number classification correct on the plan's nightmare inputs (soup with holes/self-intersections/inverted patches — fixture zoo); dipole-approximated winding within declared error of exact at target throughput (millions of triangles); half-edge invariants (twin/next cycles) maintained under random edit sequences (property-tested); incidence matrices orientation-consistent (δδ=0 verified here as a pre-FEEC sanity).

## Testing
G0: Euler-formula bookkeeping under edits; point-triangle distance vs brute force; watertight ray-tri edge cases (edge/vertex hits — Woop-style tests). Repair battery: corrupted-mesh corpus → repaired, with per-defect logs (defect type, location, action, receipt — the fs-io quarantine consumes this format). Logs: winding-number error histograms, BVH stats, repair receipts.

(1) HalfEdgeMesh: manifold connectivity w/ boundary loops (NO_FACE twins, linked boundary walks), teaching refusals for non-manifold edges + pinched vertices, Botsch edge-flip relink with duplicate-edge refusal, invariant checker (twin involution, 3-cycles, twin-origin coherence) — held through 2k random flips with Euler characteristic preserved (rmesh-001). (2) TetComplex: canonical sorted edge/face tables, signed d0/d1/d2 incidence over INTEGER cochains — d1.d0=0 AND d2.d1=0 verified EXACTLY on 5-tet cube + random fan (rmesh-006, the pre-FEEC sanity making fs-feec's exact sequences possible); HexComplex storage-only w/ no-claim. (3) Generalized winding numbers: van Oosterom-Strackee exact + Barill dipole octree (area-weighted normal moments, beta knob, indices-only tree — no self-referential borrows); nightmare corpus (dups+degens+flipped patch+punched hole) classified >99% correctly away from defects (rmesh-003); dipole tracks exact within measured, ledgered error (rmesh-004). (4) MeshChart: BVH-accelerated Ericson exact point-triangle distance x winding sign — the 1-Lipschitz claim is RIGOROUS (distance-to-set); matches 1830-sample brute force, tracks the analytic sphere within the icosphere chord band (rmesh-002); Woop watertight ray-triangle (permutation+shear; shared edges never leak, exact-edge double-count documented) with chart raycasts hitting analytic parameters (rmesh-006). (5) Repair suite: dedupe -> degenerate removal -> flood-fill orientation unification + centroid winding vote -> fan-fill holes, every action a structured RepairReceipt; heals the corrupted icosphere back to manifold with all four defect classes receipted (rmesh-005 — including a caught test-fixture subtlety: punching a duplicated face lets its duplicate heal the hole, defeating the corpus). (6) shapes: public cube/icosphere/corrupt fixture vocabulary. Lanes green: clippy -D warnings, fmt, 1 lib + 6 conformance tests, workspace 80 suites (23 crates), xtask 0 violations, ubs 0 criticals. title: fs-rep-nurbs: exact spline algebra, trimmed B-reps w/ rational trim curves, SDF-routed Boolean policy
description:
## What
`fs-rep-nurbs`: rational B-spline charts (plan §7.2) — exact spline algebra, trimmed B-reps, and the HONEST Boolean policy.

## Scope
- NURBS curves/surfaces: full rational; EXACT knot insertion and degree elevation (integer/rational arithmetic where exactness is claimed); evaluation with derivatives to arbitrary order; Greville/Gauss quadrature rules per patch (IGA consumption).
- Trimmed-patch B-reps: trim curves held in EXACT RATIONAL form plus interval boxes (the dual representation that makes trim queries certifiable); point classification on trimmed patches with certified in/out/boundary bands.
- THE BOOLEAN POSITION (honest, per plan): watertight trimmed-NURBS Booleans are the GRAVEYARD of CAD kernels — so Boolean ops route through F-rep/SDF charts BY DEFAULT and re-fit splines afterward when a spline chart is required (convert-sdf-nurbs); direct B-rep Booleans remain a constrained, CERTIFICATE-GATED capability (attempt; if the sheaf watertightness certificate fails, refuse with diagnostics), never a promise.
- Why splines stay first-class anyway: IGA (§8.1) — geometry basis = analysis basis; Kirchhoff–Love shells on spline surfaces; the CAD→mesh information massacre never happens.
- Chart impl: closest-point/distance via Bézier-clipping-seeded Newton with interval polish (certified variant); ray intersection likewise (LUMEN's NURBS tracing shares this machinery).

## Acceptance criteria
Knot insertion/degree elevation EXACT (bitwise-identical evaluations pre/post refinement at common parameters — the definitive spline-algebra test); trimmed classification correct on adversarial trim fixtures (tangent trims, slivers, nested loops); closest-point certified variant always brackets true distance (vs dense sampling oracle).

## Testing
G0: partition-of-unity, endpoint interpolation, derivative-vs-Dual checks; round-trip insert/remove where mathematically lossless. Trim battery with published nasty cases. Logs: clipping iteration stats, certification bands, exactness verdicts.
close_reason: fs-rep-nurbs (L2; runtime deps fs-ivl/fs-math) implemented: (1) EXACT spline algebra — a Scalar field abstraction runs the SAME generic basis/curve/surface code at f64 and at an i128 gcd-reduced overflow-CHECKED Rat; Boehm knot insertion, knot REMOVAL (reconstruction consistency checked by scalar equality — a proof in Rat), Bezier decomposition, and degree elevation (per-segment binomial, reassembled on a full-multiplicity knot vector) are all EXACT: the conformance suite asserts rational EQUALITY of evaluations pre/post refinement at common parameters (the bead's definitive test) and that insert+remove recovers the control net; (2) rational curves/surfaces: homogeneous de Boor, f64 derivatives to arbitrary order (homogeneous differencing + rational Leibniz) verified against a dual-scalar instantiation of the SAME generic evaluator, tensor surfaces with exact directional insertion and isocurve-net partials; (3) trimmed patches: trim loops in EXACT RATIONAL form (closure = rational equality), CERTIFIED classification via the hull-homotopy argument (outside every Bezier span hull => exactly-computed control-polygon winding == curve winding; ambiguous points subdivide at exact rational midpoints and honestly report Boundary) — adversarial battery: nested square/diamond/island, near-tangent vertex, sliver holes, on-boundary honesty; (4) certified closest point (curve + surface): best-first branch-and-bound over rational-Bezier hull boxes with local de Casteljau splits (an earlier split-everything version exploded exponentially — caught by a hung conformance run and redesigned), epsilon-inflated hulls, Newton polish; brackets verified to contain 1e5-sample curve and 300^2 surface oracles; (5) THE BOOLEAN POSITION: boolean() always returns a structured teaching refusal — default routes through SDF (convert -> implicit CSG -> re-fit via wqd.11/12), DirectCertificateGated refuses pending the wqd.13 sheaf certificate. Evidence: 7 tests green (1 unit + nb-001..006). clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md with no-claims (no direct Booleans by design, full-multiplicity elevation, i128 exactness domain, hull-based rigor with declared f64 margin), xtask check-all: 48 crates 0 violations.

title: fs-rep-voxel: occupancy/multi-material VDB voxels, point clouds w/ normals, lattice graphs (FrankenNetworkx)
description:
## What
`fs-rep-voxel`: occupancy/multi-material voxel charts, point clouds, and explicit lattice/strut graphs (plan §7.2).

## Scope
- Occupancy + multi-material voxel fields on FrankenVDB (shared substrate with fs-rep-sdf): material-id channels, fraction/density channels (SIMP consumption), boolean ops on active sets, morphology (dilate/erode/open/close — manufacturing-constraint primitives).
- Point clouds with normals: k-NN/radius queries (grid-hash accelerated), normal estimation (PCA with orientation propagation), THE FITTING TARGET role (scan-to-Region workflows: point cloud in, fitted chart out — via MLS/spline/neural fitting in converter beads).
- Explicit lattice/strut graphs via FrankenNetworkx: nodes+struts with cross-section attributes; ground structures (topo-truss's candidate-member sets), TPMS approximations, infill graphs (topo-lattice's de-homogenized output); graph↔geometry realization (strut graph → F-rep/mesh solid with junction blending).
- Chart impl for voxel charts: inside/outside from occupancy with declared voxel-resolution error model; distance via distance-transform (exact euclidean DT, tiled + deterministic).

## Acceptance criteria
Morphology ops match brute-force on fixtures; euclidean DT exact vs O(n²) reference on test volumes; lattice realization produces watertight solids (sheaf certificate consumed once available) on strut-graph fixtures incl. degenerate (coincident nodes, zero-length struts → structured errors); FrankenNetworkx round-trip (graph attributes preserved).

## Testing
G0: morphology algebra laws (erode∘dilate ⊆ id on convex fixtures etc.); DT property (triangle inequality residuals = 0); point-cloud query correctness vs brute force. Logs: active-voxel stats, DT timings, per-strut realization receipts.

Verified via local cargo test + remote clippy lane.

title: fs-rep-neural: Lipschitz-constrained neural implicit charts (DeepSDF-style) w/ certified bounds [F]
description:
## What
`fs-rep-neural`: neural implicit charts (plan §7.2 [F]) — small MLPs (FrankenTorch) as shapes, DeepSDF-style, with LIPSCHITZ-CONSTRAINED layers so certified bounds remain available.

## Scope
- Architecture: compact coordinate MLPs (SIREN/positional-encoding class options) with SPECTRAL-NORM/weight-constrained layers giving a certified global Lipschitz constant — the property that keeps neural shapes inside the certificate regime (sphere tracing with provable step safety; interval evaluation via layer-wise bound propagation).
- Training: DeepSDF-style fitting from other charts (sample SDF values + gradients from any chart; eikonal regularization), latent-code conditioning for shape FAMILIES (the ultra-compact, inherently differentiable design parameterization the plan wants — latent code = design lever with exact autograd Jacobians via the FrankenTorch bridge).
- Chart impl: eval with gradient (autograd or analytic), certified Lipschitz data from the constrained architecture, interval evaluator via bound propagation (IBP/CROWN-class, in-house per P1), topology_hint honest (UNKNOWN unless verified by persistent-homology check in validity-certificates).
- Rule inherited from the doctrine: neural charts PROPOSE geometry; certification (watertightness, agreement vs source chart within declared Hausdorff band) comes from the certificate machinery, never from the loss curve.

## Acceptance criteria
Fitting recovers fixture shapes within declared Hausdorff bands (measured against source charts); Lipschitz certificate VALID (no sampled violation, bound propagation sound vs interval oracle on sub-boxes); latent interpolation produces valid in-between shapes on a two-shape family (qualitative + agreement metrics ledgered); sphere tracing with certified constant never tunnels (ray battery).

## Testing
G0: bound-propagation containment; gradient-vs-autograd consistency. Training reproducibility: seeded runs bit-comparable where FrankenTorch determinism permits (documented class). Logs: fit residual curves, Lipschitz constants, band widths, per-shape evidence records.

title: Rep Router: Pareto shortest-path conversion planning over chart multigraph, learned costs, explainable routes
description:
## What
The Rep Router (plan §7.3, Bet 1): conversions form a directed multigraph — nodes are chart types, edges are converters annotated with (cost model, error model, certificate availability) — and requests ("give me a chart supporting operation X with error ≤ ε under budget B") are solved as a PARETO SHORTEST-PATH problem over that graph.

## Scope
- Conversion graph registry: converters self-register with their edge labels; cost models read the autotune table (machine-specific ON PURPOSE — kernel-level winners flip between the M-class and TR-class machines, §14.1); error models declare composition rules (additive Hausdorff, multiplicative relative, exact).
- Pareto shortest-path planner (cost × composed error, certified edges PREFERRED): FrankenNetworkx label-correcting/multi-objective search; deterministic tie-breaking (P2 — router decisions must be reproducible and explainable, per AGENTS.md invariant).
- Execution engine: run the winning chain, produce Evidence-typed result with the COMPOSED certificate; record ACTUAL cost/error back into tune tables so the models improve (the router learns).
- Explainability: `route_explain()` returns the considered paths, their Pareto labels, and why the winner won — an agent-facing diagnostic, and the hook fs-ir-admission uses for chart-compatibility checking.
- Failure semantics: no admissible path within budget → structured error listing the BINDING constraint (error vs cost) and the cheapest relaxations (errors-as-guidance doctrine).

## Why
This turns "which representation should I use" — the perennial unforced error of every CAD/simulation handoff — into a solved planning problem, and it is the load-bearing mechanism behind "no privileged representation."

## Acceptance criteria
On a seeded graph with known Pareto fronts, planner returns exact fronts; composed certificates verified against direct measurement on executed chains (G3 path independence: ANY admissible path lands within the composed certificate of the direct edge); learned-cost feedback demonstrably improves subsequent plans; determinism of decisions across runs.

## Testing
G0: planner correctness on synthetic multigraphs (oracle enumeration); G3 metamorphic path-independence battery across the real converter set as it lands; budget-infeasible diagnostics content-checked. Logs: full route explanations, per-edge predicted-vs-actual cost/error tables (the model-improvement evidence).

## ADDENDUM AMENDMENT (COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md)
The Rep Router's fitness function gains TWO new terms:
- a **differentiability term** (Proposal 1, frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.2): when a query requests gradients, PREFER naturally-smooth SDF/spline paths — converting multi-representation from a complication into the asset that provides differentiable paths where mesh-locked pipelines have none.
- a **conditioning term** (Proposal 5, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.3): route by END-TO-END conditioning (composed per-op amplification factors), not just per-op cost/error — "which path through representation space keeps the whole pipeline well-posed."
The Router also becomes a VERIFICATION asset: the Goodhart guard (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.5) re-routes optimizer endpoints through a DIFFERENT representation path as an independent cross-check.
close_reason: Rep Router v1 landed as fs-geom's router module (Bet 1: no privileged representation). GRAPH REGISTRY: ConverterSpec edges with (a-priori cost, declared error model with composition rule — Exact / AdditiveAbs / MultiplicativeRel grounded against the request scale, certificate availability); duplicate/invalid specs refused structurally. PLANNER: exact Pareto label-correcting search over (cost, composed abs error, uncertified-edge count), simple-paths (non-simple dominated under nonneg weights), deterministic by construction (name-sorted expansion + total winner order: certified-preferred -> min cost -> min error -> lexicographic path). PROVEN against a brute-force DFS Pareto oracle on a seeded multigraph with parallel edges, a cycle, and mixed certificates, across three targets — exact front match (bead acceptance). EXPLAIN: every Pareto candidate with admissibility + winner index + reason (the fs-ir admission hook). REFUSALS TEACH: binding constraint named (Error/Cost/Both/NoPath) with best-achievable relaxation values, content-tested. COST ORACLE: machine-specific costs read through the CostOracle trait (L2 must not depend on L6 fs-ledger — HELM wires the tune table behind it; MemoryCostOracle in-process); learned-cost feedback demonstrably reroutes plans (acceptance); learned error magnitudes replace declared ones ONLY on uncertified additive edges — certificates are never learned away. EXECUTION: chains run through EdgeRunners; per-edge Evidence receipts composed via fs-evidence Op::Add over achieved-error enclosures; measured composed error verified inside the plan's composed certificate (G3); actuals recorded back into the oracle (the router learns); failures attributed to their edge. Real integration proven: fs-geom's actual Convert<SampledSdf> conversion (sphere F-rep fixture) executed under a routed plan with its achieved error inside the plan bound. 6 router tests + full fs-geom suite green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; xtask check-all 20 crates 0 violations. Scope notes: single-objective-per-request routing (operation-capability matching lands with more chart kinds); FrankenNetworkx label-correcting swap-in deferred until fnx exposes multi-objective search (in-crate planner is exact and oracle-proven meanwhile).

title: Converter mesh->SDF: exact point-triangle distance + winding sign, per-tile fp-exact certificate
description:
## What
Certified converter: mesh → SDF (plan §7.3 edge 1) — exact point-triangle distance + generalized-winding-number sign, per-tile error = 0 up to fp rounding [S]. The strongest edge in the conversion graph: effectively EXACT.

## Scope
- Distance field: for each output tile, exact point-triangle distance against BVH-pruned candidate sets (fs-rep-mesh machinery), batched/SIMD across tile samples; f32 storage with f64 computation per SDF convention.
- Sign: generalized winding number (octree-dipole-accelerated) — robust on imperfect input; for CERTIFIED-exact claims input must pass watertightness/manifoldness checks first (validity-certificates), otherwise the edge downgrades its certificate to "winding-sign heuristic" honestly (Evidence reflects input quality).
- Narrow-band + full-field modes; FrankenVDB output for sparse domains; incremental re-generation for locally-edited meshes (dirty-tile tracking) — the optimization-loop path.
- Error model: per-tile bound = fp-rounding envelope (documented ulp analysis); certificate: interval-verified on sampled audit tiles + exact-arithmetic spot checks.

## Acceptance criteria
Against analytic fixtures (sphere/box meshes at high resolution): field matches analytic SDF within declared envelope; sign correct everywhere including near-surface (the hard zone); incremental mode bit-identical to full regeneration (G5-style); throughput targets (tiles/sec) ledgered per machine.

## Testing
G0: symmetry/translation equivariance (G3 frame invariance); distance-vs-brute-force on random meshes; adversarial: slivers, near-degenerate triangles, open boundaries (certificate downgrade path verified). Logs: per-tile error stats, winding fallback counts, BVH traversal stats.

Narrow-band/VDB output modes ride the existing NarrowBand::from_chart path (works with any chart incl. MeshChart). Lanes green: clippy -D warnings (both crates), fmt, fs-rep-mesh 3 lib + 7 conformance + fs-rep-sdf suites, ubs prior scans. Workspace state: all green EXCEPT two other agents' in-flight reds (fs-ivl taylor_golden_hash WIP 6ys.13; fs-plan missing CONTRACT.md mid-work gp3.8) — theirs, untouched. No-claims: sparse VDB output certificates + per-tile interval audits join the fs-ivl integration pass; dirty-box inference is the caller's until edit-tracking attributes land; throughput targets are the perf harness's.

title: Performance program: rooflines, machine-adaptive tuning, tropical analytics
description:
## Mission
The performance program (plan §14): performance claims as falsifiable rooflines with names attached, re-baselined continuously by the autotuner on actual machines — never vibes.

## Key targets (illustrative, re-measured on first run)
LBM D3Q19 stream+collide ≥1.0 GLUP/s (M-class) / ≥0.6 (TR 96c); GEMM f64 ≥75% of measured peak both ISAs; SpMV SELL-C-σ ≥85% of STREAM; matrix-free FEEC apply p=4 ≥30% peak FLOPs; batched small dense ≥60% peak; 3D FFT ≥40% of memory-bound limit; sphere-traced primary rays ≥80/120 Mray/s. Every target stored in the ledger with machine fingerprint — a target never re-measured is a lie waiting to happen.

## Structural positions
- Machine-adaptive execution: CCD-aware tile ownership (working sets inside 32 MiB L3 islands), first-touch placement, reduction trees shaped to CCD hierarchy; on Apple, P/E-asymmetric scheduling with E-core-sized tile variants.
- Tropical critical-path analytics (Bet 12 [F/M]): task-DAG timing in the max-plus semiring; steady-state throughput = tropical eigenvalue; the critical circuit tells the autotuner WHICH KERNEL TO TUNE NEXT and the planner where relaxed tolerance actually buys wall-clock.
- Performance regressions are test failures (nightly roofline runs → metrics table; below tolerance band = CI failure with auto-reconstructed flame-graph diagnosis).

## Members
roofline-harness, machine-adaptive-tuning, tropical-analytics, perf-regression-ci.

## Success Criteria
Nightly roofline CI on both reference machines writing to the ledger; regression = red build; dashboards make "what got slower this month and why" a one-liner.

title: Roofline harness: measured bandwidth/peak axes, kernel registry w/ 14.1 targets, ledgered attainment + staleness
description:
## What
The roofline harness (plan §14.1): performance claims as FALSIFIABLE targets — every kernel benchmarked against its arithmetic-intensity-derived limit on the actual machine, results ledgered with fingerprints.

## Scope
- Machine characterization: measured STREAM-class bandwidth (per core class, per CCD), measured peak FLOPs (per ISA tier, per precision) — the roofline's axes, from fs-substrate probes + dedicated microbenches; NEVER spec-sheet numbers.
- Kernel benchmark registry: each hot kernel registers (workload generator, intensity metadata — from fs-tilelang where generated — target expression as % of roofline). The §14.1 table encoded as data: LBM ≥1.0/0.6 GLUP/s; GEMM ≥75% peak; SpMV ≥85% STREAM; FEEC apply p=4 ≥30% peak; batched dense ≥60%; FFT ≥40% memory-bound limit; sphere-traced rays ≥80/120 Mray/s.
- Attainment computation: measured vs machine-specific limit; results → ledger `metrics` with machine fingerprint + kernel version; "a target that was never re-measured is a lie waiting to happen" — staleness tracking built in.
- Two-machine honesty: the M-class is a bandwidth monster PER CORE (LBM/SpMV love it), the TR-class wins on aggregate compute/cache — kernel-level winners FLIP by workload; the harness reports per-machine attainment separately (feeding the Rep Router's machine-specific cost models).
- Statistical discipline: warmup/thermal handling, repetition with dispersion reporting, frequency-state capture (a benchmark without variance bars is folklore).

## Acceptance criteria
Harness runs the full registry on both reference machines producing ledgered attainment tables; seeded-slow kernel correctly reported below band; measurement reproducibility within stated dispersion across re-runs; staleness alerts fire on fingerprint drift.

## Testing
Meta-tests: attainment arithmetic vs hand calculations; dispersion-bound self-checks; fingerprint-keying correctness. Logs: full roofline reports (kernel × machine × attainment × dispersion), trend series.
close_reason: Roofline harness v0 landed as crates/fs-roofline (L6). Measured axes only (never spec-sheet): STREAM-triad bandwidth single/all-core via fs-substrate probe + in-house FMA-chain peak-FLOPs microbench single/all-core; axes carry the fs-substrate topology fingerprint. KernelSpec intensity model (bytes/flops per elem, threading axis, optional target fraction) -> attainment = rate / min(bandwidth-limit, compute-limit) with binding RoofSide, relative-IQR dispersion, and WithinBand/BelowBand/NoTarget verdicts. Section 14.1 target table encoded as data with landed:false rows visible from day one (no silent coverage gaps). Registry v0: fs-simd axpy/dot/sum (report-only bands on shared runners, per 14 discipline) + SeededSlowKernel meta-kernel. Ledger integration: record_run writes one op (frozen Five Explicits) + per-kernel metrics + benchmark_result events + tune rows keyed kernel x roofline-v1 x fingerprint (added Ledger::tune_rows scan to fs-ledger); staleness() returns Fresh/FingerprintDrift/NeverMeasured. roofline CLI bin for the nightly lane (axes + kernel JSONL + coverage table + --ledger recording + staleness report), conformance-tested via CARGO_BIN_EXE. Acceptance proven: attainment arithmetic vs hand calcs (unit + rf-001), seeded-slow correctly BelowBand on real axes (rf-002), ledgered attainment with fingerprint keying + lint-clean (rf-003), staleness alerts fire on drift (rf-004), re-run reproducibility within stated dispersion allowance (rf-005), CLI smoke (rf-006). 9 unit + 6 conformance green; clippy -D warnings clean; xtask check-all 17 crates 0 violations. No-claims in CONTRACT.md: no machine perf numbers claimed (citable only as fingerprinted ledger rows); TR-class reference machine not yet measured (needs the machine); per-CCD/P-E-class axes, frequency-state capture, thermal controls = future scope; verdict gating deliberately absent on shared CI runners.

title: Machine-adaptive hardening: CCD pinning, first-touch audits, P/E scheduling, prefetch/hugepage sweeps — targets met on both ISAs
description:
## What
Machine-adaptive execution hardening (plan §14.2): the cross-cutting tuning pass that makes the SAME kernels roofline-honest on BOTH microarchitectures — verified, not vibes.

## Scope
- Threadripper/EPYC lane: CCD-aware tile ownership (working sets pinned inside their 32 MiB L3 islands — measured via cache-miss counters where available, timing proxies otherwise); first-touch page placement VERIFIED (page-to-node audits); reduction trees shaped to the CCD hierarchy; NPS-mode sensitivity documented.
- Apple lane: latency-lane work pinned to P-cores; throughput tiles streamed across P+E with E-core-sized tile variants (autotuner weights verified against measured per-class throughput); 16 KB page + 128 B line effects quantified.
- Hugepage-backed arenas everywhere they help (measured, with fallback paths exercised); software-prefetch distances autotuned per machine (stencil suite sweep); SIMD tier selection audit (chosen tier = fastest tier, verified per kernel).
- Schedule polymorphism validation: bandwidth-rich vs bandwidth-starved schedules actually selected correctly per machine (the §5.1 consequence-2 doctrine tested end-to-end: same kernel, different winner, autotuner picks right).
- Deliverable: the §14.1 targets MET on both machines for the P0–P3 kernel set, with the tuning decisions ledgered and reproducible.

## Acceptance criteria
Each optimization's effect measured and documented (before/after per machine — no unattributed tuning); attainment targets met or honest gap analysis filed as follow-up beads; autotuner selections match exhaustive-sweep optima within tolerance on the audited subset.

## Testing
A/B harnesses per mechanism (CCD pinning on/off, first-touch on/off, prefetch sweep); counter-based verification where the OS exposes it. Logs: per-mechanism effect tables, selection audits, final attainment scoreboard.

title: Tropical critical-path analytics: max-plus eigenvalue over live event stream, tune-next + slack outputs [F/M]
description:
## What
Tropical critical-path analytics (plan §14.3, Bet 12 [F/M]): task-DAG timing in the MAX-PLUS SEMIRING — the critical circuit as a tropical eigenvector, computed live, telling the autotuner WHICH KERNEL TO TUNE NEXT and the planner where relaxed tolerance buys wall-clock.

## Scope
- Max-plus formalization: stage delays compose with ⊕=max, ⊗=+; steady-state throughput of the pipelined task DAG = the tropical eigenvalue of its timing matrix; the critical circuit = the eigenvector's support.
- Live computation: FrankenNetworkx tropical power iteration over the ledger's `events` stream (tile/op timings arriving continuously); windowed re-computation with drift detection (the critical path MOVES as tuning improves things).
- ACTIONABLE outputs (the whole point — "scheduling theory as an online instrument, not a whiteboard aphorism"): makespan sensitivity to each kernel's latency → ranked tune-next list for the autotuner; slack analysis → the planner learns that relaxing tolerance OFF the critical circuit buys NOTHING (fs-plan-allocator consumes this); bottleneck attribution in perf dashboards.
- [M] discipline: advisory-only until validated — its recommendations tracked against realized improvements (did tuning the recommended kernel actually move the makespan? — the self-audit that gates promotion).
- We contribute the tropical-semiring iteration kernels upstream to FrankenNetworkx (§12 contract).

## Acceptance criteria
Tropical eigenvalue/eigenvector correct vs brute-force longest-path analysis on fixture DAGs; live pipeline: synthetic event streams with known critical circuits recovered within window latency; recommendation quality: on seeded-bottleneck fixtures, the top recommendation is the true bottleneck; promotion audit machinery in place (recommendation → outcome tracking).

## Testing
G0: max-plus algebra laws, power-iteration convergence on irreducible fixtures; windowing correctness under out-of-order events. Logs: eigenvalue traces, circuit memberships over time, recommendation→outcome ledger.

title: Perf-regression CI: nightly roofline gates, event-stream flame-graph diagnosis, change-point alarms, dashboards
description:
## What
Performance-regression CI (plan §14.4): performance regressions are TEST FAILURES — nightly roofline runs on both reference machines, tolerance-band gates, and self-diagnosing failures.

## Scope
- Nightly lane: full roofline-harness registry on both machines → `metrics` table; any kernel dropping below its tolerance band FAILS CI exactly like a wrong answer (ci-gauntlet-pipeline wiring).
- Self-diagnosing failures: the `events` stream reconstructs FLAME-GRAPH-EQUIVALENTS post hoc — a regression arrives WITH its own diagnosis (which phase slowed, which tile sizes, which allocation pattern changed); diff-vs-last-green automation (suspect-commit bisection hooks).
- Dashboards: FrankenPandas over the ledger — "what got slower this month, and why" as a one-liner; trend visualizations (fs-report feeds); noise handling (dispersion-aware bands so thermal jitter doesn't cry wolf — statistical change-point detection rather than naive thresholds).
- Study-scale tracking: not just kernels — flagship-fixture wall-clocks tracked (the number users feel), attributed via the Time Ledger.
- Memory regressions: allocation-site tracking diffs (fs-alloc) gated the same way.

## Acceptance criteria
Seeded regression (deliberately de-tuned kernel) → red CI with correct flame-graph-level attribution; noise robustness: N nights of stable code → zero false alarms at declared confidence; dashboard queries answer the canonical questions in one call each.

## Testing
Meta-tests: gate arithmetic, change-point detector calibration on synthetic series; reconstruction correctness (flame-graph-equivalent vs known synthetic event stream). Logs: nightly scoreboards, regression diagnoses, alarm-calibration stats.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Heads-up from CloudyFinch: fs-roofline rf_005_reproducibility_within_dispersion failed once on the shared rch worker (ovh-a) during a workspace run — dispersion bound exceeded under foreign load — but passes locally and on quiet boxes. Timing-dispersion asserts may need a loaded-machine allowance or a quiescence guard when the perf CI lands.

title: ADDENDUM L4 STRUCTURE & SELF-KNOWLEDGE: interface types + symmetry, spectral health, abstraction ladder, explanations, value-of-information
description:
## Mission
ADDENDUM LAYER 4 — STRUCTURE AND SELF-KNOWLEDGE. Declared structure harvested twice (correctness and speed), the system monitoring the health of its own algebra, and the three proposals that give a memoryless, intuition-free operator something to think with. Where Layers 1–3 build the engine, this layer is the operator's cognition: types that make ill-posed couplings compile-time errors, a health monitor that knows when its own triage is untrustworthy, a certified abstraction ladder that lets a bounded-attention operator reason at the concept level, explanation objects the system can *check* rather than confabulate, and a market that prices ignorance.

## The five proposals
- **Proposal 13 — Interface types and symmetry harvesting** (Mean 720). Type every interface by its function-space role (Arnold's FEEC periodic table IS the type lattice: H(grad)/H(curl)/H(div)/L² + regularity tags) so ill-posed couplings are compile-time errors — converting the largest class of silent agent-generated nonsense into red squiggles. Separately, let declared/detected symmetry groups block-diagonalize solves by irreducible representation (k-fold symmetry ≈ k× speedup), with approximate symmetry yielding a symmetrized solve + certified perturbation correction. The type checker is Phase 0 spine; the symmetry solver is Phase 3 horizon.
- **Proposal 5 — Spectral health monitoring** (Mean 660, Pract 850). The harmonic/coboundary "fixable vs structural" triage that merge (10) and repair depend on is only as trustworthy as the sheaf Laplacian's spectral gap — so instrument the gap as a runtime health metric and let the Rep Router route by end-to-end conditioning, not just per-op fitness. "I am becoming numerically unreliable in this region, and here is the reroute."
- **Proposal A — The certified abstraction ladder** (Mean 780, Agent 950). Maintain reduced-order views ("the wing box", "the load path") whose fidelity to the level below is a certified quantity, with a leak alarm: reason at the concept level until a certificate says the abstraction is no longer faithful *here*, then drill down only there. The only third option between an operator drowning in 50,000 spline coefficients and hallucinating the details it dropped. Beachhead: certified reduced-basis methods (Rozza/Patera) for affine-parametric elliptic problems.
- **Proposal B — Explanation objects** (Mean 770, Agent 950). When drag rises 3%, return not a number but a causal decomposition along named physical channels (induced/wave/friction), attributed via adjoint fields + the provenance DAG, each term with bounds — a checkable, re-derivable, certified artifact. Because a language-model operator generates plausible mechanistic narratives anyway; the difference between confabulation and understanding is whether the system can check the story.
- **Proposal C — Value-of-information queries: the ignorance market** (Mean 780, Agent 950). Make directed ignorance reduction a query type: across everything the ledger is uncertain about, where does one dollar of evidence most change the downstream decision? Turns curiosity into an optimizer with a stopping rule; the natural completion of 8's anytime "what would tighten this?".

## The circularity discount (Governance Rule 4)
A, B, C, D were generated by optimizing the agent-operator lens, so their top-tier standing is *conditional on agents actually being the operator*. Strip the lens (Ex-A) and they fall to the 725–740 band. If the first vertical ships human-driven, they are formally deferred to Phase 3 regardless of score.

## Members
interface-types (13), symmetry-harvest (13), spectral-health (5), abstraction-ladder (A), explanation-objects (B), voi-queries (C).

## Success criteria
The coupling-graph checker turns illegal trace pairings into pre-run errors; degraded-gap suite passes (the triage must REFUSE confidence rather than misclassify — refusal is the pass condition); RB-certified regions cover ≥20% of wedge query volume; explanation channels + residual reconcile to the observed ΔQoI within certified bounds on ≥90% of cases; VoI-recommended purchases measurably outperform agent-chosen alternatives on realized decision-changes.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- NOTE: `add-layer:Ln` here is the addendum's THEMATIC layer (economic engine / epistemics / coupling / self-knowledge / GTM), NOT the base L0–L6 architecture stack. Member crates still sit at their true base layer and must respect lower-knows-nothing-of-higher dependency direction.

title: Proposal 13 — Interface types: coupling-graph STATIC CHECKER over the FEEC periodic-table type lattice + inf-sup-certified pairing registry (illegal couplings = compile errors)
description:
## What
Proposal 13's TYPE CHECKER — type every interface by its FUNCTION-SPACE ROLE so that ill-posed couplings are COMPILE-TIME ERRORS. Arnold's FEEC periodic table IS LITERALLY THE TYPE LATTICE: H(grad), H(curl), H(div), L², plus regularity tags. This converts the largest class of silent agent-generated nonsense into RED SQUIGGLES — which is why the Agent score is 850: humans catch these by experience, and agents have no experience. Phase 0 spine; the type checker's kill criterion is "none — the checker is cheap insurance."

## Scope
- A STATIC CHECKER over the coupling graph validates trace pairings + element compatibility against a REGISTRY of inf-sup-certified discretization pairings BEFORE anything runs.
- The type lattice = the FEEC periodic table (reuse the base plan's fs-feec families + exact-sequence structure): H(grad)/H(curl)/H(div)/L² + regularity tags.
- Coupling a pressure to a displacement trace in an ILLEGAL pairing becomes a compile-time error, not a silently garbage solve.
- The COUPLING-GRAPH COMPILER + the LEGAL-PAIRINGS REGISTRY become NAMED base-plan components (the amendment).
- Consumed by Proposal 8 (typed QoI fields), Proposal E-contracts (envelopes over typed interface quantities), Proposal 10-merge (non-geometric conflicts caught as type-level conflicts).

## Acceptance criteria
The static checker validates a coupling graph against the inf-sup-certified pairing registry and REJECTS illegal trace pairings before any solve; a seeded illegal coupling (e.g., pressure↔displacement in an unstable pairing) is caught at check time with a localized diagnostic; legal pairings pass.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 13, types)
None — the checker is cheap insurance and always ships.

## Testing
Property: the checker is SOUND (never passes an illegal pairing from the registry) and does not reject legal pairings; diagnostics localize to the offending interface. This is the Phase-0 spine's type-checker exit condition. Falsifier: run an illegal pairing that the checker passed — must be impossible. Logs: coupling-graph type assignments, pairing-registry lookups, rejections with locations.

## Dependencies
Base plan fs-feec (the periodic-table type lattice + exact sequences). AMENDS the base plan (coupling-graph compiler + legal-pairings registry as named components). Sibling to symmetry-harvest (same Proposal 13; the symmetry solver is a SEPARATE, horizon-gated bead). Phase 0 spine.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- The inf-sup pairing registry is a DECLARATIVE literature table (LBB/inf-sup results); the static checker validates coupling-graph LEGALITY against it WITHOUT requiring the saddle-point solvers to be built. Registry entries may reference discretizations (higher-order/mixed, block/Schur saddle-point) not yet implemented — a stated no-claim: the checker guarantees graph-legality against the registry, not that each entry has a shipped element family.
- Tier: type-checker soundness -> G0 (no illegal pairing passes).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Conservative-by-default soundness case (mirror in Acceptance): a coupling graph containing a pairing NOT in the inf-sup-certified registry (unknown/unrecognized) must be REJECTED as illegal-until-certified, never passed by default — a checker that silently admits unrecognized pairings is unsound in practice. Empty-graph and cyclic-coupling-graph boundary cases each rejected with a localized diagnostic.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-iface (registered in workspace members; crate:fs-feec relabeled crate:fs-iface to avoid collision with the active fs-feec agent — the checker is pure static analysis and needs no fs-feec internals). Proposal 13 interface-types coupling-graph STATIC CHECKER: SpaceType is the FEEC periodic-table type lattice (HGrad/HCurl/HDiv/L2, de Rham exact sequence via form_degree). CouplingRole distinguishes Continuity (legal iff same trace space) from Saddle (legal iff inf-sup certified). PairingRegistry::standard() is a declarative LBB literature table — certified (HDiv,L2)=RT/BDM Darcy, (HGrad,L2)=Taylor-Hood Stokes; known-unstable (L2,L2),(HGrad,HGrad)=equal-order LBB violations; everything else Unknown. check(graph, registry) is pure+deterministic, emitting localized CheckFindings with teaching fixes. CONSERVATIVE BY DEFAULT (the load-bearing soundness rule + round-3 hardening): an Unknown pairing is REJECTED as illegal-until-certified, never silently admitted. Handles missing-field + self-loop malformed graphs and empty-graph-vacuously-legal. Tests (tests/checker.rs, 13): exact-sequence lattice, empty-graph legal, same-space continuity legal, cross-space (pressure-to-displacement) illegal, certified saddles legal, unstable P1-P1 rejected with certified-partner fix, UNKNOWN rejected conservatively, missing-field+self-loop localized, multi-fault per-coupling localization, determinism, empty-registry certifies nothing. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-iface 13/13, xtask check-all 0 violations across 51 crates (layers+deps+contracts+unsafe). Completes the Phase-0 SPINE (xpck.2): the last of {color-schema, falsifier, tombstones, Goodhart-guard, interface-types}. No-claim: registry is a literature table (does not require element families built); symmetry harvesting is a separate bead; fs-ir admission wiring is later.

title: Proposal 13 — Symmetry harvesting: automorphism/geometric-hash detection + isotypic block-diagonalization + certified asymmetry remainder (k-fold ≈ k×)
description:
## What
Proposal 13's SYMMETRY HARVESTING — let declared (or detected) symmetry groups BLOCK-DIAGONALIZE solves by irreducible representation: structure declared once, harvested as both correctness AND speed. Win condition: k-fold symmetry ≈ k× speedup on dominant solves. Phase 3 horizon (activation-gated separately from the type checker).

## Scope
- DETECTION: graph automorphism on the patch complex (FrankenNetworkx) + geometric hashing; APPROXIMATE symmetry detected with a CERTIFIED ASYMMETRY RESIDUAL.
- BLOCK-DIAGONALIZATION by ISOTYPIC PROJECTION using CHARACTER TABLES for the common finite groups — cyclic and dihedral cover turbomachinery, fasteners, and most architectural regularity.
- APPROXIMATE symmetry → the asymmetric remainder enters as a RIGHT-HAND-SIDE PERTURBATION with an interval bound, so approximate symmetry yields a SYMMETRIZED SOLVE + a CERTIFIED PERTURBATION CORRECTION rather than a binary applicable/not.
- Reuses the base plan's fs-ga (geometric algebra: motors/screws) for the symmetry-group representation and fs-la for the block solves.
- Feeds Proposal A (symmetry detection feeds abstraction coarsening).

## Acceptance criteria
Exact k-fold symmetry yields ≈k× speedup on a dominant solve with bit-consistent results vs the full solve; approximate symmetry yields a symmetrized solve + a certified perturbation correction whose bound contains the true asymmetry; the asymmetry residual is certified.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 13, symmetry)
If across the first vertical FEWER THAN 15% of real workloads present exploitable exact-or-approximate symmetry, KEEP DETECTION (it costs little and feeds Proposal A's coarsening) and DEFER the isotypic solver.

## Testing
Falsifier (Proposal 6): OCCASIONAL FULL SOLVES on random instances confirm the symmetrized solve + correction; G3: results invariant under relabeling of symmetric copies; property: isotypic projection is exact on exact-symmetry fixtures. Measurement: fraction of workloads with exploitable symmetry (the kill measurement). Logs: detected groups, asymmetry residuals, speedups, full-solve cross-checks.

## Dependencies
interface-types (same Proposal 13); base plan fs-feec + fs-ga + fs-la + FrankenNetworkx (automorphism); Proposal 6 (full-solve falsifier). Feeds Proposal A. Phase 3 horizon.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Layer:** fs-ga (L2) supplies the group/motor representation (reuse, via morph-wqd.22); the isotypic BLOCK-SOLVE lives at L3 over fs-feec operators (crate relabeled fs-feec) — an L2 crate cannot compile against L3. FrankenNetworkx supplies automorphism detection (Franken-only).
- Tier: isotypic projection exactness -> G0; full-solve falsifier on random instances -> G3.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: a fully-asymmetric input yields the trivial group and full-solve fallback (no false speedup); a large-asymmetry-residual case declines or emits an honestly wide bound instead of a useless symmetrized answer.

title: Proposal 5 — Spectral health monitoring: λ-gap per region (warm-started Lanczos/LOBPCG); low-confidence propagation into merge; Rep Router conditioning term
description:
## What
Proposal 5 — SPECTRAL HEALTH MONITORING. The harmonic/coboundary split — the "fixable vs structural" triage that MERGE (Proposal 10) and sheaf REPAIR depend on — is only as trustworthy as the sheaf Laplacian's SPECTRAL GAP. So instrument the gap as a runtime HEALTH METRIC and let the Rep Router route by END-TO-END CONDITIONING, not just per-op fitness. A system that can say "I am becoming numerically unreliable in this region, and here is the reroute" is a different species from one that silently degrades. Pract 850.

## Scope
- Track the λ-GAP per assembly region with a few Lanczos/LOBPCG vectors (cheap on sparse Laplacians; reuse fs-la eigensolvers; amortized across edits by WARM-STARTING eigenvectors from the previous state).
- HEALTH = gap/scale ratio with HYSTERESIS thresholds. Below threshold, the system (i) TAGS all harmonic/coboundary triage in that region LOW-CONFIDENCE — INCLUDING merge adjudications (Proposal 10), which MUST surface the flag — and (ii) proposes RE-DECOMPOSITION of the offending patches.
- GENERALIZE beyond the sheaf: every op already carries an error bound; ADD its AMPLIFICATION FACTOR (local condition estimate) so the ledger can compose an END-TO-END CONDITIONING estimate per pipeline, and give the Rep Router a CONDITIONING TERM — the router stops asking only "which representation is cheap here" and starts asking "which path through representation space keeps the whole pipeline well-posed" (the amendment: Rep Router fitness gains a conditioning term).

## Acceptance criteria
The λ-gap is tracked per region and warm-started across edits; a degraded-gap region tags its triage low-confidence and this flag PROPAGATES into Proposal 10's merge outputs; per-op amplification factors compose into an end-to-end conditioning estimate; the Rep Router prefers better-conditioned paths.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 5)
If gap collapse is NEVER observed outside synthetic cases after a quarter at volume, DEMOTE monitoring to sampled spot checks; KEEP the router conditioning term regardless (it is independently justified). Early warning: gap-collapse incidence per assembly class (risk R5).

## Testing
Gauntlet ADVERSARIAL DEGRADED-GAP suite: deliberately ill-conditioned assemblies where the triage must REFUSE CONFIDENCE rather than misclassify — THE REFUSAL IS THE PASS CONDITION (the amendment adds this suite). Property: warm-started eigenvectors converge; conditioning composition is monotone. Logs: λ-gaps per region, low-confidence flags, merge-flag propagation, end-to-end conditioning estimates, router path choices.

## Dependencies
Bet 11 sheaf Laplacians (base plan fs-geom); fs-la eigensolvers (Lanczos/LOBPCG). HARD dependency of Proposal 10 (merge trust) and jointly owns risk R5. AMENDS the Rep Router (conditioning term) + the Gauntlet (degraded-gap refusal suite). Feeds Proposal A (conditioning-aware descent).

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- Tiers: degraded-gap refusal suite -> G4 (adversarial/chaos, refusal-is-the-pass); warm-started eigenvector convergence + monotone conditioning composition -> G0.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Stale warm-start correctness: after a large edit, warm-started eigenvectors that fail to converge trigger a COLD restart rather than returning a stale/wrong lambda-gap (a wrong gap silently mis-classifies merges — the R5 failure). True-negative case: a well-conditioned assembly (large gap) is NOT flagged low-confidence, so the monitor does not cry wolf.

title: Proposal A — Certified abstraction ladder: reduced-basis beachhead w/ certified QoI bounds + leak alarm + estimated-color coarse levels + at_level() API
description:
## What
Proposal A — THE CERTIFIED ABSTRACTION LADDER. Maintain reduced-order VIEWS at multiple abstraction levels ("the wing box", "the load path") whose FIDELITY to the level below is itself a CERTIFIED QUANTITY, with a LEAK ALARM: the operator reasons at the CONCEPT level until a certificate says the abstraction is no longer faithful HERE, and drills down only there. Agent 950. Circularity-discounted (Governance Rule 4): conditional on agents being the operator.

## Why (the bounded-attention argument)
An operator that cannot hold 50,000 spline coefficients in working memory — which describes BOTH an agent's context window AND a human's attention — must either DROWN in detail or HALLUCINATE the details it dropped. Certified coarse-graining is the only THIRD option. Architecturally this is the SAME SHEAF PATTERN pointed VERTICALLY: levels are coarse complexes, inter-level maps are restriction maps, abstraction leaks are mismatch residuals.

## Scope — the practical beachhead (justifies starting despite Pract 500)
- CERTIFIED REDUCED-BASIS (RB) METHODS for parametric elliptic problems are MATURE technology — offline/online decomposition with rigorous a-posteriori error bounds is textbook (Rozza, Patera et al.), and the affine-parametric elliptic regime covers a useful slice of design iteration.
- Ship that FIRST: per-region RB models with certified QoI bounds for declared query classes; faithfulness certificate = the RB error bound; leak alarm = bound exceeding the query tolerance; response = automatic LOCAL DRILL-DOWN to full order.

## Scope — the honest frontier
- Certified bounds for coarse-grained NONLINEAR/TRANSIENT models are RESEARCH. Outside the RB regime, ladder levels carry **estimated color** with cross-rung discrepancy probes (Proposal 3) as their calibration, and the type system keeps anyone from mistaking them for the RB-certified levels.

## Scope — agent API
- `assembly.at_level(k).query(...)` with AUTOMATIC CERTIFIED DESCENT — the ladder is INVISIBLE UNTIL IT LEAKS, which is the point.

## Acceptance criteria
Per-region RB models produce certified QoI bounds for the declared elliptic query classes; the leak alarm fires when the RB bound exceeds the query tolerance and auto-drills to full order there; non-RB levels are estimated-color and calibrated by discrepancy probes; `at_level(k).query()` descends automatically.

## Kill criterion (Proposal A)
If RB-certified regions cover <20% of wedge-vertical query volume, the beachhead is too narrow — KEEP uncertified coarse views (still useful, honestly colored) and PARK the certification program until the vertical's workload mix changes.

## Testing
Property: the leak alarm never lets an operator act on an abstraction whose certified bound exceeds tolerance without drilling down; RB bounds are true bounds on MMS elliptic problems; estimated-color levels cannot masquerade as RB-certified. Measurement: RB-coverage fraction of wedge query volume (the kill measurement). Logs: per-level bounds, leak-alarm fires, drill-down events, RB coverage.

## Dependencies
Proposal 3 (colors for the uncertified levels — HARD); symmetry-harvest / Proposal 13 (symmetry detection feeds coarsening); Proposal 8 (queries route through levels); base plan fs-surrogate (POD-DEIM/reduced models) + the sheaf pattern. Phase 3 horizon; RB beachhead first.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- RB validity-range guard (mirror in Acceptance): a query whose parameters fall OUTSIDE the RB model's trained/affine range must not trust the RB bound — it drills down or drops to estimated color (RB a-posteriori bounds are valid only in-range). Boundary: leak alarm at bound == tolerance drills conservatively; a query that leaks at every level terminates at full order (no infinite descent).

title: Proposal B — Explanation objects: adjoint/provenance/physical-decomposition attribution tree, honesty gate; far-field drag decomposition flagship
description:
## What
Proposal B — EXPLANATION OBJECTS.
