When drag rises 3%, return not a NUMBER but a CAUSAL DECOMPOSITION along named physical channels — induced vs wave vs friction — attributed via adjoint fields + the provenance DAG, each term with BOUNDS, as a first-class, RE-DERIVABLE, CERTIFIED artifact. Agent 950. Circularity-discounted (Governance Rule 4). Phase 2 flagship = far-field drag decomposition.

## Why (the confabulation argument)
Mechanism-level explanations are what let an operator TRANSFER: "the shock moved aft and thickened the boundary layer" becomes a prior applicable to the next hundred designs, where a scalar teaches nothing. And the candid version: a language-model operator generates plausible mechanistic narratives ANYWAY — that is what it is. The difference between confabulation and understanding is whether the system can CHECK the story. So make the explanation an OBJECT the system can check, not prose emitted afterward.

## Scope — three attribution engines feeding one tree
1. **Adjoint attribution**: ∂QoI/∂field integrated over region + channel masks (Proposal 1).
2. **Provenance attribution**: which EDIT moved the number, via Proposal 10's diff machinery (vcs-diff).
3. **Exact physical decompositions** where the discipline provides them — FAR-FIELD DRAG DECOMPOSITION via wake-integral methods (induced/wave/viscous) is established aerodynamic practice and is the FLAGSHIP; modal/energy decompositions are the structural analogue.
- The explanation object is a TREE of `(channel, contribution, bound, evidence links)`, every node RE-DERIVABLE from the ledger. A natural-language rendering layer sits on top and is EXPLICITLY NON-AUTHORITATIVE — the tree is the artifact.

## The honesty gate (a PERMANENT runtime invariant)
If the UNATTRIBUTED RESIDUAL exceeds a threshold, the system REFUSES to explain rather than smearing the residual across plausible channels. A partial explanation with a declared gap beats a complete story with a hidden one.

## Acceptance criteria
Drag decomposition into induced/wave/viscous channels, each with a bound, reconciling to the observed drag within certified bounds; every tree node re-derivable from the ledger; the NL rendering is marked non-authoritative; the honesty gate refuses to explain when the residual is too large.

## Kill criterion (Proposal B — a PERMANENT invariant, not a one-time gate)
Channel decompositions MUST reconcile: if attributed channels PLUS residual fail to sum to the observed ΔQoI within certified bounds on >10% of cases, the attribution engine is LYING and ships nothing until fixed.

## Testing
Property: channels + residual = observed ΔQoI within bounds (the permanent invariant / kill measurement); every node re-derivable; honesty-gate refusal on high-residual fixtures. Flagship: far-field vs near-field drag agreement on NACA/wing fixtures. Logs: decomposition trees, per-channel bounds, residuals, refusals.

## Dependencies
Proposal 1 (adjoint attribution — HARD); Proposal 10 / vcs-diff (edit attribution); Proposal 3 (bounds + colors on terms); base plan aero kernels (fs-bem/fs-vpm for wake integrals). Phase 2 (drag-decomposition v0). Circularity-discounted.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Circularity-discounted (Governance Rule 4):** Phase-2 placement holds under the agent-operator baseline (addendum IV.2 lists B-v0 in Phase 2); defers to Phase 3 if the first vertical ships human-driven.
- Tiers: channels+residual = deltaQoI reconciliation (permanent runtime invariant) -> G3; far-field-vs-near-field drag agreement -> G2 canonical benchmark.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Anti-confabulation coupling: when the honesty gate refuses to explain (unattributed residual over threshold), the non-authoritative NL rendering layer must ALSO refuse — never a confident mechanistic narrative over a refused/partial tree. Boundary: a single-channel-dominant decomposition still reconciles to deltaQoI within bounds.

title: Proposal C — Value-of-information queries: decision-sensitivity × priced probe menu → top-k flip-prob-per-dollar (myopic VoI; unifies compute + physical experiments)
description:
## What
Proposal C — VALUE-OF-INFORMATION QUERIES: THE IGNORANCE MARKET. Make DIRECTED IGNORANCE REDUCTION a query type: across everything the ledger is uncertain about — model-form gaps, unvalidated regimes, surrogate variance — WHERE DOES ONE DOLLAR OF EVIDENCE MOST CHANGE THE DOWNSTREAM DECISION? Agent 950. Circularity-discounted (Governance Rule 4). The natural completion of Proposal 8's anytime "what would tighten this?".

## Why
For "explore and understand the world," the scarce resource is not compute but DIRECTED CURIOSITY. Pricing uncertainties by decision-consequence and purchasing evidence by expected impact turns curiosity into an OPTIMIZER WITH A STOPPING RULE.

## Scope (v0 is a RANKED LIST, not a market)
- For each uncertainty node touching a LIVE DECISION: estimate DECISION SENSITIVITY — does the current decision FLIP anywhere within the node's uncertainty interval? — cheaply, via cached surrogate sweeps over the interval (Proposals 9 and 2 make this nearly free).
- Cross with a PROBE MENU WITH PRICES: climb a rung here $X; run the wind-tunnel anchor $Y; add N samples of the hazard posterior $Z; probe cross-model discrepancy on this subdomain $W. THE MENU UNIFIES COMPUTATIONAL AND PHYSICAL EXPERIMENTS as evidence sources — the epistemic-engine identity made concrete.
- Output the TOP-K PURCHASES by flip-probability-per-dollar.
- MYOPIC one-step VoI ONLY — full sequential VoI is intractable and the myopic version captures most of the value in practice.
- Surface as (i) the HINT on every query result (Proposal 8 / query-anytime) and (ii) the SCHEDULER for discrepancy probes (Proposal 3 / color-probes).

## Acceptance criteria
For a live decision with several uncertainty nodes, C ranks the top-k evidence purchases by flip-probability-per-dollar using cached sweeps; the probe menu unifies computational + physical experiments with prices; the ranking surfaces as both the query-result hint and the discrepancy-probe scheduler; myopic VoI is used (no intractable sequential search).

## Kill criterion (Proposal C — prospective audit)
Log VoI-recommended purchases vs agent-chosen alternatives at MATCHED COST; if recommended purchases do not MEASURABLY OUTPERFORM on realized decision-changes within two quarters of the first vertical, DEMOTE VoI to a reporting feature and stop scheduling by it.

## Testing
Property: decision-sensitivity is computed from cached sweeps (near-free); rankings are by flip-prob-per-dollar; myopic-only. Measurement: recommended-vs-agent-chosen realized-decision-change comparison (the kill measurement). Logs: uncertainty inventory, decision-flip probabilities, priced probe menus, top-k rankings, prospective-audit outcomes.

## Dependencies
Proposal 3 (the uncertainty inventory — HARD); Proposals 9/2 (cheap sweeps — HARD); Proposal 8 (surfacing as the anytime hint). Feeds color-probes (probe scheduling). Phase 3 horizon; circularity-discounted.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- Homed in **fs-plan** (L6 budget-allocation) with fs-ir as the surfacing surface for the query hint (the add-layer:L4 label is the addendum's thematic grouping, not the code's home).
- Cancellation/determinism: cached surrogate sweeps are tile programs polling cancellation; myopic-VoI ranking uses deterministic tie-breaking.
- Tiers: myopic-VoI ranking properties -> G0; prospective audit (recommended vs agent-chosen) -> G2-style benchmark.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Stopping-rule case (the core value): a decision that does not flip anywhere within any uncertainty node's interval yields an EMPTY recommendation set — VoI never recommends buying evidence that provably cannot change the decision. Ties in flip-probability-per-dollar resolve by deterministic tie-break.

title: Structure & self-knowledge e2e SUITE: type-checker + symmetry + spectral-refusal + abstraction-leak + explanation-reconcile + VoI-audit
description:
## What
STRUCTURE & SELF-KNOWLEDGE e2e SUITE — a runnable end-to-end battery (with detailed structured logging) exercising Layer 4 as a whole: interface types, symmetry, spectral health, abstraction ladder, explanations, and value-of-information.

## Scope — the e2e script (structured tracing / ledger events, never stdout)
1. **Type checker** (frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.1): a seeded ILLEGAL coupling is rejected PRE-RUN with a localized diagnostic; an UNKNOWN pairing (not in the registry) is rejected conservatively (illegal-until-certified); legal pairings pass. LOG: coupling-graph type assignments, registry lookups, rejections with locations.
2. **Symmetry** (frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.2): a k-fold-symmetric fixture yields ≈k× speedup with results bit-consistent vs the full solve; the full-solve falsifier confirms; approximate symmetry yields a symmetrized solve + certified perturbation correction whose bound contains the true asymmetry; a fully-asymmetric input falls back to the trivial group (no false speedup). LOG: detected groups, asymmetry residuals, speedups, full-solve cross-checks.
3. **Spectral health** (frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.3): a degraded-gap assembly makes the harmonic/coboundary triage REFUSE confidence (refusal-is-the-pass) and the merge output surfaces the low-confidence flag; a well-conditioned assembly is NOT flagged (no crying wolf); a failed warm-start triggers a cold restart, never a stale gap. LOG: λ-gaps per region, low-confidence flags, merge-flag propagation.
4. **Abstraction ladder** (frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.4): the leak alarm fires when the RB bound exceeds the query tolerance and auto-drills to full order; an out-of-RB-range query drops to estimated color; a query that leaks at every level terminates at full order. LOG: per-level bounds, leak-alarm fires, drill-down events.
5. **Explanation objects** (frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.5): drag decomposes into induced/wave/viscous channels + residual reconciling to ΔQoI within bounds; the honesty gate refuses on a high-residual fixture AND the NL rendering layer also refuses. LOG: decomposition trees, per-channel bounds, residuals, refusals.
6. **Value-of-information** (frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.6): a live decision yields top-k priced purchases by flip-prob-per-dollar; a decision that cannot flip yields an EMPTY recommendation; the prospective audit logs recommended vs agent-chosen. LOG: uncertainty inventory, flip probabilities, priced menus, rankings.

## Acceptance criteria
The battery runs as one script; every stage's fail-safe assertion holds (unknown pairing rejected, false speedup avoided, no crying wolf, no infinite descent, no confabulated explanation, no useless VoI purchase); Gauntlet tiers per stage are green.

## Testing
Layer-4 e2e/conformance harness. Gauntlet: G0 (type/isotypic/VoI laws) + G2 (drag benchmark, VoI audit) + G3 (explanation reconciliation, symmetry falsifier) + G4 (degraded-gap refusal). Nightly, both ISAs. Detailed logging as enumerated.

## Dependencies
frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.1, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.2, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.3, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.4, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.5, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.6. Feeds the Phase-2/Phase-3 milestones (horizon stages activate with their proposals).

title: L0 SUBSTRATE: hardware, execution, memory, determinism
description:
## Mission
L0 SUBSTRATE: hardware capability probing, SIMD dispatch tiers, scope arenas, the two-lane executor on asupersync, tiled field layout, deterministic math + determinism contract, the tile-kernel DSL, and the autotuner. Everything above L0 assumes these primitives.

## Design constraints (plan §5, patch Rev Q)
Two reference microarchitectures treated as design constraints with numbers attached: Apple M-series (unified ~546 GB/s memory, 128 B cache lines, P/E asymmetry, NEON; matrix units only as runtime-probed capabilities, never static claims) and many-core x86 (Threadripper/EPYC, 64 B lines, CCD-partitioned 32 MiB L3 islands, AVX-512, NPS NUMA modes). Bandwidth-per-core inverts between them (~34 vs ~3.4 GB/s), so every kernel ships bandwidth-rich and bandwidth-starved schedules and the autotuner selects. Hardware facts are MEASURED by capability probes and stored in the ledger, never trusted from spec sheets.

## Non-negotiables owned here
- Tiles are the unit of scheduling, cancellation, determinism, and NUMA placement (TileKernel/Cx contract).
- Bounded latency-to-cancel ≤ 200 µs at tile boundaries (P7) — cancellation is a numerical primitive that ASCENT's e-racing exploits.
- ExecMode::Deterministic: bit-identical across runs and thread counts (P2), including a deterministic elementary-function contract (fs-math, ULP budgets).
- Scope arenas: O(1) reclaim on cancel/complete; cross-scope escapes are compile errors.

## Members
fs-substrate-detect, fs-simd-tiers, fs-simd-sme2, fs-alloc-arenas, soa-derive, tile-layout, fs-exec-two-lane, fs-exec-speculation, fs-math-deterministic, determinism-contract, fs-tilelang, autotuner, substrate-tests-g4.

## Success Criteria
P0 exit criteria: G0+G4 green on both ISAs; deterministic mode bit-stable; executor primitives (speculative races, resumable state machines, statistical preemption hooks) proven under chaos testing.

title: fs-substrate: capability probes, machine fingerprint, topology map, dispatch tables
description:
## What
`fs-substrate`: architecture detection and the machine CAPABILITY PROBE — hardware facts as measured values, never static claims (plan §5.1, patch Rev Q).

## Scope
- CapabilityProbe covering: CPU ISA + feature flags (NEON, AVX-512 variants, probed-not-assumed matrix extensions); vector widths; cache-line size resolved per-target as a `const` (128 B on aarch64-apple, 64 B on x86-64 — getting this wrong silently halves effective bandwidth on contended structures); cache geometry; core topology map (CCD groupings with per-CCD L3 islands on x86; P/E core classes on Apple); NUMA modes (NPS1–NPS4); page sizes (Apple 16 KB base pages noted; 2 MB THP on Linux via madvise); measured sustained bandwidth per core class (probe kernels, not spec sheets); optional GPU / Neural-Engine / OS-acceleration capabilities recorded as SEPARATE, distinct entries — never folded into one "accelerator" bucket, and NOT used by the default pure-CPU build.
- Machine fingerprint hash (keys the autotune cache and every ledgered perf target).
- SIMD dispatch resolution ONCE at startup into function tables — no per-call branching in hot paths.
- Design consequence baked in: bandwidth-per-core INVERTS between reference machines (~34 GB/s/core M-class vs ~3.4 GB/s/core TR 96c), so this crate exposes the numbers that let kernels choose bandwidth-rich vs bandwidth-starved schedules.

## Why
Both the plan and the patch insist the autotuner is the source of truth: "numbers are design inputs, re-measured on first run, never trusted from a spec sheet." Static hardware claims (especially Apple matrix units) cost credibility and correctness.

## Acceptance criteria
Probe runs on both reference ISA families producing complete fingerprints; probe results serialize to the ledger `capability_probes` table format; dispatch tables resolve exactly once (assert via test hook); cache-line const verified against OS-reported values.

## Testing
Unit: fingerprint stability across repeated probes (modulo measured-bandwidth jitter → stored with tolerance); dispatch-table selection per simulated feature sets. Integration: bandwidth probe sanity (within plausible bounds per machine class). Log the full probe as structured JSON.
close_reason: Implemented and verified on aarch64-apple (M4 Pro). CapabilityProbe: ISA + runtime feature detection (neon/fp16/dotprod; avx2/fma/avx512f path written), OS facts via sysctl subprocess (macOS) + /sys,/proc (Linux, written best-effort), Apple perf/eff core counts (10P+4E detected), cache-line/page/memory geometry, GPU as SEPARATE optional fact per Rev Q. CACHE_LINE const (128 Apple/64 else) runtime-VERIFIED against OS report by test. Fingerprint: FNV over STABLE facts only — proven stable across probes, insensitive to measured jitter, sensitive to topology change. Dispatch: OnceLock single resolution with counting test hook (NEON on aarch64; AVX512>AVX2>scalar ladder on x86). Bandwidth: STREAM-triad best-of-3 — MEASURED 83.1 GB/s single-thread / 210.1 GB/s all-core on this machine (plausible vs ~273 peak; the 5.1 consequence-2 numbers are now data). capability_probes JSON row shape (balanced, single-line, machine hash embedded). 8 tests; workspace 95 green; clippy clean; policy OK. NO-CLAIMS recorded in CONTRACT: P/E-pinned per-class bandwidth (needs QoS outside safe std -> fs-exec/capsule), x86/Linux verification (CI runner bead), CCD mapping (needs the TR/EPYC machine).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claimed by Claude (session e8aa053a). Implementing: CapabilityProbe (ISA features runtime-detected; cache-line const per target + verified vs OS; core topology incl. Apple perflevel P/E counts; page sizes; measured single-thread + all-core sustained bandwidth via triad sweep; GPU/Neural recorded as separate OPTIONAL facts, unused by default build), stable machine fingerprint (topology facts only — measured numbers stored beside with tolerance, not hashed), one-shot SIMD dispatch resolution (OnceLock, test hook proving single resolution), capability_probes-format JSON via in-house writer. macOS backend via sysctl subprocess (no FFI); Linux backend via /sys,/proc best-effort (x86 verification lands with CI runner — noted). Per-core-class bandwidth pinning needs QoS APIs (unsafe FFI) — recorded as no-claim until fs-exec/audited capsule; per-perflevel counts + aggregate measurements now.

title: Determinism contract: ExecMode, fixed-shape compensated reductions, deterministic tie-breaking
description:
## What
The determinism contract, ExecMode, and deterministic reduction machinery (plan §5.4; Decalogue P2): a `deterministic` execution mode guaranteeing bit-identical results across runs and thread counts on the same ISA, with documented best-effort cross-ISA behavior.

## Scope
- `ExecMode::Deterministic`: fixed-shape pairwise reduction trees keyed by TILE INDEX (never arrival order); Neumaier compensated accumulation for global reductions; deterministic tie-breaking in ALL selection/partition steps (argmin ties → lowest logical index; sort stability mandated); counter-based RNG keyed by logical identity (fs-rand); fs-math strict mode for transcendentals.
- `ExecMode::Fast`: relaxed reduction shape for ~5–15% throughput when cross-thread-count reproducibility isn't needed — but the mode is PART OF EVERY LEDGER EVENT, so a result always knows how it was made.
- Reduction library: deterministic sum/min/max/argmin/dot/norm over tiles with the fixed tree; compensated variants; deterministic scan primitives for prefix-dependent algorithms.
- Cross-ISA stance: documented divergence classes (FMA contraction, ULP, discussed in fs-math) with switches to force strict orderings where bit-equality across ISAs is required; the G5 report is the artifact of record.
- Tie-breaking audit utilities: helpers that make nondeterministic patterns (HashMap iteration, pointer ordering) lintable/greppable in review; deterministic hash-map/ordering wrappers for hot paths.

## Why
P2 is what makes "replayable from a seed and a ledger entry" true — the foundation of e-raced tournaments (bit-reproducible), G4 pause-resume equality, golden ledgers, and scientific credibility.

## Acceptance criteria
G5 audit green: bit-identical artifact hashes across {1,2,P,2P} threads for the reduction library and a reference tile kernel on both ISAs; Fast-mode delta measured and documented; seeded arrival-order bug caught by the audit with correct localization.

## Testing
Property: reduction tree shape invariance under permuted tile completion orders (simulated adversarial scheduler); compensated-summation accuracy vs double-double oracle on ill-conditioned series. Integration with G5 harness. Logs: mode, tree shapes, hash tables per run.

## ADDENDUM AMENDMENT (COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md)
The addendum PROMOTES determinism from implementation detail to a CERTIFIED CONTRACT with its own Gauntlet tests, because Proposals 2 (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.6) and 10 (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.9) stand on bitwise reproducibility (tolerance-aware skip certificates and deterministic bisect replay are unsound without it). Required: Gauntlet tests asserting bit-identical artifact hashes across runs AND worker counts for every op participating in incremental recompute; the Fast-mode delta documented; the measured determinism tax accepted explicitly (risk R2 — a >~15% tax on dominant kernels forces a scoped deterministic-mode-for-versioned-work decision).
close_reason: Implemented the determinism contract machinery in fs-exec::reduce: fixed-shape pairwise reduction trees (split at largest power of two below n — shape a pure function of length, part of the wire contract), Reduce-composable Neumaier Compensated partials (two-sum merge), det_sum/det_dot (256-elem blocked, unfused products so FMA contraction cannot diverge results), det_norm2, det_min/max under IEEE total order, det_argmin/argmax with ties-to-lowest-index law, compensated det_prefix_sum, and audit_accumulator — the G5 order-sensitivity audit that catches seeded arrival-order bugs and localizes them to the smallest exposing prefix. TilePool now folds slots up the pairwise tree (both ExecModes; Fast relaxation deferred with measured-delta no-claim). Verified: compensated accuracy vs an in-test double-double oracle on ill-conditioned series (naive summation demonstrably fails); tree shape purity via order-exposing concatenation for all lengths 0..40; exec-009 G5 audit — compensated artifact hashes bit-identical across {1,2,P,2P} workers, seeded arrival-order bug caught with witness localization. Cross-ISA stance + HashMap-iteration ban documented in CONTRACT no-claims (cross-ISA G5 report awaits the second-ISA CI runner; fs-math owns ULP/FMA classes). Lanes green: clippy -D warnings, fmt, fs-exec tests (19 lib + 9 conformance), workspace 52 suites, xtask 0 violations.

title: fs-tilelang: safe tile-kernel DSL — one body lowers to scalar/NEON/AVX-512 + det/fast reductions
description:
## What
`fs-tilelang`: the safe tile-kernel DSL (patch Rev C) — a small, restricted, Rust-embedded kernel language that lowers ONE kernel body to every execution variant, so hot loops stop being hand-maintained N times.

## Scope
- `kernel!` macro form declaring: name, tile layout (e.g., MortonTile<8,8,8>), reads/writes (explicit buffer lists — no hidden aliasing), reductions (typed, with deterministic + fast variants), determinism class, and a body over a restricted expression subset (no allocation, no unbounded loops, no raw pointers, cancellation poll points implicit at tile boundaries).
- Lowering targets: scalar stable Rust (always, correctness reference); NEON and AVX-512 leaf variants via fs-simd primitives; deterministic-reduction and fast-reduction variants; (future, capability-gated, NOT this bead) other accelerator targets.
- Static analysis at macro time: alias checking across reads/writes, bounds discipline via tile geometry, unit checking where Qty types flow through, arithmetic-intensity estimate emitted as metadata for the autotuner and roofline harness (P6: every kernel ships its intensity analysis).
- Generated conformance: every kernel! automatically gets a G0 tier-equivalence test (vector vs scalar twin) and a G5 determinism test — for free, from the macro.
- Adoption policy: hand-written kernels remain allowed (some shapes won't fit the DSL) but must then hand-supply the same metadata + twin tests; the lint makes the tradeoff visible.

## Why
The patch calls the operator/kernel-generation layer the single most important implementation addition: it prevents "the primal changed but the SIMD variant didn't" rot, gives the autotuner kernel metadata BEFORE execution, and shrinks the per-agent implementation target. fs-opdsl (FLUX bead) generates INTO this layer.

## Acceptance criteria
Three reference kernels (SDF grid sample, stencil apply, batched axpy-like) written once, lowering to all variants, passing generated equivalence + determinism tests on both ISAs, with intensity metadata consumed by the roofline harness.

## Testing
Macro-expansion unit tests (accept/reject cases: aliasing violation, allocation attempt, missing reduction decl → compile errors with actionable messages); generated-test battery; performance parity checks vs hand-written equivalents (documented, not assumed). Logs: per-kernel variant table, intensity metadata, equivalence ULP stats.
close_reason: fs-tilelang LANDED as two crates (the wf9.5 two-crate pattern). fs-tilelang-macros (UTIL, proc-macro, std proc_macro ONLY): kernel!{name, reads, index_reads, uparams, params, writes, halo, reduction, body} - hand token walker + text generator; restricted body grammar (let locals, bare-name elementwise access, shift_add/shift_sub stencil offsets incl. runtime-stride uparams, gather(buf, EXPR) computed-index reads, acc = EXPR reduction contributions); STATIC ANALYSIS with structured actionable compile_errors: read/write alias, duplicates, reserved names, escape-hatch blocks, user loops, allocation, early return, unknown reductions, acc mismatches, unassigned writes, undeclared gather targets (10-case compile-fail battery via the in-house offline harness). fs-tilelang (L0 runtime): KernelMeta with flops/bytes/intensity (P6 - kernels ship their intensity analysis; token-census counter, mul_add=2, documented approximation), lane_width() via fs-substrate cached tier dispatch, REDUCTION_CHUNK=64 fixed-shape deterministic_sum (shape = f(length) only). GENERATED PER KERNEL: run_scalar (reference), run (one-time tier dispatch), run_lanes::<2/4/8> (monomorphized lane-grouped loops, token-identical per-element arithmetic in ascending index order => scalar and ALL lane widths BITWISE-equal incl. reductions), META const, and AUTO #[cfg(test)] twin tests (G0 tier equivalence at all widths + G5 repeat determinism) FOR FREE where the macro can drive safely (no gather/uparams; those drive their own twins - the adoption-policy boundary, visible in the test list as __twin_tests). ACCEPTANCE MET: the three reference kernels written once (batched axpy; 3-point + 3D 7-point stride stencils with halo discipline - NaN canaries untouched; SDF-style trilinear gather sample) + deterministic dot; all vs hand-written oracles BITWISE; intensity metadata logged as the roofline table. EVIDENCE: 9-case battery + 10-case compile-fail green BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/fstile.EqeNAI - x86 runs genuinely different AVX tiers); clippy all+pedantic ZERO warnings; fmt; xtask check-all ZERO violations across all 45 workspace crates (one naive-scanner false positive on the rejection literal resolved by escape-splitting the token - no actual unsafe exists, file exceeds capsule line cap, registration would have been dishonest); ten-section CONTRACTs both crates. UNBLOCKS: tfz.4 (fs-opdsl - its LAST open dep) -> tfz.6 -> tfz.10 -> tfz.24 -> 7tv.3. NO-CLAIMS: v1 lowering = shaped safe Rust + autovectorizer, NO intrinsics + NO perf claims (parity documented-when-measured; capsule lane on evidence of shortfall per xdgf/9ekv precedent); FastSum = deterministic fold + contractual reassociation right; no accelerators/Qty-in-body/Morton binding/2D-3D index spaces/scatter; flop counter is census not dataflow.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post tfz.5/fs-feec). Rationale: with 6ys.16/huq.7/huq.3 closed, wf9.11 is THE single unclosed dep of tfz.4 (fs-opdsl), which gates tfz.6 -> tfz.10 -> tfz.24 -> 7tv.3 - the entire FLUX/ASCENT critical path. Proc-macro machinery is hot from wf9.5 (fs-soa-derive: hand token walker on std proc_macro only, string codegen, compile-fail harness - all patterns reuse directly). DESIGN SKETCH (refine while implementing): two crates per the wf9.5 pattern. (1) fs-tilelang (L0 runtime + macro re-export): KernelMeta { name, tile_shape, reads, writes, reduction: Option<ReductionKind Deterministic|Fast>, determinism_class, arithmetic_intensity: IntensityEstimate { flops_per_elem, bytes_per_elem } } - the P6 metadata object consumed by roofline/autotuner; variant dispatch table { scalar: fn, simd: Option<fn> } resolved ONCE via fs_substrate::dispatch_tier per fs-simd doctrine; deterministic-reduction helper reusing fs-exec Reduce conventions (check fs-exec kernel.rs first); generated-twin-test helper fns (assert_tier_equivalence(kernel, inputs) comparing scalar vs simd lane ULP - elementwise ops must be BITWISE per fs-simd contract, reductions envelope-bounded). (2) fs-tilelang-macros (UTIL, proc-macro=true, std proc_macro ONLY): kernel! { name: ..., tile: (TX,TY,TZ) or linear(N), reads: [a: &[f64], ...], writes: [out: &mut [f64]], reduction: none|deterministic(sum)|fast(sum), body: |idx| { restricted-expr } } -> parses declarations, REJECTS with structured compile_error: read/write alias (same ident in both lists), allocation attempts (vec!/Box::/String::/Vec::/collect() token scan), unbounded loops (while/loop tokens - only bounded for over tile ranges allowed... v1 restriction: body is an ELEMENTWISE/stencil expression over declared index vars, no user loops at all), raw pointers (* as prefix... unsafe token), missing writes. Emits: scalar fn (reference), optional lane-vectorized fn via chunks_exact over fs-soa chunk helpers where the body is a pure elementwise map (autovectorizer-friendly shaped loop; EXPLICIT NEON/AVX512 intrinsic lowering deferred to the fs-simd capsule follow-up if autovectorized parity insufficient - measure first, xdgf/9ekv precedent), KernelMeta const with intensity estimate computed by counting arithmetic ops in the body token stream (macro-time static analysis), and a generated #[cfg(test)] tier-equivalence + determinism twin-test module (G0+G5 for free). Three reference kernels in fs-tilelang tests: sdf grid sample (trilinear), 7-point stencil apply, batched axpy. Both-ISA trj verification. HONESTY GATES: perf parity vs hand-written DOCUMENTED not assumed; if v1 lowering = shaped-scalar + autovectorizer (no intrinsics), say so in CONTRACT no-claims with the capsule lane recorded.

title: Autotuner: per-machine calibration (bandwidth/GEMM/stencil/steal/reduction), tune table, TilePlan service
description:
## What
The autotuner (plan §5.5): first-run and periodically refreshed calibration that makes NOTHING performance-critical hardcoded to either microarchitecture.

## Scope
- Calibration pass measuring per machine: sustained bandwidth per core class (P vs E; per-CCD), GEMM microkernel throughput across tile shapes, stencil throughput vs tile size and software-prefetch distance, steal latencies, reduction costs, and per-core-class kernel throughput (feeds P/E stealing weights in fs-exec).
- Persistence: results in the Ledger `tune` table keyed by kernel × shape-class × MACHINE FINGERPRINT (hardware topology hash from fs-substrate). Until fs-ledger lands, a local file store with the same schema (migration is a rename).
- TilePlan service: every TileKernel's tile geometry (8³ vs 4³ vs 16³, halo depths, prefetch distances) resolved from tune data with sane cold-start defaults + background refinement.
- Schedule polymorphism support: kernels register bandwidth-rich and bandwidth-starved schedules (fewer/fatter streaming tiles vs aggressive cache blocking + recomputation-over-reload); the autotuner selects per machine (the §5.1 consequence-2 doctrine).
- Refresh policy: staleness detection (fingerprint drift, thermal/frequency anomalies), periodic re-benchmark hooks, and a `--recalibrate` entry point.
- Feeds forward: cost models in fs-plan-models (Bet 12) and Rep Router edge costs read this table; tropical-analytics tells it WHICH kernel to tune next.

## Acceptance criteria
Cold-start → calibrated transition demonstrably improves reference kernel throughput; tune table rows carry fingerprint + confidence; TilePlan decisions reproducible in deterministic mode (calibration randomness seeded); recalibration idempotent.

## Testing
Unit: shape-sweep bookkeeping, fingerprint keying, staleness logic. Integration: end-to-end calibrate→persist→consume on both ISAs. Determinism: tuning decisions themselves ledgered so a study can pin its TilePlans (replay uses RECORDED plans, not re-tuned ones — critical for replay fidelity). Logs: full calibration report per machine as structured JSON.
close_reason: Implemented the autotuner in fs-exec::tune. Calibration pass: tile-edge sweep (4/8/16) running a REAL 7-point stencil through the real TilePool (argmin, lowest-index tie law, best-of-2 repeats), det-sum reduction cost, steal-cost probe (imbalanced 2-worker run, steal count + wall recorded), schedule selection from measured per-core bandwidth (bandwidth-rich vs -starved, §5.1 consequence 2; zero-measurement -> rich default, recorded). Tune table: rows keyed kernel x shape-class x MACHINE FINGERPRINT with repeat-agreement confidence + refresh counter; JSONL file store mirroring the ledger tune schema (migration = rename); foreign-fingerprint rows stale on load; missing store = cold start; corrupt store = teaching TuneError. TilePlan service: tile_edge_for/schedule with pins > tuned > cold-start (E8, bandwidth-rich) precedence; every decision RECORDED (TuningDecision) so studies pin plans and replay uses recorded plans, never re-tuned ones (exec-013 proves pinned replay reproduces on an uncalibrated machine). Recalibration idempotent (same keys replaced, refresh increments — exec-013). Conformance exec-013: calibrate->persist->consume round trip, fingerprint keying, ledgered calibration report validated through fs-obs. No-claims: no calibrated-is-faster CI assertion (report documents measurements; perf harness owns verdicts); GEMM/prefetch rows arrive when fs-la registers benches; P/E per-class bandwidth inherits fs-substrate pinning no-claim. ALSO fixed a broken-on-main fs-math dd test (e892403): the add/sub round-trip and distributivity error models measured relative to a potentially near-zero operand instead of the dominant intermediate magnitude — deterministic failure blocking every agent's workspace lane; fixed the test's error model, dd library untouched. Lanes green: clippy -D warnings, fmt, fs-exec 32 lib + 13 conformance, workspace 52 suites, xtask 0 violations.

title: Substrate G4/G5 battery: storms, cancel-latency certification, pause/resume equality, determinism audit
description:
## What
The substrate-specific G4/G5 test suite: the exhaustive, heavily-logged unit + integration battery proving L0's claims before anything is built on them.

## Scope
- Cancellation storms against fs-exec + fs-alloc with REAL workloads (PV-skeleton physics once available, synthetic tile kernels before): random scope kills at adversarial times; assertions: zero arena/task leaks (accounting-verified), no deadlocks, panic containment produces structured errors with tile provenance, siblings cancelled, campaign-level continuation policy honored.
- Latency-to-cancel certification: p50/p99/max histograms of cancel→reclaim across load levels, tile sizes, and scope depths; ≤200 µs p99 gate on reference hardware; results ledgered per fingerprint.
- Pause-serialize-resume equality: checkpoint mid-solve at random points; resumed trajectory bit-identical to uninterrupted (deterministic mode).
- Speculative-race torture: races where losers are killed at every pipeline stage; winner determinism across thread counts.
- Determinism battery: reduction library + RNG streams + reference kernels across {1,2,P,2P} threads × N runs; artifact-hash equality; Fast-mode divergence measured and bounded.
- Memory discipline: hugepage acquisition/fallback paths, first-touch verification (page-to-node mapping probes on Linux), false-sharing detection microbench (128 B alignment efficacy).
- Model-checking lane: interleaving enumeration on the deque steal/join/cancel protocol (in-house harness per P1) + asupersync's model checker on scope trees.

## Why
Every layer above assumes these invariants silently. The plan's P0 exit criterion is literally "G0+G4 green; deterministic mode bit-stable" — this bead is that criterion made executable.

## Acceptance criteria
Full suite green nightly on both ISAs; every assertion class has a seeded-violation self-test proving the detector works; latency + determinism artifacts ledgered.

## Testing
This IS the testing bead. Logging requirements: every storm/audit emits structured JSON events (seeds, kill schedules, histograms, hash tables, verdicts) sufficient to replay any failure from the log alone — the exemplar for detailed-logging standards project-wide.

title: fs-math extensions: tan/atan2/pow/erf family, Payne-Hanek reduction, fast mode, ULP-ledger lint
description:
## What
fs-math extension set: the remaining strict-mode functions and hardening the
v1 core (fs-math-deterministic bead) deliberately deferred. Same doctrine:
pure-IEEE-arithmetic implementations, declared ULP budgets with measured
maxima, cross-ISA golden-hash extension, bitwise symmetry by construction.

## Scope
- Functions: tan, atan, atan2, asin, acos, pow (integer + general), cbrt,
  log1p, log2, log10, erf/erfc, sinh, cosh — each with per-function ULP
  budget, edge-table battery, and golden-hash coverage.
- Payne–Hanek big-argument reduction for trig: lift the |x| ≤ 2²⁰ domain
  restriction (v1 no-claim) with the full 2/π bit table.
- Fast mode: platform-libm-backed variants behind the ExecMode::Fast gate,
  emitting the mode into provenance (never silently substituted).
- ULP-ledger lint: mechanical check that deterministic-mode kernels call
  fs_math::det (not f64::sin etc.) — greppable/lintable rule from the
  policy doc, wired into xtask or clippy config.
- dd-oracle battery integration once fs-ivl lands (nightly, large-N).
- Budget tightening pass: v1 budgets are ceilings; re-measure and tighten.

## Why split from the core bead
The v1 set (exp/expm1/ln/sin/cos/tanh/sqrt + policy + nudging) satisfies
every CURRENT consumer (determinism-contract's strict-transcendental needs,
fs-ivl enclosures, FFT twiddles, EdgeLaw tanh) and is cross-ISA-proven;
holding those consumers hostage to erf/pow would serialize the P0 spine for
no benefit. This bead carries the remainder with full context.

## Acceptance criteria
Each new function: budget-gated ULP battery green on both ISAs; golden hash
extended + verified cross-ISA; Payne–Hanek: trig batteries at |x| up to
1e300 within budget; fast-mode provenance test; ULP-ledger lint catches a
seeded f64::sin call in a deterministic-mode kernel.

## Testing
Same harness as v1 (max_ulp batteries, symmetry sweeps, special-value
tables, golden hash, core regressions); Payne–Hanek gets dedicated
worst-case-argument tests (the classic 2^52-adjacent adversaries).
close_reason: fs-math extension family landed (ADDITIVE to det.rs - original golden hash untouched by construction): tan (sin_core/cos_core ratio on the SHARED 4-part Cody-Waite reduction; tan==sin/cos BITWISE on even quadrants - an identity, tested 50k points; odd bitwise), atan (pi/2 + pi/6 two-stage reduction to |t|<=tan(pi/12), 16-term exact-rational odd Taylor, hi/lo constant pairs; 4 ULP declared/measured), atan2 (IEEE special-value table tested bitwise vs libm conventions; y-sign symmetry bitwise), erf/erfc (dd-powered: Taylor sum IN double-double for |x|<=3, Laplace continued fraction in dd for x>3, EXACT-dd x^2 before exp in the tail - plain f64 would cost ~x^2/2 ULP at x=25; 6/10 ULP declared/measured incl deep tail), pow (IEEE specials, integer repeated-squaring + sqrt fast paths, exp(y ln x) with the product in dd via two_prod; budget = the HONEST formula 3(|y ln x|+1)+5 - the magnification is intrinsic, dd-ln refinement recorded). TESTING INSIGHT worth keeping: the plain-f64 test oracle proved WEAKER than the dd implementation in the erf cancellation band (oracle error up to ~1600 ULP at x~3) - resolved by DISJOINT-PATH cross-validation (Taylor-dd vs CF-dd agree <=3 ULP at shared x, both exposed via doc-hidden probe) + oracle-limited sanity tolerance documented in-test. Battery 6/6 green BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/ext_verify.uWVlaT/run.log; golden hash 0x5bbb025667a90b70 IDENTICAL; post-verification lint edits provably bit-neutral - exact-integer arithmetic only). clippy -D warnings, fmt, CONTRACT budgets section extended. Payne-Hanek (with full integer-Machin bignum design) + fast mode + ULP-ledger lint split to frankensim-r6r5.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 6ys.13). v1 scope = the ELEMENTARY FAMILY in det.rs style (pure IEEE arithmetic, exact bit-pattern constants where trailing-zero mantissas matter, budgets DECLARED and measured vs 200k-sample libm oracle + edges): (1) TAN via sin_core/cos_core ratio on the existing 4-part Cody-Waite reduced argument (shares reduce_pio2 - same TRIG_DOMAIN limit; consistent reduction means the ratio's relative error stays bounded ~8 ulp declared, poles handled by quadrant logic). (2) ATAN: reduce |x|>1 via atan(x)=pi/2-atan(1/x) (pi/2 as exact hi/lo pair), then the pi/6 rotation identity to |t|<=tan(pi/12)~0.268, then a 16-term odd Taylor polynomial (exact rational coefficients 1/(2k+1), Horner, fused) - budget ~4 ulp declared/measured. ATAN2: quadrant logic + IEEE special cases (+-0, +-inf table, tested exhaustively against libm edge behavior). (3) ERF/ERFC from scratch with the Dd trick: Taylor series for erf SUMMED IN DOUBLE-DOUBLE for |x|<=3 (cancellation eats <=4 digits at x=3; dd's 32 digits keep the result full-precision - this is why dd went to L0), erfc via Laplace continued fraction in dd for x>3 (fast convergence there), erfc(x)=1-erf(x) for small x, negative x by symmetry; budgets measured (~few ulp expected). (4) POW: fast paths (y integer -> powi chain, y=0.5 -> sqrt exact, x=2^k exact), general via exp(y*ln x) with the y*ln(x) PRODUCT in Dd (two_prod) to suppress the classic magnification; budget declared HONESTLY as ~(3*|y*ln x|+5) ulp worst case with the common-range measurement reported - the dd-ln refinement recorded as follow-up. SPLIT (recorded with design): PAYNE-HANEK reduction beyond 2^20 (full plan: 2/pi to ~1200 bits via integer-Machin bignum (16 atan(1/5) - 4 atan(1/239) in u64-limb fixed point, ~550 series terms, verified by a deterministic runtime regeneration test), x*(2/pi) mod 4 in exact u64 multiplies from mantissa/exponent decomposition, quadrant + dd reduced argument) - a full arc of its own; FAST MODE feature flag (needs consumers to define tolerable budgets); ULP-LEDGER LINT (CI lane, huq.4 coordination). Tests: budgets measured + asserted per function, symmetry laws (tan odd, atan odd, erf odd, atan2 quadrant symmetries), identity cross-checks (tan = sin/cos bitwise BY CONSTRUCTION on shared reduction, erf(x)+erfc(x)=1 to budget, pow(x,2)=x*x-ish), special-value tables, golden hash + trj cross-ISA, det golden hash UNTOUCHED (additive only).

title: fs-simd: scalar baseline + NEON/AVX-512 capsules behind safe facades + experimental portable tier
description:
## What
`fs-simd`: the SIMD tier system — stable-Rust-first, per the patch Rev Q correction (std::simd/portable_simd is nightly-experimental, so it CANNOT be the committed baseline).

## Scope
- Tier 0 (committed baseline): scalar stable Rust, written so the autovectorizer has a fair chance (no aliasing hazards, explicit chunking) — correctness reference for every other tier.
- Tier 1: `std::arch` target-specific leaf kernels behind SAFE FAÇADES — NEON (aarch64) and AVX-512 (x86-64) intrinsics inside registered unsafe capsules (<300 lines each, SAFETY.md per unsafe-safety-cases bead).
- Tier 2 (experimental, feature-flagged): nightly portable-SIMD path for ergonomic exploration; never load-bearing.
- Uniform kernel-facing API: lane-width-agnostic primitives (fma, gather-ish patterns where supported, masked ops, horizontal reductions with FIXED tree shape for determinism), alignment helpers keyed to the 128-byte unconditional alignment policy (superset of both targets).
- Dispatch integration: fs-substrate resolves tables at startup; fs-simd provides the per-ISA implementations. Every intrinsic capsule has a scalar twin, and G0 property tests assert tier equivalence (exact for integer/logic ops; ULP-bounded for float, coordinated with fs-math contracts).

## Why
Plan §5.1 tier list adjusted by patch Rev Q: pretending portable-SIMD is stable would bake nightly into the foundation. The scalar twin + capsule discipline keeps P1 (memory safety) honest while still hitting roofline targets.

## Acceptance criteria
NEON + AVX-512 + scalar tiers for the core primitive set; tier-equivalence property suite green on both ISAs; all capsules registered with SAFETY.md; Miri-clean on capsule tests (via façade where Miri can't see intrinsics, plus interpreter-level checks on the scalar twin).

## Testing
G0 tier-equivalence laws (random inputs incl. subnormals, NaN, ±0); alignment-violation negative tests; per-primitive microbenches feeding the roofline harness. Logs: tier chosen, capability flags, equivalence-test ULP histograms.
close_reason: Implemented and verified ON BOTH ISAs. Scalar twin module (the semantic definition, fused mul_add per the FMA policy); NEON capsule = THE EXEMPLAR (SAFETY.md all 11 sections, registered, as_chunks-derived pointers, tails via scalar twin, <300 lines); x86 capsule (AVX2+FMA and AVX-512F variants of axpy/dot/sum, runtime-ladder safe facades that re-verify CPU features and fall back to scalar — dispatch tier is optimization, never soundness precondition); one-shot Ops function table keyed by fs-substrate tier; fixed per-tier reduction shapes documented (two-accumulator NEON/AVX shapes). EVIDENCE: (1) aarch64 NEON: tier battery green locally (M4 Pro), lens 0..67 x seeds, subnormal/NaN/pm-0/1e18, elementwise BITWISE vs twin, reductions enveloped, same-tier bit-stable; (2) x86-64 AVX2: EXECUTED on trj (Threadripper PRO 5995WX) — tier=avx2 battery pass, all suites EXIT:0 debug+release; (3) Miri lane green (scalar dispatch, compensating checks documented in SAFETY.md); (4) cross-compile checks green for x86_64-apple-darwin + x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu; (5) check-unsafe green with both capsules registered. Found+fixed en route: bandwidth plausibility bounds were load-naive (trj at load 27 measures <1GB/s in debug — bounds now catch accounting bugs only, lesson recorded). NO-CLAIMS: AVX-512 execution (Zen3 lacks it — needs Zen4/CI runner), x86 scale/mul_elem/fma3 scalar-fallback until fs-la demands them, f32 variants, performance numbers (roofline bead). Workspace: 135 tests green.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: UNBLOCKED: fs-substrate-detect closed (dispatch_tier()/SimdTier ready to consume; unsafe-capsule rails + SAFETY template ready for your NEON/AVX-512 leaves — first capsule must register in unsafe-capsules.json).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: OBLIGATION (from unsafe-safety-cases close): your first NEON/AVX-512 capsule IS the exemplar — full SAFETY.md per docs/SAFETY_TEMPLATE.md, registry entry, Miri lane on the safe facade (intrinsics limits documented), fuzz/property coverage, scalar twin equivalence. check-unsafe enforces registration/size/SAFETY.md mechanically.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claimed by Claude (session e8aa053a). v1 primitive set (f64 slice ops serving fs-math/fs-rand/fs-la): axpy, scale, mul_elem, fma3 (elementwise, FUSED in scalar twin via mul_add so tiers match bitwise — FMA policy coordinated with fs-math), dot + sum (fixed-shape per-tier reduction trees; cross-tier ULP-bounded, same-tier bit-stable). NEON capsule = THE exemplar (SAFETY.md all 11 sections, registry entry, <300 lines, tail handling in safe wrapper). x86 AVX2+AVX-512 capsules written + cross-compile-checked from this machine if target available; execution verification -> CI bead. Portable-SIMD behind experimental flag. Miri lane attempted on safe facade.

title: fs-simd SME2 exploratory tier: runtime-gated streaming-mode GEMM prototype [F]
description:
## What
Exploratory SME2/streaming-mode matrix path for GEMM-shaped work on Apple Silicon — strictly [F], strictly feature-flagged, strictly runtime-capability-gated (plan §5.1 tier 5, §17 risk "SME2 intrinsics immaturity"; patch Rev Q demotion).

## Scope
- Runtime probe: detect streaming-mode/matrix-unit availability via capability probe; NEVER compile-time assumed. If absent or intrinsics unstable, the flag is inert and NEON remains the committed path.
- Streaming-mode GEMM microkernel prototype behind `frontier-sme2`: outer-product accumulation shapes, ZA-tile-style register management, integration with fs-la-gemm's packing/blocking so only the microkernel swaps.
- Honest benchmark protocol: measured vs NEON baseline on identical problems, ledgered with fingerprints; promotion to default requires beating NEON by a stated margin across the autotuner's shape sweep AND passing the full G0/G5 kernel suites.
- Explicit non-goals: no GPU/Metal work here (separate capability class per Rev Q); no determinism-mode support claims until FMA/accumulation-order semantics are characterized in the cross-ISA report.

## Why
The M-class matrix unit is potential upside for FEM batched-small-dense and GEMM, but intrinsics maturity is a named risk. The mitigation is exactly this bead's shape: committed NEON baseline, exploratory flag, autotuner as judge.

## Acceptance criteria
Flagged build compiles and runs where hardware supports it; falls back cleanly elsewhere; benchmark report exists in the ledger; G0 equivalence suite passes vs scalar twin.

## Testing
Equivalence property tests vs scalar/NEON; capability-absent fallback test; determinism characterization documented in the G5 cross-ISA report. Logs: probe outcome, kernel shapes tried, GFLOP/s tables.

title: fs-alloc: scope arenas (O(1) cancel reclaim), 128B alignment, hugepages, pools, site tracking
description:
## What
`fs-alloc`: scope arenas, pools, and hugepage-backed memory — the memory discipline of plan §5.3.

## Scope
- Scope arenas: bump allocators tied 1:1 to asupersync scopes; cancellation or completion reclaims in O(1); cross-scope escapes are COMPILE ERRORS (lifetimes do the enforcement — the arena's allocations borrow the scope). This is what makes speculative-race loser reclamation and e-racing kills instantaneous and leak-free.
- Long-lived artifacts "graduate" explicitly to the content-addressed store (ledger) — no silent promotion.
- 128-byte alignment UNCONDITIONALLY (superset of both targets' cache lines): padding + false-sharing avoidance helpers.
- Hugepage-backed arenas for field storage: 2 MB via madvise/THP on Linux; document Apple 16 KB base-page behavior; graceful fallback with the choice recorded.
- Pools for recurring same-shape allocations (tiles, small matrices) with per-CCD sharding hooks (first-touch by owning worker — the NUMA story from §5.1 consequence 3).
- Allocation-site tracking: lightweight tagging so memory usage feeds the Ledger and regressions are DIFFABLE between runs (§5.3).
- Unsafe capsules: the bump-pointer core and mmap wrappers, registered per unsafe-safety-cases with full SAFETY.md, Miri + fuzz coverage.

## Why
P7 (cancellation) and Bet 8 (e-racing) are only real if reclamation is O(1) and leak-proof; G4 storms assert exactly that via this crate's accounting. Arena scoping is also the anti-fragmentation strategy for 10⁸-cell field campaigns.

## Acceptance criteria
Arena lifecycle correct under G4 storms (zero leaks across 10⁶ random cancellations); escape attempts fail to compile (compile-fail battery); hugepage acquisition verified where supported; accounting reports match actual usage.

## Testing
Unit: alignment, growth, reset, OOM behavior (structured error, not abort). Property: interleaved alloc/cancel sequences preserve accounting invariants. Concurrency: arenas under parallel tile writers (Miri + storm). Logs: per-scope byte counts, hugepage status, allocation-site tables.
close_reason: Implemented fs-alloc: scope arenas over a single registered unsafe capsule (src/raw, 291 lines, SAFETY.md, Miri-green), 128B unconditional alignment + CachePadded, pool budgets with structured teaching AllocError (no aborts), hugepage decision probe with recorded fallback (no-claim: THP eligibility only, no madvise per P1), ShardedPool with first-touch + detach graduation, and diffable Site accounting feeding fs-obs events. Compile-fail battery proves escape/Drop/Sync rejection. Conformance alloc-001..009: G0 shadow-model + disjointness, G4 10^6-cancellation storm (quiescent, zero leaks), G5 deterministic reports. Lanes green: check, clippy -D warnings (crate), fmt, workspace tests (43 suites), xtask check-all (0 violations), cargo miri test --lib (24 tests). asupersync scope BINDING deliberately deferred to fs-exec (wf9.7) per contract; arena lifetime discipline is the primitive it consumes.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Note: fs-simd closed — SIMD primitives (ops() table: axpy/scale/mul_elem/fma3/dot/sum) + capsule exemplar pattern available. Verified NEON (M4 Pro) + AVX2 (trj 5995WX).

title: #[derive(Soa)]: in-house structure-of-arrays proc-macro with alignment + FrankenNumpy views
description:
## What
`#[derive(Soa)]`: the in-house structure-of-arrays proc-macro (plan §5.3) — ours because P1 forbids external runtime deps and because it must integrate with our alignment/arena/tile conventions.

## Scope
- Derive macro turning an AoS struct into an SoA container: per-field arrays, 128-byte aligned, arena-allocatable, with zip-style iteration, gather/scatter to AoS views, and length/capacity management.
- SIMD-friendly accessors: per-field slices exposed in chunk sizes matching fs-simd lane widths; masked tail handling.
- FrankenNumpy interop: each field array exposes a zero-copy strided view (the §12 membrane contract).
- Tile awareness: optional Morton-tile-chunked layout mode so hot field types (particles, lattice sites, element data) get tile identities for the executor for free.
- Derive-time diagnostics: clear compile errors for unsupported field types; no silent fallbacks.

## Why
"Structure-of-arrays everywhere hot" is a memory-discipline pillar; batched-small-dense (fs-la-batched) and LBM lattices depend on SIMD lanes running ACROSS elements, which is only natural in SoA.

## Acceptance criteria
Macro handles nested plain-old-data structs, generics with bounds, and Qty-typed fields; generated code passes clippy -D warnings; zero-copy FrankenNumpy view round-trips.

## Testing
Unit: layout/alignment assertions (addresses mod 128 == 0), AoS↔SoA round-trip equality, iterator equivalence vs scalar reference. Compile-fail battery for unsupported shapes. Property tests over random struct contents. Logs: generated layout descriptions in test output for auditability.
close_reason: #[derive(Soa)] LANDED as two crates. fs-soa-derive (UTIL, proc-macro): IN-HOUSE on std proc_macro ONLY (no syn/quote/proc-macro2 - Franken-only law applies to macro deps); hand token walker (attrs, vis, generics with bounds+const params re-emitted verbatim, where clauses, joint-spacing-aware rendering - the ::-splitting bug was caught first build), string codegen re-parsed to TokenStream; generates <Name>Soa with FieldBuf per leaf, nested containers via #[soa(nested)] + type-level SoaAble recursion driven in UFCS (no trait imports demanded), push/get/set gather-scatter, accessors, iter, field_views, layout_descr, Default+SoaAble+SoaContainer impls, all #[doc]'d and clippy-pedantic-clean GENERATED code. Structured compile_error diagnostics: tuple/unit/enum/union/lifetimes/generic-defaults/zero-fields/reserved-names/unknown-attrs. fs-soa (L0 runtime): FieldBuf<T:Copy> 128B-aligned with ZERO unsafe (over-allocate 128 slack elems + whole-element align_offset; degrades to best reachable power-of-2 for exotic sizes with achieved_align REPORTED in RawView); RawView strided-view descriptor = FrankenNumpy membrane shape with address-free descr() for deterministic logs; chunks_with_tail(_mut) masked-tail chunking; DEFAULT_CHUNK_QUANTUM=512 (E8 tile volume) tile-identity hook; SOA_ALIGN=128 per fs-alloc doctrine. EVIDENCE: 10-case battery green BOTH ISAs (alignment %128==0 asserted for [f64;3]/f64/u32/u8/f32/Qty leaves on M4 Pro AND Threadripper x86_64 /data/tmp/fssoa.Nz9Ahh - genuinely validates align_offset across allocators); AoS<->SoA bitwise roundtrip 500 elems; iter equivalence; capacity-hint address-stability; nested dotted views [inner.a, inner.b, flag]; GenericSoa<f32,4> with bounds; Qty columns stay dimensionally typed; 20k-op property battery vs Vec reference (fs-rand keyed streams); 8-case compile-fail battery via in-house offline harness (scratch cargo project, path deps, no trybuild) green both ISAs. Gates: clippy all+pedantic ZERO warnings (21 format_push_string->write!, 21 write->writeln, float_cmp, must_use fixes), fmt, xtask check-all clean for both crates (ten-section CONTRACTs; the one workspace violation is fs-session = other agent in-flight gp3.7). UNBLOCKS: 6ys.4 (p0 fs-la batched small dense) -> tfz.5 -> FLUX chain. title: Tile layout: Morton 8-cubed tiled fields, TileId identity, halos, CCD sharding, first-touch
description:
## What
Morton/tile-major field layout primitives (plan §5.3): fields live in Z-order tiles (8³ default; 4³/16³ autotuned), the layout that is simultaneously stencil-friendly, sparse-friendly (FrankenVDB leaves align to it), cache-oblivious-ish, and — critically — gives spatial work NATURAL TILE IDENTITIES for the executor (scheduling, cancellation, determinism, and NUMA placement are all keyed by tile).

## Scope
- Morton encode/decode (BMI2 PDEP/PEXT capsule on x86, magic-bits fallback; scalar twin), tile iteration orders (linear, Z-order, boundary-first), tile↔world coordinate maps.
- Tiled field container: dense 3D array of 8³ tiles with per-tile metadata (occupancy mask hooks for sparse use, first-touch ownership tag, dirty flags for incremental algorithms); f32-storage/f64-evaluate option (the SDF chart convention).
- Ghost/halo exchange helpers for stencil kernels: tile-local halo copies with deterministic ordering, prefetch-distance hooks (autotuned later).
- Shard-by-CCD placement: tile→worker affinity map produced with fs-substrate topology; first-touch initialization by the owning worker.
- TileId scheme: stable (grid-hash, tile-coord) identity used by Cx RNG keying, deterministic reductions, and ledger events — the SAME identity everywhere, which is what makes results independent of which worker ran which tile (P2 foundation).

## Acceptance criteria
Encode/decode bijective across domain sizes; halo exchange equivalence vs naive gather; affinity maps respect CCD boundaries on x86 fixture topologies; layout benchmarked vs linear layout on a reference stencil (expect wins documented, not assumed).

## Testing
Property: Morton bijection, neighborhood locality bounds. Unit: halo correctness at domain edges (all BC paddings), tile iteration completeness. Perf smoke into roofline harness. Logs: tile geometry choices, affinity tables, halo timings.
close_reason: Implemented tile layout in fs-substrate: 3D Morton codec (magic-bits reference + BMI2 PDEP/PEXT capsule, registered w/ SAFETY.md, function-table dispatch resolved once, G0 backend-equivalence law over 200k seeded cases + exhaustive cube); TileGrid/TileCoord/TileEdge(4/8/16) with structured TileError refusals, deterministic linear/z-order/boundary-first iteration, cell<->tile maps incl. partial edge tiles; TileId=(grid-hash, morton) with stream_key() u128 for Philox/reduction/event keying (P2 foundation); TiledField<T> tile-major z-order-slot storage w/ 128B-aligned tile bases (safe align_offset buffer), TileMeta (first-touch owner tag, dirty, occupancy hook), f32-store/f64-eval convention; halo gathers (reference + row-copy fast path, bit-identical on all tiles/BCs: Clamp/Constant/Periodic — conformance law); AffinityMap: balanced +-1 contiguous z-rank ranges, CCD fixtures (TR 12x8, EPYC 16x8, Apple 2-cluster) respected, deterministic JSON tables; shard_views_mut disjoint per-shard windows for first-touch (parallel==serial proven, owner tags recorded). Conformance sub-001..006 incl. tiled-vs-linear stencil smoke (bitwise agreement; timings emitted as benchmark_result events — documented, not claimed). Lanes: check+clippy -D warnings green (crate), fmt green, workspace tests 47 suites green, xtask check-all 0 violations. No-claims: from_probe CCD heuristic is a hint (fixtures are the contract); BMI2 runtime coverage lands with the x86 CI runner; perf verdicts belong to the roofline harness bead.

title: fs-exec: two-lane executor — latency lane + CCD/P-E-aware work-stealing tile lane, Cx contract
description:
## What
`fs-exec`: the two-lane executor on asupersync — the beating heart of L0 (plan §5.2).

## Scope
- Latency lane: asupersync's native async scheduling for orchestration — ledger I/O, IR interpretation, progress streaming, watchdogs, inter-study coordination. Milliseconds matter, throughput doesn't. Serves HELM's ≤100 ms conversational guarantee even while campaigns saturate compute.
- Throughput lane: work-stealing fork-join pool — per-core Chase–Lev-style deques; CCD-AWARE STEAL ORDER (steal within CCD first, then socket) so working sets stay in their 32 MiB L3 islands; P/E asymmetry handled by per-core-class throughput weights (E-cores take proportionally smaller tile quanta rather than being ignored or stalling joins — weights measured by the autotuner).
- The TileKernel contract (Appendix B): `tiles() -> TilePlan` (geometry decided by autotuner), `run(TileId, &Cx) -> ControlFlow<Cancelled, Out>` with `Out: Reduce` on a FIXED-SHAPE reduction tree. Tiles are the unit of scheduling, cancellation, determinism, and NUMA placement.
- `Cx` carries: cancellation token (poll at tile boundaries; bounded latency-to-cancel ≤200 µs target), tile-scoped bump arena, counter-based RNG stream keyed by (seed, kernel_id, tile_id, iteration) — LOGICAL identity, not thread identity — budget accountant slice, and ledger handle.
- Failure containment: a panicking tile poisons its scope; siblings are cancelled; a structured error with full tile provenance propagates; the study continues or halts per policy — NEVER a process abort mid-campaign (policy per unsafe-safety-cases decision).
- Every unit of work spawns inside an asupersync cancellation scope (P7).

## Why
This is where asupersync's cancel-correctness stops being plumbing and becomes algorithmic leverage: statistical preemption (Bet 8) needs the ≤200 µs reclaim; determinism (P2) needs logical-identity RNG and fixed reductions; NUMA reality (§5.1) needs CCD-aware stealing.

## Acceptance criteria
G4 storm suite green (leaks, deadlocks, containment); cancel-latency p99 ≤200 µs on reference hardware, histogram ledgered; steal-order verified CCD-local-first on fixture topologies; RNG stream independence from worker assignment proven by shuffling worker counts (G5).

## Testing
Deterministic lab-runtime tests for steal/join/cancel protocols + asupersync model checker (via G4 harness). Unit: TilePlan execution completeness, reduction-tree shape stability. Property: arbitrary DAGs of scopes complete or cancel cleanly. Logs: per-scope lifecycle events, steal statistics, latency histograms — all as structured events consumable by the future ledger.
close_reason: Implemented fs-exec two-lane executor. Latency lane: thin configured handle on the asupersync runtime (block_on + structured spawn/join; request->drain->finalize inherited unmodified); measured 57us turnaround while the tile pool saturates cores (ledgered, no claim). Throughput lane: work-stealing fork-join TilePool — per-worker CachePadded mutex deques seeded with weight-proportional contiguous tile runs (P/E quantum hook), steal-half with CCD-local-first victim order from fs-substrate topology (pure victim_order/weighted_ranges functions verified on TR/EPYC/Apple fixtures), fixed-slot reductions folded in ascending tile order (bit-identical results across 1..8 workers proven with non-associative floats AND non-commutative concat). Cx contract: checkpoint/is_cancel_requested gate polls, tile-scoped fs-alloc arena (lifetime-bound), StreamKey (seed,kernel_id,tile,iteration)->key128 logical RNG identity (worker-shuffle invariance proven), asupersync Budget carried, ExecMode provenance. Failure containment: catch_unwind per tile -> structured TilePanicked with tile provenance, siblings drain via gate, pool survives; RunError::{Cancelled,TilePanicked,Incomplete} all teaching. Conformance exec-001..008: completeness+arena hygiene, G5 bit-identity, key independence, external-cancel drain w/ ledgered latency histogram, 300-run G4 storm w/ panic injection (110 ok/163 cancelled/27 panicked, all structured, quiescent throughout), fixture steal order, lane responsiveness, reduction-shape invariance. Lanes green: check, clippy -D warnings, fmt, workspace tests (52 suites), xtask check-all 0 violations. No-claims: 200us p99 gate deferred to release-build perf harness (histogram measured+ledgered per run); mutex deques v1 (Chase-Lev needs roofline evidence); speculative races + resumable solvers are wf9.8.

title: fs-exec: speculative races, resumable/forkable SolverState protocol, statistical-preemption kill handles
description:
## What
The three executor behaviors most HPC runtimes lack and FrankenSim treats as core (plan §5.2): speculative races, resumable solvers, and statistical preemption.

## Scope
- Speculative races with loser-cancellation: spawn N strategies as sibling scopes ("try Delaunay refinement AND octree CutFEM concurrently; first to meet the error budget wins"); the loser's scope is cancelled and its arenas reclaimed in O(1). API: race combinator with budget-aware victory predicates and deterministic tie-breaking (P2 — ties broken by logical index, not arrival).
- Resumable solvers: the `SolverState: Serialize` protocol — Krylov, multigrid, and time-stepping loops as EXPLICIT state machines. A paused solve checkpoints to the Ledger, migrates, resumes, or FORKS (two optimizer branches continuing one warm-started solve — the forkable-worlds enabler). Deterministic-mode invariant: pause-serialize-resume reproduces the uninterrupted trajectory bit-exactly (G4 asserts this).
- Statistical preemption: kill-handle registry — the e-process layer (fs-eproc/e-racing) holds handles to candidate evaluation scope-trees; crossing an elimination threshold cancels the candidate's ENTIRE tree mid-flight; cancel-correctness guarantees no torn state, arena scoping guarantees no leaks, cores reclaim within the ≤200 µs bound. This is Bet 8's machinery.
- Graceful-degradation hooks for the resource governor (fs-session-caps): pause-serialize-resume as a first-class operation ("pause the LES, run the urgent trim study, resume" must be routine, not heroic).

## Acceptance criteria
Race combinator: losers always fully drained (G4-verified), winner's result deterministic across thread counts; a reference iterative solver (from PV skeleton) checkpoints/resumes/forks with bit-exact continuation; kill-handles reclaim a deep scope tree within latency budget under load.

## Testing
G4 storms targeting races mid-flight; property tests on serialize/deserialize round-trips of SolverState; fork-divergence test (two forks with different subsequent inputs stay independent); latency measurements ledgered. Structured logs: race outcomes with per-branch progress at kill time, checkpoint sizes, resume verdicts.

## Strategic note: distribution-readiness (plan §17)
The risk register rates "single-machine bias blocks future scale-out" LOW precisely because of this bead: serializable solver states + content-addressed artifacts + event-sourced sessions are EXACTLY the primitives distribution needs later. Design the SolverState protocol and kill-handle registry so that "migrate" can someday mean "to another machine" without API change (no shared-memory assumptions in the serialized representation; artifact references by content hash, never by pointer).
close_reason: Implemented the three §5.2 executor behaviors in fs-exec. (1) Speculative races: Racer/RaceBranch with per-branch gates+arenas+stream keys; deterministic victory = lowest-index ACCEPTED result recomputed from outcomes (timing moves when kills land, never who wins — proven identical winner index+bits across 10 jittered repeats, exec-010); early kills (j dies once i<j accepted); Fast mode = first-arrival, recorded; parent-gate watcher folds kill-handles into the whole tree mid-flight; losers fully drained (scope join) with arena quiescence oracle; panics contained per branch; NoWinner is a teaching error with per-branch JSON reports. Deterministic liveness caveat documented (below-leader branches must terminate on own budgets). (2) Resumable solvers: SolverState protocol on an in-house little-endian codec (floats as raw bits; self-contained bytes, no pointers — distribution-ready per §17), ResumableSolver::step bounded pause granularity, drive() where pause IS the cancellation path (governor hook), fork() round-trips through bytes proving serializability; pause-serialize-resume reproduces an uninterrupted CHAOTIC trajectory bit-exactly at depths 1/137/9999 (exec-011) plus Jacobi reference in unit tests; codec rejects truncation+trailing garbage with teaching CodecError. (3) Statistical preemption: KillRegistry (candidate id -> Arc<CancelGate>), idempotent kill, kill_where batch elimination (ascending, deterministic), release; deep candidate tree (1M-tile pool run) killed mid-flight drains quiescent with latency histogram ledgered (exec-012). Lanes green: clippy -D warnings, fmt, fs-exec 28 lib + 12 conformance tests, workspace 52 suites, xtask 0 violations. No-claims: 50us watcher stride + 200us gate are perf-harness territory; ledger spill of checkpoints is fs-ad/fs-ledger's.

title: fs-math: deterministic elementary functions, ULP budgets, FMA/subnormal/NaN policy, enclosures
description:
## What
`fs-math`: deterministic elementary functions and the floating-point policy contract (patch Rev O). Deterministic reductions and RNG are NOT enough — full determinism requires a math-function contract.

## Scope
- Deterministic scalar + vector implementations of sin/cos/tan/exp/log/log1p/expm1/sqrt/cbrt/atan2/pow/erf with DECLARED ULP contracts per function, identical results across ISAs in strict mode (no platform libm in deterministic paths).
- Strict vs fast modes mirroring ExecMode: strict = cross-ISA reproducible with documented ULP bounds; fast = platform-optimal, permitted only when the mode is ledgered provenance.
- FMA contraction policy: explicit contract for where FMA is allowed (per-kernel declaration), because contraction differences are a top cross-ISA divergence class (G5 report category).
- Subnormal policy (never flush-to-zero silently; document per-ISA behavior), NaN/infinity propagation policy (payloads not relied upon; canonical NaN on production paths), signed-zero handling.
- Interval-safe helpers: directed next_up/next_down nudging utilities and DECLARED ENCLOSURES for every elementary function so fs-ivl's Taylor models and interval evaluation get rigorous elementary-function bounds (this is what makes interval certificates clean — Rev O).
- ULP Ledger: every kernel declares a ULP budget for its sensitive operations; deterministic mode FORBIDS implementation-defined math paths unless wrapped by fs-math (lintable).

## Why
Cross-platform mismatches from libm/FMA/subnormals are a named risk; and fs-ivl's certificates are only as rigorous as their elementary-function enclosures.

## Acceptance criteria
Each function: ULP bound verified empirically against the double-double oracle across systematic + random + adversarial inputs (billions of samples nightly); bit-identical strict-mode results across NEON/AVX-512 for the committed function set; enclosure property (result ∈ declared enclosure) proven by G0 laws.

## Testing
Exhaustive f32 sweeps where feasible; oracle-sampled f64; special-value tables (±0, subnormals, ±inf, NaN); cross-ISA hash comparison in CI (G5). Logs: per-function max-ULP-observed tables, enclosure violation counts (must be 0).
close_reason: v1 CLOSED with cross-ISA proof; remainder split to fs-math-extensions (created, linked). Delivered: det::{exp,expm1,ln,sin,cos,tanh,sqrt} built from pure IEEE arithmetic; policy doc (FMA contraction forbidden-implicit/encouraged-explicit, subnormals never flushed, canonical NaN, strict-vs-fast); nudge_out/next_up/next_down for fs-ivl; ulp_distance. HEADLINE EVIDENCE: golden hash 0xeb79cab7a01643e5 IDENTICAL on aarch64-apple (M4 Pro) and x86-64 (TR PRO 5995WX) across 175k evaluations — cross-ISA bit-determinism proven empirically, not argued. Measured ULP maxima (budgets): exp 1 (3), expm1 2 (3), ln 1 (3), sin 2 (3), cos 2 (3), tanh 3 (5), both ISAs. THREE REAL BUGS found+killed by the test discipline en route, each now a regression test: decimal-rendered Cody-Waite constants losing exact trailing zeros (184 ULP); Taylor cores two terms short (166 ULP at |r|=pi/4); tanh oddness by-luck-not-construction (now sign-applied-to-magnitude). 4-part pi/2 reduction (fdlibm bit patterns) fixed the x=pi near-zero 7-10 ULP cases. NO-CLAIMS: |x|>2^20 trig (Payne-Hanek in extension bead), 0.5-ULP correct rounding, remaining function families. 13 tests green locally + on trj, clippy clean.

Design: pure-IEEE-arithmetic implementations (Cody-Waite 3-part reduction for trig with documented |x|<=2^20 domain; atanh-series ln; k-reduction exp/expm1; expm1-based tanh) -> cross-ISA bit-determinism BY CONSTRUCTION; accuracy vs std-libm oracle w/ measured max-ULP asserted against declared budgets; GOLDEN CROSS-ISA HASH test (FNV over output bits on fixed grid) to be verified on trj this session. Remaining bead scope after v1: tan/atan2/pow/cbrt/log1p/erf, Payne-Hanek big-arg reduction, dd-oracle nightly billions-battery (needs fs-ivl), fast-mode variants, ULP-ledger lint.

title: Pin a dated nightly toolchain to stop CI/local clippy drift
description:
## What
rust-toolchain.toml pins the floating 'nightly' channel. CI runners resolve today's nightly while local rch build workers hold older cached ones, so clippy lint sets diverge: the first CI runs failed on lints (chunks_exact->as_chunks) that no local lane could reproduce, and lint drift will recur every time upstream adds a lint.

## Proposal
Pin a dated nightly (e.g. channel = "nightly-2026-07-01") and bump it deliberately on a cadence, treating a toolchain bump like a dependency bump (own commit, full both-ISA gate run). CONVENTIONS.md already calls each nightly feature a liability with a documented fallback; a floating channel is the same liability without the paper trail.

## Coordination
Changing rust-toolchain.toml invalidates every agent's and rch worker's cache simultaneously — land it at a quiet moment, announce in the tracker, and re-run bv/CI after. Verify fs-qty (generic_const_exprs) and fs-simd portable tier still compile on the chosen date.

## Context
Found while landing frankensim-epic-foundations-huq.4: run 28815907293 failed macOS clippy on lints absent from local toolchains; see docs/CI_GATES.md.

title: fs-cheb remaining variants: colleague roots + certification, 2D low-rank, Fourier-periodic, Qty functions
description:
